gmw_00001647.name: NGC 4526
gmw_00001647.overview: NGC 4526 is a highly inclined, barred lenticular galaxy located in the foreground of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/9907345, arXiv:1310.2607]. It features a filamentary dust disk and a dynamically cold central stellar disk that host ongoing star formation [arXiv:0709.3987, arXiv:1507.05094].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4526 as a Virgo lenticular with a well-developed nearly edge-on CO disk, unusually broad CO profile, dust rings, and gas aligned with the stellar body.

quotes.quote: In NGC 4526 the CO lies in a well-developed, nearly edge-on disk. The integrated CO profile shows the familiar double-horned shape (Figure n4526spect ), with both horns approximately equally bright. The systemic velocity of the galaxy is 613 \ 10 \ and emission is detected over a total width of 683 . A line width of this magnitude, while not unknown, is unusually large for lenticulars.

Individual channel maps (Figure n4526chans ) show a close correspondence between the CO and dust disks. Unsharp-masked HST images show that the dust is confined to radii less than 14 \ - 15 . Although the inclination is quite high, there appear to be at least two dominant dust rings of radii 14 \ and 10 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4526 as an outlier whose inner dust lane and young stellar disk redden its [3.6]-[4.5] color and alter escape-velocity trends.

quotes.quote: An outlier in Fig. mass_met8 is NGC 4526. It is instructive to study its colour profile, to understand why this galaxy is an outlier. In Fig. n4526 one can see that the galaxy has a regular colour profile, the colour becoming redder slowly going outward, starting at about 12 '' .

The galaxy has a disk in the inner 12 '' , which can be seen in extinction in the continuum image. The disk also contains younger stellar populations, which can be seen from its high values in the H absorption image. Note that this image has been corrected for emission: the inner disk also contains strong emission lines (see Paper VI). As a result of the young stars, the CO-absorption band in the 4.5 m filter becomes less deep, causing the [ 3.6 ] [ 4.5 ] colour to redden.

sources.summary: The paper derives a GCLF distance to NGC 4526 and places it in the foreground of Virgo, arguing this revised distance makes Virgo-supernova comparisons misleading.

quotes.quote: However, an individual distance of its host galaxy, NGC 4526 , other than that of the Virgo cluster, has not yet been published. We determined the distance by the method of globular cluster luminosity functions on the basis of HST archive data. A maximum-likelihood fit returns apparent turn-over magnitudes of 23.16 0.16 mag in V and 21.96 0.09 mag in I . The corresponding distance modulus is 30.4 0.3 mag, where the error reflects our estimation of the absolute distance scale.

If the universality of the TOM of 0.2 mag (Whitmore ) and the uncertainty in the horizontal branch calibration of 0.1 mag is included as external errors we obtain _ NGC4526 =30.4 0.3 mag and D_ NGC4526 =12.0 1.6 Mpc as our final value for the distance modulus and distance. Thus, NGC 4526 is located in the foreground of the Virgo cluster and a comparison with apparent magnitudes of other SNe in the Virgo cluster is misleading. This result is consistent within 1 with Tonry's unpublished SBF distance modulus 30.68 0.13 mag, which was used by Richmond et al. ( ).

sources.summary: NGC 4526 is a Virgo early-type galaxy with a molecular and dust disk whose radio, 24 micron, and optical spectroscopy support inner-disk star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4526 NGC 4526 is the third of our Virgo Cluster early-type galaxies with well-developed molecular and dust disks (Figure n4526 ). The gas/dust disk is seen at high inclination and has a sharp outer edge at a semimajor axis of 15 \ = 1.2 kpc. The radio continuum morphology closely matches that of the molecular gas; the 24 \ emission is described by a plateau or a flat-topped disk of semimajor axis 7.5 \ (0.6 kpc) and a more extended, rounder exponential disk of scale length 28 \ (2.4 kpc).

The plateau contains 82 of the total flux density; given its size scale and orientation it is highly likely that this 24 \ component is associated with the radio continuum emission via star formation in the molecular gas. The more extended, fainter and rounder 24 \ component could well be attributed to circumstellar dust as in temi07 , but it only contributes 18 of the 24 \ flux density. Again, similar to the case of NGC 4459, the optical spectroscopy traces star formation activity and its after-effects in an ionized gas disk sarzi06 and a young, dynamically cold stellar disk kuntschner06,emsellem04 that have similar sizes to the molecular disk.

sources.summary: NGC4526 is included as a lenticular galaxy where molecular clouds in high-pressure regions appear nearly virialized under self-gravity and internal motions.

quotes.quote: Our galaxy sample comprises eight local galaxies spanning a wide range of stellar mass and morphology. We include one dwarf irregular: the LMC, two low-mass spirals: M33 and NGC300, two massive spirals: the Milky Way and M51, one starburst galaxy center: NGC253, one green-valley galaxy: M31, and one lenticular galaxy: NGC4526. We compile global measurements of the stellar mass ( M_ ), atomic ( M_ atom ) and molecular gas mass ( M_ mol ), and star formation rate (SFR) from the literature or we derive them from our own data. Our sample covers about two orders of magnitude in various host galaxy properties: M_ = 2 10^9 - 10^ 11 , SFR = 0.03 - 4.2 , sSFR = 2 10^ 12 2 10^ 10 yr ^ 1 , and f_ gas = M_ gas / (M_ gas + M_ ) = 0.01 - 0.5 .

To first order, ``self-gravitating'' clouds have high surface densities ( _ gmc 10^2 10^4 ) and are found in high ambient pressure environments ( P_ ext 4 10^4 - 7 10^7 K cm ^ 3 ) as in the central and inner Milky Way, M51, NGC253, and NGC4526. Despite the high ambient pressure in their environments, these clouds appear nearly virialized considering only their self-gravity and internal motions.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA molecular-line data for NGC 4526, finding broadly spiral-like line ratios but low abundance ratios and evidence that cluster processing stripped diffuse gas.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA data on the continuum emission, CO isotopologues ( , , and ), and high-density molecular tracers (HCN, , HNC, HNCO, CS, CN, and ) in NGC 4526. These data enable a detailed study of the physical properties of the molecular gas in a longtime resident of the Virgo Cluster; comparisons to more commonly-studied spiral galaxies offer intriguing hints into the processing of molecular gas in the cluster environment. Many molecular line ratios in NGC 4526, along with our inferred abundances and CO/ \ conversion factors, are similar to those found in nearby spirals.

We find surprisingly low [ / ] abundance ratios of 7.8^ +2.7 _ 1.5 and 6.5^ +3.0 _ 1.3 at radii of 0.4 kpc and 1 kpc. The emission from the high-density tracers HCN, , HNC, CS and CN is also relatively bright, and CN is unusually optically thick in the inner parts of NGC 4526. These features hint that processing in the cluster environment may have removed much of the galaxy's relatively diffuse, optically thinner molecular gas along with its atomic gas.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4526 as a circumnuclear star-forming fast rotator and says available star-formation tracers consistently point to ongoing star formation in its central disk.

quotes.quote: Circumnuclear Star-Forming Fast Rotators The \ galaxies (NGC 524, NGC 4459, NGC 4526, NGC 5838, NGC 5845) appear to represent a completely different physical state than the \ systems described above. In Figure sfrmstar , these galaxies are seen to be those star-forming systems with higher masses and therefore lower molecular gas fractions and specific star formation rates. Consequently, the current star formation events do not dominate the SSP ages, all of which are measured to be intermediate to old in the regions of star formation. Unlike in the \ galaxies, these regions of star formation are spatially very distinct, with all of the star formation, younger SSP ages, ionized gas, molecular gas, dust, and stellar kinematic structures being spatially coincident in well-defined central disks (see the definition of this class in SFdyn ).

In some systems (NGC 4459, NGC 4526), all star formation indicators (PAHs, CO, / , UV, low ionized gas velocity dispersion), where available, paint a consistent picture of on-going star formation in the region of the central disk.

sources.summary: The paper reports consistent 13CO flux for NGC4526, detects C18O to estimate optical depth, and notes similar line widths but centrally peaked methanol emission.

quotes.quote: ^ 13 CO was detected in NGC4526 by , who found a main beam brightness temperature of 5.95 0.28 K . In this paper we report a flux of 5.63 0.13 K .

In NGC4526 we also detect C ^ 18 O(1-0), and so have a second independent measure of the optical depth which we calculate assuming a Milky Way ^ 12 C/ ^ 13 C ratio of 250; 1994ARA&A..32..191W . Both estimates of the ^ 12 CO optical depth are consistent ( _ 13 =19.8 0.9, _ 18 =17.4 2.1), suggesting that ^ 13 CO is not significantly optically thick in this object.
NGC4526 has a similar line width in all transitions, but the detected methanol emission line peaks much more strongly around the systemic velocity than the other lines.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4526 as a likely spurious outlier in dark-matter and blue-GC trends, attributing its deviation to kinematic-substructure and ACS coverage corrections.

quotes.quote: Two effects may explain the deviation of NGC 4526 from the general trend. Firstly, it has the highest correction in our sample for kinematic substructure (Alabi et al. 2016). Uncorrected it would have f_ DM 0.7 . It also had a fairly large correction applied by Peng et al. (2008) as the GC system is not fully covered by the ACS field-of-view, i.e. uncorrected f_ blue = 0.48. If either of these corrections were not applied then NGC 4526 would be consistent within the errors with the general trend.

sources.summary: NGC4526 is a Virgo lenticular with central molecular rings and spirals; its stellar-population and dynamical M/L profiles are flat except near the largest CO ring.

quotes.quote: NGC4526 is a lenticular galaxy in the Virgo cluster with multiple rings/spirals of molecular gas in its centre (see 2013Natur.494..328D,2015ApJ...803...16U ). The stellar population M/L of this galaxy is flat with radius within the molecular rich region, which is consistent with the flat gradient predicted dynamically from our CO observations. A small peak in the dynamically inferred M/L is present around 200pc, consistent with the location of the largest CO ring. Apart from this small feature the IMF mismatch parameter in this source seems to be flat with radius, with a Salpeter-type normalisation.

sources.summary: The paper uses HST ACS/WFC halo imaging to derive a first TRGB distance for NGC 4526, tying the result to the LMC zero-point and comparing with Cepheid distances.

quotes.quote: We present here deep Hubble Space Telescope ( ) ACS/WFC imaging of the resolved stellar populations in the metal-poor halos of the host galaxies , , and . These three Virgo constellation galaxies are prime targets for calibrating the extragalactic distance scale given their relative proximity in the local Universe and their low line-of-sight reddenings. Anchoring the TRGB zero-point to the geometric distance to the Large Magellanic Cloud via detached eclipsing binaries, we measure extinction-corrected distance moduli of _ stat _ sys mag, _ stat _ sys mag, and _ stat _ sys mag for , , and , respectively, or _ stat _ sys Mpc, _ stat _ sys Mpc, and _ stat _ sys Mpc.

For these three galaxies, the distances are the first based on the TRGB, and for and , they are the highest precision distances published to date, each measured to 3 . Finally, we report good agreement between our TRGB distances and the available Cepheid distances for and , demonstrating consistency between the distance scales currently derived from stars of Population I and II. stars: Population II, cosmology: distance scale, galaxies: individual: NGC 4424, galaxies: individual: NGC 4526, galaxies: individual: NGC 4536

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4526 as a highly inclined Virgo S0 galaxy whose velocity and dispersion fields reveal a large, nearly edge-on stellar disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4526 is a highly inclined SAB0 ^0 (s) galaxy in the Virgo cluster ( M_B = --20.7). The stellar disk is not readily visible in the reconstructed image, but it is evident in the velocity and velocity dispersion fields. As in NGC 3384, the rotation along the major axis first increases, then decreases, and increases again in the outer parts. However, the extent of the disk is much larger than in NGC 3384. The disk appears almost edge-on, giving rise to an elongated depression across the (hot) spheroid in the velocity dispersion map, and completely overwhelming the central velocity dispersion peak.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC4526 as an S0 barred system with a boxy bulge, homogeneous stellar populations, and an embedded cold stellar disk with gas likely formed by inflow.

quotes.quote: The best examples are NGC7332 (Falc o n-Barroso et al. \ 2004) and NGC4526 (Fig. fig:sauron ; Emsellem et al. \ 2004; Sarzi et al. \ 2005; Kuntschner et al. \ 2005). Both S0 galaxies have a boxy bulge and clearly show the stellar kinematic signatures of a bar. They also possess homogeneous stellar populations (age and metallicity) across the disk and bar/bulge components, except in the central parts, as expected from simple bar-driven evolution models (e.g. \ Friedli Benz 1995). NGC4526 also clearly shows a cold central stellar disk embedded in its triaxial bulge, as traced by a strong pinching of the stellar isovelocities in the center, a wide local central minimum, and a strong central h_3-V anti-correlation (while the rest of the bulge displays an h_3-V correlation as expected from a thick bar). This central stellar disk coexists with an ionized and molecular gas disk (Young et al. \ 2005), presumably formed through bar-driven inflow (and ensuing star formation).

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4526 as a Virgo lenticular with a young cold stellar disk, star-formation indicators, and molecular gas that can only add a small young component.

quotes.quote: The most dramatic example is the Virgo cluster lenticular NGC 4526 (see Fig. fig:ngc4526 ; ), which has embedded in its bulge a dynamically cold kpc-scale stellar disk with a substantially younger mean age than the rest of the bulge ( ; ). The [ O III ] /H line ratios are also suggestive of current star formation activity ( ) and the ionized gas kinematics follow the molecular kinematics, albeit at somewhat smaller velocity amplitude. Based on the FIR flux, the star formation rate in NGC 4526 is roughly 1 M _ yr ^ 1 , giving a gas depletion timescale of 7 10^8 yr. But the total molecular mass now present in the galaxy is only 0.6 of the total stellar mass, so that even if there was substantially more molecular gas in the recent past it can not make more than a small frosting of young stars.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4526 as a normal S0 with a massive molecular disk aligned to the old stellar rotation and grouped with S0s having elevated star-formation indicators.

quotes.quote: In the remaining two S0 galaxies observed by Young, Bureau Cappellari (2008) -- NGC 4459 (disk radius: 0.67 kpc, mass: 1.6 10^8 M_ ) and NGC 4526 (disk radius: 1.1 kpc, mass: 5.7 10^8 M_ ) -- the molecular disks are well-aligned with the rotating system of old stars. These appear to be more normal S0 galaxies. NGC 4459 and 4526, together with NGC 3156 and 3489, form a tight group of four S0 galaxies in Figure 5 having nearly the same L_ FIR /L_ Ks and 24 m residuals, but with SFRs greater than normal elliptical galaxies. It is remarkable that among these four S0 galaxies both L_ FIR and L_ Ks vary by about an order of magnitude yet they share the same location in Figure 5.

sources.summary: NGC 4526 is described as an edge-on barred lenticular with possible spiral structure, embedded components, and a cold central disk transitioning into a hotter co-rotating bulge.

quotes.quote: This is an edge-on barred lenticular (SB0) galaxy with indications of possible spiral structure Michard:1994tl . Our combined velocity map exhibits a steep decrease to very low values below our resolution. The central regions of both the and h_4 maps suggest embedded components along the major and minor axes. There is a cold central disk Bureau:2002a in the inner parts that transitions into a hotter co-rotating bulge.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4526 as an inclined Virgo S0 whose bulge is surrounded by a filamentary dust disk with ongoing high-mass star formation powering enhanced 24 micron emission.

quotes.quote: The SDSS images show that NGC 4526 is an inclined S0 (i 78 ) with a smooth outer disk and a prominent bulge surrounded by a substantial dust disk. This structure is well-resolved in Hubble ACS images (Fig. fig:HST_v which show a complex, filamentary dust distribution, visible at all angles around the bulge. Both the multicolor Hubble images and integral field spectra from SAURON (Kuntschner:06) indicate the presence of ongoing high-mass star formation with this dust disk. Reprocessed UV light from these young stars powers the enhanced 24 m emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4526 as a comparison case for cloud velocity-dispersion scaling, arguing its molecular clouds lie above the Milky Way relation and that weaker spiral shocks fit its inner-kpc molecular gas.

quotes.quote: We note that the L trends for several nearby spiral galaxies are indeed weak - these include NGC 4526 Utomo2015 M33 Gratier2012 and M51 Colombo2014 In the case of both M51 and NGC 4526, most clouds lie well above the L relation derived for the Milky Way Solomon1987 .

More explicitly, the spiral shocks of NGC 4526 must be weaker than those of the Milky Way to be consistent with the model. This is reasonably consistent with the fact that the molecular gas in NGC 4526 is concentrated within the inner 1 kpc Davis2013 , where we would expect spiral shock strengths to be reduced. The model further predicts that the relatively strong magnetic fields in the central regions of NGC 4526 will only affect the L relation if the shocks are sufficiently weak.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4526 has a prominent dust disk affecting kinematics, a fast rotating stellar component, a velocity drop, and h3 sign reversal.

quotes.quote: This S0 object has a prominent dust disc which is not only visible in the reconstructed \ image, but also influences the observed kinematics. The dumbbell shape in the dispersion map is due to a fast rotating stellar component, well visible in the velocity map. The data of Pel97 also shows the drop in velocity between 10 and 20 , which corresponds to this component. h_3 reverses sign at about 20 \ along the major-axis.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4526 as a regular early-type galaxy whose ionised gas is confined to a fast-rotating central disk with low line ratios linked to a prominent dust disk.

quotes.quote: A similarly regular case is that of NGC 4526, although in this case the ionised gas is confined to a well defined, fast rotating disk in the central kpc of the galaxy.

The ratio is moderately high in NGC 2768, NGC 2974, and NGC 4278, while it is very low in NGC 4526. In this last galaxy, the location of the low / \ values corresponds to the location of a prominent dust disk (as shown in the unsharp-masked image), and suggests ongoing star formation.
The prominent dust disk in NGC 4526 traces closely the distribution of the gas.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4526 as the SAURON sample's most CO-rich galaxy, with a large regular dust disk, a dynamically cold stellar disk, and young stellar ages.

quotes.quote: NGC4459 and NGC4526 were detected with smaller signal-to-noise ratios with the Kitt Peak 12 m telescope by sw89 . Their larger beam suggests that the CO emission is not extended much beyond our own 23 beam.

The dust morphologies across the sample range from well developed large disks ( 10 diameter; e.g. \ NGC2685, NGC3032, NGC4459 and NGC4526) to small nuclear features ( 5 ; e.g. \ NGC3156, NGC4150 and NGC4477) and even larger scale irregular patches or lanes (e.g. \ NGC3156, NGC4150, NGC3489 and NGC4278).

For example, NGC 4526 is the most CO-rich galaxy in the SAURON sample, and it has a large dynamically cold stellar disk eetal04 which shows young stellar ages in its H absorption line strengths ketal06 .

sources.summary: NGC 4526 hosts SN 1994D; the paper says its GCLF distance was the first individual distance for the galaxy and places it foreground to Virgo.

quotes.quote: Primarily, this is SN 1994D in the S0 galaxy NGC 4526 , located in the southern extension of the Virgo cluster. This supernova is an interesting case because it has been suspected to violate the decline-rate--luminosity relation by being too bright in spite of an overall completely normal photometric and spectroscopic behaviour . However, our GCLF distance to NGC 4526 was the first published individual distance determination for this galaxy, which shows that NGC 4526 lies in the foreground of the Virgo cluster. We derived a distance modulus of 30.4 0.3 mag.

sources.summary: The paper reports an asymmetric CO(3-2) profile in NGC4526, estimates 8 percent missing flux using CARMA data, and assigns it a low-temperature molecular-gas model.

quotes.quote: The ^ 12 CO(3-2) line profile in NGC4526 is asymmetric, probably caused by a combination of a small pointing error and the emission region being slightly larger than the beam at this frequency (causing some ``missing'' flux at low velocities).
However, we do have recently acquired interferometric ^ 12 CO(2-1) CARMA data of NGC4526 (Davis et al. in prep.), and estimate 8 of missing flux.

The kinetic temperatures obtained for the best-fit models are either low ( T_ K =10 20 K for IC1024, NGC1222, NGC1266, NGC2764, NGC3032, NGC3665, NGC4150, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC4694, NGC4710 and NGC5866), intermediate ( 30 70 K for IC0676, NGC3607, PGC058114 and UGC09519) or high ( 120 K for NGC6014 and 150 K for NGC7465).

gmw_00001648.name: NGC 4536
gmw_00001648.overview: NGC 4536 is a late-type spiral galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1210.4171, arXiv:1312.0604] that features a pseudobulge with nuclear spiral structure [arXiv:0705.0973] and two spiral arms [arXiv:1312.0604]. The galaxy is undergoing a nuclear starburst [arXiv:1201.2405] and exhibits possible weak active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:1202.2713, arXiv:2303.12141].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4536 has unusually weak dense-gas tracers: only HCO+ is detected, dense-gas fraction is low, no AGN is found, and feedback is proposed.

quotes.quote: Based on the current results, the real deviations can be seen for NGC 4527, NGC 4536, NGC 3810, and NGC 5907. Especially, only HCO ^+ 1-0 is detected in NGC 4536 at 4.5 level, while other three lines remain non-detected. If the conversion factor of dense gas mass from dense gas tracer luminosity in these sources are similar to that of other galaxies, it will imply that there are less dense gas than expected.

In NGC 4536, the metallicity is within normal range and with strong CO 1-0 (525.67 4.49 Jy km s ^ 1 ) and ^ 13 CO 1-0 (54.51 3.98 Jy km s ^ 1 ) emission 2017ApJ...847...33C . While even HCO ^+ 1-0 detected in NGC 4536 is only 0.23 0.05 K km s ^ 1 , which corresponds to about 1.2 Jy km s ^ 1 . The dense gas fraction is really low, based on the line ratio of HCN and HCO ^+ 1-0 to CO and ^ 13 CO 1-0.

No AGN activity is found in NGC 4536. Thus, we suggest that the most possible reason for the lack of dense gas in these galaxies, including NGC 4536, is that star formation activities are at late evolutionary stages with strong feedback to the molecular gas, which causes the low dense gas fraction.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4536 as hosting a nuclear starburst whose high-IRX data points are excluded from several attenuation-relation fits because they deviate from the selected HRS galaxy envelope.

quotes.quote: There are a number of points that present a significantly higher IRX at a given in comparison to the envelope described by the points. These points are roughly compatible with the relations defined for starburst galaxies. A close inspection reveals they belong to NGC 4536 which is undergoing a nuclear starburst. This shows that several different regimes in terms of IRX-- can coexist within a single galaxy.
The data points affected by the starbursting region in NGC 4536 have been discarded by only selecting points with IRX<0.5 +1.5 .
Discrepant points in NGC 4536 have been discarded.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4536 as a high-quality, nearly face-on starburst system whose [FeII] and supernova-rate maps trace central emission and extended star formation better than Br emission.

quotes.quote: Focusing on NGC 4536, the Br emission has a bright knot directly north of the nucleus with secondary emission peaks to the north-west and south-east of the nucleus. The [ FeII ] emission is concentrated around the galactic center with a long plume of emission coming from the nucleus and extending towards the north-west corner of the galaxy. There is a small knot of faint [ FeII ] emission at the location of the Br peak. Similar to the [ FeII ] emission, the SNrate peaks around the center with an arm extending towards the north-east corner.

However, NGC 4536 is a nearly face-on galaxy with relatively low extinction and high signal-to-noise spectra, and the NIR data should be reliable. Thus, NGC 4536 is undergoing star formation that is closer to instantaneous but extended in time.

sources.summary: The Tolman-Bondi model places NGC 4536 in the Virgo southern extension, where its velocity mismatch can be reconciled by random motions and class 1 or 2 membership.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies belong to the Virgo southern extension (NGC 4496A and NGC 4536) and lie at more than 8 degrees from M 87. However, their position in the velocity-distance diagram should be explained by our model, if we assume that no other cluster perturbes them. However, we find that the maximum velocity explained by our model for NGC 4536 is 1563 \ km s^ 1 at 0.83 \ d_ Virgo , too small compared to its observed velocity of 1641 \ km s^ 1 . For this galaxy, our model predicts it to be in the class 3, with a distance of 1.79 \ d_ Virgo . This in turn would lead to an inacceptable Virgo distance of d_ Virgo = 8.07 \ Mpc , using the observed galaxy distance of 14.45 Mpc.
We therefore assume that this is the origin of the small discrepancy observed for NGC 4536.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4536 as a pseudobulge example with nuclear spiral structure, patchiness, a pseudobulge-dominated inner light profile, and a nearly exponential bulge profile.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, NGC 4536 is a typical example of a galaxy with nuclear spiral structure and patchiness (i.e. a pseudobulge). A decomposition of the stellar surface brightness profile shows that the pseudobulge dominates the light profile to a radius of 9 arcseconds. The WFPC2 F606W image in the middle panel implies that the entire pseudobulge appears to exhibit spiral structure. Notice that spiral structure exists in the small region inside the box on the wider field SDSS image. Also notice that the classical bulge has a () ^ 1/3.4 profile, while the pseudobulge is nearly exponential.

sources.summary: The paper describes 4536 as a Virgo spiral with ambiguous bar evidence, low-luminosity AGN indicators, vigorous inner star formation, and distinct circumnuclear molecular and ionized gas morphologies.

quotes.quote: 4536 is a large spiral galaxy located in the Virgo cluster. While it has been classified as a SAB(rs)bc galaxy , the evidence for the presence of a bar is not yet clear (see for example Pompea Rieke 1990; Shaw et al. 1995, Erwin et al. in preparation). Although optical line ratios place this galaxy in the H ii region range Ho1997 , the presence of high-ionization lines Satyapal2008 and its X-ray spectrum McAlpine2011 suggest that this galaxy hosts a low-luminosity AGN in its nucleus. Additionally, there is abundant evidence pointing to vigorous star formation taking place in the inner 20'' 30'' region of this galaxy e.g., Puxley1988, Pogge1989b, Telesco1993, Davies1997, Jogee2005 . This is also a typical `single-peak' type galaxy, with the CO molecular gas being concentrated in a nuclear disc of 10'' radius and an unresolved compact core at the nucleus e.g., Sofue2003, Jogee2005 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 4536 as a large Virgo spiral with prominent arms, uncertain weak-bar evidence, axisymmetric central structure, and possible non-circular inner gas motions.

quotes.quote: 4536 4536 is another large spiral galaxy in the Virgo Cluster, classified as SAB(rs)bc deVaucouleurs1991 and notable for two very prominent spiral arms. In contrast to 4501, it does not show any clear signatures of ram pressure stripping of its H i gas, though a weak interaction with its neighbour 4533 has been suggested Chung2009 . Fig. f_n4536_im shows Spitzer IRAC1 isophotes of the whole galaxy (left-hand panel) and a close-up of the inner 60 60 arcsec region (middle panel); the right-hand panel shows an unsharp mask of the innermost 10 10 arcsec region (using an HST NICMOS2 F160W image) with our SINFONI FOVs marked. Our analysis of the outer-disc isophotes (using SDSS and IRAC1 images) suggests a PA for the LON of 125^ 5^ and an ellipticity of 0.6 0.04 ; for an assumed disc thickness of /a = 0.2 , this indicates an inclination of 69 3 . These values are very consistent with previous estimates from large-scale kinematics: e.g. Chemin2006a found a kinematic PA of 301 2 (or 121 2 for comparison with the photometric) and i = 68 3 from their H velocity field, while Kuno2007 report PA = 124 \ and i = 70 \ from their CO observations.

sources.summary: The paper uses HST ACS/WFC halo imaging to derive a first TRGB distance for NGC 4536, tying the result to the LMC zero-point and comparing with Cepheid distances.

quotes.quote: We present here deep Hubble Space Telescope ( ) ACS/WFC imaging of the resolved stellar populations in the metal-poor halos of the host galaxies , , and . These three Virgo constellation galaxies are prime targets for calibrating the extragalactic distance scale given their relative proximity in the local Universe and their low line-of-sight reddenings. Anchoring the TRGB zero-point to the geometric distance to the Large Magellanic Cloud via detached eclipsing binaries, we measure extinction-corrected distance moduli of _ stat _ sys mag, _ stat _ sys mag, and _ stat _ sys mag for , , and , respectively, or _ stat _ sys Mpc, _ stat _ sys Mpc, and _ stat _ sys Mpc.

For these three galaxies, the distances are the first based on the TRGB, and for and , they are the highest precision distances published to date, each measured to 3 . Finally, we report good agreement between our TRGB distances and the available Cepheid distances for and , demonstrating consistency between the distance scales currently derived from stars of Population I and II. stars: Population II, cosmology: distance scale, galaxies: individual: NGC 4424, galaxies: individual: NGC 4526, galaxies: individual: NGC 4536

sources.summary: NGC 4536 is described as a late-type barred spiral at 15.8 Mpc whose nuclear and off-nuclear X-ray emission, variability, and [Ne V] emission point to AGN activity beyond X-ray binaries.

quotes.quote: Our prime focus in this paper is the multiwavelength study of two ULX candidates (X-2 and X-3) in NGC 4536, a late-type barred spiral galaxy at a distance of 15.8 Mpc in the Virgo constellation Mcalphine2011,Earnshaw2019,Hatt2018,deVaucouleurs1976 . Mcalphine2011 studied this galaxy with the aim of probing the nature of the nuclear and off-nuclear X-ray emission using XMM-Newton , supplemented with optical data from HST , infrared imaging data from the Spitzer and GALEX (Galaxy Evolution Explorer). They detected statistically significant variability which provides evidence of an AGN. They also explored the possibility of X-ray emission due to existence of X-ray binaries (XRBs) and found that XRBs could be responsible for only a small fraction of the X-ray luminosity detected, and that the significant [ Ne v ] emission in the galaxy cannot be explained by the presence of XRBs alone. Singha2019 performed a spectral and temporal analysis of ULXs in NGC 4536 and identified three X-ray point sources (X-ray counts > 100 counts/sec) and concluded that these sources are ULXs with X-ray luminosity of > 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4536 as the most extreme dust-obscured case, where high-M/L regions contribute little H-band light but substantial stellar mass.

quotes.quote: As a result, galaxies with a broad M/L distribution have large differences between resolved and unresolved mass estimates, up to approximately 40 per cent. As we can see comparing Fig. fig_mass_MLdistrib and fig_sample_RGB , this is especially the case for galaxies with substantive dust-obscured regions (i.e. NGC4569, NGC4536, NGC3521 and NGC5713).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4536 as a late-type HII/starburst galaxy with possible weak AGN activity, central star formation, ring-like radio emission, and core X-ray sources.

quotes.quote: NGC 4536 is a late-type HII/Starburst galaxy that shows [ Ne V ] line emission, which may indicate that the nucleus harbours a weak AGN 2005AJ....130...73H . Previous observations indicate vigorous star formation in the central nuclear region, shown through Br emission 1988MNRAS.234P..29P . The diffuse radio emission with three central peaks 1990MNRAS.242..379V,2006AJ....131..701L may indicate that this occurs in a ring around the nucleus. There are two ultra-luminous X-ray sources, one of which is located in the core of the galaxy and possibly associated with the weak AGN 2005ApJS..157...59L .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4536 as an anomalous galaxy whose Spitzer 70-micron flux likely traces warmer dust, making dust-based gas masses too low for calibration use.

quotes.quote: There is no strong relationship between the two, with one obvious outlier, the galaxy NGC 4536. For NGC 4536, we used a Spitzer 70- m flux, and it seems likely that in this individual case the low value of and the high temperature are signs that much of the 70- m flux is from dust that is warmer than the bulk of the dust. Therefore, for this galaxy it seemed likely that the estimate of the gas mass from the dust emission would be too low, and so we decided to omit NGC 4536 from the calibration sample.

sources.summary: The paper observes NGC 4536 with JWST as an SN Ia host, measuring its fiducial JAGB luminosity-function mode from 461 stars per region.

quotes.quote: NGC 4536 NGC 4536 (also known as PGC 41823 or UGC 07732) is a spiral galaxy that was observed with JWST in January 2023, and was host to SN 1981B.
In NGC 4536, the fiducial mode was measured using JAGB stars with colors between 2.6<(F115W-F444W)<3.2 mag and an LF smoothed with a smoothing parameter of _s=0.30 mag.
In NGC 4536, each region had 461 JAGB stars.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 4536 as a possible weak AGN, identifying it as one of two additional candidates from relatively high [N II] line ratios.

quotes.quote: Also, in an attempt to find any unknown AGN we use [ N 2 ] and [ S 2 ] line flux ratios (relative to H ) and find tentative evidence for weak AGN in NGC 1300 and NGC 4536.
Two possible additional weak AGN, indicated by the relatively high values of the [ N 2 ] ratios are NGC 1300 and NGC 4536.
colina1 ) we attempted to identify any unknown AGN by using [ N 2 ] and [ S 2 ] line flux ratios (relative to H ) and found tentative evidence for weak AGN in NGC 1300 and NGC 4536.

gmw_00001649.name: PKS 1741-038
gmw_00001649.overview: PKS 1741-038 is one of the brightest radio blazars in the sky [arXiv:2306.16797] and is associated with two high-energy neutrino events [arXiv:2306.16797, arXiv:2311.00281]. These neutrino arrivals coincided with lower-energy flares [arXiv:2306.16797] and a rapid increase in the source's radio flux density [arXiv:2009.10523].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 1731 038 as the largest contributor to its correlation, a bright radio blazar associated with two high-energy neutrinos and lower-energy flares.

quotes.quote: The largest contribution to the correlation we find here comes from PKS 1731 038, associated with two HE neutrinos, both accompanied by lower-energy flares. It was claimed as a probable neutrino source in the first study of radio blazars and HE neutrinos by neutradio1 , based on the event of 2011-09-30. Subsequently, the second HE neutrino arrived from its direction on 2022-02-05 PKS1741ATel,neutradio2022 . Since it was claimed as a neutrino source in the radio-blazar analysis, and not elsewhere, dropping it from the sample would not be justified.

However, we checked that even without the two events associated with PKS 1731 038, our result remains pronounced ( 3.0 ). PKS 1731 038 is among the brightest radio blazars in the sky and the only blazar associated with two HE neutrinos in the sample we use. It also dominates the correlation between HE neutrinos and hard X-ray blazars reported by neutxray . The present study further motivates this individual association.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1743 0350 as a blazar whose neutrino arrival coincided with a large radio outburst, including a fast 37 GHz flux increase and matching 15 GHz rise.

quotes.quote: For example, in the source J1743 0350, the coincident neutrino arrives during the rising part of a large outburst (Fig. Figpage2 ) but because the light curve includes other flares of similar strength, the overall mean flux density of the light curve is high, resulting in a smaller activity index.

One of these is J1743 0350, which did not meet the formal criteria of strong flares in the OVRO data as discussed above. In the Mets ahovi light curve (Fig. Figmhovi ), it can be seen that the neutrino arrival time coincides with an extremely fast increase of the radio flux density at 37 GHz. At 15 GHz, the substructures of the flares are not as clearly visible, but there the neutrino arrival time also coincides with a fast increase of flux density.

sources.summary: The review describes PKS 1741-038 as a powerful radio blazar highlighted as a likely neutrino source, with IceCube events coincident with its direction.

quotes.quote: PKS 1741-038. One of the most powerful radio blazars in the sky, it was highlighted among the four most likely sources of neutrinos, based on the coincidence with the IC110930 neutrino event Event identifiers indicate the experiment, IC -- IceCube, GVD -- Baikal-GVD, and the date of detection. For track events, only the most probable value of the neutrino energy is given, errors of determination of which are huge . and on high flux density of the radio emission from a compact component. In 2022, from the same direction, another neutrino, IC220205, has arrived that satisfied all the selection criteria used in .

gmw_00001650.name: NBv1.78
gmw_00001650.overview: NBv1.78 is a high-redshift, gravitationally lensed galaxy [arXiv:1801.01831, arXiv:1802.07430] that exhibits co-spatial stellar and dust emission [arXiv:1801.01831]. The background source is highly elongated, containing two partially blended emission knots [arXiv:1801.01831] and showing signs of merger-driven velocity fields [arXiv:1802.07430].

sources.summary: The paper compares SMA, Keck, and HST modelling of HATLASJ133008.4+245900, finding co-spatial stellar and dust emission and an elongated background source with two partially blended knots.

quotes.quote: HATLASJ133008.4+245900 : Besides the lens modelling performed by B13 on SMA data, this system was also analysed by using Keck /AO K_ s band observations, where the background galaxy is detected. The configuration of the multiple images is similar in the near-IR and in the sub-mm suggesting that the stellar and dust emission are co-spatial. We derive an Einstein radius _ E =1.03^ , higher than B13's result ( _ E =0.88^ ). Our estimate is instead in agreement with the finding of and Negrello et al. (in prep.; based on HST /WFC3 imaging data).

Interestingly, the reconstructed background source is very elongated. This is due to the presence of two partially blended knots of emission, a main one extending across the tangential caustic and a second, fainter one located just off the fold of the caustic.

sources.summary: The paper reports CO(7--6) and marginal CI(2--1) measurements for NBv1.78, interpreting its high line ratios and broad linewidth as signs of mechanically heated molecular gas.

quotes.quote: The spectra extracted from the SEPIA5 receiver are shown in Figure Spectra . The CO(7--6) line is clearly detected in all three sources and the corresponding fluxes and line widths resulting from the Gaussian best fit are listed in Table table:data . The (2--1) line is detected at a 4 level in G12v2.30, marginally ( 2 ) in NBv1.78 and not detected in G12v2.43. The corresponding fluxes or upper limits and line widths are listed in Table table:data , together with the CO(1--0) fluxes taken from har+12 , CO(3--2) in NBv1.78 flux from omo+13 , the IR luminosities and magnification values from zha+18 .

Two of the three targets also show very large linewidths, so at least for G12v2.30 and NBv1.78 it can be interpreted as a possible sign of merger-driven velocity fields with expected large mechanical energy available as a source of energy for their molecular gas.
The high values of CO(7-6)/FIR (and also of CI(2-1)/FIR ) in NBv1.78 and G12v2.30 are consistent with a chemistry driven by shocks per+10 .

gmw_00001651.name: G12v2.43
gmw_00001651.overview: G12v2.43 is a strongly lensed, high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.1 [arXiv:2304.08563]. It features a compact star-forming core with warm, dense molecular clouds [arXiv:2304.08563], and it exhibits carbon monoxide line ratios similar to those of local starburst galaxies [arXiv:1802.07430].

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA and lens reconstruction to study water emission in HATLASJ113526.2-01460, inferring warm dense ISM conditions, compact star formation, and possible AGN activity.

quotes.quote: We report ALMA high-resolution ( 0.3 arcsec) observations of water emission lines p- H_2O (2_ 02 1_ 11 ), o- H_2O (3_ 21 3_ 12 ) , p- H_2O (4_ 22 4_ 13 ) , in the strongly lensed galaxy HATLASJ113526.2-01460 at redshift z 3.1. From the lensing-reconstructed maps of water emission and line profiles, we infer the general physical properties of the ISM in the molecular clouds where the lines arise. We find that the water vapor lines o- H_2O (3_ 21 3_ 12 ) , p- H_2O (4_ 22 4_ 13 ) are mainly excited by FIR pumping from dust radiation in a warm and dense environment, with dust temperatures ranging from 70 K to 100 K, as suggested by the line ratios.

We aim to investigate this issue by taking advantage of the high spectral and spatial resolution offered by the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) on a strongly-lensed Optical/NIR-dark galaxy HATLASJ113526.2-01460 (hereafter J1135) at redshift z 3.1. The lensed system was originally discovered in the Gama 12^ th field of the Herschel ATLAS survey and is found to be remarkably peculiar, given the strong faintness of the background source and the foreground deflector in the optical/near-IR(NIR) regime. analyzed its continuum, the CO(8-7), and [ CII ] spectral line emissions detected from ALMA Archival Data. The detailed lens modeling of these components, revealed that J1135 is magnified by a factor 6-13 .

Thanks to gravitational lensing, that stretches the angular sizes by factors 3 , it is possible to locate the SFR region in a compact core of 0.5 kpc, with no clear evidence of gas rotation or ongoing merging events.

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(7--6) but not CI(2--1) in G12v2.43, finding its CO ratio and FIR ratio closer to local infrared galaxies than the other two lenses.

quotes.quote: The spectra extracted from the SEPIA5 receiver are shown in Figure Spectra . The CO(7--6) line is clearly detected in all three sources and the corresponding fluxes and line widths resulting from the Gaussian best fit are listed in Table table:data . The (2--1) line is detected at a 4 level in G12v2.30, marginally ( 2 ) in NBv1.78 and not detected in G12v2.43. The corresponding fluxes or upper limits and line widths are listed in Table table:data , together with the CO(1--0) fluxes taken from har+12 , CO(3--2) in NBv1.78 flux from omo+13 , the IR luminosities and magnification values from zha+18 .

G12v2.43 has a CO(7-6) CO(1-0) value similar to the local starbursts and the Galactic centre, while the same ratio for the two other lenses is extreme and shows even more extreme conditions than ultraluminous IR galaxies (ULIRGs).
G12v2.43 has a value of (0.1-1.3) 10^ 4 consistent with the characteristic values of the local IR galaxies, a result which confirms previous finding for SMGs and local galaxies lu+15 .

gmw_00001652.name: Q2122-444
gmw_00001652.overview: Q2122-444 is a typical type 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1010.5243] that exhibits strong optical variability [arXiv:1010.5243] and lacks a radio jet [arXiv:1010.5243]. Its X-ray spectrum reveals a point-like, bright source [arXiv:astro-ph/0703269] with broad Balmer lines [arXiv:1010.5243] and little intrinsic absorption [arXiv:1010.5243].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies Q2122-444 from a putative naked type 2 AGN to a typical type 1 AGN, with broad Balmer lines, no radio jet, little absorption, and a high Eddington-rate estimate.

quotes.quote: Based on previous spectral and temporal optical studies, Q2122-444 has been classified as a naked AGN or true type 2 AGN, that is, an AGN that genuinely lacks a broad line region (BLR). Its optical spectrum seemed to possess only narrow forbidden emission lines that are typical of type 2 (obscured) AGNs, but the long-term optical light curve, obtained from a monitoring campaign over more than two decades, showed strong variability, apparently ruling out the presence of heavy obscuration. Here, we present the results from a 40 ks XMM-Newton observation of Q2122-444 carried out to shed light on the energetics of this enigmatic AGN. The X-ray analysis was complemented with ATCA radio data to assess the possible presence of a jet, and with new NTT/EFOSC2 optical spectroscopic data to verify the actual absence of a BLR.

The higher-quality optical data revealed the presence of strong and broad Balmer lines that are at odds with the previous spectral classification of this AGN. The lack of detection of radio emission rules out the presence of a jet. The X-ray data combined with simultaneous UV observations carried out by the OM aboard \ confirm that Q2122-444 is a typical type-1 AGN without any significant intrinsic absorption. New estimates of the black hole mass independently obtained from the broad Balmer lines and from a new scaling technique based on X-ray spectral data suggest that \ is accreting at a relatively high rate in Eddington units.

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra imaging and spectroscopy of Q2122-444 as a naked AGN, finding a point-like, bright, lightly absorbed X-ray source with Seyfert-like photon index.

quotes.quote: Three of the naked AGNs discovered by Hawkins, Q2122-444, Q2130-431, Q2131-427, were observed with \ ACIS-S in December 2005. Since we had no X-ray information on these sources, for precaution the observations were carried out using subarray modes (with frame time ranging between 0.4 and 0.8 s) to minimize possible pile-up effects.

In order to fully exploit the unsurpassed spatial resolution of , for the two brightest sources Q2122-444 and Q2130-431 (left and middle panels) the images were extracted from ACIS-S event files after rebinning with a factor of 0.2 (yielding pixel size of 0.1 ) and smoothing with the subpackage +fadapt+ of +FTOOLS+, with a circular top hat filter of adaptive size in order to achieve a minimal number of 10 counts under the filter.

Spectral analysis Two of the three AGNs observed with , Q2122-444 and Q2130-431, have enough counts to allow a direct fitting of the un-binned spectrum using the C- statistic. Both sources are reasonably well fitted with a simple power-law model absorbed by Galactic N_ H . The values of the photon indices ( =1.8 0.4 for both Q2122-444 and Q2130-431) are typical for Seyfert 1 galaxies (e.g., Nandra et al. 1997), although with substantial uncertainties due to the limited photon statistics. The spectra and residuals of Q2122-444 and Q2130-431 are shown in Figure figure:fig2 .

gmw_00001653.name: Q2130-431
gmw_00001653.overview: Q2130-431 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a Seyfert 1.8 or intermediate Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1202.0062, arXiv:1010.5243]. It features a point-like, bright X-ray source with low absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0703269, arXiv:1202.0062] and exhibits an intrinsically weak or transient broad-line region [arXiv:1202.0062, arXiv:1010.5243].

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra imaging and spectroscopy of Q2130-431 as a naked AGN, finding a point-like, bright, lightly absorbed X-ray source with Seyfert-like photon index.

quotes.quote: Three of the naked AGNs discovered by Hawkins, Q2122-444, Q2130-431, Q2131-427, were observed with \ ACIS-S in December 2005. Since we had no X-ray information on these sources, for precaution the observations were carried out using subarray modes (with frame time ranging between 0.4 and 0.8 s) to minimize possible pile-up effects.

In order to fully exploit the unsurpassed spatial resolution of , for the two brightest sources Q2122-444 and Q2130-431 (left and middle panels) the images were extracted from ACIS-S event files after rebinning with a factor of 0.2 (yielding pixel size of 0.1 ) and smoothing with the subpackage +fadapt+ of +FTOOLS+, with a circular top hat filter of adaptive size in order to achieve a minimal number of 10 counts under the filter.

Spectral analysis Two of the three AGNs observed with , Q2122-444 and Q2130-431, have enough counts to allow a direct fitting of the un-binned spectrum using the C- statistic. Both sources are reasonably well fitted with a simple power-law model absorbed by Galactic N_ H . The values of the photon indices ( =1.8 0.4 for both Q2122-444 and Q2130-431) are typical for Seyfert 1 galaxies (e.g., Nandra et al. 1997), although with substantial uncertainties due to the limited photon statistics. The spectra and residuals of Q2122-444 and Q2130-431 are shown in Figure figure:fig2 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses Q2130-431 as a Seyfert 1.8 galaxy with weak broad Balmer emission, high accretion, low absorption, and an intrinsically weak or transient broad-line region.

quotes.quote: Panessa et al. (2009) have more recently discussed an interesting Seyfert 1.8 galaxy, Q2130-431, that has weak broad H and H emission. It has an Eddington ratio of L_ Bol /L_ Edd 0.4 , absorption column density N_ H <10^ 20 cm^ 2 and a Balmer decrement of 0.34 in light of H /H ratio, indicating that the BLR is not suffering from heavy reddening and is not obscured. These authors concluded that the weakness of BLR in Q2130-431 is intrinsic in spite of the high accretion rates. This object seems to challenge the popular scenario that the absence of a BLR is driven by the low Eddington ratios. However, this could be clear evidence for the presence of a transient BLR in phase I suggested by the present model.

sources.summary: The paper describes Q2130-431 as a previously selected naked AGN whose newer optical data showed weak broad lines, making it a possible intermediate Seyfert and challenging the original sample classification.

quotes.quote: More recently, Q2131-427 and Q2130-431, two of the original naked AGNs detected by hawk04 , have been observed quasi-simultaneously in the X-rays with \ and in the optical band with the NTT by panes09 . The high-quality optical spectroscopic data confirmed the ``naked" nature of Q2131-427 but revealed weak broad lines for Q2130-431. On one hand, these results combined with the lack of intrinsic absorption inferred from \ led panes09 to the conclusion that Q2131-427 is a ``true" type 2 AGN and Q2130-431 might be a ``true" intermediate Seyfert with an intrinsically weak BLR. On the other hand, the detection of broad lines in the optical spectrum of Q2130-431 casts some doubts on the optical classification of the original sample of hawk04 , since it is based on relatively low-sensitivity optical spectral data.

gmw_00001654.name: Q2131-427
gmw_00001654.overview: Q2131-427 is a naked active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0703269, arXiv:1010.5243] that has been confirmed as a true type 2 AGN lacking a broad-line region [arXiv:1010.5243]. X-ray observations of the source reveal a spectrum consistent with a simple power law and a typical Seyfert-like photon index [arXiv:astro-ph/0703269].

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra observations of Q2131-427 as a naked AGN, finding few counts but a hardness ratio consistent with a simple power law and Seyfert-like photon index.

quotes.quote: Three of the naked AGNs discovered by Hawkins, Q2122-444, Q2130-431, Q2131-427, were observed with \ ACIS-S in December 2005. Since we had no X-ray information on these sources, for precaution the observations were carried out using subarray modes (with frame time ranging between 0.4 and 0.8 s) to minimize possible pile-up effects.

This procedure cannot be applied to Q2131-427 for the low number of counts. As a consequence, for this source we simply extracted a raw image, which is shown in the right panel of Fig figure:fig1 . All the sources are clearly detected during the short \ exposures, and no nearby objects are present within a radius of 1 \ from the central AGN.

The third source, Q2131-427, has less than 20 counts in the 0.5--8 keV energy band, which are not enough for a meaningful spectral fitting: The spectrum can be fitted with a simple power law, but the photon index, =2.1 0.9 , is basically unconstrained. Nevertheless, the spectral properties of Q2131-427 can be probed by computing the hardness ratio HR=(h-s)/(h+s)=-0.60 0.27 (where s refers to the 0.5--2 keV energy band and h to 2--8 keV). The photon indices, obtained by fixing N_ H to the Galactic value, along with the hardness ratios, the observed fluxes, and the intrinsic luminosities are summarized in Table 3. Since the uncertainties are dominated by the errors on the photon index, the errors on fluxes and luminosities have been derived by varying by 1 . The similarity of the hardness ratio of Q2131-427 with the HR values obtained for Q2122-444 and Q2130-431 (see Table 3) suggests that also the spectrum of Q2131-427 is consistent with a simple power law with a typical Seyfert-like photon index.

sources.summary: The paper cites Q2131-427 as a previous naked-AGN target whose X-ray and optical observations confirmed a true type 2 AGN with no broad-line region.

quotes.quote: More recently, Q2131-427 and Q2130-431, two of the original naked AGNs detected by hawk04 , have been observed quasi-simultaneously in the X-rays with \ and in the optical band with the NTT by panes09 . The high-quality optical spectroscopic data confirmed the ``naked" nature of Q2131-427 but revealed weak broad lines for Q2130-431. On one hand, these results combined with the lack of intrinsic absorption inferred from \ led panes09 to the conclusion that Q2131-427 is a ``true" type 2 AGN and Q2130-431 might be a ``true" intermediate Seyfert with an intrinsically weak BLR.

Of the six ``naked AGN" originally selected by Hawkins (2004), three have been observed with deep X-ray observations coupled with high S/N optical spectroscopy and only one, Q2131-427, confirmed the genuine lack of a BLR.

gmw_00001655.name: ESO 338-IG04
gmw_00001655.overview: ESO 338-IG04 is a luminous blue compact starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0306522, arXiv:astro-ph/0609774] featuring a chaotic velocity field [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179, arXiv:1208.5461] and a 5 kpc stellar tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179]. This system is likely a recent merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0503320, arXiv:1208.5461] containing numerous young, compact star clusters [arXiv:astro-ph/0306522, arXiv:astro-ph/0609774] and a distant companion galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0106179, arXiv:1208.5461].

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 338-IG04 as a Ly-alpha-emitting blue compact galaxy, finding concentrated Ly-alpha emission, super star clusters, and little neutral-gas velocity shift.

quotes.quote: ESO 338-IG04 (or just ESO 338 as we might refer to it in this paper) has slightly narrower emission lines than Haro 11 (FWHM 180 km/s) and the Ly emission is concentrated to a bright central region. ESO 338 is riddled with "super star clusters"; small knots of intense star formation ostlin-1998,ostlin-2003,ostlin-2007 . The ongoing starburst is about 40 Myr old and was likely triggered by a merger with a small galaxy or from an interaction with remaining debris from a previous encounter with the companion galaxy ESO 338-IG04b ostlin-2001, cannon-2004 . When we discuss ESO 338 below, we mainly focus on the bright central region, which we refer to as knot A cf. Figure Fig_Slits hayes-2005 (also known as cluster 23 in the 'inner' sample of ostlin-1998 ).

For ESO 338-IG04, we measure no significant shift of neutral gas. The best fit velocity is -15 16 km/s. For Haro 11, we see an outflow from knot B at 44 13 km/s and infalling gas towards knot C with 32 12 km/s. Based on the relative strength of the Na D absorption lines, we estimate low covering fractions of neutral gas (down to 10 ) in all three cases. The Na D absorption likely occurs in dense clumps with higher column densities than where the bulk of the Ly scattering takes place. Still, we find no strong correlation between outflowing neutral gas and a high Lyman alpha escape fraction. The Lyman alpha photons from these two galaxies are therefore likely escaping due to a low column density and/or covering fraction.

sources.summary: The paper describes Tol 1924-416 as a nearby star-forming galaxy with an isolated He II emitter and no detected dust attenuation across the tested methods.

quotes.quote: For this study, we selected star-forming regions in eight nearby galaxies ( 0.0010 z 0.0095 , at distances of 3-37 Mpc) with existing He II 1640 HST-STIS UV observations from the Mikulski Archive for Space Telescopes (MAST). These UV spectra were previously obtained for He 2-10, NGC 3125, Mrk 33, NGC 4214, NGC 4670, and Tol 1924-416 as part of program 9036 (PI: C. Leitherer) taken in 2001–2003, and for NGC 3049 and Tol 89 as part of program 7513 (PI: C. Leitherer) observed in 1999-2000. Additional optical HST-STIS observations, providing He II 4686 line measurements were obtained from the HST program GO-15846 (PI: C. Leitherer), executed in 2020-21. These optical observations were carried out at the exact same location, same orientation, and with the same aperture sizes as the UV observations.
The He II emitter in the galaxy Tol 1924-416 is fairly isolated and has a diameter of 0.5 , excluding most possible sources of contamination.

Tol 1924-416    This is a bright source with no significant direct neighbor. This makes it very suitable for our Balmer-line measurement with Archive MUSE observation. Interestingly this source appears to have ejected all its dusty envelope since we do not detect any dust attenuation with all three of the used methods.

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 338-IG04 as a chaotic starburst with a blue stellar tail, complex H I structure, merger-like kinematics, and a likely merger-triggered burst.

quotes.quote: ESO 338-IG04 ( Tololo 1924-416 ) This well-known BCG has an almost chaotic velocity field, with strong gradients and an extended tail with little internal velocity structure (Fig. contig04a ). It has a companion at a projected distance of 70 kpc to the south-west (see next subsection). Approximately 10 \ east of the centre, just at the border of the central star-forming region, there is a velocity component whose kinematical axis is perpendicular to the photometric major axis of the galaxy (se Paper I). The radial light distribution indicates that the observed mean rotational velocity (if a such is at all meaningful to define in view of the irregular velocity field, see Fig. massmod1st4 ) cannot support the system gravitationally. There is a 5 kpc long tail towards the east, and large scale isophotal assymetries down to the 26 mag/arcsec ^2 level.

The morphology and velocity field of the H i complex is consistent with a close interaction/merger of two gas-rich galaxies or H i clouds. The companion (see next subsection) is detected in H i but lies further away. Although we cannot exclude that the starburst in ESO 338-IG04 is triggerred by interaction with the companion, a merger appears more likely in view of the complex velocity field and the non-rotating arm.

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 338-IG04 as a luminous blue compact galaxy whose starburst has been active for about 40 Myr, with compact clusters tracing propagating star formation and galactic-wind structures.

quotes.quote: ESO 338-IG04 , also known as Tololo 1924-416 , is a well known luminous blue compact galaxy in the local universe. Images obtained with the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) have shown that the central starburst region is composed of numerous bright point sources - young star clusters, surrounded by a population of old and intermediate age globular clusters. In this paper we use ultra-violet (UV) and optical HST photometry in five bands, and an extensive set of spectral evolutionary synthesis scenarios to investigate the age and masses of 124 star clusters. The very small reddening makes ESO 338-IG04 an excellent laboratory for studying the formation of such objects.

We have used the star clusters to trace the temporal and spatial evolution of the starburst, and to put constraints on the star formation activity over a cosmological time-scale. The present starburst has been active for about 40 Myr and shows evidence for propagating star formation and structures triggered by galactic winds. A standard Salpeter initial mass function (IMF) extending up to 120 M_ provides the best fit to the data, although a flatter IMF cannot be excluded. The compact star clusters provide 30-40 of the UV luminosity and star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper revisits ESO338-IG04 as a blue compact dwarf with high star-formation intensity and widely distributed recent star and cluster formation, deriving a lower young-cluster fraction.

quotes.quote: The three blue compact dwarf (BCD) galaxies ESO185-IG13, ESO338-IG04, and Haro11 have played a central role on the observational side because they have some of the highest known _ SFR and published values of . We present new estimates of for these BCDs in three age intervals (1-10 Myr, 10-100 Myr, 100-400 Myr), based on age-dating which includes H photometry to better discriminate between clusters younger and older than 10 Myr.

Galaxy Properties Color images of ESO185, ESO338, and Haro11 taken in the BVI filters with the HST are shown in Figure fig:sample . ESO185 has a bright, bar-like structure crossing the center, and tidal features indicating the system experienced a recent merging event. Recent star and cluster formation is more evenly spread throughout ESO338 than in ESO185.

The fraction of stars formed in 1-10 Myr clusters, or _ 1-10 Myr , was previously estimated to be 50 for the BCD galaxies ESO338 and Haro11, and 26 for ESO185 Adamo10, Adamo11a . In this work, we found significantly lower values of 15 4 (ESO185), 39 10 (ESO338), and 18 5 (Haro11), based on deeper HST observations which include narrow-band H imaging taken as part of the Clusters, Clumps, Dust and Gas (CCDG) survey.

sources.summary: The paper presents VIMOS integral-field results for Tol 1924-416, finding a rich central spectrum, complex H-emission velocity structure, and possible merger signatures linked to starburst-driven winds.

quotes.quote: Here, we show preliminary results of one object of our VIMOS sample, Tol 1924-416. Figure fig:Tol1924_Spec shows an integrated spectrum of the central part of Tol 1924-416. This high S/N spectrum shows many faint Helium and metal lines that are not commonly seen in BCDs.

Figure fig:Tol1924_Map presents maps of velocity and velocity dispersion as derived from the H emission line. While the emission line morphology is similar to the broad-band appearance, the velocity structure is very complex. While local starburst-driven winds might contribute to this structure, it is tempting to take it as a sign of merging, similar to what previous investigations of this galaxy concluded from optical broad- and narrow-band observations HV95,DCC99 .

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO 338-IG04 to study BCG cluster formation, finding a recent enhancement, a young peak, and roughly half of star formation in clusters.

quotes.quote: ESO 338-IG04 The analysis of the star cluster population in ESO 338-IG04 (ESO 338) has been presented in 2003A&A...408..887O . The masses have been obtained from models that assume a Salpeter initial mass function IMF, 1955ApJ...121..161S . We show, in Figure  CMF_eso338 , the cluster formation history during the last 40 Myr of galaxy evolution.

In agreement with 2003A&A...408..887O , we observe a cluster formation enhancement between 20 and 30 Myr ago. However, the peak of cluster formation is younger than 10 Myr, similarly to what is found in Haro 11, ESO 185, and Mrk 930. ESO 338 is forming roughly 1.6 yr ^ 1 of stars in clusters.

sources.summary: The paper keeps Tol 1924-416 in the analysis despite interaction signs, noting its distant companion, three star-forming knots, and weak expected tidal influence from ESO 338-IGO4B.

quotes.quote: Only one galaxy in Table Table:Galaxies presents signs of current interaction (Tol 1924-416). It is still included in the following analysis because its companion is quite distant (see below), and its emission lines behave as the other isolated galaxies. Tol 1924-416 appears to be composed of at least three star-formation knots (Fig. 1, third row). The source is documented as interacting with ESO 338-IGO4B, 72 kpc away 2004ApJ...608..768C . However, the distance is large compared with the physical size of the galaxy ( 5 kpc effective radius), and so the companion is not expected to exert a significant tidal force on the galaxy e.g., Sect. 3.4 2015ApJ...802...82F .

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 338--IG04 as a nearby luminous blue compact starburst galaxy with many young clusters, old globular clusters, low metallicity, and unusually luminous clusters.

quotes.quote: ESO 338--IG04, also known as Tol 1924-416, is a luminous ( M_B=-19 ) blue compact galaxy (BCG) --- the closest in a class of galaxies that are rare in the local universe but that become increasingly important towards higher redshifts and the peak in the cosmic star formation rate at z 1 ( \ Werk \ ). ESO 338--IG04 shows both vigorous star formation and a rich population of more than 100 young SSCs and old globular clusters ( O stlin \ ) Following O stlin \ ( ) we adopt a distance of 37.5 Mpc to ESO 338--IG04.

In addition ESO 338--IG04 hosts a population of cosmologically old (age 10  Gyr) globular clusters. What makes ESO 338--IG04 particularly interesting as a star cluster formation laboratory is its combination of small reddening, many clusters, and a low metallicity ( 12+ ( O/H )=8.0 ). Compared to other metal-poor star-forming galaxies in the local universe (such as NGC 1569, NGC 1705, and He 2-10) the clusters in ESO 338--IG04 are more luminous and numerous.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 338-04 as having Ly absorption embedded in dusty regions plus surrounding diffuse emission, with clumpy dust and Ly decoupled from FUV continuum.

quotes.quote: ESO 338-04  -- In the second column of Fig. images_1 , the Ly image shows three main absorption regions and a surrounding bright emission. The absorption sites correspond to relatively dusty regions of the galaxy seen in the extinction map, which traps Ly \ photons, while the emission is not correlated with the dust content. The last component is, again, the diffuse emission around and overall the galaxy, with a low surface brightness, which is showing up the resonant decoupling of Ly \ photons. Many dust features are clearly visible in the E(B-V) map, with a clumpy-like structure, and following roughly the H \ structure.

sources.summary: ESO338--I04 is described as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy with recent star formation, intermediate-age clusters, merger-like chaotic kinematics, and a spectroscopically confirmed companion.

quotes.quote: ESO338--I04 This Wolf-Rayet galaxy 1999A&AS..136...35S has been thoroughly studied in the optical broadband and narrowband e.g. 1984Msngr..38....2B,1986A&AS...64..469B,1985A&A...146..269B,2002A&A...390..891B . It has undergone previous and rather recent star--formation events, as evidenced by its rich population of intermediate--age globular clusters 1998A&A...335...85O , which are unresolved in our images. Based on its morphology, 2003ApJS..147...29G classify it as iE BCG, but its chaotic velocity field is significant indication that this galaxy is in fact a recent merger 2001A&A...374..800O . It has a spectroscopically confirmed companion at 70 kpc projected distance 1999A&AS..137..419O .

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 338-IG04 as a luminous blue compact starburst galaxy with merger-like morphology, young compact clusters, and ACS Lyman imaging of the galaxy and continuum.

quotes.quote: ESO 338-IG04 (Tololo 1924-416) is a well known, luminous ( M_V = -19.3 ) Blue Compact Galaxy in the local universe. It has a complex morphology indicating a recent merger and/or close interaction and contains a central young starburst with compact star clusters of ages 40 Myr. The galaxy was imaged using the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) onboard the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) in the Lyman line and continuum.

gmw_00001656.name: SL2S 0217
gmw_00001656.overview: SL2S 0217 is a low-mass, metal-poor dwarf galaxy at a redshift of z=1.844 [arXiv:1803.02340, arXiv:2101.00841]. It is strongly gravitationally lensed by a foreground elliptical galaxy, producing a bright Einstein arc that exhibits extreme star-forming conditions [arXiv:1803.02340, arXiv:2101.00841].

sources.summary: SL2SJ021737-051329 is described as a strongly magnified, low-mass, very low-metallicity lensed galaxy whose UV spectrum shows extreme star-forming conditions, strong nebular lines, little outflowing gas, and hard stellar ionization.

quotes.quote: We report new observations of SL2SJ021737-051329, a lens system consisting of a bright arc at z=1.84435 , magnified 17 by a massive galaxy at z=0.65 . SL2SJ0217 is a low-mass (M <10^9 M _ ), low-metallicity (Z 1/20 Z _ ) galaxy, with extreme star-forming conditions that produce strong nebular UV emission lines in the absence of any apparent outflows. Here we present several notable features from rest-frame UV Keck/LRIS spectroscopy: (1) Very strong narrow emission lines are measured for C 1548,1550, He 1640, O ] 1661,1666, Si ] 1883,1892, and C ] 1907,1909.

(2) Double-peaked Ly emission is observed with a dominant blue peak and centered near the systemic velocity. (3) The low- and high-ionization absorption features indicate very little or no outflowing gas along the sightline to the lensed galaxy. The relative emission line strengths can be reproduced with a very high-ionization, low-metallicity starburst with binaries, with the exception of He , which indicates an additional ionization source is needed.
Therefore, SL2S0217 serves as a template for the extreme conditions that are important for reionization and thought to be more common in the early Universe.

sources.summary: SL2S 0217 is a strongly lensed z=1.844 metal-poor dwarf EELG whose faint [C II] and absent CO emission are used to probe its neutral and molecular gas.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA [ C 2 ] and PdBI CO(2--1) observations of a z=1.8 , strongly lensed EELG SL2S 0217, a bright Lyman- emitter with a metallicity 0.05  Z_ . We obtain a tentative ( 3-4 ) detection of the [ C 2 ] line and set an upper limit on the [ C 2 ] /SFR ratio of 1 10^6 L_ /( M_ yr ^ 1 ), based on the synthesized images and visibility-plane analysis. The CO(2-1) emission is not detected.

Target description SL2S 021737–051329 (henceforth SL2S 0217, J2000 02h 17m 37.237s -05d13m29.7s) is a redshift z=1.844 EELG, strongly gravitationally lensed by a z=0.6459 elliptical galaxy. SL2S 0217 was serendipitously discovered by source ID SXDF-iS-170569 Geach2007 . Thanks to its position under the cusp of the lensing caustic, SL2S 0217 is lensed into a brilliant, 2.5-arcsec long Einstein arc, with a fainter counter-image.

gmw_00001657.name: NGC 6328
gmw_00001657.overview: NGC 6328 is an early-type LINER galaxy [arXiv:2111.02611, arXiv:2310.02033] hosting a young, compact symmetric radio source [arXiv:2002.12870, arXiv:1412.4216] that exhibits gamma-ray emission from its expanding lobes [arXiv:2111.02611, arXiv:2303.11357]. It features a massive, warped neutral hydrogen and molecular disk [arXiv:1409.0566, arXiv:2310.02033] containing a jet-induced cold gas outflow [arXiv:2310.02033, arXiv:2111.00453].

sources.summary: 1718--649 is described as a very young, compact, nearby CSO and the first bona fide gamma-ray CSO, with Fermi emission attributed to jet activity rather than star formation.

quotes.quote: Gamma-ray Emitters 1718--649 1146+596 1718--649 and 1146+596 are the two lowest-luminosity sources in our sample, which are rather unique objects for several reasons. 1718--649 is particularly young and compact LS 2 pc, 100 yr; Tingay97,Tingay03,Giroletti09 , located at the distance of 60 Mpc only, and most importantly, it is the first bona-fide CSO detected in the high-energy ray range with Fermi Large Area Telescope LAT; see the analysis and the discussion in Migliori16 .

The question follows if the observed ray emission in both sources is related to the jet activity and compact radio lobes in particular Stawarz08,Kino09 , or is instead due to the star formation activity within the hosts, analogous to the LAT-detected, star-forming and starburst galaxies Ackermann12,Hayashida13,Rojas16 . In order to address this question, we have collected all archival infrared data for both sources, including near-infrared 2MASS and MIR Spitzer spectra, as well as mm fluxes from WMAP, and estimated the integrated 8-1000 m luminosities, and next the star-formation rates assuming the Kennicutt98 scaling relation SFR M_ yr^ 1 = 1.7 L_ 8-1000 m 10^ 10 L_ , with the initial mass function factor = 0.79 Ackermann12 . The resulting values, given in Table tab:SFR , are consistent with the SFR estimates by Willett10 for 1718--649, based on either Ne lines or PAH features in the Spitzer high-resolution spectra (see the Table).

Meanwhile, the integrated 0.1-100 GeV photon flux of the high-energy ray counterpart of 1718--649 provided by Migliori16 , (11.5 0.3) 10^ 9 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , along with the best-fit photon index _ = 2.9 0.3 , yields the luminosity L_ 0.1-100 GeV 2 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , which is three orders of magnitude larger than the one expected for the ISM emission as estimated above. Similarly, for 1146+596, based on the integrated 1-100 GeV photon flux of the high-energy ray counterpart provided in 4FGL, (2.17 0.35) 10^ 10 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , along with the best-fit photon index _ = 2.06 0.11 , we derive the luminosity L_ 0.1-100 GeV 6 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , which is again orders of magnitude larger than the expected emission for the ISM component alone. Hence, we conclude that the ray emission detected with Fermi LAT from 1718--649 and 1146+596, is undoubtedly related to the jet activity in the sources, and not the star formation activity.

sources.summary: PKS 1718 649 is modeled as a nearby, century-old compact radio source in NGC 6328, with gamma rays from lobes and ambiguous X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: We present a model for the broadband radio-to- ray spectral energy distribution of the compact radio source, . Because of its young age (100 years) and proximity ( z=0.014 ), \ offers a unique opportunity to study nuclear conditions and the jet/host galaxy feedback process at the time of an initial radio jet expansion. \ is one of a handful of young radio jets with ray emission confirmed with the Fermi /LAT detector. We show that this ray emission can be successfully explained by Inverse Compton scattering of the ultraviolet photons, presumably from an accretion flow, off non-thermal electrons in the expanding radio lobes.

\ is a well known radio source in NGC 6328 classified as a low-ionization nuclear emission-line region (LINER) galaxy with photoionization as the main excitation mechanism of emission lines (Filippenko 1985). It hosts a supermassive black hole with a mass of the order of 10 ^8 M _ (Willett et al. 2010). The radio source has convex radio spectrum peaking in the GHz range (Tingay et al. 2015) and it belongs to the class of Gigahertz-Peaked Spectrum sources (GPS; e.g.

PKS 1718 649 was the first CSO detected in the ray band (Migliori et al. 2016). The reported /LAT test statistic was TS = 36 ( 5 ) band and TS = 18.5 ( = 4.3 ) in the 0.1--100 GeV and 0.2--100 GeV band, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports extended X-ray emission around 1718 649, models it with an added beta component, and notes that deeper observations are needed to isolate the extended medium.

quotes.quote: The surface brightness profile for the third object, 1718 649, is shown in Figure fig:profile . An excess of the observed emission above the PSF profile is visible at the distances within 2--6 ( 0.6-1.8 kpc) from the radio source position indicating an extended X-ray emission present in this source. We add another model component (the standard beta model, beta1d in Sherpa ; King 1962) to account for this emission. We obtained a 1 lower limit of > 0.55 in our best fit model. Deep \ observations are needed to better constrain the properties of this emission.

Even though we detected an extended X-ray emission in 1718 649, the lowest redshift CSO in our sample, we find that the X-ray spectrum of this source is quite well modeled with an absorbed power law. However, our Chandra spectrum has only 224 15 counts (see Table tab:chandra-fit ) and any detailed analysis of potential contributions from emission associated with the extended medium would require higher quality X-ray data.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC6328, a nearby radio galaxy with a GPS core, finding a warped molecular disk likely from a merger and detached inner gas structures interpreted as a jet-induced cold outflow.
