quotes.quote: We report the detection of outflowing molecular gas at the center of the nearby radio galaxy NGC6328 (z=0.014), which has a gigahertz-peaked spectrum radio core and a compact ( 2 ) young double radio lobe tracing jet. Utilizing Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) and observations, as well as a novel code developed to fit the 3D gas distribution and kinematics, to study the molecular gas kinematics, we find that the bulk of the gas is situated within a highly warped disk structure, most likely the result of a past merger event. Our analysis further uncovers, within the inner regions of the gas distribution ( R<300 ) and at a position angle aligning with that of the radio jet ( 150 ), the existence of two anti-diametric molecular gas structures kinematically detached from the main disk. These structures most likely trace a jet-induced cold gas outflow with a total lower limit mass of 2 10^6 mass, corresponding to an outflow rate of 2 M_ yr^ 1 and a kinetic power of 2.7 10^ 40 erg s^ 1 .

NGC 6328 is an early-type galaxy with a well-studied gigahertz-peaked source (GPS) radio core (PKS B1718-649) that exhibits clear evidence of out-of-dynamic-equilibrium kinematics in both its cold and warm gas phases maccagni2014, maccagni2016a, maccagni2018 . Due to its proximity ( D=62.88 at z=0.014 ), NGC6328 is an ideal target for studying the impact of young jets on the ISM with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA) and it is part of the CO-ARC survey on the molecular gas content of radio galaxies audibert2022 . PKS B1718-649 shows a typical GPS spectrum, with a turnover point at around 3.2 tingay1997 .

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS B1718--649 as a very young radio galaxy with a large H I disk; past interaction formed the gas reservoir, while local gas clouds may fuel activity.

quotes.quote: We present new Australia Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) observations of the young ( <10^2 years) radio galaxy PKS B1718--649 . We study the morphology and the kinematics of the neutral hydrogen ( H I ) disk ( M_ H I =1.1 10^ 10 M _ , radius 30 kpc). In particular, we focus on the analysis of the cold gas in relation to the triggering of the nuclear activity.

Since the radio source is very young ( 10^2 years), there is a significant time delay between the episode that formed the disk and the beginning of the radio activity. Even though the past interaction provided the galaxy of a massive reservoir of cold gas, the triggering of the radio activity must be attributed to another phenomenon, which let some amount of cold gas loose angular momentum and fall into the SMBH, without perturbing the overall regular rotation of the disk. In absorption, we detect two separate lines.

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1718 649 as a GPS/CSO radio galaxy whose variable peaked spectrum is best explained by free-free absorption plus high-frequency adiabatic expansion.

quotes.quote: We find that the PKS 1718 649 spectrum and its variability are best explained by variations in the free-free optical depth on our line-of-sight to the radio source at low frequencies (below the spectral peak) and the adiabatic expansion of the radio source itself at high frequencies (above the spectral peak). The optical depth variations are found to be plausible when X-ray continuum absorption variability seen in samples of Active Galactic Nuclei is considered. We find that the cause of the peaked spectrum in PKS 1718 649 is most likely due to free-free absorption. In agreement with previous studies, we find that the spectrum at each epoch of observation is best fit by a free-free absorption model characterised by a power-law distribution of free-free absorbing clouds.

PKS 1718 649 (NGC 6328: z=0.014428 0.000023 from mey04 ) is a well-studied gigahertz-peaked spectrum (GPS) radio galaxy, displaying all the classic attributes of this class of object, as presented in previous studies tin97,tin03 : a compact double radio morphology on parsec scales; low radio variability; low radio polarisation; a persistently peaked radio spectrum; and a kinematically complicated, high gas density host galaxy environment. Recently the GPS classification has been supplanted by the Compact Symmetric Object (CSO) classification, where CSOs are suggested to be young radio galaxies and possess the characteristics that PKS 1718 649 displays wil94 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6328 as the early-type host of a compact radio source with a massive warped H I disk whose regular rotation argues against recent interaction-triggered activity.

quotes.quote: As seen in many radio sources, \ is hosted by an early-type galaxy, NGC 6328 . Nevertheless, it has a massive ( M_ H I = 1.1 10^ 10 ) face-on \ disk, extending out to 30 kpc from the nucleus (as shown in fig:intensity , Veron-C etty et al., 1995). Deeper and higher resolution observations were recently taken with the Australian Telescope Compact Array (ATCA) in order to study the kinematics of the \ disk and, consequently, to trace the interaction history of the host galaxy (see Maccagni et al.

(2014) for a detailed analysis). These observations are well matched by a warped disk regularly rotating up to the distance of 23 kpc, where the neutral hydrogen starts deviating from circular motions. This radius (in dashed blue in Fig.

sources.summary: NGC 6328 is described as a nearby massive radio galaxy whose warped molecular disk contains high-velocity gas best explained as a jet-driven molecular outflow.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of outflowing molecular gas in the center of the nearby (z=0.014) massive radio galaxy NGC 6328. The radio core of the galaxy, PKS B1718-649, is identified as a Gigahertz Peaked Spectrum source with a compact (2 pc) double radio lobe morphology. We used ALMA CO(2-1) and CO(3-2) observations at 100 pc resolution to study the gas kinematics up to 5 kpc from the galaxy center.
The molecular gas in NGC 6328 is distributed in an asymmetric, highly inclined, and clumpy disk extending up to 4.5 kpc, closely correlated with the dust geometry.

Also, the low-luminosity AGN ( L_ AGN / 4.3 10^ 31 dyn ) at the center of NGC 6328 cannot provide the total radiation pressure needed to sustain the outflow momentum rate even if we adopt a high momentum boost factor of 20. On the other hand, the jet power of 2 10^ 43 ergs/s wojtowiczJetProductionEfficiency2020 is 3 orders greater than the kinetic power of the outflow and can easily drive this outflow.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1718--649 as a lobe-dominated gamma-ray CSO, finding low GeV luminosity, steep spectrum, no obvious variability, and likely mini-lobe emission.

quotes.quote: The ray emitting compact symmetric objects (CSOs) PKS 1718--649, NGC 3894, and TXS 0128+554 are lobe-dominated in the radio emission. In order to investigate their ray radiation properties, we analyze the 14-yr Fermi/LAT observation data of the three CSOs. They all show the low luminosity ( 10^ 41 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) and no significant variability in the ray band.

PKS 1718--649 has a steep spectrum at the GeV band with a photon spectral index of _ =2.49 0.16 . Its 14-yr average luminosity at the GeV band is (1.02 0.19) 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . No obvious variability is observed with TS_ var =35.6 , only four detection points are presented in the whole light curve, and the first three detection points are obtained with the first 7-yr observations.

sources.summary: PKS 1718--63 is presented as a very young radio galaxy with narrow redshifted and blueshifted absorption lines, unsettled inner gas clouds, and atomic/molecular signatures of SMBH feeding.

quotes.quote: Another extremely interesting case is the very young ( 100 yr) radio galaxy PKS 1718--63 where very narrow absorption lines (both redshifted and blueshifted) were detected. This radio source is only 2 pc in size and the presence of narrow absorption components at different velocities suggests they trace unsettled gas clouds in the inner regions Veron00,Maccagni14 . Extra constraints about their nature come from the detection of redshifted absorption and emission due to molecular clouds in this object Maccagni16,Maccagni18 . Thus, also in this object, both atomic and molecular gas show signatures of clouds that can be involved in feeding the recently activated SMBH. The discovery of such small clouds with unsettled velocities supports the scenario of AGN fuelling known as chaotic cold accretion, now predicted by several numerical simulations King15,Gaspari13,Gaspari17a,Gaspari17b .

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1718 649 as the next-nearest low-redshift GPS radio galaxy, contrasting its compact classical-double morphology and higher radio luminosity with the two closest GPS sources.

quotes.quote: PKS 2254 367 and the radio source in NGC 1052 (PKS 0238 084; D =17.2 Mpc) are the two closest GPS radio sources to us, far closer than the next closest example, PKS 1718 649 ( D=59 Mpc).

NGC 1052 (z=0.005037) at 17.2 Mpc and PKS 1718 649 (NGC 6328: 0.014428) at 59 Mpc, in the same frame of reference, are the only two other GPS radio sources within 100 Mpc, for which the detailed kinematics of the host galaxies can be studied (see, e.g., Labiano et al. \ 2007).

PKS 1718 649 has a classical double morphology with an extent of 2 pc and a monochromatic luminosity at 5 GHz of 2 10^ 24 W Hz ^ 1 (Tingay et al. \ 1997). Unlike NGC 1052, no motion in the structure of PKS 1718 649 has been detected following multi-epoch VLBI observations, with an estimated upper limit on the separation speed of the double components of 0.08 (Tingay et al. \ 2002).

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1718--64 as the smallest and youngest source in the sample, showing infalling molecular clouds likely tied to AGN fuelling rather than jet disruption.

quotes.quote: The only case where we could identify infalling clouds of molecular gas, likely connected with the fuelling of the AGN, is PKS 1718--64 Maccagni18 . Interestingly, this is the smallest (and youngest) radio source in the sample. With only 2 pc in size, the radio plasma may not have been able yet to affect the surrounding medium. The detection of infalling cloud appears to be consistent with a fuelling process, as predicted by the chaotic cold accretion (e.g. \ Gaspari17 ) and similar to the case of PKS 2322-123 in Abell 2597 Tremblay16 .

sources.summary: The paper reports moderate PAH emission in PKS 1718 649, a smooth-shell-like silicate ratio, and one of the lowest star-formation rates in the sample.

quotes.quote: 1146+59, 4C +31.04, 1946+70, PKS 1718 649, and 4C +37.11 all show moderate PAH emission strongest at 11.3 and 12.7 .
The silicate ratio for PKS 1718 649 is consistent with the sir08 models for smooth, geometrically thick spherical shells of dust.
The star formation rates from 1146+59 and PKS 1718 649, however, are the lowest measured in the sample ( <2 M _ /yr, from both neon and PAH data).

sources.summary: The review describes PKS1718 649 as a well-studied peaked-spectrum source with spectral variability, limited molecular gas mass, and gamma-ray emission consistent with inverse-Compton scattering in compact lobes.

quotes.quote: In the well-studied PS source PKS1718 649 (NGC 6328) variability was found both below and above the peak, where variations in the optically thick part have been attributed to variations in optical depth while in the optically thin part to adiabatic losses of the synchrotron-emitting lobes Tingay2003a,Tingay2015a .

The evidence for large gas masses in individual sources suspected of FFA is mixed -- Table t:gas-mass shows high molecular gas masses ( > 10^ 10 M_ ) for 0108+388 and OQ208, but not for PKS 1718 649 and TXS 1946+708.
The detection of ray emission from the nearby CSO PKS 1718 649 is consistent with IC scattering of ambient emission by the relativistic electrons in the compact lobes Migliori2016b .

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 1718 649 as a bona fide CSO with diffuse Chandra X-rays, central starburst-linked host emission, and gamma-ray emission interpreted from radio lobes.

quotes.quote: Chandra observations of PKS 1718 649 (J1723-6500) show the X-rays diffuse emission extending out to about 2.5 kpc distance from the nucleus and linked to a starburst activity in the central regions of the host galaxy. Three bona fide cso have been detected in rays with Fermi LAT: PKS 1718 649 2016ApJ...821L..31M , TXS 0128+554 (J0131+5545) 2020ApJ...899..141L and NGC 3894 (J1148+5924) 2020AA...635A.185P . Given the cso ' radio morphology rays are predicted to originate in the radio lobes 2008ApJ...680..911S,2021MNRAS.507.4564P and this process was successful in explaining the rays detected in PKS 1718 649 2021arXiv211102611S .

gmw_00001658.name: damped Ly galaxy at redshift z = 3.4 toward QSO 0000--2620
aliases_local: damped Ly galaxy at redshift z = 3.4 toward QSO 0000--2620
gmw_00001658.overview: The damped Lyman-alpha galaxy at redshift z = 3.4 is located along the line of sight toward the luminous, radio-quiet quasar Q 0000-263 [arXiv:astro-ph/0011470, arXiv:2110.07065]. High-resolution spectra of this high-redshift system reveal molecular hydrogen absorption [arXiv:astro-ph/0011470], while the background quasar exhibits soft-band X-ray variability [arXiv:2110.07065].

sources.original_name: damped Ly galaxy at redshift z = 3.4 toward QSO 0000--2620
sources.summary: The paper reports UVES/VLT detection of H2 absorption in the z=3.4 DLA galaxy and derives molecular column density, excitation temperature, and a very small molecular fraction.

quotes.quote: Tiepolo 11, 34131 Trieste, Italy European Southern Observatory, 85748 Garching bei M u nchen, Germany New results from the search for H _2 absorption in the damped Ly galaxy at redshift z = 3.4 toward QSO 0000--2620 ( z_ em = 4.1 ) are reported. The high-resolution ( / = 48,000 ) spectra of Q0000--2620 were obtained using the Ultraviolet - Visual Echelle Spectrograph (UVES) on the 8.2m ESO Kueyen telescope. The ortho-H _2 column density is found to be N(J=1) = (5.55 1.35) 10^ 13 cm ^ 2 ( 2 C.L.). The combination of N(J=1) with the limits available for other low rotational levels restricts the excitation temperature T_ ex in the range (290 - 540) K.

Absorption from H _2 in the z = 3.4 DLA system toward Q0000--2620 was not detected in the 1 resolution spectrum obtained with the Multiple Mirror Telescope (MMT) and only an upper limit of f( H _2) < 3 10^ 6 has been reported previously . New observations with the UVES/VLT, having approximately 11 times higher resolution, revealed H _2 absorption in this DLA. Exactly at the expected position of the L(4-0)R(1) line where the MMT gave the limit W_ rest < 114 m (3 ), an absorption line with the equivalent width W_ rest 6 m was detected .

sources.summary: The paper monitors Q 0000-263 as one of four luminous high-redshift radio-quiet quasars, adding Chandra epochs, stacked imaging, and finding soft-band X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: The sources, Q 0000-263 , BR 0351-1034 , PSS 0926+3055 , and PSS 1326+0743 , were selected out of the sample of as having the highest accessible redshifts for economical Chandra observations and having at least two archival \ epochs. In this paper, we present three additional \ epochs for the \ sources (nine \ epochs in total), that allow us to extend our time-series analysis and produce tighter constraints on the X-ray properties of these sources.

The resulting stacked images of PSS 0926+3055 and PSS 1326+0743 contain observations from \ Cycles 3, 12-15 , and 19-21 , amounting to 40 ks each (spanning 1300 days in the rest frame), and Q 0000-263 and BR 0351-1034 are composed of observations from Cycles 12-15 and 19-21 , amounting to 70 ks each (spanning 700 days in the rest frame).

We find that two of our four sources, Q 0000-263 and PSS 0926+3055 , are \ variable in the soft band and three sources, BR 0351-1034 , PSS 0926+3055 , and PSS 1326+0743 , are variable in the hard band.

gmw_00001659.name: NGC 564
gmw_00001659.overview: NGC 564 is an elliptical galaxy located in the Abell 194 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0212262]. It has a radial velocity of approximately 5800 km/s, which has been used to measure its distance scale via surface brightness fluctuations [arXiv:astro-ph/0212262].

sources.summary: The paper uses deep VLT I-band surface-brightness-fluctuation observations of NGC 564 in Abell 194 to measure its distance scale and infer H0 near 70 km/s/Mpc.

quotes.quote: Occhialini, Via Bassini 15, 20133 Milano, Italy European Southern Observatory, Karl-Schwarzschild-Strasse 2, 85748 Garching, Germany Received June 28, 2002; We have measured the Hubble constant H_0 in NGC 564 at cz 5800 km s ^ 1 and in NGC 7619 at cz 3700 km s ^ 1 with deep I--band Surface Brightness Fluctuation distance measurements at the ESO Very Large Telescope (VLT). We obtain H_0 = 70 7 6 km s ^ 1 /Mpc for NGC 564 and H_0 = 68 6 6 for NGC 7619.
Deep I--band SBF observations were obtained for two galaxies : NGC 564, in Abell 194, at cz 5800 km s ^ 1 , and NGC 7619 in the Pegasus cluster at cz 3700 km s ^ 1 .
For NGC 564, the nights of observation were on 5 October, 3 November, 2, 19, 22 December 2000, and 1, 18 and 21 January 2001 for the I--band and on 19 August 2001 for the V--band.

gmw_00001660.name: Mrk 297
gmw_00001660.overview: Mrk 297 is a clumpy irregular galaxy [arXiv:1710.03149] undergoing a late-stage merger [arXiv:0708.2703] between two perpendicularly oriented galaxies [arXiv:1710.03149]. This interaction has triggered a strong starburst [arXiv:0708.2703] dominated by very young stellar clusters [arXiv:2008.12794] and a highly disturbed, asymmetric distribution of neutral hydrogen [arXiv:0708.2703].

sources.summary: The paper maps disturbed HI and compact CO in Mrk 297, interpreting its asymmetric gas, tidal tail, strong starburst, and high gas masses as evidence for a late-stage merger.

quotes.quote: Individual Galaxies Mrk 297 Figures f:MRK297.HI.MOM0 and f:MRK297.HI.SDSS show a disturbed, asymmetric distribution of \ in Mrk 297, with a north-south elongation. A slight extension is also visible in the optical image. The \ velocity field of Mrk 297 (Figure f:MRK297.HI.MOM1 ) shows a velocity gradient across the main part of the galaxy, but the north-south extension is at a nearly constant higher velocity. The measured \ mass, 6.3 10 ^9 M _ , is 90 of that measured with the GBT.

This galaxy has been interpreted as the result of a collision between two late-type spirals 1979A&A....78L...5A , or a spiral and an irregular 1988Afz....28...47B , which induced star formation throughout the galaxy. found, using numerical N-body simulations, that the optical morphological properties of Mrk 297 can be explained as the result of a co-planar radial penetration collision between two disk galaxies. They suggest that in this collision, the target galaxy (on left) was deformed into the north-south ``wing'' by the face-on intruder galaxy. Our \ map (Figure f:MRK297.HI.SDSS ) shows a tidal tail that is much more extended and lopsided than the optical ``wing'' to which matched their simulation. Our \ observations do not appear to support the co-planar radial penetration model; more likely Mrk 297 is in a later stage of merging than suggested by this model.

sources.summary: The survey treats NGC 6052 as a [C I]-dark candidate: CO is detected, [C I] is not, and its low [C I]/CO may relate to a young merger-driven starburst.

quotes.quote: In NGC 6052 and NGC 7679, ^ 12 CO J =4--3 was detected but [ C 1 ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 was not detected with a [ C 1 ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 / ^ 12 CO J =4--3 ratio of 0.08 . Two possible scenarios of weak [ C 1 ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 emission are C ^0 poor/CO-rich environments or an environment with an extremely large [ C 1 ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 missing flux.

ALMA/ACA recently identified a galaxy, NGC 6052 Michiyama_2020 , which may be a counterexample to the recent concept of the ubiquitous [ C 1 ] /CO distribution during the cloud lifetime and the C ^0 rich/CO-poor region due to the CO dissociation process, proposed by e.g., Papa_2004_theory, Papadopoulos_2018 . During this survey, we further discovered the [ CI ] non-detection case of gas-rich type 2 AGN, NGC 7679. Figure fig:Mdust_CO_SFR-CI shows that the upper limits of [ C 1 ] are small when we compare the global properties of galaxies, i.e., M_ dust , SFR, and L'_ CO(1-0) , demonstrating that NGC 6052 and NGC 7679 are candidates for the [ C 1 ] dark galaxies.

For example, Michiyama_2020 show that the small [ C 1 ] /CO line ratio in NGC 6052 is possibly due to the dense gas compressed at the collision front. In addition, the other [ C 1 ] non-detected galaxy, NGC 7679, also shows a young starburst, possibly triggered by a galaxy interaction (see details in Section sec:ND ). Therefore, a small [ C 1 ] /CO ratio may be common in merging galaxies.

sources.summary: ALMA observations of merging galaxy NGC 6052 detect CO but not [C I], implying dense merger-driven ISM conditions and warning that [C I] may undertrace its molecular gas.

quotes.quote: We report sensitive [ C 1 ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 and ^ 12 CO J =4--3 observations of the nearby merging galaxy NGC 6052 using the Morita (Atacama Compact) Array of ALMA. We detect ^ 12 CO J =4--3 toward the northern part of NGC 6052, but [ C 1 ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 is not detected with a [ C 1 ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 to ^ 12 CO J =4--3 line luminosity ratio of 0.07 . According to models of photodissociation regions, the unusual weakness of [ C 1 ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 relative to ^ 12 CO J =4--3 can be explained if the interstellar medium has a hydrogen density larger than 10^5 cm ^ 3 , conditions that might arise naturally in the ongoing merging process in NGC 6052.

Its [ C I ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 emission is also weaker than expected given the molecular gas mass inferred from previous measurements of ^ 12 CO J =1--0 and ^ 12 CO J =2--1. This suggests that [ C I ] ^3P_1 - ^3P_0 may not be a reliable tracer of molecular gas mass in this galaxy. NGC 6052 is a unique laboratory to investigate how the merger process impacts the molecular gas distribution.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC6052 as an earlier/intermediate merger with many very young clusters, disturbed arms, star-forming clumps, and uniformly young stellar populations.

quotes.quote: The remaining systems, NGC3256, NGC3690, and NGC6052 have increasing number of clusters with ages younger than 100 Myr and peaked below 10 Myr, suggesting a recent enhancement in their SFR.

NGC6052 shows a strongly perturbed morphology and distorted arms, morphologically dominated by star-forming clumps and stellar clusters. Clusters younger than 10 Myr are detected along the main body of the system. This uniform age distribution is supported by the MIR imaging and spectroscopic study of the galaxy performed by whelan07 . They find that the 16 m emission and MIR emission lines in the detected knots are consistent with being produced by ionisation from a very young stellar population ( < 6 Myr).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6052 as a colliding, metal-rich low-redshift system whose weak [C i] emission can be reproduced by models with low C/O ratios.

quotes.quote: NGC 6052 (Mrk 297, Arp 209) is a low-redshift ( z=0.01581 ) system of colliding galaxies with an SFR 18 M _ yr ^ 1 Michiyama20 . The latter group reported nondetection of [ C i ] (1-0) and a bright emission of the CO J=4-3 , resulting in a luminosity ratio of L_ CI 1-0 /L_ CO 4-3 <0.08 . In their analysis, Michiyama20 used PDR models by assuming MW abundances and they found that such a ratio can be reproduced if the observed number densities are n_ H >10^5 cm ^ 3 which may be reasonably explained from the strong shocks produced due to the merging process. However, our models can reproduce the observed ratio if a low C/O ratio is considered. More specifically, NGC 6052 is observed to be metal rich, with 12+ ( O/H )=8.22-8.85 Sage93,James02,Shi05,Rupke08 corresponding to O/H =-0.47 to +0.16 , which matches -on average- with O _0 models.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6052, alias Mkn 297, as a clumpy irregular close merger of two perpendicular galaxies with high radio brightness and high-resolution continuum observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 6052, which became known as a so-called `clumpy irregular galaxy' under its label Mkn 297 1979aers.conf..205H , is revealed as a close merger of two galaxies oriented perpendicularly by HST images 1996AJ....112..416H . It has a high radio brightness. High-resolution radio continuum observations of this system were reported by deeg93 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Arp 209 as an exception to the inclined-disc explanation and reports a stable inner center followed by variation, with high A1, A2, and A3 values.

quotes.quote: This could be used to explain the high values of A _2 seen in many galaxies in our sample. In fact this effect is seen in all the galaxies except Arp 221 (Class I), Arp 212 (Class II) , and Arp 209 (Class III). Thus, in most cases studied, the merger remnants seem to indicate a preferred disc plane.
Arp 209: The centre co-ordinates are constant in the inner 4", and vary thereafter. The values of A _1 , A _2 and A _3 are high.

gmw_00001661.name: Mrk 314
gmw_00001661.overview: Mrk 314 is an isolated, optically peculiar elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0608179] that may be a polar ring galaxy [arXiv:0708.2703]. It contains an unusually large amount of cool gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0608179] and hosts an inner polar gaseous disk likely formed through a merger with a gas-rich dwarf [arXiv:astro-ph/0411457].

sources.summary: The paper finds diffuse irregular HI around Mrk 314, discusses a possible polar-ring structure and nearby companion, and links detected gas clumps to that structure.

quotes.quote: Mrk 314 Figure f:MRK314 shows diffuse, irregularly shaped \ emission in Mrk 314. The \ and optical peaks are roughly coincident. The \ emission extends well beyond the optical portion of the galaxy, although there are optical features at the ends of two \ extensions to the south. There is an east-west velocity gradient across the southern half of the galaxy, while the northern portion of the galaxy has a confused velocity field (Figure f:MRK314.HI.MOM1 ). attempted to map Mrk 314 in CO(J=1--0) using the OVRO--MMA but did not detect the galaxy.

Mrk 314 is classified as an elliptical in both the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) and HyperLeda 2003A&A...412...45P , and may be a polar ring galaxy, as suggested by . Polar ring galaxies are composed of a gas-rich ring around an early-type, often S0, galaxy, and are most likely formed by tidal accretion of gas from a companion 2003A&A...401..817B . obtained deep optical images of Mrk 314 and suggested that optical clumps seen to the south and southwest (lower right) may be part of the polar ring structure. This is consistent with the \ emission we detect. We also detect clumps of \ to the east (left) of the main galaxy which are not associated with any optical emission.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 7468 lacks a kinematically decoupled outer polar ring but contains a 1.5 kpc inner polar gaseous disk likely formed by collision and capture of a gas-rich dwarf.

quotes.quote: We present our spectroscopic observations of the galaxy NGC 7468 performed at the 6-m Special Astrophysical Observatory telescope using the UAGS long-slit spectrograph, the multipupil fiber spectrograph MPFS, and the scanning Fabry-Perot interferometer (IFP). We found no significant deviations from the circular rotation of the galactic disk in the velocity field in the regions of brightness excess along the major axis of the galaxy (the putative polar ring). Thus, these features are either tidal structures or weakly developed spiral arms. However, we detected a gaseous disk at the center of the galaxy whose rotation plane is almost perpendicular to the plane of the galactic disk. The central collision of NGC 7468 with a gas-rich dwarf galaxy and their subsequent merging seem to be responsible for the formation of this disk.

sources.summary: NGC 7468 is singled out as an isolated, optically peculiar elliptical with unusually large cool gas content, likely from recently acquired nearby atomic gas.

quotes.quote: The outstanding exception is NGC 7468 (Markarian 314), which has almost as much gas as predicted by Ciotti et al. (1991) - most of this is HI. Although isolated, its optical structure is clearly peculiar. Perhaps NGC 7468 has recently acquired additional atomic gas from nearby.

gmw_00001662.name: SDSSJ0834+0139
gmw_00001662.overview: SDSSJ0834+0139 is a starburst galaxy that exhibits asymmetric, diffuse neutral hydrogen emission [arXiv:0708.2703]. It is interacting with a companion galaxy to the northeast, which has extended emission and may have had its ordered motion disturbed by the interaction [arXiv:0708.2703].

sources.summary: The paper maps asymmetric HI in SDSSJ0834+0139 and extended emission from its companion, noting that interaction may have disturbed the companion motion.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ0834+0139 Figures f:SL0834.HI.MOM0 and f:SL0834.HI.SDSS show asymmetric, diffuse \ emission from SDSSJ0834+0139, and extended, more diffuse \ emission from its companion to the northeast (upper left). Together, the galaxies are known as UGC 04480, and are classified as a spiral by NED and Hyperleda, while described them as a starburst galaxy (SDSSJ0834+0139) with a lens (the companion) attached to an arm . The \ from the companion galaxy is much more extended than the optical emission. The velocity field (Figure f:SL0834.HI.MOM1 ) shows an east-west velocity gradient across the galaxy, while its companion has a constant, higher velocity. It is possible that the interaction with SDSSJ0834+0139 has disturbed any ordered motion that this companion may have had in the past, or that the companion is seen face-on, as suggested by the optical image.

gmw_00001663.name: Mrk 538
gmw_00001663.overview: Mrk 538, also known as NGC 7714, is a spiral starburst galaxy [arXiv:0803.3000, arXiv:0805.1167] that is optically classified as a LINER [arXiv:0803.3000] and may host an unobscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.01273]. It is interacting with its companion NGC 7715 [arXiv:1406.6345, arXiv:2209.08147], forming an extended gas and optical bridge [arXiv:0708.2703, arXiv:astro-ph/0011428] and a tidal tail [arXiv:1406.6345, arXiv:astro-ph/0311030].

sources.summary: The paper cites HI maps of Mrk 538 showing a bridge, disk kinematic disturbances, and a recent collision in the Arp 284 starburst system.

quotes.quote: Mrk 538 show maps of \ emission and the velocity field of Mrk 538 and its companion. The \ emission is extended, forming a bridge between the two galaxies. While the inner portion of Mrk 538 shows a normal velocity gradient for a rotating disk, deviations from circular motion are present in the outer portions of the galaxy disk. The companion galaxy also exhibits signs of rotation 1997ApJ...483..754S . The total \ mass for the Mrk 538 system, within a region approximately 4 ^ 8 ^ , is M _ HI = 7.0 10 ^9 M _ .

This pair of galaxies, known as Arp 284, is one of the prototypical collisional starburst systems. Numerical models and the observed stellar and gas morphologies suggest a recent collision, between 100 to 200 Myr ago 2003ApJ...589..157S . Multi-wavelength data, summarized in , reveal young, intermediate age, and old stellar populations in Mrk 538. found, using population synthesis models, that the starburst is responsible for only a small portion of an extended star formation episode, triggered approximately 10 ^8 years ago.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 7714's Spitzer IRS starburst spectrum, deriving a lower star-formation rate and using the spectrum to constrain emission lines, abundance, and compact H II regions.

quotes.quote: As a final example of the fitting process, we compare our fit with the detailed Spitzer Space Observatory IRS low resolution spectra of NGC 7714 from Brandl06 . The fit is shown in figure fig:NGC7714 . Note that this object has a much lower star formation rate (8.0 ) than the previous two examples. The star formation rate is very well constrained by the normalization process, and can be determined to an accuracy of better than 5 , assuming that the IRS aperture integrates the full extent of the star forming region. For this object, the older stellar component is not well-constrained, as the IRS spectra do not quite extend to short enough wavelengths to measure it.

Note that the line emission spectrum is quite well fitted by the model, except for the strength of both the [ S 4 ] and the [ Ar 3 ] lines in the vicinity of the 9.7 \ silicate absorption band. Again, this may indicate a rather higher obscuration of the young \ regions than in the model. In this spectrum the abundance is fairly well constrained by the strength of the PAH features and the equivalent width of the emission lines, while the 20-35 \ slope constrains the values of C and f_ UCHII .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7714 as a prototypical SBc starburst interacting with NGC 7715; its young stellar component, northern intermediate-age population, bridge, and tidal-tail evidence are tied to that interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 7714: a SBc galaxy at 37.3 Mpc classified as a prototypical SB by weed81 . This galaxy is in interaction/merger with its companion NGC 7715, and such interaction could be responsible for the star formation bursts kinney93 . In fact, lancon01 show the bridge between NGC 7714 and its companion NGC 7715 (see their Fig. 2).

NGC 7714 The analysis of the flux contribution of the SP vectors from Fig. hist7714 a clearly shows the predominance of the young SP component along the galaxy. While the contribution of this young SP component increases toward southeast, the intermediate age SP component increases in the opposite direction. bern93 has presented an evolutionary model for the pair NGC 7714 and NGC 7715 and stated that NGC 7715 is in the postburst phase, starting its star formation around 10 ^8 yr ago, similar to our results. A tidal tail between these two galaxies can bee seen in smwa92 , and we suggest that the SP distribution northward (increasing intermediate age SP component) can be related to this poststarburst population in NGC 7715.

sources.summary: The SETI paper describes NGC 7714 as a nearby interacting starburst galaxy paired with NGC 7715 and uses the system to constrain powerful long-duty-cycle transmitters.

quotes.quote: The field BL J233623.1+020609.0 provides a good example of an interacting pair of galaxies - NGC 7714 at z = 0.009333 and NGC 7715 at z = 0.009243 . NGC7714 (also known as Arp 284) is a prototypical starburst galaxy that is interacting with its smaller neighbour NGC 7715 - the average star formation rate is 1 M _ y^ 1 and its properties are comparable to M82 (see Lancon2001 and references therein). NGC 7714 is detected across the electromagnetic spectrum, and is also associated with NVSS J233614+020918, 2 supernova events (SN 1999dn SN 2007fo) and 2 possible Ultra-luminous X-ray candidates. Located at a distance of 40 Mpc this system is only a little further away than the BL galaxy sample's limit ( < 30 Mpc).
Fig. f2 shows the field BL J233623.1+020609.0, containing NGC 7714 and NGC 7715.

For example, in the case of NGC 7714 and 7715, no evidence is found for transmitters with an EIRP in excess of 7.5 10^ 23 W. We define the prevalence of extraterrestrial transmitters as the fraction of stars in this interacting system that host a powerful ( EIRP > 7.5 10^ 23 W) long-duty cycle transmitter at the time the observations were made. For this particular system, the prevalence is < 10^ 12 .

sources.summary: The paper applies spectral inversion to NGC 7714, describing its nuclear starburst, mixed stellar populations, inhomogeneous central extinction, recent SFR peak, and older enhanced star formation possibly tied to interactions.

quotes.quote: NGC 7714 is a well studied interacting spiral galaxy which hosts a starburst in its nucleus (Arp 1966, Weedman et al. 1981, Calzetti 1997). The peaked ground based morphology of the starburst, the small inclination of the galaxy disk seen from our perspective, and the relatively low extinction suggested by the ultraviolet (UV) brightness (Markarian Lipovetskij 1974), motivated detailed studies of this apparently simple system. As a result of high resolution imaging and spectroscopy, the simplest models for the nucleus had to be successively excluded (Gonz alez-Delgado et al. 1995, 1999, and references therein). The central 330 pc of the galaxy were found to contain a variety of stellar populations, ranging from very young ( 5 Myr) to old (the population of the underlying spiral).

The present results mimic what imaging suggests is the real situation in the central 300 pc of NGC 7714 (LGLG01): a significant fraction of the UV-emitting stars are heavily attenuated in the UV, and contribute mainly to Br , while a smaller fraction of these hot stars is practically unobscured, explaining the small apparent reddening at UV wavelengths. The SFR found here exceeds 1 M _ yr ^ 1 only in the last 20 Myr. This timescale is consistent with the dynamical models of Struck Smith (2002). Nevertheless, there is an enhancement in the SFR about 7 10 ^8 yr ago (from about 0.1 to about 0.5 M _ yr ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7714 as a super-solar-metallicity spiral in the sample, notes its LINER classification, and argues its mid-infrared and Chandra properties support a pure starburst interpretation.

quotes.quote: These galaxies range in metallicity from extremely low (such as I Zw 18, with Z/Z_ = 1/50) to super-solar metallicity ( 1 Z_ ) galaxies such as NGC 7714. To differentiate between low and high-metallicity galaxies, we use a metallicity (12 + log [ O/H ] ) cut-off value of 8.85 - just less than solar, as per Paper I. The galaxies range widely in morphology from blue compact dwarfs such as I Zw 18 to spirals such as NGC 7714 and IC 342.

None of the galaxies in our sample are known to harbour AGNs. This is important, as PAH destruction can occur close to an AGN due to the hard ionization environment (e.g. Sturm et al. 2000). In addition [ FeII ] emission can be elevated in galaxies harbouring AGN. One possible exception is NGC 7714, which is optically classified as a LINER tho02 . However, the lack of [ NeV ] emission at 14 and 24 m and the absence of any evidence for an obscured AGN by recent Chandra imaging bran04,smi05 strongly suggests that NGC 7714 is a pure starburst and it is therefore included in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 7714 as an active starburst with dominant 1 Gyr and younger stellar bursts, CN absorption, and merger-related star formation from an off-center collision.

quotes.quote: NGC 7714 NGC 7714 (Mrk 538, Arp 284) has been classified by weed81 as a prototypical starburst galaxy. The optical stellar populations in the inner 5 kpc of this source have also been studied by FLL03. They find a 100 Myr old ( 33 ) dominant population. As for NGC 1614, we have combined the SSPs in the proportion calculated by FLL03 but the synthetic template did not match the observed NIR spectrum in the inner 115 pc. Our synthesis results indicates that NGC 7714 is an active starburst galaxy.

The results agree with the facts that this galaxy displays a proeminent 1.1 \ CN band riffel07 and that this source is in merging process due to a recent off-center collision (100-200 Myr) with NGC 7715 ss03 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7714 as one of the interacting Arp 284 spirals, with an optical/HI bridge to NGC 7715, an asymmetric circumnuclear ring, and several circumnuclear HII regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 7714 and NGC 7715 (at 2 ' (21 kpc) to the E; v_ sys = 2770 km s ^ 1 , D = 36.7 Mpc) form the Arp 284 system of interacting spirals, as is evident from the HI and optical bridge (Smith, Struck Pogge 1997; Papaderos Fricke 1998) between them. The inclination of NGC 7714 is 42 (Chapelon, Contini Davoust 1999). A striking feature of NGC 7714 is an asymmetric circumnuclear ring of diameter 20 '' 40 '' (3.6 7.2 kpc), with most of the emission located to the E of the nucleus. This ring is largely devoid of (GD95) and HI emission (Smith et al. 1997), and has NIR colours similar to the old disk population (Bushouse Werner 1990).

There are three main circumnuclear HII regions in NGC 7714, labelled A, B and C by GD95, and located in the bulge/disk of the galaxy at 5 '' (900 pc) E, 12 '' (2.1 kpc) NW and 14 '' (2.5 kpc) SW from the nucleus, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper reports a possible obscured supernova near the nucleus of starburst galaxy NGC 7714 and gives follow-up radio non-detections and limits.

quotes.quote: Comparison of archival (UKIRT IRCAM3) data with our INGRID images yielded the discovery of a possible SN with m_ K = 17.3 only 1 kpc projected distance from the nucleus of the starburst galaxy, NGC 7714 [ 18 ] . From the non-detection of this SN in the H-band we estimated a lower limit for the extinction towards the SN of Av 6.

We obtained VLA observations of NGC 7714 in the L and C-bands on 11 April 2002, but the possible SN was not detected. The 3 upper limits in the L and C-bands are 0.213 mJy and 0.147 mJy respectively (C. Stockdale, private communication). These upper limits indicate that the SN was probably fainter than the type IIn SN 1998S at radio wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7714 as Arp 284's more massive primary and finds mostly young tidal-tail clusters, with older clusters sharing a similar spatial distribution in the imaged disc.

quotes.quote: The peculiar morphology of the Arp 284 system results from an off-centre collision between two disc galaxies, with the primary (NGC 7714) having roughly three times the mass of the companion (NGC 7715).

Clusters in the tidal tails of NGC 7714 are generally found to have ages less than 20 Myr, though observational limits make the significance of this result uncertain. Older clusters, though not numerous, have nearly the same spatial distribution within the imaged portion of NGC 7714 as young clusters. The cluster population in the bridge connecting the two galaxies appears to be older, but the data in this part of the system is too limited to draw firm conclusions. The ages of the giant regions in NGC 7714 are generally older than those of their constituent clusters, possibly indicating that the young clusters we detect are surrounded by their dispersed predecessors.

sources.summary: GNOSIS observations of NGC 7714 show its FeII emission between OH lines, visible in both suppressed and non-suppressed spectra with less apparent suppression benefit.

quotes.quote: We discuss how future instruments can best benefit from the potential of OH suppression and what changes are necessary to do so ( sec:disc ). We give two examples of [ FeII ] emission from the Seyfert galaxies NGC 7674 and NGC 7714.
In both cases we compare a single OH suppressed fibre to the single control fibre, shown in Figure fig:seyferts .

We find that in regions where the OH line density is high, as for NGC 7674, the low resolution of IRIS2 makes it difficult to identify the [ FeII ] emission in the control spectrum, but that the line is obvious in the suppressed spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7714 as a compact-starburst galaxy with Chandra-studied nuclear X-ray emission, spectral modeling, and a possible unobscured AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 7714 is a well observed galaxy with a compact starburst nuclei e.g., Gonzalez-Delgado1995,Smith2005 . Smith2005 studied the X-ray emission from NGC 7714 with Chandra /ACIS. They report that the spectrum of the nuclear region can be fitted to a MEKAL function or an absorbed power-law function with a column density of N _ H = 2.2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 . Therefore, NGC 7714 possibly hosts an unobscured AGN.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7714 as a worked spectral example, comparing HST/COS and IUE data and reporting equivalent-width measurements for its detected ultraviolet absorption lines.

quotes.quote: As an example, the HST/COS and IUE spectra for one galaxy in our sample, NGC 7714, are plotted in Figure fig2 , showing substantial, but expected differences.
An example of this fit and the subsequently normalized continuum of the IUE spectrum of NGC 7714 are shown in Figure fig6 .

As an example, we give the measurements for the detected lines in the spectra of NGC 7714. The quoted values are EWs in \ for IUE, COS original, and COS smoothed to the resolution of IUE, respectively: S 2 1253 + Si 2 1260: ( 2.23 0.40, 2.53 0.01, 2.32 0.01 ), O 1 1302 + Si 2 1304: ( 2.70 0.44, 3.75 0.01, 3.18 0.01 ), C 2 1335: ( 2.83 0.54, 3.18 0.01, 3.22 0.01 ), Si 4 1393,1402: ( 6.96 0.70, 6.00 0.01, 5.89 0.01 ), and C 4 1548,1550: ( 6.91 0.70, 11.3 0.10, 9.45 0.01 ).

sources.summary: NGC 7714 is identified as the prototypical starburst Arp 284, with extremely intense central star formation and H-alpha knots in its tail toward NGC 7715.

quotes.quote: The sample includes some interacting galaxies, including UGC 12699 (Fig. Figf ), better known as the prototypical starburst (Weedman et al. ) NGC 7714 or Arp 284. As expected, our data show extremely intense central star formation in NGC 7714, but interestingly none at all from the small companion NGC 7715 (as was also noted by Gonz alez-Delgado et al. from their H imaging), although there are some H + [ N ii ] knots from the tail apparently joining the two galaxies.

gmw_00001664.name: H2356-309
gmw_00001664.overview: H2356-309 is a high-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae blazar [arXiv:1501.07115, arXiv:2009.02289] hosted by an elliptical galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.165 [arXiv:2009.02289, arXiv:1801.04423]. Very-long-baseline interferometry reveals a parsec-scale radio structure consisting of a 20 mJy core and a southeastern jet [arXiv:1205.2399, arXiv:2310.10206].

sources.summary: The paper describes H 2356-309 as a bright X-ray HBL and likely IceCube counterpart, fitting parameters that enhance photohadronic emission and suppress SSC emission.

quotes.quote: H 2356-309 at z=0.165 falomo91 . This is one of the brightest HBLs in X-rays, which led to its early X-ray detection with UHURU forman78 . Its X-ray emission is variable both in flux and in spectral shape (for more information, see abramowski10 ). TeV ray emission from H 2356-309 was observed for the first time in 2004 by H.E.S.S. aharonian06b .

H 2356-309 H 2356-309 is one of the sources in the sample that has also been detected in TeV energies by H.E.S.S. and is the most probable counterpart for the IceCube neutrino with ID 10. Our model SED and neutrino flux are shown in Fig. h2356-309 . In contrast to PG 1553+113, the Fermi/LAT spectrum can be approximated as F_ ^0 within the error bars and the ray flux at 0.1 TeV is 1 order of magnitude below the peak flux of the low-energy hump. The properties of the observed SED of H 2356-309 allows us, therefore, to search for parameters that favour the emission and suppress the contribution of the SSC component, contrary to the cases of PG 1553+113 and 1ES 1011+496.

sources.summary: The paper focuses on H 2356-309, an elliptical-hosted high-frequency-peaked blazar at z=0.165, using XMM-Newton data to study its X-ray variability and spectral curvature.

quotes.quote: The blazar H 2356-309 is hosted by an elliptical galaxy Schwartz (1989) located at a redshift z = 0.165 ( 0.002) Falomo (1991) . H 2356-309 is classified as a high frequency peaked blazar as the X-ray spectrum of this blazar was characterized by a broken power law with a synchrotron peak lying at 1.8 keV using BeppoSAX observations Costamante (2001),Giommi Colafrancesco (2005) . It was discovered in the VHE regime by the H.E.S.S. Cherenkov telescope during June to December 2004 Aharonian et al. (2006) . At X-ray energies, H 2356-309 was initially detected by the UHURU satellite Forman (1978) and subsequently by the HEAO-I satellite Wood (1984) . It has been observed with many observing facilities, like BeppoSAX Costamante (2001) , NuSTAR Pandey (2018) , and Cosmic Origin Spectrograph (COS), on board the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) Fang (2014) .

In the current study, we present XMM-Newton observations of blazar H 2356-309 held in between June 2005--December 2013. Our aim is to study their flux and spectral variability in the 0.3--10 keV energy range. Additionally, we fit the spectra of H 2356-309 using various models to study their X-ray spectral curvature and to constrain its break energy.

sources.summary: The paper describes H 2356 309 as an HBL detected at VHE energies, reports CANGAROO-III upper limits, and models its GeV-TeV SED with SSC emission.

quotes.quote: H 2356 309 is a high-frequency peaked BL Lac (HBL) at a redshift of z=0.165 falomo_91 , which was discovered in X-rays by the UHURU satellite forman_78 . Some authors classified this object as "extreme HBL" costamante_01, ghisellini_02 based on its synchrotron peak at extremely high frequency. In 2004, the H.E.S.S. \ group discovered this object in the VHE gamma-ray band above 0.2 TeV at the 10 level of significance aharonian_06a, aharonian_06b , as predicted by costamante_02 .

CANGAROO-III upper limits from observations in 2005 are indicated by the closed red circles, and the average Fermi/LAT spectrum obtained by the analysis of the data between 2008 August and 2011 May is plotted by the open red squares. The average H.E.S.S. \ spectrum from 2004 to 2007 abramowski_10 , which includes our observation period, is indicated by the closed purple triangles. Our upper limits have not imposed a strict limit, however the GeV spectrum seems to connect to H.E.S.S. \ TeV flux smoothly. No time variation of either TeV gamma-ray flux abramowski_10 or GeV gamma-ray flux from this object was found during these observational periods.

sources.summary: The paper describes H 2356-309 as a high-frequency-peaked BL Lac in an elliptical host at z=0.165, monitored across X-ray, optical, radio, and VHE bands, with an EBL-affected VHE spectrum.

quotes.quote: H 2356-309 The high frequency peaked BL Lac object H 2356-309 is hosted by an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.165 and was first detected in X-rays by the satellite experiment UHURU and subsequently by the Large Area Sky Survey experiment onboard the HEAO-I satellite . Also in optical band it was observed . In 2004, H 2356-309 was observed simultaneously in X-rays by RXTE, in optical by ROTSE-III, in radio by Nancay decimetric telescope (NRT) and in VHE for about 40 hours (June to December 2004) by HESS telescopes. It was observed that during this period, the X-ray spectrum measured above 2 eV was softer and the flux was 3 times lower than the one measured by BeppoSAX in 1998 in the same energy band but in a comparatively quiescent state. Since 2004, H 2356-309 has been monitored by HESS for several years (from 2005 to 2007) and little flux variability is observed on the time scale of a few years.

The VHE spectrum of H 2356-309 is strongly affected by the EBL and to calculate the intrinsic spectrum, we have used the EBL-F and EBL-I. Observed flux is proportional to the attenuation factor as shown in Eq.( fluxrelat ) and by assuming F_ ,int a constant in both the EBL models, we tried to fit the observed data which are shown in Figs. fig:309eblFran and fig:309eblInoue respectively.

sources.summary: The paper presents H 2356-309 as a possible transitional EHBL whose flat TeV slope and likely few-TeV IC peak sit between HBL-like and hard-TeV subclasses.

quotes.quote: The source H 2356-309 may represent a transitional case because it is located, considering the errors, where the slope in this energy range is on average flat. This could suggest that the IC peak of this source is located in the region around few TeV, and that the source may be starting the descending part of the IC hump. However, to fully understand this feature we would need more precise TeV gamma-ray data. Except for H 2356-309, the objects on the right of the plot and those on the left are largely incompatible in their TeV slopes, and they support the evidence for two distinct populations of EHBLs.

Another important category that could emerge with a more detailed statistics of EHBL sources in the VHE gamma-ray regime could be represented by objects like H 2356-309. Such source, with its flat TeV slope value, is probably peaking at few TeV and might represent a ``transitional'' type of EHBLs with spectral properties between the HBL-like and the hard-TeV EHBLs.

For example, in our sample the source H 2356-309 might represent a ``transitional'' type of EHBL with IC hump peaking at few TeV and intermediate spectral features with respect to HBL-like and hard-TeV EHBLs.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first strong VHE gamma-ray detection of H 2356 309, describing it as an extreme high-frequency-peaked BL Lac at z=0.165 with X-ray and TeV spectra.

quotes.quote: The extreme synchrotron BL Lac object H 2356 309, located at a redshift of z = 0.165 , was observed from June to December 2004 with a total exposure of 40 h live-time with the \ (High Energy Stereoscopic System) array of atmospheric-Cherenkov telescopes (ACTs). Analysis of this data set yields, for the first time, a strong excess of \ rays (10 standard deviations above background ) from H 2356 309, corresponding to an observed integral flux above 200 GeV of I( > 200 GeV) = \ (statistical error only) . The differential energy spectrum of the source between 200 GeV and 1.3 TeV is well-described by a power law with a normalisation (at 1 TeV) of N _0 = \ and a photon index of = . H 2356 309 is one of the most distant BL Lac objects detected at very-high-energy rays so far.

The high frequency peaked BL Lac object (HBL) H 2356 309, identified in the optical by schwartz:1989a , is hosted by an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.165 falomo:1991a . The object was first detected in X-rays by the satellite experiment UHURU forman:1978a and subsequently by the Large Area Sky Survey experiment onboard the HEAO-I satellite wood:1984a . The spectrum of H 2356 309 as observed by BeppoSAX costamante:2001a is not compatible with a single power law model, indicating that the peak of the synchrotron emission lies within the energy range of BeppoSAX .

sources.summary: The paper uses deep Chandra LETG spectra of the blazar H 2356-309 as a background sightline to constrain WHIM absorption in several intervening galaxy large-scale structures.

quotes.quote: We make use of a 500 ks Chandra HRC-S/LETG spectrum of the blazar H 2356-309, combined with a lower S/N (100 ks) pilot LETG spectrum of the same target, to search for the presence of warm-hot absorbing gas associated with two Large--Scale Structures (LSSs) crossed by this sightline, and to constrain its physical state and geometry. Strong (logN _ OVII 10^ 16 cm ^ 2 ) OVII K absorption associated with a third LSS crossed by this line of sight (the Sculptor Wall, SW), at z=0.03 , has already been detected in a previous work. Here we focus on two additional prominent filamentary LSSs along the same line of sight, at z=0.062 (the Pisces-Cetus Supercluster, PCS) and at z=0.128 (the ``Farther Sculptor Wall'', FSW).

The blazar H 2356-309 has been observed twice with the Chandra LETG, as part of two different observational programs. A first 100 ks LETG observation was performed in October 2007 and has been already analyzed in buote09 . A second, deeper LETG observation was carried out over the September--December 2008 period, through ten different pointings with exposures ranging from 15 ks to 100 ks, for a total of 496.4ks.

sources.summary: The paper uses H2356-309/H 2356-309 as the z=0.165 background blazar for Chandra and XMM observations, with its sight-line through the Sculptor Wall enabling confirmation of WHIM O VII absorption.

quotes.quote: In a previous paper we reported a 3 detection of an absorption line from the Warm-Hot Intergalactic Medium (WHIM) using the Chandra and XMM X-ray grating spectra of the blazar H2356-309, the sight-line of which intercepts the Sculptor Wall, a large-scale superstructure of galaxies at z 0.03 . To verify our initial detection, we obtained a deep (500 ks), follow-up exposure of H2356-309 as part of the Cycle-10 Chandra Large Project Program.

In 2007 we performed such a "targeted" study of the WHIM by observing the blazar, H 2356-309 ( z=0.165 ), with Chandra and XMM. The blazar's sight-line passes through multiple large-scale galaxy structures, including the Sculptor Wall superstructure ( z 0.03 ; see Figure 1 of Buote et al. 2009; B09 hereafter). Blazars are preferred for this type of observation, not just because they are X-ray bright, but also because they typically lack intrinsic absorption features that may complicate the search for intervening features.

sources.summary: The paper uses H 2356-309 as the bright background blazar for Chandra HRC-S/LETG spectroscopy, reporting a 44.8 absorption line and describing Chandra and XMM-Newton observations of the source.

quotes.quote: We report a detection of an absorption line at 44.8  in a >500  ks Chandra HRC-S/LETG X-ray grating spectrum of the blazar H 2356-309.

H2356-309 has been observed twice with Chandra with the HRC-S/LETG grating configuration for a total of 600  ks. The first observation was performed in October 2008 (96.49 ks). The second observation (496.4 ks) consists in 10 pointings with exposures ranging from 15 ks to 100 ks and was carried out during the last four months of 2009. H 2356-309 was also observed with XMM- Newton for 130 ks.

sources.summary: The paper observes H 2356-309 as the faintest, southernmost source in its TeV blazar sample, resolving a 20 mJy core and southeastern parsec-scale jet.

quotes.quote: We observed the five TeV blazars 1ES 1101-232, Markarian 180, 1ES 1218+304, PG 1553+113, and H 2356-309 at five epochs each from 2006 to 2009 with the VLBA under observation code BP146, at frequencies from 5 to 22 GHz, for a total of 25 new VLBA images produced for this paper.
All sources were detected with direct fringe fitting at all epochs, with peak flux densities ranging from 14 to 148 mJy beam ^ 1 .

H 2356 309 Observations of the z =0.165 source H 2356 309 were made at 5 and 8 GHz. H 2356 309 is both the faintest and the southernmost of the five blazars observed in this series, making the imaging more difficult. The parsec-scale structure of this source consists of a 20 mJy core and a jet extending several mas to the southeast that is modeled by two components.

sources.summary: The paper models H 2356-309 with HESS and Fermi data, finding many source models compatible with zero IGMF and secondary-dominated low-energy HE bins.

quotes.quote: Observations of H 2356-309 were obtained over the period of June - September 2004, for a total exposure of 40 hours. The time-averaged dFED was provided for eight bins over the energy range from 200 GeV - 1.23 TeV ( ). The flux in the first energy bin is weakly detected for most simulated models (9 < TS < 25), but the second and third bins exhibit a strong detection (TS > 25).

As illustrated in Figure H2356_B0 , this source has a very large set of models compatible with the H0 . Most of these models, however, suggest that the flux in the two lowest energy bins of the HE regime is dominated by secondary radiation.

sources.summary: The paper treats H 2356-309 as one of two H.E.S.S. blazars whose unexpectedly hard TeV spectra imply a low optical/near-infrared EBL close to resolved galaxy-count limits.

quotes.quote: The recent H.E.S.S. results on the blazars 1ES 1101-232 and H 2356-309 represent a breakthrough on this issue. Their unexpectedly hard spectra allow an upper limit to be derived on the EBL in the optical/near-infrared range, which is very close to the lower limit given by the resolved galaxy counts. This result seems to exclude a large contribution to the EBL from other sources (e.g. Population III stars) and indicates that the intergalactic space is more transparent to rays than previously thought.

Showing an unexpected hardness for their redshift, the combined spectra of the HBLs 1ES 1101-232 and H 2356-309 (now supported by two further TeV sources) provide strong circumstantial evidence for a low density of the EBL. With minimal assumptions on the blazar properties, the spectra allow the derivation of an upper limit on the EBL in the Opt-NIR range which is very close to the lower limit given by the integrated light of resolved galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats H 2356-309 as a blazar behind the Sculptor Wall; its sightline crosses z=0.028-0.032 structure, and low-state spectra revealed candidate WHIM O VII absorption.

quotes.quote: We present \ RGS and \ LETG observations of the blazar, H 2356-309, located behind the Sculptor Wall, a large-scale galaxy structure expected to harbor high-density Warm-Hot Intergalactic Medium (WHIM).

The part of the Sculptor Wall intercepted by the sight-line of H 2356-309 represents a redshift range of 0.028-0.032. Although the observations were designed as Targets-of-Opportunity to catch H 2356-309 in outburst, the apparent outbursts were not long enough to enable the follow-up (10-40 hours after triggering) to catch the blazar in a high state.

Despite H 2356-309 being observed in its low state ( 10^ 11 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 0.5-2.0 keV flux), we detected a candidate WHIM O 7 resonance line in the Sculptor Wall from a simultaneous fit of the \ and \ data.

sources.summary: The paper includes H 2356-309 as an HBL detected by HESS, with simultaneous optical/X-ray data, comparable 2004 and 2005 fluxes, and low X-ray state.

quotes.quote: H 2356-309. A HBL object was detected with VHE emission by HESS from June to December 2004, with a integral flux above 200 GeV of 4.1 0.5 10^ 12 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 (Aharonian et al. 2006b). A simultaneous observation with HESS in 2004 at lower energy bands was performed by ROTSE-III (optical) and RXTE (X-rays). A broadband SED obtained simultaneously by XMM-Newton and HESS on June 2005 (HESS Collaboration et al. 2010b) is presented in this work, in which the flux levels in X-ray and TeV bands are comparable with that measured in 2004. In the VHE band, significant small-amplitude variations on time scales of months and years were detected.

sources.summary: The paper identifies H 2356-309 as one of two HESS BL Lac objects whose hard TeV spectra drove low-EBL constraints and challenged high-density EBL interpretations.

quotes.quote: In 2005, however, the HESS observations of the BL Lac objects 1ES 1101-232 ( z =0.186) and H 2356-309 ( z =0.165) provided a fundamental breakthrough ( ). The observed ray spectra (detected between 0.2 and 1-3 TeV) were much harder than expected for their redshift, implying extremely hard intrinsic spectra ( _ int 0 ) in case of high EBL densities (Fig. fig2 ). Such hard spectra were never seen in the closer, less absorbed objects and were at odds with all the known blazar physics and phenomenology. They were also not supported by same-epoch multiwavelength observations of their SED ( ).

sources.summary: The review discusses H 2356-309 as a hard TeV blazar detected by H.E.S.S., using its spectrum and filament-crossing sightline to assess EBL and cascade interpretations.

quotes.quote: The breakthrough was achieved in 2005 , with the H.E.S.S. detections of 1ES 1101-232 ( z =0.186) and H 2356-309 ( z =0.165). The observed ray spectra (measured between 0.2 and 1-3 TeV) were much harder than expected for their redshift, implying extremely hard intrinsic spectra ( _ int 0 ) in case of high EBL densities (Fig. nature ). To accomodate _ int 1.5 , the upper limit on the EBL becomes (14 4) \ around 1--2 , which is very close to the lower limits given by the integrated light of galaxies , and even closer to the most recent NIR estimates .

However, day-scale variability is seen also in hard sources (e.g. 1ES 1218+304 ) and the line of sight of another hard blazar (H 2356-309 , =3.1 0.2 , z=0.165) is known to intercept at least 3 LSS filaments at z=0.03 (Sculptur Wall), z=0.062 (Pisces-Cetus supercluster) and z=0.128 (Farther Sculptur Wall).

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes Chandra and XMM spectra of blazar H 2356-309, arguing the reported z~0.03 WHIM absorption is not required and any OVII contribution is much smaller than earlier claims.

quotes.quote: In this Letter we demonstrate that the two claims of z 0.03 OVII K absorption lines from Warm Hot Intergalactic Medium (WHIM) along the lines of sight to the blazars H 2356-309 (Buote et al., 2009; Fang et al., 2010) and Mkn 501 (Ren, Fang Buote, 2014) are likely misidentifications of the z=0 OII K line produced by a diffuse Low-Ionization Metal Medium in the Galaxy's Interstellar and Circum-Galactic mediums. We perform detailed modeling of all the available high signal-to-noise Chandra LETG and XMM-Newton RGS spectra of H 2356-309 and Mkn 501 and demonstrate that the z 0.03 WHIM absorption along these two sightlines is statistically not required.

Our results, however, do not rule out a small contribution from the z 0.03 OVII K absorber along the line of sight to H 2356-309. In our model the temperature of the putative z = 0.031 WHIM filament is T = 3 10^5 K and the OVII column density is N _ OV II 4 10^ 15 cm ^ 2 , twenty times smaller than the OVII column density previously reported, and now more consistent with the expectations from cosmological hydrodynamical simulations.

sources.summary: The paper reports H 2356--309 has a convex, broken-power-law X-ray spectrum, a synchrotron peak near 1.8 keV, Galactic column density, and PDS detection to 50 keV.

quotes.quote: H 2356--309 The spectrum of this source is not compatible with a single power--law model: there is clear evidence for a convex spectral shape, that is best fitted by a broken power--law with high significance ( >99.9 , see Table 3). This locates the synchrotron peak around 1.8 keV. The found column density is around the galactic values. This source has been detected also in the PDS instrument up to 50 keV (see Fig. 12) and the data lie on the extrapolation of the MECS power--law.

sources.summary: The paper highlights blazar H2356-309 as an HBL whose position coincides with three IceCube neutrino events, with associated synchrotron energies and neutrino flux tabulated.

quotes.quote: We found that the blazar H2356-309 is within the error circles of three IceCube events.
The HBL, H2356-309 is within the error circles of three IceCube events 7, 10 and 21 and their corresponding synchrotron energies, n'_ , f and neutrino flux are shown in Table 1.
The position of the HBL, H2356-309 coincides with three IceCube events.

sources.summary: RBS 2070, also identified as H 2356-309, is analyzed with multi-epoch XMM-Newton data; its spectra constrain curvature and break energy and its count rates show bimodal behavior.

quotes.quote: RBS 2070 (H 2356-309) conducted a thorough analysis of RBS 2070 using the observations made by RXTE Satellite, HESS, ROTSE-IIIc, Nancay radio telescope, XMM-Newton and ATOM telescope.
Additionally, they fitted the spectra of RBS 2070 using various models to study the source's X-ray spectral curvature and to constrain its break energy.
RBS 2070 (H 2356-309) We looked into nine XMM-Newton observations scattered on a timescale between June 2005 and December 2013.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2356 309 as an extreme synchrotron-peaked blazar whose VLBI core-jet structure is reproduced by the new TANAMI images.

quotes.quote: 2356 309: The source has been reported as an extreme synchrotron peaked blazar since its low-energy SED component peaks in the X-rays costamante01 . VLBI observations of tiet12 at 5 and 8 GHz show a core and a jet extending a few mas from the core. Our new TANAMI images reveal a similar structure.

gmw_00001665.name: 1ES1011+496
gmw_00001665.overview: 1ES1011+496 is a high-frequency peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1602.05239, arXiv:1603.06776] located at a redshift of z = 0.212 [arXiv:1410.5380, arXiv:1603.07308]. The active galactic nucleus features a radio jet extending to the west [arXiv:0711.1158] and exhibits variability across radio, optical, X-ray, and very-high-energy gamma-ray bands [arXiv:1110.4747, arXiv:1603.06776].

sources.summary: The paper models 1 ES 1011+496 as a BL Lac blazar whose low-state SED favors high magnetic fields, hybrid proton-electron emission, and delayed VHE variability.

quotes.quote: Despite prominent absorption lines used to pin down the redshift to z = 0.212 the blazar 1 ES 1011+496 was originally classified as a high frequency peaked BL Lac object albert01 . The VHE emission of 1 ES 1011+496 was discovered with the MAGIC Air-Cherenkov telescope in 2007, and it was the most distant blazar observed in rays while down to the present day this is 3C 279. However, the true nature of this blazar seems to be quite unclear compared to well studied objects like Mkn 501, PKS 1218+304 or 3C 279 veritas, weidinger02, boettcher02 . The first and only multiwavelength observation, including MAGIC in the VHEs, Swift in X-rays and KVA in the optical band was in 2008 reinthal01 .

1 ES 1011+496 was used as an example blazar with its low state emission already pointing out a relatively high magnetic field to be present within its jet, see Chapter 3. The steady state observed in the MWL campaign including the MAGIC telescopes and the Swift satellite is reliably described with our hybrid model, while a consistent leptonic approach fails to model the synchrotron peak of 1 ES 1011+496 (see Table 1 for the parameters used). One can of course assume various breaks with corresponding spectral indices in the electron distribution to describe the X-ray peak leptonically, but in general a physical motivation for these is rather hard to find. Hence, self-consistently speaking, the VHE peak is not due to inverse Compton photons, but consists of proton synchrotron radiation and emerging cascade radiation.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes a February-March 2014 VHE flare of the BL Lac 1ES 1011+496, deriving night-wise spectra and using the average spectrum to constrain EBL attenuation.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1011+496 (RA: 10^ h 15^ m 04.1^ s , DEC: +49^ 26^ m 01^ s ) is an active galactic nucleus (AGN) classified as a high-frequency peaked BL Lac (HBL), located at redshift =0.212 MAGICdiscovery2007 . HBLs have spectral energy distributions (SED) characterized by two peaks, one located in the UV to soft X-ray band and the second located in the GeV to TeV range, which makes it possible to detect them in very-high-energy (VHE, E > 100GeV) rays. 1ES 1011+496 was discovered at VHE by the MAGIC Collaboration in 2007 following an optical high state reported by the Tuorla Blazar Monitoring Programme MAGICdiscovery2007 .

After background suppression cuts, 6132 gamma-like excess events above an energy of 60 GeV were detected within 0.14^ of the direction of 1ES 1011+496. Three control regions with the same ray acceptance as the ON-source region were used to estimate the residual background recorded together with the signal. The source was detected with a significance of 75 , calculated according to eq. 17 LiMa .

We have reported the observation of the extraordinary outburst from 1ES 1011+496 observed by MAGIC from February 6th to March 7th 2014 where the flux reached a level 14 times the observed flux at the time of the discovery of the source in 2007. The spectrum of 1ES 1011+496 during this flare displays little intrinsic curvature over >1 order of magnitude in energy, which makes this an ideal observation for constraining the EBL.

sources.summary: The paper characterizes 1ES 1011+496 as a z = 0.212 blazar observed from radio to TeV energies, generally quiescent, moderately variable, polarized, and consistent with one-zone SSC modeling.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1011+496 ( z = 0.212 ) was discovered in very high-energy (VHE, E >100 GeV) rays with MAGIC in 2007. The absence of simultaneous data at lower energies led to an incomplete characterization of the broadband spectral energy distribution (SED). We study the source properties and the emission mechanisms, probing whether a simple one-zone synchrotron self-Compton (SSC) scenario is able to explain the observed broadband spectrum.

The source 1ES 1011+496 was found to be in a generally quiescent state at all observed wavelengths, showing only moderate variability from radio to X-rays. A low degree of polarization of less than 10 was measured in optical, while some bright features polarized up to 60 were observed in the radio jet. A similar trend in the rotation of the electric vector position angle was found in optical and radio. The radio maps indicated a superluminal motion of 1.8 0.4 , which is the highest speed statistically significant measured so far in a high-frequency-peaked BL Lac. For the first time, the high-energy bump in the broadband SED of 1ES 1011+496 could be fully characterized from 0.1 GeV to 1 TeV, which permitted a more reliable interpretation within the one-zone SSC scenario.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first multiwavelength campaign with VHE coverage for blazar 1ES 1011 + 496, confirming VHE emission and finding it in a relatively high state.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1011 + 496 is a BL Lac object, first detected as an X-ray source elvis1992 , located at a medium redshift of z= 0.212 albert2007 . It was discovered at VHE by MAGIC in 2007 following an optical high state reported by the Tuorla Blazar Monitoring Program http://users.utu.fi/kani/1m http://users.utu.fi/kani/1m albert2007 . At the time of the discovery, 1ES 1011 + 496 was the most distant source known to emit VHE rays. Previous observations of this source at VHE showed only a hint of a signal (see e.g. albert2008a ) and the results presented here constitute the first follow-up observation of 1ES 1011 + 496.

In this paper we report the first MWL campaign including VHE coverage on the blazar 1ES 1011 + 496. The campaign was performed regardless of the state of the source. Compared to archival data the source appeared to be in a rather high state during these observations. The MAGIC observations presented in this paper confirm the source as a VHE ray emitter.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 1011+496 as a MAGIC-discovered VHE blazar, reporting optical-flare detection, weak 2006 evidence, a higher 2007 VHE flux, and HBL classification.

quotes.quote: In 11 cases the source was detected by MAGIC. These 11 flares were detected in 9 sources: Mrk 421, 1ES 1959+650, PG 1553+113, 3C 66A, 3C 279, BL Lac, Mrk 180, 1ES 1011+496, S5 0716+714 (see Fig. 2).
For 1ES 1011+496 the observation from 2006 showed only weak evidence for a VHE ray signal at 3.5 . The VHE ray flux was >40 higher in March-May 2007 than in March-April 2006.

The optically triggered Target of Opportunity observations have resulted in the discovery of three new VHE ray emitting blazars. Mrk 180 and 1ES 1011+496 are classified to be HBLs, while S5 0716+714 is classified as IBL or even LBL. However, the BL Lac population is continuous rather than bimodal or trimodal (see, e.g. ), and in all of the three discovered sources the synchrotron peak frequency is not too far from the optical band (see e.g. publicly available data at asdc.asi.it/blazars and Fig. 6. in ).

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 1011+496 as an intermediate-redshift HBL with optical and TeV variability, using a hybrid SED where SSC emission dominates and neutrino flux is lower than gamma rays.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1011+496 at z=0.212 albert07 . This is an HBL source at indermediate redshift, that has been detected for the first time in TeV rays in 2007 with MAGIC albert07 . The discovery was the result of a follow up observation of the optical high state reported by the Tuorla blazar monitoring program http://users.utu.fi/kani/1m . The previous non-detections in TeV rays suggest that 1ES 1011+496 is variable both in optical and VHE rays.

1ES 1011+496 The hybrid SED is shown in Fig. 1es1011 and the resemblance to the model SED of PG 1553+113 is obvious. The ray emission is mainly attributed to the SSC emission of primary electrons, since the flux of the component is only a small fraction of the observed ray one. The fact that the model neutrino peak flux is lower than the peak ray flux should come as no surprise after the discussion in Sect. pg1553 .

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 1011+496 as an HBL at z=0.212 whose VHE emission and highest-energy SED points require a UHECR component beyond pure leptonic emission.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1011+496 : The optical data, X-ray data and radio-to-X-ray flux ratio show typical properties of an HBL Padovani_95, Ahnen_16 . It is situated at a redshift z=0.212 and the VHE ray emission was first discovered by MAGIC observations triggered by an optical outburst in March 2007 Albert_07 . The source has been well observed in 0.1 300 GeV band by Fermi-LAT Fermi_3FGL and 0.3-10 keV band by Swift-XRT Abdo_10 . The source is also listed in the second catalog of hard Femi-LAT sources.

We find, the pure-leptonic model is unable to cover the highest energy data points due to EBL attenuation. Increasing E_ e,cut worsens the fit for synchrotron spectrum. In the lepto-hadronic fit, photon spectrum from UHECR interactions can indeed explain the highest energy data points.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 1011+496 as a high-frequency BL Lac modeled with MAGIC observations, Fermi-LAT variability, redshift-dependent optical depths, and a one-zone SSC emission region.

quotes.quote: The high-frequency peaked BL Lac object 1ES 1011+496 has been observed by both VERITAS and MAGIC . It has been observed by VERITAS for 10.4 hours and by MAGIC for 19.4 hours (after quality cuts). We will use the observations from MAGIC since they extend over the largest period of time. The flux observed by MAGIC extends up to 0.75 TeV, or an optical depth of 1.6 using a redshift of z = 0.212 . The HAWC observation at 7 TeV is at an optical depth of 5.2 .

The MAGIC collaboration has also performed an analysis of the multi-wavelength spectrum of 1ES 1011+496 and used this to model the SED of the source . They have found that the SED can be described by a one-zone SSC model with the following parameters: B = 0.048 G, = 26 , R = 3.25 10^ 16 cm.

sources.summary: The paper studies 1ES 1011+496 across multiple epochs, finding lognormal gamma-ray variability and SED fits requiring smooth curvature in the underlying electron distribution.

quotes.quote: A detailed multi-epoch study of the broadband spectral behaviour of the very high energy (VHE) source, 1ES 1011+496, provides us with valuable information regarding the underlying particle distribution. Simultaneous observations of the source at optical/ UV/ X-ray/ ray during three different epochs, as obtained from Swift-UVOT/ Swift-XRT/ Fermi-LAT, are supplemented with the information available from the VHE telescope array, HAGAR. The longterm flux variability at the Fermi-LAT energies is clearly found to be lognormal. It is seen that the broadband spectral energy distribution (SED) of 1ES 1011+496 can be successfully reproduced by synchrotron and synchrotron self Compton emission models.

Notably, the observed curvature in the photon spectrum at X-ray energies demands a smooth transition of the underlying particle distribution from a simple power law to a power law with an exponential cutoff or a smooth broken power law distribution, which may possibly arise when the escape of the particles from the main emission region is energy dependent.
