While absolute quenching timescales are difficult to constrain due to uncertainties in the initial conditions for our semi-analytic model, it is clear from our results that starvation is the primary quenching mechanism for Antlia and Antlia B under our fiducial model choices.

sources.summary: The paper reports deep B-band evidence for tidal tails in the Antlia Dwarf galaxy, aligned toward NGC 3109, and argues that repeated interactions may be altering Antlia's morphology and evolution.

quotes.quote: Using deep B band imaging down to _ B = 26 mag arcsec ^ 2 , we present evidence for tidal tails in the Antlia Dwarf galaxy, one of the most distant members of the Local Group. This elongation is in the direction of Antlia's nearest neighbor, the Magellanic-type NGC 3109. The tail is offset by <10^ from a vector linking the centers of the two galaxies, indicative of interactions between the pair.

Combined with the warped disc previously identified in NGC 3109, Antlia and NGC 3109 must be at a small separation for tidal features to be present in Antlia. We calculate that Antlia cannot be completely disrupted by NGC 3109 in a single interaction unless its orbit pericenter is <6 kpc, however multiple interactions could significantly alter its morphology. Therefore despite being located right at the edge of the Local Group, environmental effects are playing an important role in Antlia's evolution.

sources.summary: The paper reports an H I detection of the Antlia dwarf galaxy, measures its neutral gas mass and velocity width, and argues its gas velocity matches NGC 3109's warp, suggesting a past mild encounter.

quotes.quote: We report a positive detection in \ of the nearby Antlia dwarf galaxy, and measure its total neutral gas mass to be (6.8 1.4) 10^5 . We show the warp in 3109 to lie at exactly the same radial velocity as the gas in the Antlia dwarf galaxy and speculate that Antlia disturbed the disk of 3109 during a mild encounter 1 in the past.

The velocity width is (30 2) . We note that the detection by is almost certainly real, contrary to the suggestion of that it is an instrumental artifact. Summing the flux in the \ profile of Antlia yields a total flux of (1.7 0.1) . Under the usual assumptions, and adopting a distance of (1.3 0.1) to Antlia, this yields an \ mass of (6.8 1.4) 10^5 .

sources.summary: Antlia is listed as an original known member of the NGC 3109 dwarf-galaxy association, part of an unusually tight, elongated Local Group alignment with a velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: We re-consider the association of dwarf galaxies around NGC 3109, whose known members were NGC 3109, Antlia, Sextans A, and Sextans B, based on a new updated list of nearby galaxies and the most recent data. We find that the original members of the NGC 3109 association, together with the recently discovered and adjacent dwarf irregular Leo P, form a very tight and elongated configuration in space. All these galaxies lie within 100 kpc of a line that is 1070 kpc long, from one extreme (NGC 3109) to the other (Leo P), and they show a gradient in the Local Group standard of rest velocity with a total amplitude of 43 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , and a .m.s. scatter of just 16.8 km s ^ 1 . It is shown that the reported configuration is exceptional given the known dwarf galaxies in the Local Group and its surroundings. We conclude that (a) Leo P is very likely an additional member of the NGC 3109 association, and (b) the association is highly ordered in space and velocity, and it is very elongated, suggesting that it was created by a tidal interaction or it was accreted as a filamentary substructure.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Antlia as a likely Milky Way escapee among Local Group field dwarfs and notes old halo structure with a young core for this candidate set.

quotes.quote: Comparison of the radius-velocity distributions of VLII populations and measurements of Local Group galaxies presents a strong likelihood that Tucana, Cetus, NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, Antlia, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT, and NGC185 have passed through the Milky Way.

From Table t4 we expect that the following Local Group Objects have with high likelihood ( > 50 per cent) at some point in time passed through the virial radius of the Milky Way: NGC3109, SextansA, SextansB, Antlia, Cetus, Tucana, NGC6822, Phoenix, LeoT, and NGC185.
Antlia, NGC6822, Leo T, NGC 185 and Phoenix have all have extended old haloes, no population of intermediate-age stars, and a dynamically cold, young core Hwang11,McQuinn10 .

gmw_00001680.name: AP Librae
gmw_00001680.overview: AP Librae is a low-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae blazar [arXiv:1602.03430, arXiv:1611.02103] located at a redshift of approximately 0.049 [arXiv:1111.2775, arXiv:1410.5897]. It is optically active [arXiv:1111.2775, arXiv:1309.6893] and hosts an extended, non-thermal X-ray and radio jet [arXiv:1111.2775, arXiv:1309.6893] that exhibits a complex, double-sided structure [arXiv:2310.10206].

sources.summary: The paper models Ap Lib as a rare TeV-detected low-synchrotron-peaked blazar whose SED needs parsec-scale jet, blob-jet interaction, accretion-disk, and external photon-field components.

quotes.quote: Ap Lib is one of the rare low-synchrotron-peaked blazars detected so far at TeV energies. This type of source is not properly modelled by standard one-zone leptonic synchrotron self-Compton (SSC) emission scenarios. The aim of this paper is to study the relevance of additional components that should naturally occur in an SSC scenario for a better understanding of the emission mechanisms, especially at very high energies (VHE).

The multi-lambda emission of Ap Lib is modelled by a blob-in-jet SSC scenario including the contribution of the base of the VLBI-extended jet, the radiative blob-jet interaction, the accretion disk, and its associated external photon field. We show that signatures of a strong parsec-scale jet and of an accretion disk emission are present in the spectral energy distribution. We can link the observational VLBI jet features from MOJAVE to parameters expected for a VHE-emitting blob accelerated near the jet base.

In this scenario, Ap Lib appears as an intermediate source between BL Lac objects and flat-spectrum radio quasars. Ap Lib could be a bright representative of a specific class of blazars, in which the parsec-scale jet luminosity is no more negligible compared to the blob and contributes to the high-energy emission through inverse-Compton processes.

sources.summary: The paper describes AP Librae as an LBL blazar with VHE emission and an extended radio/X-ray jet, arguing extended-jet emission may explain much of its modeled SED.

quotes.quote: The low-frequency peaked BL Lac object (LBL) AP Librae exhibits very-high-energy (VHE, E>100 GeV) ray emission and hosts an extended jet, which has been detected in radio and X-rays.

The X-ray morphology of AP Librae's jet exactly follows the radio morphology as observed with the VLA. This suggests a common origin of the radio and X-ray radiation. The detection of the extended X-ray jet further demonstrates AP Librae's peculiar state.

Instead, it is demonstrated that emission from the extended jet may be responsible for many parts of the SED. The synchrotron component is due to a superposition of the one-zone blob, and the jet on pc-scales. This model is supported by VLBI observations of the MOJAVE program, which resolves the jet on these scales. For the VHE emission we have invoked the on arcsec scales.

sources.summary: The paper models AP Librae as a low-frequency peaked BL Lac whose extended jet may produce X-ray to very-high-energy gamma-ray emission by proton synchrotron radiation, but with extreme energetics.

quotes.quote: AP Librae a low frequency peaked BL Lac, being at a redshift of 0.0486 ( ), has been observed in radio to TeV gamma rays by several detectors. In comparison with other extragalactic sources which have been detected in X--rays(from their extended jets) [ e.g., 3C 279, PKS 0637-752 ] AP Librae has the distinguishing feature of being also detected in VHE -rays. As a consequence of this uniqueness in the SED of AP Librae the approach to modelling it presents some very interesting opportunities of investigation especially in view of the fact that the VHE region of the spectra cannot be specifically attributed to a particular region inside the source (central core/ parsec scale jet/ extended jet).

This paper discusses the possibility of applying the proton synchrotron model to the extended jet of AP Librae to explain the HE and VHE ray data. Our results indicate that in order to successfully explain the VHE emission from the extended jet of AP Librae via proton synchrotron radiation the following conditions are essential :-- i) the ambient magnetic field in the extended jet must be 1 mG. ii) the existence of extremely high energy protons >10^ 21 eV in the extended jet. Although the above conditions are consistent with the Hillas criterion it is evident that the value of the ambient magnetic field required in the proton synchrotron model to explain the extended jet emission is at least an order of magnitude higher than that required in the IC/CMB model. We note that it would be possible to lower the magnetic field if we increase the value of from the present value of 3. However, the jet power of the extended jet remains much higher than the Eddington's luminosity of AP Librae.

sources.summary: The paper describes AP Librae as an optically active low-energy peaked BL Lac blazar at z=0.049 with TeV emission and a clearly detected non-thermal X-ray jet.

quotes.quote: The low-energy peaked BL Lac object AP Librae is well known as one of the most active blazars in optical. It has a redshift of z=0.049 and is located at _ J2000 = 15^ h 17^ m 41.8^ s , _ J2000 = -24^ 22' 19'' . It has been classified as a BL Lac object by and .

Extended X-ray jet AP Lib is the first LBL with detected TeV ray emission and a clearly visible extended non-thermal X-ray jet. We detected the X-ray jet in our own analysis of the 14ks Chandra observation of 4. July 2003 (see Fig. 1). The jet is located in the south-east direction of the source. The jet is dominated by non-thermal emission.

sources.summary: The paper reports Chandra discovery of AP Librae's extended non-thermal X-ray jet, linking its morphology to radio emission and finding inverse-Compton-dominated core and jet spectra.

quotes.quote: Chandra observations of the low-energy peaked BL Lac object AP Librae revealed the clear discovery of a non-thermal X-ray jet. AP Lib is the first low energy peaked BL Lac object with an extended non-thermal X-ray jet that shows emission into the VHE range. The X-ray jet has an extension of 15'' ( 14 kpc ) . The X-ray jet morphology is similar to the radio jet observed with VLA at 1.36 GHz emerging in south-east direction and bends by 50^ at a distance of 12'' towards north-east.

The low-energy peaked BL Lac object AP Librae (AP Lib, PKS 1514-241) has a redshift of z=0.0486 Disney1974 and is located at _ J2000 = 15^ h 17^ m 41.81313^ s 0.00002^ s , _ J2000 = -24^ 22' 19.4759'' 0.0003'' as determined from VLBI observations by . It has been classified as a BL Lac object by and . AP Lib is well known as one of the most active blazars in the optical band.

sources.summary: The paper treats AP Librae as a TeV-detected low-frequency-peaked BL Lac whose spectrum requires an extended jet inverse-Compton component beyond a compact one-zone model.

quotes.quote: Here we present a case where additionally to the small region a > kpc-scale jet is required to successfully reproduce the spectrum and especially the TeV emission, namely the low-frequeny peaked BL Lac object AP Librae detected in the TeV domain by the H.E.S.S. experiment. Given that other parts of the spectral energy distribution follow the characteristics implied by the source classification, the inverse Compton component spans 10 orders of magnitude, which cannot be reproduced by the one-zone model. Additionally, observational constraints in both the synchrotron and inverse Compton compoenent strongly constrain the parameters of a self-consistent model ruling out the possibility of TeV photon production in the vicinity of the galactic center. We discuss the possibility that the TeV radiation is emitted by highly energetic particles in the extended, arcsec-scale jet, which has been detected at radio and X-ray energies.

The blazar AP Librae, located at a redshift z=0.0486 and at R.A. _ J2000 = 15^h 17^m 41.8^s , DEC. _ J2000 = -24^ 22^ 19.5^ , exhibits a monotonically increasing X-ray energy spectrum. This in combination with the lack of optical emission lines has led to its classification as an LBL. Interestingly, some observational features of this source do not fit into this category.

sources.summary: The paper studies AP Librae as a low-frequency-peaked BL Lac detected from HE to TeV gamma rays, with a measured redshift, multiwavelength associations, and extended X-ray jet.

quotes.quote: The ray spectrum of the low-frequency-peaked BL Lac (LBL) object AP Librae is studied, following the discovery of very-high-energy (VHE; E>100 GeV ) ray emission up to the TeV range by the \ experiment. This makes AP Librae one of the few VHE emitters of the LBL type.

AP Librae falls into the category of ``low-frequency-peaked BL Lac'' (LBL), defined by an X-ray to radio flux ratio of f_x/f_r<10^ 11 1995ApJ...444..567P , and of the more recently introduced ``low frequency synchrotron peaked'' (LSP) class of blazars defined by a synchrotron emission peak in the spectral energy distribution (SED) at _ s, peak 10^ 14 Hz see 2010ApJ...716...30A . AP Librae was amongst the first few objects to be classified as a member of the BL Lac class 1972ApJ...175L...7S , for which a reliable redshift could be measured ( z=0.049 0.002 ; 1974ApJ...194L..79D ).

Following this announcement, 2010tsra.confE.199F showed the first radio-to-TeV SED, based on the preliminary analysis of an HE-VHE data set included in the larger one presented here, while 2011ICRC....8..199K also pointed out the existence of an X-ray jet resolved with Chandra, making Ap Librae the only known TeV BL Lac object with an extended jet in X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper models Ap Librae as a TeV-detected low-frequency peaked blazar with an X-ray jet and a broad high-energy component explained by photohadronic emission.

quotes.quote: Ap Librae is one out of a handful of low-frequency peaked blazars to be detected at TeV rays and the only one with an identified X-ray jet. Combined observations of at high energies (HE) and of at very high energies (VHE) revealed a striking spectral property of ; the presence of a broad high-energy component that extends more than nine orders of magnitude in energy and is, therefore, hard to be explained by the usual single-zone synchrotron self-Compton model. We show that the superposition of different emission components related to photohadronic interactions can explain the ray emission of without invoking external radiation fields. We present two indicative model fits to the spectral energy distribution of where the VHE emission is assumed to originate from a compact, sub-pc scale region of the jet. A robust prediction of our model is VHE flux variability on timescales similar to those observed at X-rays and HE rays, which can be further used to distinguish between a sub-pc or kpc scale origin of the TeV emission.

sources.summary: The paper describes AP Librae as MOJAVE-monitored with a complex 30 mas jet; TANAMI recovers a double-sided S-band jet and high apparent jet speed.

quotes.quote: 1514 241: AP Librae has been part of MOJAVE between 1997 and 2012 . 15 GHz observations show a 30 mas extended jet with a complex structure. In S band we recover a double-sided jet. The maximum jet speed from MOJAVE observations is 6.21 0.26 lister19 . If we assume Doppler factors above 10 , this suggests the jet viewing angle to fall below 5 2013AJ....146..120L .

sources.summary: AP Librae is presented as the nearest new VHE blazar, a low-frequency-peaked BL Lac with broad high-energy emission and possible external-Compton VHE origin.

quotes.quote: AP Librae ( ) is the closest of the new blazars presented here, at a redshift of 0.049 , and was targeted for \ observations thanks to the \ bright source catalogue. This AGN has been discovered by \ after 11 hours of livetime with a significance of 7 and announced in . The SED (see , at this conference) of this source shows that \ is a low-frequency peaked BL Lac (LBL) and presents an extremely broad high-energy component which extends from X-ray to VHE, showing that, for this source, synchrotron X-ray brightness is not required to produce VHE \ and that an external Compton component may be responsible for detected VHE emission. \ is the third LBL detected at VHE and the first one discovered in the southern hemisphere.

sources.summary: The review describes AP Lib as a low-frequency-peaked BL Lac detected at VHE, with a hard LAT spectrum and unusually broad high-energy SED component.

quotes.quote: AP Lib, a low-frequency-peaked BL Lac object, was discovered as a VHE ray emitter with at 7 CL in 11 h . From data analysis, it was found that this object also exhibits a hard ray spectrum at high energy with =2.1 0.1 in the LAT energy range. Together with contemporaneous Swift and RXTE data, the spectral energy distribution of AP Lib displays an atypical, unusually broad component at high energy, with respect to other VHE emitters (see Fig. fig:aplib ). Such a component seriously challenges simple SSC scenarios, and multiple zone models , external inverse Compton or hadronic processes may have to be invoked to interpret its multi-wavelength emission.

sources.summary: The paper uses Ap Librae as an LSP TeV BL Lac with a kpc-scale X-ray jet and broad high-energy hump to simulate HEX-P sensitivity to leptonic and hadronic components.

quotes.quote: To illustrate the capabilities of HEX-P in identifying spectral breaks due to the transition from leptonic to hadronic emission components, we choose Ap Librae, an intriguing LSP at redshift z 0.049 Disney_1974 that has been been detected at VHE 2015A&A...573A..31H . It is the only TeV BL Lac object with a detected kpc-scale X-ray jet kaufmann+2013 . VHE data in combination with the X-ray and high-energy (HE, 0.1-300 GeV) ray observations with Fermi-LAT, revealed an unexpected broad high-energy hump, spanning more than nine orders of magnitude in energy (from X-rays to TeV rays). demonstrated that the superposition of different emission components related to photohadronic interactions in the blazar core can explain the broad high-energy component of Ap Librae without invoking external radiation fields or particle acceleration at kpc scales for large-scale jet scenarios, see Section sec:jet_resolve and, e.g., ZachariasWagner16, Roychowdhury+22 . We adopt Model A from (see left panel in Figure fig:aplib ) to simulate spectra for HEX-P, assuming a 50 ks exposure.

sources.summary: AP Librae showed significant positive color-magnitude correlation in the full dataset and yearly intervals, with modest flux variations and optical spectral indices around 2 to 3.

quotes.quote: AP Librae: The source was observed during 112 nights in 2010--2012. Significant positive CM correlation is detected in the entire dataset and also in shorter (one-year-long) intervals. Modest, 1 mag flux variations corresponded to the _ BR 2-3 range covered.

gmw_00001681.name: HSC J124353.93 + 010038.5
gmw_00001681.overview: HSC J124353.93 + 010038.5 is a low-luminosity quasar at a redshift of 7.07 [arXiv:2104.05738]. The host galaxy exhibits rotation-dominated gas dynamics [arXiv:2104.05738], star-forming activity [arXiv:2104.05738], and a fast, quasar-driven outflow [arXiv:2104.05738].

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA observations of J1243 + 0100, finding a star-forming z = 7.07 quasar host with quasar-driven [C II] outflow and rotation-dominated gas dynamics.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA [ C 2 ] 158 line and underlying far-infrared (FIR) continuum emission observations ( 0 .70 0 .56 resolution) toward HSC J124353.93 + 010038.5 (J1243 + 0100) at z = 7.07 , the only low-luminosity ( M_ 1450 > -25 mag) quasar currently known at z > 7 .
The narrow core component is likely due to the host galaxy of J1243 + 0100. This implies that the ISM physical conditions are not very different between J1243 + 0100 and the local starbursts.

Thus we conclude that the fast [ C 2 ] outflow of J1243 + 0100 is quasar-driven . The radial velocity is 3--5 times larger than the velocity dispersion in all bins, clearly indicating that the gas dynamics of J1243 + 0100 is rotation-dominated .

sources.summary: J1243+0100 is treated as a z > 7 quasar with a low Eddington ratio; its slow accretion tightens allowed black-hole seed masses and favors low radiative efficiencies.

quotes.quote: Remarkably, two quasars detected at z > 7 with low accretion rates (J1243+0100 and J0313--1806) already tighten the available parameter space, requiring M_ , seed > 10^ 3.5 and < 0.1 .

Interestingly, the two quasars J1243+0100 and J0313--1806 show much tighter parameter spaces, because their estimated Eddington ratios are = 0.34 0.2 Matsuoka_2019a and = 0.67 0.14 Wang_2021 , respectively. These quasars are accreting at lower Eddington rates (i.e., at a slower pace), and this significantly tightens the allowed parameter space, with M_ , seed > 10^5 for J1243+0100 and M_ , seed > 10^ 3.5 for J0313--1806, while also requiring lower radiative efficiencies 0.1 .

gmw_00001682.name: NGC 615
gmw_00001682.overview: NGC 615 is an unbarred spiral galaxy that features a pair of HII regions located symmetrically around its nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622]. The galaxy possesses an oval-shaped inner disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622] and exhibits disturbed gas kinematics with non-circular motions [arXiv:astro-ph/0409622].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 615 gas and stellar kinematics with earlier work and uses its high-quality disc spectrum to test detectability of counterrotating stellar components.

quotes.quote: Ionized gas velocity curves have been already measured along the major axes of NGC 615, NGC 1620, NGC 2708, NGC 2815, NGC 3054, NGC 3200, NGC 4682, NGC 6118, NGC 7083 and NGC 7531. However, these measurements had previously been obtained with a lower spatial and/or velocity resolution with respect to ours. In Figure fig:gascomparison we plot our kinematic measurements compared to the available data.

We assumed the stars in the disc of NGC 615 as the prograde component of the model. We selected the spectrum of NGC 615 since the absorption lines in the wavelength and radial ranges where we are going to measure the stellar kinematics are nearly symmetric with respect to the centre, have a large radial extension and are characterized by a high signal to noise ( S/N 30 per \ for ||>20'' , and S/N 50 per \ for || 20'' ). We obtained the retrograde component of model by flipping the spectrum of NGC 615 about the centre of the slit.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 615 has symmetric HII regions, disturbed gas kinematics, and an oval inner disk consistent with a triaxial central potential.

quotes.quote: The most prominent feature of the monochromatic H image (Fig. ngc615 b) is a pair of HII regions located symmetrically with respect to the nucleus at the radius of 20 23 . They are slightly elongated along the radius and look like shock fronts at the edges of a bar (though the galaxy is classified as unbarred). At the same distance from the center the gas velocity field is disturbed by non-circular motion resulting in a turn of the kinematic major axis by more than 20 at =15 30 .

We have found here several spots with the line-of-sight velocities differing by 40 - 50 from the mean rotation field. These kinematic details may be evidence for a triaxial potential within =25 30 in this galaxy. Indeed, the recent photometric analysis of Sil'chenko et al. (2001) has revealed that NGC 615 possesses a separate inner compact disk of oval shape especially notable at =15 30 where it introduces non-monotonic changes of isophote orientation and ellipticity.

gmw_00001683.name: NGC 2815
gmw_00001683.overview: NGC 2815 is a spiral galaxy characterized by distinct stellar and gas kinematics [arXiv:astro-ph/0404558]. In this galaxy, the ionized gas rotates faster and exhibits a lower velocity dispersion than its stellar component [arXiv:astro-ph/0404558].

sources.summary: The paper reports new gas and stellar kinematics for NGC 2815, finding a discrepancy with earlier stellar data on the southeast side while placing it in the normal gas-faster-than-stars class.

quotes.quote: In Fig. fig:starcomparison we compared our kinematic measurements for the stellar component of NGC 615, NGC 2815 and NGC 3200 to the data derived by Bottema (1992), Sil'chenko, Vlasyuk Alvarado (2001) and Vega Beltr a n et al. \ (2001), respectively. The overall agreement is good both for velocities and velocity dispersions, except for NGC 2815. Indeed, we measured on the south-east side of this galaxy lower rotation velocities and higher velocity dispersions with respect to those of Bottema (1992).

Galaxies in which ionized gas rotates faster than stars and has a lower velocity dispersion than the stars (i.e., > \ and < ): NGC 210, NGC 615, NGC 1620, NGC 2590, NGC 2708 (although it has asymmetric rotation curves for both gas and stars), NGC 2815, NGC 3200, NGC 3717, NGC 6118, NGC 6878, NGC 6895 and NGC 7083.

gmw_00001684.name: NGC 7083
gmw_00001684.overview: NGC 7083 is a spiral galaxy with a well-defined galaxy disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0404558]. Kinematic measurements of the galaxy's ionized gas reveal a velocity curve that has been studied to resolve past discrepancies in its rotation data [arXiv:astro-ph/0404558].

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 7083 ionized-gas rotation data with prior measurements and highlights a northeast-side systematic discrepancy attributed to earlier wavelength calibration.

quotes.quote: Ionized gas velocity curves have been already measured along the major axes of NGC 615, NGC 1620, NGC 2708, NGC 2815, NGC 3054, NGC 3200, NGC 4682, NGC 6118, NGC 7083 and NGC 7531. However, these measurements had previously been obtained with a lower spatial and/or velocity resolution with respect to ours. In Figure fig:gascomparison we plot our kinematic measurements compared to the available data. The agreement is good and the discrepancies ( V 20 ) are due to the different spatial sampling and accuracy of velocity measurements.

Only in the case of NGC 7083 do we notice a systematic difference ( V 80 ) on the north-east side of the rotation curve between our data and those of Rubin et al. \ (1982). We attribute such a discrepancy to the wavelength calibration of Rubin et al. (1982) since it is not present when we consider the data of Mathewson, Ford Buchhorn (1992).

gmw_00001685.name: NGC 3054
gmw_00001685.overview: NGC 3054 is classified as a weakly barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0404558]. It exhibits peculiar kinematics in its gas and stellar components, which suggests the presence of either a warped gaseous disc or non-circular gas motions [arXiv:astro-ph/0404558].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3054 as a weakly barred spiral with peculiar gas and stellar kinematics, requiring either a warped gas disc or non-circular gas motions.

quotes.quote: The weakly barred spirals NGC 3054 and NGC 7531 show peculiar kinematics that are unexpected if the gas is moving in circular orbits in a disc coplanar to the stellar one. In fact, in NGC 3054 \ and < \ for || 10'' , and > \ and \ at larger radii, while NGC 7531 has intermediate properties between the two classes. It is characterized by \ and < \ for || 10'' , and > \ and \ for ||>10'' . These cases cannot be explained without invoking a warp in the gaseous disc or non-circular gas motions.

gmw_00001686.name: NGC 7531
gmw_00001686.overview: NGC 7531 is a weakly barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0404558] featuring a high surface brightness inner disk and tightly wound outer spiral arms that appear optically ring-like [arXiv:2304.00515]. Its neutral hydrogen gas is not settled into a ring [arXiv:2304.00515], and the galaxy exhibits peculiar kinematics that suggest non-circular gas motions or a warped gaseous disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0404558].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7531 as optically ring-like through tightly wound spiral arms, but its HI is not settled into a ring and shows only slight extensions.

quotes.quote: NGC 7531: The top left panels in Fig. fig:mosaic4 show both the optical image and the 1 velocity map of the galaxy NGC 7531. This galaxy is observed to have a high surface brightness inner disc with two tightly wound outer spiral arms almost forming a ring. In terms of the 1 gas, we find that the gas is not settled into a ring-like structure. The ring-like nature of this galaxy is only identifiable via its optical image. The 1 velocity maps show fairly ordered rotation in the galaxy with no significant distortion to the velocity field, but we do observe some slight 1 extensions towards both the northeast and southeast ends of the galaxy, but it is not clear if this is actual emission or if it is some residual noise/artefact that may have remained in the image.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7531 as a bulge with unusual age behavior, no metallicity or alpha-enhancement gradient, and a corotating but structurally decoupled component.

quotes.quote: The age of the stellar population of NGC 7531 rises from a central value of about 2 Gyr to 12 Gyr at 4 arcsec. Further out it decreases to 2 Gyr at 6 arcsec from the centre. Despite this change, no gradient in both metallicity and / Fe enhancement was found. We suggest that this is due to the presence of a component which is corotating but structurally decoupled with respect to the rest of the galaxy e.g., mcdetal06 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7531 as a weakly barred spiral with intermediate but peculiar kinematics, suggesting a warped gaseous disc or non-circular gas motions.

quotes.quote: The weakly barred spirals NGC 3054 and NGC 7531 show peculiar kinematics that are unexpected if the gas is moving in circular orbits in a disc coplanar to the stellar one. In fact, in NGC 3054 \ and < \ for || 10'' , and > \ and \ at larger radii, while NGC 7531 has intermediate properties between the two classes. It is characterized by \ and < \ for || 10'' , and > \ and \ for ||>10'' . These cases cannot be explained without invoking a warp in the gaseous disc or non-circular gas motions.

gmw_00001687.name: GRB 000418 host
gmw_00001687.overview: The GRB 000418 host galaxy is a compact, smooth starburst galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.118 [arXiv:astro-ph/0307153, arXiv:astro-ph/0508197]. It exhibits a young, dusty stellar population with a high star formation rate [arXiv:astro-ph/0407066, arXiv:0708.4014], though its morphology has also been fit by an early-type profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0508197].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 000418 host as a compact z=1.118 starburst galaxy with optical-line SFR evidence and HST morphology consistent with a blue compact nuclear starburst.

quotes.quote: HST/STIS observations performed on 4.17 UT June 2000 (46.76 days after the GRB) revealed that the OA occurred in a very compact host galaxy with a half-light radius of 0 13 (Metzger et al. ) corresponding to about 1 kpc. The redshift of the host galaxy was determined to be z=1.118 (Bloom et al. , ). A preliminary BVRI band SED fitting analysis showed that the host galaxy SED can be fitted with starburst galaxy templates, but not with an evolved stellar population (Gorosabel et al. ). This result has been recently supported by Bloom et al. ( ) who based on the optical emission lines suggest that the host is a starburst galaxy, rather than a LINER or a Seyfert 2 galaxy. Bloom et al. ( ) estimate an unobscured star formation rate (SFR) of 55 M_ yr ^ 1 based on the [ O II ] emission line diagnostic method (Kennicutt ).

The analysis of optical/NIR observations presented in this paper confirms that the GRB 000418 host is a starburst galaxy. This result has been independently achieved by fitting synthetic and empirical templates to the photometric points. This conclusion is also consistent with the morphological information derived from the HST/STIS images, where the host is seen as a blue compact galaxy with no evidence for more widespread star formation.

sources.summary: The paper treats the GRB 000418 host as a well-sampled star-forming GRB host whose SED rejects continuous star formation and whose best fit may be a young dusty starburst.

quotes.quote: Indeed, very accurate photometric redshifts can be determined if the photometric uncertainties are small and the Balmer jump is well sampled as is the case for the GRB 000418, GRB 000210, and GRB 990712 hosts.

However, this was not the case for the GRB 990712, GRB 000210, and GRB 000418 hosts, which gave ^2/ d.o.f. = 14 , 24, and 6, respectively. These hosts are the only ones which have extensive multiband photometric measurements, and their SEDs are well constrained. This implies that a continuous SFR is not in agreement with the observed SED.

The large errors for the photometry of the other hosts make their SEDs less well constrained. A special comment is needed for the host of GRB 000418. In goro03 , the final reported results for the SED fits are consistent with those reported here in Table tab:hypsed . However, the actual best fit having the minimum ^2 /d.o.f. for a Salpeter IMF and a Calzetti extinction law, is obtained with a 0.004 Gyr old starburst template with an extinction of A_V=1.38 (see Table 3 in ).

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB 000418 host among four submillimetre/radio-bright GRB hosts, finding young starburst SEDs, blue optical colors, ages of 0.09-2.0 Gyr, and high star-formation rates.

quotes.quote: We present optical to radio spectral energy distribution fitting of the host galaxies of four long gamma-ray bursts: 980703, 000210, 000418 and 010222, which were detected at submillimetre and/or radio wavelengths. We find that only very young starburst galaxy models are consistent with the data having both blue optical colors and a pronounced submm emission. For each host we are able to construct a model consistent with the short- and long-wavelength parts of the spectra. We find galaxy ages ranging from 0.09 to 2.0 Gyrs and star formation rates ranging from 138 to 380 M_ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper finds the GRB 000418 host better fit by a de Vaucouleurs profile, consistent with an early-type interpretation, while also compact, smooth, and strongly star-forming in submillimeter data.

quotes.quote: In the cases of GRB 991208 and GRB 000418, the data are better fit with a de Vaucouleurs profile, although for these galaxies the difference in ^2 between the two models is not very large. An early type interpretation however agrees with that found using the CAS methodology (Table 1; 3.2).

The host of GRB 980613 was previously studied by Hjorth et al. (2002), who find an asymmetric and chaotic structure, while Gorosabel et al. (2003) argue that the host of GRB 000418 is compact and smooth, both in agreement with our results (Table 1).

Two of these, GRB 000418 and GRB 010222, have a very high star-formation rate inferred from sub-mm observations (Frail et al. 2002; Berger et al. 2003), which might seem contradictory to their elliptical appearances.

gmw_00001688.name: GRB 000210 host
gmw_00001688.overview: The GRB 000210 host galaxy is a subluminous starburst galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of approximately 0.846 [arXiv:astro-ph/0212334]. It is a young, isolated system with no detected companion galaxies [arXiv:astro-ph/0212334] and an observed spectral energy distribution that is inconsistent with a continuous star formation model [arXiv:astro-ph/0407066].

sources.summary: The paper presents broad-band photometry and SED fitting for the GRB 000210 host, finding a spectroscopic-consistent redshift, subluminous luminosity, no detected companions, modest UV star formation, and a young low-extinction starburst template.

quotes.quote: We present UBVRIZJsHKs broad band photometry of the host galaxy of the dark gamma-ray burst (GRB) of February 10, 2000. These observations represent the most exhaustive photometry given to date of any GRB host galaxy. A grid of spectral templates have been fitted to the Spectral Energy Distribution (SED) of the host. The derived photometric redshift is z=0.842^ +0.054 _ 0.042 , which is in excellent agreement with the spectroscopic redshift ( z=0.8463 0.0002 ) proposed by Piro et al. \ ( ) based on a single emission line. Furthermore, we have determined the photometric redshift of all the galaxies in an area of 6^ 6^ around the host galaxy, in order to check for their overdensity in the environment of the host.

We find that the GRB 000210 host galaxy is a subluminous galaxy ( L 0.5 0.2 L^ ), with no companions above our detection threshold of 0.18 0.06 L^ . Based on the restframe ultraviolet flux a star formation rate of 2.1 0.2 M_ yr ^ 1 is estimated. The best fit to the SED is obtained for a starburst template with an age of 0.181^ +0.037 _ 0.026 Gyr and a very low extinction ( A_ V 0 ).

sources.summary: The paper includes the GRB 000210 host among well-constrained GRB host SEDs, with accurate photometric redshift behavior and evidence against a continuous star-formation model.

quotes.quote: Indeed, very accurate photometric redshifts can be determined if the photometric uncertainties are small and the Balmer jump is well sampled as is the case for the GRB 000418, GRB 000210, and GRB 990712 hosts.

Detailed analyses of the SEDs of the host galaxies of GRB 000210, GRB 000418, and GRB 990712 are presented by goro02 , goro03 , and lise03 , respectively. Similar thorough individual SED analyses are beyond the scope of this paper. Results from the SED fittings are given in Table tab:hypsed .

However, this was not the case for the GRB 990712, GRB 000210, and GRB 000418 hosts, which gave ^2/ d.o.f. = 14 , 24, and 6, respectively. These hosts are the only ones which have extensive multiband photometric measurements, and their SEDs are well constrained. This implies that a continuous SFR is not in agreement with the observed SED.

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB 000210 host among four submillimetre/radio-bright GRB hosts, finding young starburst SEDs, blue optical colors, ages of 0.09-2.0 Gyr, and high star-formation rates.

quotes.quote: We present optical to radio spectral energy distribution fitting of the host galaxies of four long gamma-ray bursts: 980703, 000210, 000418 and 010222, which were detected at submillimetre and/or radio wavelengths. We find that only very young starburst galaxy models are consistent with the data having both blue optical colors and a pronounced submm emission. For each host we are able to construct a model consistent with the short- and long-wavelength parts of the spectra. We find galaxy ages ranging from 0.09 to 2.0 Gyrs and star formation rates ranging from 138 to 380 M_ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00001689.name: GRB 980703 host
gmw_00001689.overview: The GRB 980703 host is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.97 [arXiv:astro-ph/0301149, arXiv:1111.1406]. It is characterized as a young, dusty starburst galaxy with blue optical colors and a high star-formation rate [arXiv:0708.4014, arXiv:astro-ph/0301149].

sources.summary: The paper notes that GRB 980703 host magnitudes come from afterglow light-curve fits and that its SED extinction estimate changes substantially between IMF assumptions.

quotes.quote: For example, the GRB 980703 host magnitudes were derived this way in vrees99 . Data obtained more than one year after this particular burst gave magnitudes which are consistent with those reported in Table tab:allmag holland01 .

The host of GRB 000418 and GRB 980703 are treated separately below. Using different extinction laws resulted in differences in A_V smaller than 0.1, and furthermore, the estimated ages remained constant for any applied extinction law.

As seen in Table tab:hypsed the estimated extinction for the GRB 980703 host changes by a large factor for the two applied IMFs. However, the lower limit on the extinction derived for the Miller Scalo IMF fit is consistent with that derived for the Salpeter IMF fit.

sources.summary: The paper models the GRB 980703 host among four submillimetre/radio-bright GRB hosts, finding young starburst SEDs, blue optical colors, ages of 0.09-2.0 Gyr, and high star-formation rates.

quotes.quote: We present optical to radio spectral energy distribution fitting of the host galaxies of four long gamma-ray bursts: 980703, 000210, 000418 and 010222, which were detected at submillimetre and/or radio wavelengths. We find that only very young starburst galaxy models are consistent with the data having both blue optical colors and a pronounced submm emission. For each host we are able to construct a model consistent with the short- and long-wavelength parts of the spectra. We find galaxy ages ranging from 0.09 to 2.0 Gyrs and star formation rates ranging from 138 to 380 M_ yr ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 980703 host as a z=0.9662 galaxy whose B-band deficit and continuum SED were modeled using spectra plus BVR_cI_c photometry.

quotes.quote: In the case of the host galaxy of GRB 980703 ( z=0.9662 ) the observed deficit in the B-band (see Figure f:Fig2 ) can be explained by the excess of extinction near 2200 , which is characteristic of the extinction law similar to that of the Milky Way.

So, in two cases (see Table tab:simple ) of pure host galaxy spectra: GRB 970508 ( M_ B_ rest = -18.62 ) and GRB 980703 ( M_ B_ rest = -21.27 ) we performed the theoretical modeling of the continuum spectral energy distribution of these hosts, making use of the spectra and our BVR_cI_c photometry Sokolov2001b .
These two examples demonstrate that BVR_cI_c photometrical spectral distribution describes well the spectral continua of the host galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats the GRB 980703 host as an intermediate dusty GRB host, arguing its radio detection, redshift, color, and K luminosity resemble infrared-luminous starbursts.

quotes.quote: Naturally, intermediate cases should also exist, as illustrated by the VLA detection of the GRB 980703 host galaxy Berger01b . Assuming that the radio/far-infrared correlation still holds for high redshift sources, this host pinpointed by an optical transient of a GRB occuring at z = 0.97 could be indeed a dusty galaxy luminous in the infrared. In fact, we note that its R-K colour 2.8 and its absolute K magnitude -24.0 would be consistent with this source being similar to the NIR counterparts of the ISO dusty starbursts (see Figs. fig:colours and fig:k_abs_z ).

gmw_00001690.name: GRB 990123 host
gmw_00001690.overview: The GRB 990123 host is a luminous, large irregular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9903175] at a redshift of z=1.600 [arXiv:astro-ph/0407066]. It is classified as a young starburst galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0407066] featuring non-axisymmetric structures and several nearby compact star-forming knots [arXiv:astro-ph/9903175].

sources.summary: The probable GRB 990123 host is described as a luminous large irregular, starburst-like galaxy with non-axisymmetric structure and nearby compact star-forming knots.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: It is located at = 15^ h 25^ m 30 32, +44 45 59 7 .

quotes.quote: The probable host galaxy has V_0 = 24.25 0.07 ( 0.716 0.046 Jy) and the optical transient is located 0 65 ( = 5.5 kpc) south of the galaxy's nucleus. We fit and subtracted a scaled point-spread function to the optical transient and found evidence for three bright knots situated within 0 5 ( = 4.3 kpc) of the optical transient. Each knot has V_0 28.1 0.3 , a rest-frame V band luminosity of L_V 5 - 8 10^8 L_ , and a star-formation rate of at least 0.1 - 0.2 M _ yr ^ 1 .

The host galaxy appears to be a large irregular galaxy with several non-axisymmetric components. The total AB magnitude of the galaxy (excluding the OT) is 24.31 0.07 . Castro-Tirado et al. \ ( ) find a flat spectrum ( 0 ) consistent with a starburst galaxy. Adopting this spectrum, and assuming no internal reddening in the galaxy, we find V_0 = 24.25 0.07 and R_0 = 24.07 0.07 for the host galaxy, corresponding to fluxes of 0.716 0.046 Jy and 0.711 0.046 Jy respectively. The total luminosity is L_V = (2.4 0.2) 10^ 10 L_ = (1.0 0.1) L^ _V .

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 990123 host as having an inaccurate photometric redshift estimate and derives a UV-continuum star-formation rate from its observed U-band magnitude.

quotes.quote: Only for the GRB 990123 host the redshift estimate is inaccurate ( z>0.3 ), but taking into account the uncertainty of the estimate the difference is only 1.6 . A reason for the relatively large discrepancy could be that the Balmer jump is not well sampled.

For the GRB 000926 host at z=2.037 and the GRB 990123 host at z=1.600 we use the observed B and U band magnitudes, respectively, as a rough measure of their continuum flux at 1500 in the rest frame to get an independent estimate of the SFR (uncorrected for extinction). Using the relation in madau98 SFR (M )=1.3 10^ 28 L_ [ erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 ] we find SFR = 4.3 2.0 and 7.4 3.0 M \ not corrected for internal extinction for the two hosts, respectively.

The GRBs which occurred in these galaxies comprise a collection of a dark burst (GRB 000210), a dim one (GRB 000418), a bright one (GRB 990712), and a very bright one (GRB 990123). Comparing the SEDs of the GRB hosts with template spectra we find that they are young starburst galaxies with moderate to low extinctions ( A_V<1 ).

gmw_00001691.name: CGCG 1510.8+0725
gmw_00001691.overview: CGCG 1510.8+0725 is an isolated luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1505.06934, arXiv:1509.08468] featuring an undisturbed spiral disk [arXiv:1509.08468] and a compact, highly obscured nucleus [arXiv:1505.06934, arXiv:1908.04058]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1810.02371] and exhibits a nuclear molecular outflow [arXiv:1711.05321] alongside low-luminosity OH megamaser activity [arXiv:1307.5071, arXiv:1509.08468].

sources.summary: The paper studies Zw 049.057 as a luminous infrared galaxy with a compact obscured nucleus, rich molecular gas, warm dense nuclear components, and possible buried activity.

quotes.quote: Smith 7 Department of Earth and Space Sciences, Chalmers University of Technology, Onsala Space Observatory, 439 92 Onsala, Sweden niklas.falstad@chalmers.se Universidad de Alcal a de Henares, Departamento de F sica, Campus Universitario, E-28871 Alcal a de Henares, Madrid, Spain Leiden Observatory, Leiden University, P.O. \ Box 9513, NL-2300 RA Leiden, The Netherlands Naval Research Laboratory, Remote Sensing Division, 4555 Overlook Ave SW, Washington, DC 20375, USA Department of Astronomy, University of Maryland, College Park, MD 20742, USA Department of Physics, Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, VA 24061, USA Harvard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics, 60 Garden Street, Cambridge, MA 02138, USA The luminous infrared galaxy Zw 049.057 contains a compact obscured nucleus where a considerable amount of the galaxy's luminosity is generated. This nucleus contains a dusty environment that is rich in molecular gas. One approach to probing this kind of environment and to revealing what is hidden behind the dust is to study the rotational lines of molecules that couple well with the infrared radiation emitted by the dust. We probe the physical conditions in the core of Zw 049.057 and establish the nature of its nuclear power source (starburst or active galactic nucleus). We observed Zw 049.057 with the Photodetector Array Camera and Spectrometer (PACS) and the Spectral and Photometric Imaging Receiver (SPIRE) onboard the Herschel Space Observatory in rotational lines of H _ 2 O, H _ 2 ^ 18 O, OH, ^ 18 OH, and [ O I ] .

We present the full high-resolution SPIRE FTS spectrum of Zw 049.057, along with relevant spectral scans in the PACS range. We find that a minimum of two different components (nuclear and extended) are required in order to account for the rich molecular line spectrum of Zw 049.057. The nuclear component has a radius of 10-30 pc, a very high infrared surface brightness ( 10^ 14 L _ kpc ^ 2 ), warm dust ( T_ d >100 K), and a very large H _ 2 column density (N _ H_ 2 =10^ 24 10^ 25 cm ^ 2 ). The modeling also indicates high nuclear H _ 2 O ( 5 10^ 6 ) and OH ( 4 10^ 6 ) abundances relative to H _ 2 as well as a low ^ 16 O/ ^ 18 O-ratio of 50-100 .

sources.summary: The paper maps IRAS 15107+0724 formaldehyde megamaser emission, finding a two-component spectrum, central and off-nuclear emission regions, an S-shaped continuum, and merger-like velocity components.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15107+0724 has been first detected at Arecibo Observatory Baan93 with a peak flux of 1.91 mJy. A representative spectrum, presented in Figure fig:IR15107spec Araya04 , shows a spectral line with a mean velocity of 3880 and a total width of 320 , but clearly consisting of two components. No other tentative features can be seen in the spectrum. An OH spectrum also shows two emission components centred at 3782 and 3900 , whereas the HI spectrum shows a broad absorption feature centred at 3902 Baan87 .

The main emission feature straddles the central continuum peak and has an extent of 52 pc at PA = 140 . This orientation agrees well with the orientation of the 2Mass image of the galaxy at PA = 155 Skrutskie06 . Two other compact emission regions, South and NorthWest, are superposed on the extended radio continuum structure.

sources.summary: The paper presents Zw 049.057 as a luminous infrared galaxy with a compact obscured nucleus, molecular outflow signatures in CO/OH, possible radio-feature connection, and OH excitation suggesting a megamaser-to-kilomaser transition.

quotes.quote: The luminous infrared galaxy Zw 049.057 harbours a compact obscured nucleus with a possible far-infrared signature of outflowing molecular gas. Due to the high optical depths at far-infrared wavelengths, however, the interpretation of the outflow signature is uncertain. At millimeter and radio wavelengths, the radiation is better able to penetrate the large columns of gas and dust responsible for the obscuration. We aim to investigate the molecular gas distribution and kinematics in the nucleus of Zw 049.057 in order to confirm and locate the molecular outflow, with the ultimate goal to understand how the nuclear activity affects the host galaxy.

We interpret the wings in the spectral lines as signatures of a nuclear molecular outflow. A relation between this outflow and the minor axis radio feature is possible, although further studies are required to investigate this possible association and understand the connection between the outflow and the nuclear activity. Finally, we suggest that the differing OH excitation conditions are further evidence that Zw 049.057 is in a transition phase between megamaser and kilomaser activity.

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 1510.8+0725 as an extreme [CII]-deficient compact infrared/radio galaxy with a broad H line indicative of a Seyfert nucleus.

quotes.quote: In the extreme cases of NGC 4418, IC 0860 and CGCG 1510.8+0725, [ CII ] emission is undetected at the 3 level of 2cii =5-0.5 10^ 4 . Emission in the other prominent PDR cooling line [ OI ] at 63 is undetected at similar levels, as are [ OIII ] ( 88 ) and [ NII ] ( 122 ). In Figure 1 we show 3 upper limits on the flux in the [ CII ] 158 transition. The absence of [ CII ] emission in these galaxies is very striking because the upper limits on the in these galaxies are up to 40 times smaller than the narrow range in measured for two-thirds of the sample. Yet these galaxies have normal atomic and molecular gas components.

The mid-infrared images at 7 and 15 are unresolved at 7 resolution of ISOCAM (Silbermann et al. 1997) and they are compact in radio continuum at 1.4 GHz (Condon et al 1990): NGC 4418 measures 0.5'' 0.3''(32 ) , CGCG 1510.8+0725 measures 0.5'' 0.5'' (85 ) and IC 860 0.4'' 0.3 '' (75 ) . CGCG 1510.8+0725 shows a broad H line indicative of a Seyfert nucleus (Kim et al. 1995) but no H , (possibly due to obscuration), causing it to be classified as a HII galaxy (Veilleux et al. 1995).

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS F15107+0724 as a low-luminosity OH megamaser with all four 18 cm OH lines detected, partially conjugate satellite behavior, and evidence for two OH inversion mechanisms.

quotes.quote: IRAS F15107+0724. This OHM was reported in Baan1987 , and is among the least intrinsically luminous OHMs, with an isotropic luminosity of 15 L_ . In our observations, all four 18 cm OH lines are detected (Figure fig:15107 ). Emission in the 1612 MHz line falls predominantly between 3,910--3,980 , and at its peak it is nearly as strong as the 1665 MHz line at the same velocity. Over this same velocity range, the 1720 MHz line appears in absorption, with a similar line shape, but at a weaker level than the 1612 MHz emission. The areas of conjugate line shape represent competition between the 1612 MHz and 1720 MHz transitions for pumping photons.
Thus, within the same range of velocities in IRAS F15107+0724, there must be two separate OH inversion mechanisms acting.

sources.summary: The paper discusses Zw 049.057 as an HCN-vib luminous LIRG with possible lower-velocity outflows, centimeter OH outflow signatures absent in far-infrared lines, and polar dust clouds suggesting episodic feedback.

quotes.quote: Possible outflow signatures are also found in Zw 049.057 fal18 as well as in NGC 4418 and IRAS 22491-1808 flu18 , although at lower velocities.

In fact, at least three of the five most obscured sources Arp 220, IRAS 17208-0014, and Zw 049.057; baa89,fal18 have outflows that are seen in the OH lines at centimeter wavelengths but not in the far-infrared. In Zw 049.057, infrared and optical images reveal multiple clouds in a polar dust structure, which might suggest that the outflow in this galaxy is not steady in time (Gallagher et al. in prep.).

For example, a lot of nuclear power is required in order to push large columns of gas to high velocities, and LIRGs like NGC 4418 and Zw 049.057 may therefore never develop powerful wide-angle outflows detectable in the far-IR OH lines. Instead, they might evolve along the horizontal axis in Fig. fig:HCN_vs_OH .

sources.summary: The paper models Zw 049.057 as an obscured compact nucleus with high H2 column, bright HCN emission, compact-core luminosity near 10^11 solar luminosities, and matching dust temperatures.

quotes.quote: Zw 049.057 : An obscured and compact nucleus was identified by from high-lying far-IR molecular absorption, with a most plausible radius of 15-25 pc at 56 Mpc ( =(1.0-2.7) 10^ 2 arc sec ^2 ) and a column density of N_ H2 10^ 24.5-25 cm ^ 2 . The HCN _2=1^f J=3-2 line was detected by with a flux of 6.8 Jy km s ^ 1 , yielding 250-700 Jy km s ^ 1 arc sec ^ 2 . From Fig. hcngreenhousecolH2 , we estimate _ IR =(0.5-2) 10^8 L _ pc ^ 2 for the upper and lower limit of , respectively. This gives a luminosity of (1.0-1.4) 10^ 11 \ for the compact core, in agreement with the estimate by from far-IR molecular absorption lines ( (0.7-1.2) 10^ 11 ). Our predicted range of T_ dust across the far-IR photosphere brackets the range favored by ( 90-130 K).

sources.summary: The paper describes this isolated OH megamaser galaxy as having an older blue stellar population, star-formation emission lines, composite shock-like contribution, and an undisturbed spiral disk.

quotes.quote: IRAS F15107+0724 (CGCG 049-057) This galaxy bears a low-luminosity OH megamaser Martin88 , and was classified as an region galaxy by Kim95,Veilleux95 . Our IFS data are dominated primarily by an older stellar population in the blue, with the emission lines primarily indicative of ongoing star formation plus some composite contribution, likely shocks given the velocity dispersion distribution and relatively high 6.2 PAH eqw value of 0.51 Stierwalt13 . An HST NICMOS H-band image is the highest resolution data available, showing what appears to be a relatively undisturbed spiral disc Haan11b . This is in keeping with it's status as a isolated galaxy Yuan10 .

sources.summary: CGCG 049 057 meets an X-ray hardness AGN criterion despite low counts, has a rising hard spectrum, nuclear hard-band counts, and a radio AGN classification.

quotes.quote: [ 120 ] CGCG 049 057 : This source, despite only having a total of 30 cts in the 0.5-7 keV band of the available Chandra observation, has a hardness ratio of HR=-0.04 0.09 , and so meets one of the X-ray AGN selection criteria. The spectrum of the source shows, despite the large error bars, a tendency to rising flux toward higher energies. The X-ray image in the 2-7 keV band shows about 5 cts originating from the innermost region of the source, and thus we classify it as an AGN. BaaKlo2006 also classified it as an AGN based on radio observations.

sources.summary: The survey confirms earlier formaldehyde emission in IRAS 15107+0724, newly detects the second transition in absorption, and associates the emission with a compact nuclear source.

quotes.quote: IRAS 15107+0724: Baan1993 detected the 1_ 10 1_ 11 line in emission, and the GBT line properties are in fair agreement with the discovery detection and in good agreement with Araya2004 . We also detect the 2_ 11 2_ 12 line in absorption. imaged the CO J= 1 0 emission from this galaxy, deriving a compact nuclear source with size 3^ .

sources.summary: The paper reports new NH3 absorption discoveries toward IR 15107+0724 and derives its kinetic temperature from NH3 (1,1) and (2,2).

quotes.quote: IR 15107+0724: The NH _3 (1,1), (2,2), and (4,4) absorption spectra measured toward this galaxy, representing new discoveries of NH _3 , have the same velocity structure measured in the H _2 CO 1_ 10 1_ 11 and 2_ 11 2_ 12 transitions reported in Mangum2013 . Due to the relative weakness of the NH _3 (4,4) absorption ( 4.4 in integrated intensity), the kinetic temperature derived is based on the NH _3 (1,1) and (2,2) measurements toward this galaxy. The background continuum source size is estimated to be 1^ see and references therein Condon1990 .

gmw_00001692.overview: 0507+179 is a quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.42 [arXiv:1202.4209]. Between September 2007 and January 2011, the source exhibited polarization in 21 out of 22 measurements alongside a smoothly evolving 3-mm flux density [arXiv:1202.4209].

sources.summary: The paper gives millimeter polarimetric monitoring results for 0507+179, including detection frequency, polarization behavior, flux evolution, variability, and rotation-measure constraints.

quotes.quote: 0507+179 This quasar, located at z 0.42 , shows significant polarization in 21 out of 22 measurements obtained between September 2007 and January 2011. The average polarization level is m_L 7.4 . In 2007, the degree of polarization is m_L 6 , declines to m_L 3 in the first half of 2008, rises again to levels up to m_L 11 , and decreases again to m_L 8 . In 2007 and 2008, the polarization angle varies in the range 50...110^ , remaining around 100^ thereafter. The degree of polarization fluctuates at rates of _m 0.4 d ^ 1 and _m/ m_L 0.05 d ^ 1 , respectively.

The 3-mm flux evolved smoothly, with values S_ 0.7 Jy in 2007, peaking at S_ 1.5 Jy at the beginning of 2009, and decreasing again to S_ 0.5 Jy. The average flux density is found to be S_ 1 Jy. It fluctuates with _S 0.1 Jy d ^ 1 , corresponding to a normalized fluctuation rate of _S/ S_ 0.1 d ^ 1 .

gmw_00001693.overview: The high-redshift active galactic nucleus 0528+134 is a bright blazar [arXiv:astro-ph/0608240] classified as a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1503.04780, arXiv:1405.4168]. It features a relativistic radio jet with distinct bends [arXiv:astro-ph/0608240] and exhibits flux and polarization variability [arXiv:1202.4209, arXiv:1607.02053].

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 0528+134 as a bright high-redshift blazar/AGN, mapping its VLBI jet bends, core identification, superluminal components, and a new component linked to a radio burst.

quotes.quote: As one of the brightest active galactic nuclei, PKS 0528+134 has been intensively studied at the radio, X-ray, and gamma-ray bands. It is one of the furthest blazars detected by EGRET above 100 Mev (Mukherjee et al. ) with red-shift z=2.07 (Hunter et al. ). There are relatively less optical observations compared with the observations at other bands because of its location close to the Galactic plane (galactic latitude b= 11 0'43'' ) and thus high Galactic extinction.

The five high-resolution VLBI maps made at frequencies 2.3, 5.0, 8.4, 15.4, and 22.2 GHz show that PKS 0528+134 has a jet structure extending in the north east direction, and the jet bending is clearly seen at about 0.3 and 1.4 mas from the core. Our registration of VLBI components a, f, and g is consistent with the one in B99. We also detect a new VLBI jet component n2, which is likely to be ejected during the radio burst at about 1999.5. We estimate the ejection epoch of component a to be 1991.94 by the linear back-extrapolation of its proper motion, but cannot determine which flux-density burst is truly related to the ejection of component a. No counter-jet is seen in our maps, but its existence cannot be ruled out.

sources.summary: The paper gives millimeter polarimetric monitoring results for 0528+134, including detection frequency, polarization behavior, flux evolution, variability, and rotation-measure constraints.

quotes.quote: 0528+134 (OG+134) For this high-polarization quasar located at z 2.1 , 18 out of 32 polarimetric observations, obtained between February 2007 and December 2008, detected linearly polarized emission. The average degree of polarization is m_L 3.2 . During the observation timeline, polarization levels varied in the range m_L 2...6 , whereas the polarization angles varied in the range 0...140^ . The degree of polarization fluctuates at rates of _m 0.3 d ^ 1 and _m/ m_L 0.1 d ^ 1 , respectively.

The 3-mm flux density was at its peak of S_ 5 Jy when observations commenced in 2007 and decreased steadily to levels of S_ 1 Jy in 2010. The average of the flux density is S_ 3.5 Jy. The flux density fluctuates with _S 1.1 Jy d ^ 1 , the normalized fluctuation rate being _S/ S_ 0.3 d ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper uses quasi-simultaneous X-ray and VLBA data for PKS0528+134, finding a revised inverse Compton Doppler factor of 37.9 and showing week-scale separations stay consistent while month-scale separations become unrealistically high.

quotes.quote: For PKS0528+134 observations were conducted between September 2009 and November 2009. The X-ray observations were performed by the XMM-Newton observatory Jansen2001 between 08/09/09 and 14/09/09. The radio VLBA observations were performed as part of Boston University group http://www.bu.edu/blazars monthly monitoring program between 16/09/09 and 28/11/09 Palma2011 .
For PKS0528+134 Palma2011 our new estimates is 37.9 whereas the previous value was 3.77.

In order to explore the maximum time separation between observations that will allow as to have an accurate estimate, we calculated the Doppler factor for PKS0528+134 with X-ray and radio observations separated from a few days to few months (Table tab:dated_obs ). We can conclude from the values for PKS0528+134 (Fig. frac_dif ), that even with observations obtained one week apart, the results are consistent with these from the simultaneous observations. When the separation of the observations is more than a month apart, it is clear that the values for the Doppler factor become unrealistically high.

sources.summary: The paper notes prior multiwavelength modeling of PKS 0528 + 134, significant Galactic-plane extinction, blue optical slope, high redshift, and possible I-band emission-line excess.

quotes.quote: For example, Mukherjee (1999) describe a model with both synchrotron and IC emission components fitted to nine epochs of monitoring data for the source PKS 0528 + 134. While considerable effort has been expended in fitting the high-energy spectrum (X-rays and above) of this source, the information in the low-energy part of the spectrum, from optical to radio energies, has largely been ignored.

For two objects, PKS 0528 + 134 and PKS 1622 297, the extinction is large due to their location close to the Galactic plane. We note that Zhang (1994) found evidence for excess absorption over the Galactic value for PKS 0528 + 134 from X-ray observations, which would imply a larger E(B-V) value than that from Schlegel (1998) (and hence a larger correction to the observed magnitudes).

This would be expected for high-redshift sources such as PKS 0528 + 134, as the observed optical emission is probing the rest-frame UV. The remaining three sources have much redder optical slopes. Again comparing to the PHFS sources in Whiting (2001), we find that these slopes are in the middle of the distribution, corresponding to sources with synchrotron-dominated spectra.

sources.summary: The paper models 0528+134 as an FSRQ whose concave near-IR spectrum marks a jet-to-QSO transition, requiring a weaker torus and brighter disc.

quotes.quote: 0528+134 The near-IR spectra traced by both 2MASS and GASP data in faint states reveal a concave shape, suggesting the transition from a spectrum dominated by jet emission to a spectrum dominated by QSO-like emission. The H and H emission lines affect the K and H band fluxes, respectively. Modelling the near-IR spectrum with a log-parabola plus the SWIRE TQSO1 template would overproduce the mid-infrared flux detected by WISE because of the prominent infrared contribution from the dust torus. This implies that the IR/optical flux ratio of the QSO-like contribution in this source must be smaller.

However, this composite template cannot explain the curvature of the near-IR spectrum at intermediate flux levels, which requires a brighter disc. We thus added a black-body component (grey dashed line in Fig. \ sed_fsrqs ) to the composite template to obtain a final QSO-like contribution (blue solid line) that, once added to the jet emission (dot-dashed blue line), can produce a reasonable fit (black solid line) to the observing data.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0528+134 as a distant bright gamma-ray blazar whose 2009 low-state XMM-Newton observation showed no significant IDV.

quotes.quote: PKS 0528+134 PKS 0528+134 is one of the most distant (z = 2.07) and bright gamma-ray blazar. The 2009 XMM-Newton observation of the source was part of a multi-wavelength campaign during its quiescent state (Palma et al. 2011). They reported absence of flux and spectral variability in the gamma-ray, X-ray and radio bands on IDV timescales, but found significant flux variation on IDV timescales in optical bands.

They have also reported STV of moderate strength in the X-ray and radio regime on 1-2 week timescales. The simultaneous multi-wavelength SED shows that the bolometric luminosity is dominated by gamma-ray emission. As usual the low-energy SED peak lies within infrared/optical bands, and the high-energy SED hump peaks at MeV -- GeV energies.

sources.summary: The paper describes quasar 0528 + 134 as showing a slow 8 GHz rise, persistent 2 GHz scintillation, and no matching ISS-amplitude increase at the onset of the intrinsic burst.

quotes.quote: 0528 + 134 As shown in figure f:flux0528 , this quasar was quiescent at 8 GHz over 1988--1992. It then started a slow increase about doubling its flux density by 1994. Then over 1995.2--1996.0 it rose to 8 Jy, doubling its flux density again in less than 1 yr.

The ISS at 2 GHz is 5 on a timescale of 12 d persisting throughout the 8 years of monitoring. We looked for the associated increase 2 GHz ISS amplitude as the 8 GHz burst began, as seen in 0235 + 164. However, though the flux density at 2 GHz did start to increase, the ISS amplitude did not, although it became somewhat faster.

sources.summary: The paper fits J0531 with extended jet models, using its synchrotron break and accretion-disc spectrum to infer a transition region and black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:1 b shows the fits to the spectrum of J0531 in which the jet has a transition region located within the dusty torus and further along the jet corresponding to a distance 10^ 5 R_ s as in the jet of M87. This is interesting since for these large distances the inferred black hole mass of the jet agrees with the black hole mass found independently from fitting the accretion disc spectrum to J0531 only if the transition region occurs at 10^ 5 R_ s , as we have assumed based on observations of M87.

Let us now use this formula to estimate the radius of the transition region from the spectrum of J0531. We shall use the physical parameters from Table tab1 , derived from the ballistic particle dominated jet fit to J0531 shown in Figures fig:1 b and fig:2 . We see clear evidence for this in the spectrum of the blazar J0531 where the synchrotron emission starts self-absorbed and close to flat and retains a steep gradient when the spectrum becomes optically thin (see Figure fig:4 b).

sources.summary: The paper says PKS 0528+134 was poorly fit by the same SSC-cooled model, implying that another cooling mechanism or downstream particle re-acceleration may shape its optical spectrum.

quotes.quote: Application of this model to multiwavelength observations of the ray blazar PKS 0528+134 had much less success in reproducing the optical spectral shape (Mukherjee et al. \ 1999). The observed optical emission was significantly harder than that of 3C 279 while the model parameters used to fit the PKS 0528+134 SEDs were consistent with cooling predominantly by SSC emission. This suggests that some other cooling mechanism controls the particle evolution, or as was proposed by Mukherjee et al., that some additional processes, such as particle re-acceleration downstream in the bulk outflow, are occurring.

sources.summary: The paper gives a 15 GHz core circular-polarization limit for 0528 + 134, contrasts it with strong 1996 detections, and notes that the source was then in outburst.

quotes.quote: 0528 + 134: We find a limit of |m_c| < 0.18 in the core at 15 GHz in February of 2003. HW99 detected multiple epochs of significant circular polarization at about the m_c = -0.5 level in the core at 15 GHz during 1996. During 1996, 0528 + 134 was undergoing a large outburst, peaking with an integrated core flux 4.6 times larger than observed in February 2003.

gmw_00001694.overview: The active galactic nucleus 0954+658 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1901.10731, arXiv:1005.2177] that features a bent jet structure [arXiv:1005.2177, arXiv:astro-ph/0612149] and a companion galaxy [arXiv:1005.2177]. It exhibits extreme optical and radio flaring [arXiv:2102.04520], rapid intraday variability [arXiv:2310.11108, arXiv:astro-ph/0612149], and highly variable polarization [arXiv:2310.05104, arXiv:2102.04520].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 0954+658 has an inverted-to-flat radio core spectrum, sharply fading extended emission, limited reliable core-shift measurements, and inferred magnetic-field properties.

quotes.quote: In Fig. 0954spix we show the spectral index maps for 0954+658 from 4.6--43 GHz. We observe a strongly inverted spectrum from 4.6--22 GHz in the core region (Fig. 0954spix a, b, ), which then flattens considerably at the highest frequencies (Fig. 0954spix d). We note the spectral index gradient across the jet in the 4.6--7.9 GHz image, which is discussed in . The region of inverted spectrum at 3 mas from the core in the 8.9--12.9 GHz map (Fig. 0954spix b) may also be connected with the change in the jet direction in the plane of the sky. The extended emission in this source falls off sharply with increasing frequency, which is why we could not obtain core-shift measurements at frequencies higher than 8.9 GHz.

We obtained two reliable core-shift measurements for the jet of 0954+658, with the angle of the shifts ( 127^ ) consistent with the inner jet direction at 43 GHz of 59^ (Table 11). In this case, we assume a value of k_r=1 from 4.6--43 GHz due to our lack of measurements, and calculate the shifts with which to align our images from this. The spectral index maps in Fig. 0954spix indicate that this is a reasonable assumption between 4.6 and 43 GHz, providing a flat to inverted core spectrum with a steep spectrum jet as expected.

sources.summary: The paper follows S4 0954+658 through a 2011 multi-waveband outburst, finding rapid optical brightening, a >300-degree polarization rotation, a new superluminal knot, and additional links between jet ejecta and optical flares.

quotes.quote: We present the results of optical ( R band) photometric and polarimetric monitoring and Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) imaging of the blazar S4 0954+658 , along with Fermi ray data during a multi-waveband outburst in 2011 March-April. After a faint state with a brightness level R 17.6 mag registered in the first half of January 2011, the optical brightness of the source started to rise and reached 14.8 mag during the middle of March, showing flare-like behavior. The most spectacular case of intranight variability was observed during the night of 2011 March 9, when the blazar brightened by 0.7 mag within 7 hours. During the rise of the flux the position angle of optical polarization rotated smoothly over more than 300 . At the same time, within 1 uncertainty a new superluminal knot appeared with an apparent speed of 19.0 0.3 .

We have very strong evidence for association of this knot with the multi-waveband outburst in 2011 March-April. We also analyze the multi-frequency behavior of S4 0954+658 during a number of minor outbursts from August 2008 to April 2012. We find some evidence of connections between at least two more superluminal ejecta and near-simultaneous optical flares.

sources.summary: The paper treats S5 0954+658 as an optically active BL Lac, observed from low to high states with strong flares and variability attributed to injected relativistic electrons.

quotes.quote: S5 0954+658 S5 0954+658 is a BL Lac object with a claimed redshift of z=0.367 (Stickel et al. \ 1993); however, Landoni et al. \ (2015) found a featureless spectrum and a limit of z > 0.45 , which is based on non-detection of the host galaxy. This source is optically active and its optical variability was first studied by Wagner et al. \ (1993). Raiteri et al. \ (1999) studied a four year long optical light curve and detected fast large amplitude variations.

The source was in a low optical state during the period MJD 6400--7000, with the mean magnitude of this source during this period 17. After this period, the source flux increased significantly and reached up to an R magnitude of 13 as was also reported by Bachev (2015). Until MJD=7600 this source displayed many flare-like events. There also was a strong flare peaking around MJD=7810 which was only caught in V band measurements; it involved a rise of 2 mag over 100 days and a more rapid decline.

Hence, the variability of S5 0954+658 can be interpreted in terms of a non-thermal variable component that has a continuous injection of relativistic electrons with a steep power-law energy distribution of p 4.64.

sources.summary: The paper monitors BL Lac S4 0954+65 during its 2022 high state, finding historic optical and ray maxima, extreme intraday variability from tiny jet subregions, and polarization behavior shaped by geometry and intrinsic processes.

quotes.quote: We have presented the photometric and polarimetric optical data obtained by the WEBT Collaboration during a very active phase of the BL Lac-type object S4 0954+65 in 2022, together with the ray data from the Fermi satellite. In this period the source reached its historical brightness maxima in both optical and ray band. Many unprecedented, extreme episodes of optical intraday variability were detected. A model fit to one of the fastest flares implies a variability time scale of about 17 min and thus a size of the optical emitting region less than 3 10^ 14 cm (about 10^ 4 parsec). This means that we are likely observing emission coming from jet sub-regions, confirming earlier suggestions arising from the detection of minute-scale variability also at GeV shukla2020 and TeV energies aharonian2007,albert2007 .

We have analysed the behaviour of the optical polarization, which shows large fluctuations in both the polarization degree P and angle, and a general anticorrelation between P and the optical flux density F_R . The presence of this defined trend together with strong dispersion around it may indicate that we are seeing the combination of different processes. We have shown that if the long-term trend of the optical flux density is due to a variation of the Doppler factor caused by orientation changes of the jet emitting region, then a simple helical magnetic field in a possibly rotating jet can well describe the observed average behaviour of P .

sources.summary: The paper reports optical circular-polarization observations of the BL Lac blazar S4 0954+65, giving strict nondetection limits and constraints on jet magnetic field and pair composition.

quotes.quote: Here we report on the first observations of the BL Lac type object S4 0954+65 in high linear polarized states. While no circular polarization was detected, we were able to place upper limits of <0.5 at the 99.7 confidence. Using a simple model and our novel optical circular polarization observations we can constrain the allowed parameter space for the magnetic field strength and composition of the emitting particles. Our results favor models that require magnetic field strengths of only a few Gauss and models where the jet composition is dominated by electron-positron pairs.

Here we report on the first OCP observations of the LSP S4 0954+65 has a synchrotron peak frequency of _ syn =1.1 10^ 13 Hz Ajello2020 BL Lac-type Object S4 0954+65. S4 0954+65 sits at RA=09h58m47.24s , Dec=+65 ^o 33 ^ 54.81 ^ and z=0.367. It is one of the few LSP blazars that has been detected in TeV rays MAGIC2018 , often showing a very high degree ( >30 ) of optical linear polarization (OLP, e.g., Blinov2021,Raiteri2021 ).
Our observations provide strict upper limits below <0.5 at the 3 level.

sources.summary: The paper gives millimeter polarimetric monitoring results for 0954+658, including detection frequency, polarization behavior, flux evolution, variability, and rotation-measure constraints.

quotes.quote: 0954+658 This BL Lacertae object, located at z 0.37 , shows significant polarization in 35 out of 37 measurements obtained between March 2007 and January 2011. The average polarization level is m_L 7.4 . 0954+658 shows by far the highest degrees of polarization among our sources, ranging up to m_L 18 in 2007. In 2009, the polarization drops to m_L 5 and increases again up to m_L 10 in 2010/2011. The polarization angles remain around 0^ for most of the observing timeline; the only notable exception occurs in 2009 when values of 10...30^ occur. The degree of polarization fluctuates at a rate of _m 0.5 d ^ 1 and a normalized rate _m/ m_L 0.07 d ^ 1 , respectively.

The average of the 3-mm flux density is S_ 1.2 Jy.
