The flux varies remains within the range S_ 0.5...1.5 Jy throughout the observing timeline, the only exception being a short peak with S_ 2.5 Jy occurring mid-2008. The flux fluctuation rate is _S 0.1 Jy d ^ 1 , corresponding to a normalized fluctuation rate of _S/ S_ 0.1 d ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper flags PCCS1 G145.78+43.13 as a BL Lac with extreme polarization variability, coincident optical and radio flaring, changing spectral index, sharply reduced polarization fraction, and RM rising to 18300 rad m^-2.

quotes.quote: PCCS1 G145.78+43.13 is a notable outlier in polarization fraction variation ( >46 ). The total intensity and polarization fraction (in percent) light curves for this source are shown in Fig. Fi:PCCS34_lc . It underwent an exceptionally high optical flare in 2015 (MJD = 57067), coinciding with the emergence of a new knot detected by the VLBA-BU-Blazar Monitoring Project 2018A&A...617A..30M . Our first observing epoch of this source happened to coincide with the optical flare, and the polarization fraction measured at this epoch of 13.6 agrees with the VLBA measurement of 10 . In the most recent epoch, 28/01/2019, although the total intensity flux density as measured with the VLA has stayed relatively constant at 1.5 Jy, the total intensity spectral index has changed dramatically from +0.06 to +0.62 ; OVRO and VLBA monitoring data confirm that the source is undergoing a radio flare.

The polarization fraction has decreased to 0.7 , in line with the VLBA polarization values; the polarization fraction spectral index has steepened significantly from +0.06 to 0.92 ; and the RM has significantly changed from 440 to 18300 rad m ^ 2 . These changes could be attributed to the integrated flux density during the flare state containing a significant contribution from the emerging knot of plasma.

sources.summary: The paper studies TeV blazar S4 0954 + 65 with 2017-2023 optical photometry and polarimetry, finding large optical brightness changes, no strong spectral variations, variable polarization, and no clear flux-polarization correlation.

quotes.quote: To investigate the extreme variability properties of the TeV blazar S4 0954 + 65 using optical photometric and polarization observations carried out between 2017--2023 using 3 ground-based telescopes. We examined an extensive dataset comprised of 138 intraday (observing duration shorter than a day) light curves (LCs) of S4 0954 + 65 for flux, spectral, and polarization variations on diverse timescales.

During the course of our observing campaign, the source brightness changed by 4 magnitudes in V and R bands; however, we did not find any strong spectral variations. The slope of the relative spectral energy distribution was 1.37 0.04. The degree of polarization varied from 3 to 39 during our monitoring. We observed a change of 120 degrees in polarization angle within 3 hr on 2022 April 13. No clear correlation was found between optical flux and the degree of polarization.

sources.summary: The paper reports MAGIC very-high-energy detection of S4 0954+65 during an exceptional optical flare, discussing its uncertain blazar classification, redshift, jet activity, and broadband emission.

quotes.quote: The very-high-energy (VHE, 100 GeV) MAGIC observations of the blazar 4, were triggered by an exceptionally high flux state of emission in the optical. This blazar has a disputed redshift of z =0.368 or z 0.45 and an uncertain classification among blazar subclasses. The exceptional source state described here makes for an excellent opportunity to understand physical processes in the jet of 4 and thus contribute to its classification.

The optical high brightness state of February 2015, presented here, is however exceptional for the object, with a brightening of more than 3 magnitudes in the R-band with respect to the average monitored state http://users.utu.fi/kani/1m/S4 0954+65.html . This not only spurred many alerts in the community see ATel 6996, 7001, 7057, 7083, 7093; atel6996,atel7001,atel7057,atel7083,atel7093 , but also the first and only detection of the object at very high energies (VHE, E 100 GeV), thanks to observations by the MAGIC Telescopes. This detection by MAGIC and the MWL data collected alongside it are the focus of the present work.

sources.summary: The paper studies S4 0954+65 with TESS and WEBT, finding variable optical time-scales, radio-optical delays, polarization changes, month-scale quasi-periodicity, and a rotating helical jet interpretation.

quotes.quote: We here investigate the case of the BL Lac object S4 0954+65 with data acquired in 2019--2020 by the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite ( TESS ) and by the Whole Earth Blazar Telescope (WEBT) Collaboration. The 2-min cadence optical light curves provided by TESS during three observing sectors of nearly one month each, allow us to study the fast variability in great detail. We identify several characteristic short-term time-scales, ranging from a few hours to a few days. However, these are not persistent, as they differ in the various TESS sectors. The long-term photometric and polarimetric optical and radio monitoring undertaken by the WEBT brings significant additional information, revealing that i) in the optical, long-term flux changes are almost achromatic, while the short-term ones are strongly chromatic; ii) the radio flux variations at 37 GHz follow those in the optical with a delay of about three weeks; iii) the range of variation of the polarization degree and angle is much larger in the optical than in the radio band, but the mean polarization angles are similar; iv) the optical long-term variability is characterized by a quasi-periodicity of about one month.
We explain the source behaviour in terms of a rotating inhomogeneous helical jet, whose pitch angle can change in time.

sources.summary: The paper says 0954+658 has a compact radio structure, a W-NW VLBI jet, no reliable intermediate-scale component, and an apparently large misalignment likely amplified by projection and beaming.

quotes.quote: 0954+658 : this object has a relatively small size, even on the arcsecond-scale. With a PA=74 it belongs to the B group of Fig. fig1 . The VLBI image (Fig. 0954e ) confirms the presence of a jet in the W-NW direction, slightly resolved, but there are no evident components on the intermediate scale. The weak structure near the core in the MERLIN image (Fig. 0954m ) appears not reliable.

The small size of the source on the arcsecond-scale and the absence of intermediate scale components lead to a jet slightly bent, with a high PA due to projection effects. The lack of (visible) jet at the intermediate scale can be explained by beaming effects: we see the parsec and kpc-scale jet, while the intermediate scale jet is not detectable because it is highly Doppler boosted away from the line of sight.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0954+658 as a BL Lac object behind a CO cloud, places it on the cloud edge, and links a rapid ESE to a turbulent boundary layer.

quotes.quote: Due to the existence of a CO-HLC near the IDV sources 0917+624 and 0954+658 we first used the 230 GHz SIS-receiver and were able to detect the CO(2-1)-line in the direction of the BL Lac object 0954+658.
The map is centered on the position of 0954+658. A CO cloud eastern of the IDV source is seen extending in the north-south direction. 0954+658 is located in the outer western part of the cloud.

In March 2000 an even more rapid ESE in 0954+658 has been observed (Cim o et al. 2002). In view of our recent findings such event suggests a very clumpy and extremely turbulent outer envelop or boundary layer around the CO cloud in Figure 1.

sources.summary: The paper detects optical quasi-periodicities in the blazar S4 0954+658 with TESS and favors an origin in the inner accretion disk near its black hole.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of several quasi-periodicities around 0.6--2.5 days in the optical emission of the blazar S4 0954+658. The source was observed by the Transiting Exoplanet Survey Satellite (TESS) in six sectors and it showed these features in all but one of them, with a QPO of 1.52 days apparently present in portions of four of them. We used the generalized Lomb-Scargle periodogram method to search for significant signals and we confirmed them using a weighted wavelet transform for time-frequency domain analyses. We discuss several possible explanations for these rapid quasi-periodic variations and suggest that an origin in the innermost part of the accretion disk is most likely. Within this framework, we provide estimates for the mass of the black hole at the core of this blazar.

sources.summary: The paper classifies 0954+658 as a pre-merger BL Lac with a companion, optical periodicity, high-energy variability, bent jets, and ambiguous post-merger status.

quotes.quote: * 0954+658 (BL Lac, z=0.368 ): Besides its companion (at a projected separation 474 kpc), this source exhibits periodicity in the optical, and variability in rays. It has a bent jet on both VLA and VLBA scales. This is a misaligned source. The nature of this object is ambiguous, as it may also be classified as being in the post-merger phase.

sources.summary: The paper presents multi-wavelength observations of S4 0954+65 during its 2015 optical flare, finding hard GeV emission, X-ray softening, MAGIC VHE detection, and an inverse-Compton interpretation.

quotes.quote: BL Lacertae objects: individual (S4 0954+65) We report on Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) and multi-wavelength results on the recently-discovered very-high-energy (VHE, E>100 GeV) blazar \ ( z=0.368 ) during an exceptionally bright optical flare in 2015 February. During the time period (2015 February, 13/14, or MJD 57067) when the MAGIC telescope detected VHE ray emission from the source, the \ data indicated a significant spectral hardening at GeV energies, with a power-law photon index of 1.8 0.1 --compared with the 3FGL value (averaged over four years of observation) of 2.34 0.04 . In contrast, /XRT data showed a softening of the X-ray spectrum, with a photon index of 1.72 0.08 (compared with 1.38 0.03 averaged during the flare from MJD 57066 to 57077), possibly indicating a modest contribution of synchrotron photons by the highest-energy electrons superposed on the inverse Compton component. Fitting of the quasi-simultaneous ( <1 day) broadband spectrum with a one-zone synchrotron plus inverse-Compton model revealed that GeV/TeV emission could be produced by inverse-Compton scattering of external photons from the dust torus.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0954+658 as a BL Lac intraday-variable source with bent jet structure and VLBI speeds up to 9; it showed stronger 22 GHz variability, optical activity, and optical spectral flattening during a flare.

quotes.quote: 0954+658 This BL Lac object is another famous IDV source ( 1993A&A...271..344W,1995ARA&A..33..163W,2000MNRAS.315..229G ). The jet structure shows many bends from milliarcsecond to arcsecond scales and speeds of up to 9 were measured with VLBI ( 1996MNRAS.283..759G,1992AJ....104.1687K ). The long-term radio light curve (Fig. fig:lc ) displays some variability, which is more pronounced at 22 GHz, while greater activity characterizes the optical behaviour (Fig. fig:olc ). This becomes even more evident in the SEDs, where in addition one can notice the flattening of the optical spectrum during the flare of January 2005 (Fig. fig:SEDs ).

sources.summary: The paper describes S4 0954 + 65 as a TeV blazar whose 2007 X-ray spectra are simple power laws with photon index 1.96.

quotes.quote: S4 0954 + 65: This source is a TeV blazar. Tanaka et al. (2016) analyzed Swift/XRT observations of this source in optical flaring state and found softening of the X-ray spectrum, with a photon index of 1.7 (compared to the earlier outburst with 1.38) possibly indicating a modest contribution of synchrotron emission by the highest-energy electrons superposed on the inverse Compton component. We analyzed the X-ray spectra of this blazar on two occasions in 2007 and found them to be well described with simple power law model with photon index 1.96. Free N_ H in the fitting models did not alter the results.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 0954 + 658 as a BL Lac object with intrinsic year-scale radio variations, a prior extreme scattering event, and typical 2 GHz scintillation.

quotes.quote: 0954 + 658 This BL Lac object shows 10-20 variations over a year at 8 and 2 GHz, with the time delay from high to low frequencies characteristic for intrinsic bursts. It is well known since it produced the best and most definitive ESE in 1981 (Fiedler et al., 1987a). Our analysis also shows that it clearly exhibits typical 2 GHz ISS on a timescale of 4 days at 3.4 scintillation index.

gmw_00001695.overview: 1418+546 is a BL Lacertae object at a redshift of approximately z = 0.15 [arXiv:1712.06314, arXiv:1202.4209] that exhibits a disturbed morphology indicative of a post-merger galaxy [arXiv:1005.2177]. The source features a polarized compact core [arXiv:2310.08989], a western radio hotspot [arXiv:2310.08989], and a southeast-directed VLBI jet [arXiv:1712.06314, arXiv:astro-ph/0111279].

sources.summary: The paper describes 1418 + 546/OQ 530 as a z=0.153 BL Lac with a radio halo and lobe, plus a southeast VLBI jet lacking rapid superluminal motion.

quotes.quote: 1418 + 546 (J1419 + 5423) 1418 + 546 (OQ 530) is a BL Lac source at a redshift of z = 0.153 2012ApJ...748...49S . It was not detected by the EGRET, but detected by Fermi /LAT (3FGL J1419.9+5425) with 20.1 2015ApJS..218...23A . The 1.6-GHz VLA map displays a one-sided kpc-scale halo with a component west of the compact core at a separation of about 32 1993MNRAS.264..298M . The diffuse halo was also detected in the 1.4-GHz VLA D-array image 1999A&AS..139..601C . The western lobe was confirmed by the 1.4-GHz observations made by the VLA in its B-array configuration and the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WRST) 1999A&AS..139..601C .

However, the VLBI images revealed a single-sided jet to the southeast, in an opposite direction of the kpc-scale lobe: the 5-GHz VLBA image (Fig. fig:1418+546 a) shows the jet extending to the southeast to approximately 25 mas 2007ApJ...658..203H ; the 15-GHz VLBA image Fig. fig:1418+546 b, 2016AJ....152...12L shows consistent morphology with the 5-GHz image. The VLBA structure does not show rapid structural change or superluminal motion. A maximum jet speed of 0.93 0.27 was determined based on the 15-GHz data over a time baseline of 18 years from 1995 to 2013 2016AJ....152...12L .

sources.summary: The paper describes PG1418+546/OQ530 as a polarized, variable BL Lac; uGMRT imaging finds a compact core and western hotspot consistent with FRII-like morphology.

quotes.quote: PG1418+546 a.k.a. OQ +530 This source, also known as OQ530, is a radio-selected BL Lac and a member of the 1 Jy BL Lac sample Kuehr1981 . It is highly polarized at optical and near-infrared frequencies Mead1990 . During the periods when the source displays high polarization Marchenko1985 have found that this source might have a preferred polarization direction of near 120 . This blazar is intraday optically variable Heidt1996 and has also displayed optical microvariability Miller1991 . It is an LSP blazar Lister2018, Ajello2022 .

Our uGMRT 650 MHz image (Figure fig5:PG1418 ) also shows a polarized compact core and a hotspot to the west, consistent with an FRII morphology. The spectral index image shows both the core and the hotspot to be relatively flat. The inferred B-field is indicative of shocked regions with the fields aligned with the edges in the hotspot region.

sources.summary: The paper gives millimeter polarimetric monitoring results for 1418+546, including detection frequency, polarization behavior, flux evolution, variability, and rotation-measure constraints.

quotes.quote: 1418+546 (OQ+530) For this BL Lacertae object located at z 0.15 , 52 out of 76 polarimetric observations, obtained between January 2008 and January 2011, detected linearly polarized emission. The average degree of polarization is m_L 5.0 . Polarization levels vary in the range m_L 3...10 with a tendency for the highest values to occur at the beginning and the lowest values to be found at the end of the observed period. The degree of polarization fluctuates at rates of _m 0.3 d ^ 1 and _m/ m_L 0.06 d ^ 1 , respectively. The angles of polarization are located in the range 110...150^ . From inspecting the corresponding time--frequency diagram (Fig. fig_polplane ) we note a ``swing pattern'' for the polarization angles, with the highest values of to be found around the beginning of 2010.

The 3-mm lightcurve shows a double-peak morphology, with values ranging from S_ 0.5 Jy at the beginnings of 2009 and 2011 to S_ 1.3 Jy at the beginnings of 2008 and 2010. The average value of the flux is S_ 0.8 Jy. The flux density fluctuates with _S 0.04 Jy d ^ 1 , the normalized fluctuation rate being _S/ S_ 0.05 d ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J1419+5423 as an AGN radio source with a compact core, southwest jet components, and improved three-frequency synthesis maps of its jet structure.

quotes.quote: (3) J1419+5423. This source is represented by observational data simultaneously at three frequencies: 5, 8.4, and 15.3 GHz (see Table 1). As a result, we have the possibility of three-frequency synthesis. The intensity maps obtained at individual frequencies are shown in the upper panel of Fig. 6. As can be seen, the source consists of a compact core and an extended jet extending southwestward of the core to a distance of about 25 mas.

The compact features of the jet at distances of about 5 and 18 mas from the core are clearly seen on the maps constructed from the data at 5 and 8.4 GHz. The map constructed from the data at 15.3 GHz has a higher angular resolution and clearly shows the inner component of the jet at a distance of about 1 mas from the core, but, at the same time, the component at 5 mas is barely seen and the component at 18 mas is completely invisible.

sources.summary: The paper finds 1418+546 has an inverted-to-flat core spectrum, steeper jet emission at higher frequencies, core-shifts consistent with equipartition, and an acceleration scale suitable for VLBI monitoring.

quotes.quote: Fig. 1418spix shows the spectral index distribution from 4.6--43 GHz for the source 1418+546. We find an inverted core spectrum between the lower frequency pairs (Fig. 1418spix a, b, ) with a flat spectrum core region between 22 and 43 GHz (Fig. 1418spix d). In the 4.6--7.9 GHz map (Fig. 1418spix a) the jet spectral index is 0.2, while at higher frequencies it becomes steeper with a typical value of 0.5 (Fig. 1418spix b, ). The best fit line to the measured shifts listed in Table 8 gives a y-intercept of 0.003 0.004 assuming k_r=1 and the slope provides a characteristic _ =3.9 0.2 pc GHz (Fig. 12). The average position angle of the shift is 52 13^ , consistent within the errors with the inner jet direction at 43 GHz of 137^ .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1418+546 as a post-merger galaxy candidate, citing disturbed morphology, transitory colors, multiwavelength periodicity, radio quasi-periodicity, and infrared/optical variability.

quotes.quote: * 1418+546 (OQ 530) (BL Lac, z=0.151 ): This object has a disturbed morphology. That it is positioned in the transitionary region of the color-color diagram and it exhibits periodicities in different wavelengths are both indicative of a post-merger galaxy. Its VLBI jet does not exhibit bends. It shows almost periodical variability in the radio. It is also variable in the infrared and optical.

sources.summary: The paper says 1418+546 has a straight southeast VLBI jet that bends sharply on intermediate scales, with the misalignment explained as projected smooth curvature.

quotes.quote: 1418+546 : in this case PA=150 . The VLBI image (Fig. 1418e ) shows a straight jet directed toward the SE direction. On the intermediate scale (Fig. 1418m ) this source shows a sharp bending perpendicular to the VLBI jet direction. For this source, as in the previous one, the misalignment and the structure are easily explained with a smoothly bending jet, whose curvature is enhanced by the projection.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1418+546 as a blazar with a bent rotation-measure profile and higher-frequency evidence for a reversed, much weaker profile consistent with relativistic motion.

quotes.quote: OSul-Gabu:09 present profiles for six blazars, as measured in a variety of frequency bands. In at least two cases the profiles display the bent shape characteristic of helical motion: 1156+295 and 1418+546. In 1418+546 this is seen clearly in a profile constructed from observations between 4.6 and 8.9 . At higher frequencies, the profile shows evidence of being reversed, though the absolute is reduced by nearly two orders of magnitude, again consistent with the presence of relativistic motion. In contrast, at 4.6 - 8.9 , 1156+295 exhibits a nearly linear profile, while at 7.9 - 15.4 , the profile becomes dramatically bent. At higher frequencies ( 12.9 - 43 ), it becomes roughly linear again, which may be evidence of reduced u^z at small radii, a particularly fortuitous view of the transition to a reversed profile, or failure to fully resolve the gradient.

gmw_00001696.overview: 1637+574 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.75 [arXiv:1202.4209]. Between February 2007 and August 2009, the galaxy exhibited polarization in 17 out of 26 measurements alongside a variable 3-mm flux density [arXiv:1202.4209].

sources.summary: The paper gives millimeter polarimetric monitoring results for 1637+574, including detection frequency, polarization behavior, flux evolution, variability, and rotation-measure constraints.

quotes.quote: 1637+574 (OS+562) This Seyfert 1 galaxy, located at z 0.75 , shows significant polarization in 17 out of 26 measurements obtained between February 2007 and August 2009. The average polarization level is m_L 3.0 . Degrees of polarization are found in the range m_L 1...5 , with the lowest values occurring at the beginning of the observed period. The angles of polarization swing from 150^ to 90^ . The degree of polarization fluctuates at a rate of _m 0.2 d ^ 1 and a normalized rate _m/ m_L 0.05 d ^ 1 , respectively.

The 3-mm flux density is initially observed at values S_ 1.2 Jy, increases up to S_ 2 Jy in 2008 and decreases again to S_ 1 Jy in 2009. The average flux is S_ 1.2 Jy. The flux fluctuation rate is _S 0.17 Jy d ^ 1 , corresponding to a normalized fluctuation rate of _S/ S_ 0.12 d ^ 1 .

gmw_00001697.name: J0902+0443
gmw_00001697.overview: J0902+0443 is a radio-loud Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:1801.03519, arXiv:1809.01558] characterized by a flat-spectrum radio emission [arXiv:1801.03519] and flux variability [arXiv:1801.03519]. It features a partially resolved, elongated structure extending 6.5 kiloparsecs to the southeast [arXiv:1801.03519] and hosts a high star formation rate [arXiv:1801.03519, arXiv:1809.01558].

sources.summary: The source remains flat-spectrum, has high flux and luminosity, shows significant variability, and has a partially resolved south-east structure with weak star-formation contribution.

quotes.quote: J0902+0443 SDSS J090227.16+044309.5 was included in the F sample by Foschini15 , showing a broad-band spectral index of 0.07 0.01. Our results confirm this classification. The source is one of the few showing a flux larger than 100 mJy, with a luminosity of 6 10 ^ 42 . A significant flux variability ( 10 mJy) was found by previous measurements at 5 GHz with the NRAO 91m telescope Becker91, Griffith95, Yuan08 , and is also consistent with our result. Our map, shown in Fig. fig:J0902p0443 shows a partially resolved structure of 6.5 kpc at PA 142 ^ , in the South-East direction.

An elongated structure was already visible on VLBA scales, although its direction appears to be slightly different Gu15 . The source was detected at 22 GHz, but not at 37 GHz. This source has a very high star formation rate ( 500 M _ yr ^ 1 ), which however is not strongly contributing to the radio emission, as suggesting by the q22 = -0.65.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0902+0443 as a representative radio-loud NLS1 with high radio power, high black-hole mass, high inferred star formation, and nondetected APEX submillimeter emission.

quotes.quote: The two objects are : J0902+0443 (z=0.532) and J2021-2235 (z=0.185). J0902+0443 is among the most powerful radio NLS1 sources of the sample, it has a radio power of 1.1x10 ^ 26 W/Hz, its black hole mass is 5x10 ^7 M _ sun , and also it has one of the highest star formation rate (SFR 500 M _ sun /year) according to the estimate of Cac15.

None of the objects was detected at 3 , and perhaps the reason lies in the fact that the expected RMS was not achieved. Nevertheless, upper limits may also be very relevant to constrain some scenarios. We present the SED of these 2 objects in Figure fig2 . In both cases it is clear that the emission in the sub-millimeter regime is not dominated by the relativistic jet since the upper limits are well below the extrapolation to the sub-millimeter band of the non-thermal emission observed in the radio domain.

gmw_00001698.name: J2021-2235
gmw_00001698.overview: J2021-2235 is a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1807.08953, arXiv:1809.01558] hosted by a late-type disk galaxy [arXiv:1807.08953]. It belongs to an interacting system of two galaxies [arXiv:1807.08953, arXiv:1809.01558] and exhibits a high estimated star formation rate [arXiv:1809.01558].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IRAS 20181-2244 as a jetted NLS1 in an interacting two-galaxy system, favoring a late-type disk host with red nuclear color and high star formation.

quotes.quote: In this work we present the results of a morphological analysis of the host galaxy of the jetted NLS1 IRAS 20181-2244 observed with the 6.5m Baade Telescope of the Las Campanas Observatory. The GALFIT analysis ran on the Ks image, along with additional spectroscopic observations performed with the Nordic Optical Telescope, clearly revealed the presence of an interacting system of two galaxies. The data suggest that this NLS1 is hosted by a late-type galaxy, although the result is not conclusive.

Initially classified as a Seyfert 2 Elizalde94 , it was reclassified as a NLS1 by Halpern98 . Its optical B-band magnitude derived from the USNO-B catalog Monet03 is m _ B = 16.86, which provides an absolute magnitude of M _ B = - 22.81. Hence, IRAS 20181-2244 could formally be classified as a narrow-line type 1 quasar Schmidt83 . However, to avoid confusion with similar sources in the literature, in the following we will stick to the NLS1 classification.

sources.summary: The paper presents J2021-2235 as a representative radio-loud NLS1 with moderate radio power, high inferred star formation, interaction-system membership, and nondetected APEX submillimeter emission.

quotes.quote: The two objects are : J0902+0443 (z=0.532) and J2021-2235 (z=0.185). J0902+0443 is among the most powerful radio NLS1 sources of the sample, it has a radio power of 1.1x10 ^ 26 W/Hz, its black hole mass is 5x10 ^7 M _ sun , and also it has one of the highest star formation rate (SFR 500 M _ sun /year) according to the estimate of Cac15. Not equally extreme, J2021-2235 (z=0.185) has a radio power of 2x10 ^ 24 W/Hz, and an estimated SFR of 300 M _ sun /year (Cac15). One of the most interesting characteristics of this object is that it belongs to an interacting system ( ).

None of the objects was detected at 3 , and perhaps the reason lies in the fact that the expected RMS was not achieved. Nevertheless, upper limits may also be very relevant to constrain some scenarios. We present the SED of these 2 objects in Figure fig2 . In both cases it is clear that the emission in the sub-millimeter regime is not dominated by the relativistic jet since the upper limits are well below the extrapolation to the sub-millimeter band of the non-thermal emission observed in the radio domain.

sources.summary: The paper describes J2021 2235 as a radio-loud NLS1 with high radio luminosity, small black-hole mass, steep spectrum, compact kpc-scale core, and interaction with a nearby galaxy.

quotes.quote: J2021 2235 This target is one of the RL NLS1s in the sample with a flux density of S_ int 9.47 mJy. It is a radio bright source having L_ int 40.70, which is consistent with previous study Komossa2006 . The black hole mass is relatively small with (M_ BH / M_ ) 6.65, which lies in the range of (M_ BH / M_ ) 6.48-7.57 according to different methods Komossa2006,Foschini2015 . It has an associated counterpart of NVSS J202104 223520 at 1.4 GHz with a flux density of S_ 1.4 24.6 mJy and a luminosity of L_ 1.4 40.50. The spectral slope is steep with _ in-band 0.95 and _ 1.4-5.5 0.70.

sources.summary: The paper finds only weak evidence for spectral features in IRAS 20181-224, with slight improvements from broad absorption, narrow emission, or thermal-plasma models.

quotes.quote: There may be an emission or absorption feature in the spectrum of IRAS 20181-224, but the improvement in the fit upon adding either is rather small. A broad absorption line gives a slight improvement in the fit, as noted in Table 4, but adding one or two edges in the range 0.6--3 keV gives a significantly a worse fit. A narrow Gaussian emission line at 1.11 0.05 keV also slightly improves the fit ( ^ 2 / =438/387 ). The mekal model gives a similar result with a plasma temperature of kT= 1.7^ +0.6 _ 0.3 keV.

gmw_00001699.name: AT20GJ040848-750720
gmw_00001699.overview: AT20GJ040848-750720 is a narrow-line Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.69 [arXiv:1907.00299, arXiv:2308.10071]. It features a faint core and two bright, highly depolarized radio lobes [arXiv:1907.00299], and it exhibits a young stellar population and a far-infrared excess [arXiv:2308.10071].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AT20GJ040848-750720 as a resolved FR II radio galaxy with excess high-frequency image-peak flux, a second higher-frequency component, polarised emission, and depolarized bright lobes.

quotes.quote: In particular, there is one object, AT20GJ040848-750720, for which the excess of flux density (with respect to UVFLUX) measured in correspondence of the peak in the image reaches 361 at 33 GHz and 352 at 38 GHz. We show images for this source (see Fig. fig:j0408 ) at all the 5 frequencies at which they could be obtained in total intensity. Polarised emissions are instead displayed by contour levels. It can be seen that the object is marginally resolved at the lower frequencies. At the higher frequencies a second component appears and the polarised emission mainly comes from it.
According to Morganti et al. (1999) AT20GJ0408-7507 is a bright FRII radio galaxy at z 0.7 , dominated by two bright lobes. Both lobes have high depolarization, slightly higher in the eastern one.

sources.summary: The paper discusses AT20GJ040848-750720 as a z~0.69 FR-II source resolved by ALMA into bright depolarized lobes and a faint core.

quotes.quote: AT20GJ040848-750720 . This is an FR-II source at z 0.69 . It was unresolved by ATCA at 20 GHz, although the centroid in polarization was slightly offset from that in total intensity. The ALMA image (with 0.5 arcsec resolution) shows that the emission is dominated by two bright lobes ( cf. Fig. fig:AT20GJ040848-750720_composed ). Both exhibit a high depolarization, slightly higher in the eastern one.
The latter also shows a double structure in the polarized emission. The core sits midway of the two lobes and is quite faint, i.e. 1.5 mJy ( cf. Fig. fig:AT20GJ040848-750720_composed ).

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 0409-75 as a powerful radio source whose GMOS-S morphology shows disturbed structure, companions, shells, fans, dust, tails, bridges, or multiple nuclei indicating interaction or merger activity.

quotes.quote: This NLRG/FRII with evidence for a YSP based on its optical spectrum Holt07 , FIR excess and infrared colours Dicken09 , is the most distant object in our sample. Our GMOS-S i' band image reveals a secondary nucleus of similar brightness to the radio galaxy 8 kpc to the East along PA=84 (Figure pks0409_online ). Note that the axis of the double nucleus is misaligned by 40 degrees from the axis of the large-scale radio structure (PA=124 ). The double nucleus system lies in a crowded field, and there are several galaxies within a 20 radius.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS0409-75 as the most distant and radio-powerful 2Jy source, with LABOCA flux following the radio SED and lobes likely contributing to long-wavelength far-infrared flux.

quotes.quote: Indeed, the NRLG/FRII PKS0409-75 ( z = 0.693 ) is both the most distant radio source and the most powerful at radio wavelengths in the 2Jy sample. Although not observed with SPIRE, the LABOCA sub-mm flux falls directly on a power-law extrapolation of the high frequency radio SED for this object. Because of the relatively small angular extent of the source (largest angular size = 9 arcsec or 85 kpc), which is dominated by two lobes of emission morganti99 , we might expect the radio lobes to contribute at the longer far-IR wavelengths. This emphasises that the powerful, steep-spectrum emission from the lobes of high-z radio galaxies can make a substantial flux contribution at the longer far-IR wavelengths, potentially compromising SFR and dust mass estimates based on fluxes measured at those wavelengths.

sources.summary: G4Jy 417 is described as a narrow-line FR II radio galaxy lacking a DSS optical counterpart, but with a mid-infrared counterpart, rich environment, young stars, and far-infrared excess.

quotes.quote: G4Jy 417: does not have an optical counterpart in the DSS image we retrieved from the archive, but it was identified with the same mid-IR counterpart listed in the G4Jy catalog in the literature hunstead71b,alvarez93 . However, we did not report any optical position and/or magnitude and we assigned it an IDF=4.1. G4Jy 417 (a.k.a. PKS 0409-752) also belongs to the MS4 catalog and it is classified as a narrow line radio galaxy with a typical FR II radio morphology at z= 0.694 alvarez93,tadhunter93,diserego94 . It is harbored in a galaxy-rich environment ramos11 and shows evidence for a young stellar population holt07 and a far infrared excess dicken09b .

gmw_00001700.name: z=2.9 LAE (field ID 1534)
gmw_00001700.overview: The z=2.9 LAE (field ID 1534) is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy characterized by a compact, bright core and an extended halo [arXiv:2311.07019]. This halo contains two tail-like regions and exhibits spatially varying double-peaked spectra [arXiv:2311.07019].

sources.summary: The paper reports the z=2.9 MAGPI LAE as an extended halo with a compact bright core, tail-like regions, strong blue peak emission, and spatially varying peak ratios.

quotes.quote: i) z=2.9 LAE (field ID 1534) shows a compact `continuum-like' bright core and an extended halo (Fig. FigGam1 , left panel in top row). Such spatial profiles are characterised by a `two-component model' see Wisotzki_16, Leclercq_17 . Two `tail-like' regions are identified within the halo. A faint continuum is also detected at the source location. Due to source crowding, faint continuum emissions from neighboring sources were also detected.

The halo displays spatially varying double-peaked spectra, with the brightest core featuring the strongest blue peak with F_ blue /F_ total = 0.74 ( EW _ blue,0 = 46.3 3.7 \ and EW _ red,0 = 14.4 1.7 ). Fig. FigGam1 (bottom row, left panel) presents a continuum-subtracted 1D profile of the core, extracted adopting a circular aperture of 0.8'' radius.

gmw_00001701.name: z = 3.6 LAE (field ID 2302)
gmw_00001701.overview: The z = 3.6 LAE (field ID 2302) is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy surrounded by an extended halo [arXiv:2311.07019]. This halo contains three spatially separated bright regions that all display double-peak emission, with the brightest core exhibiting strong blue peak emission [arXiv:2311.07019].

sources.summary: The paper describes the z=3.6 MAGPI LAE as an extended halo with three separated bright regions, strong blue emission in region A, and spatially varying peak dominance.

quotes.quote: ii) We find an extended halo around the z = 3.6 LAE (field ID 2302), showing three distinct bright regions labeled as A, B C (Fig. FigGam1 , top row middle panel), all displaying double-peak emission. Remarkably, these bright regions are spatially separated, a unique distribution seen in \ halos. Due to source faintness and low S/N, a continuum light was not immediately visible. However, after applying a median-filter to the data cube, faint continuum light at the brightest core's location is revealed.

Region A, the brightest core, displays prominent, strong blue peak emission with F_ blue /F_ total = 0.60 ( EW _ blue,0 = 47.2 13.0 \ and EW _ red,0 = 37.8 11.8 ) and is encircled with an aperture of 0.8'' radius. The entire halo shows a symmetric double-peaked profile with almost equal peak strengths.

gmw_00001702.name: LAE at z=4.8 (field ID 1208)
gmw_00001702.overview: The Lyman-alpha emitter at z=4.8 (field ID 1208) is a high-redshift galaxy with a compact spatial profile resembling a point source [arXiv:2311.07019]. Continuum light is detected at the location of its Lyman-alpha emission, which features a broader and stronger blue peak [arXiv:2311.07019].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the z=4.8 MAGPI LAE as a compact point-source-like system with detected continuum light, a broader stronger blue peak, and central-to-outskirts peak-separation variation.

quotes.quote: iii) The LAE at z=4.8 (field ID 1208) shows a compact spatial profile (Fig. FigGam1 , top row, right panel) resembling `point-source LAEs' as studied by . Continuum light is detected at the location of \ emission in this source. The \ profile shows a broader and stronger blue peak emission. The flux-ratio map (Fig. FigGam2 , top row, right panel) confirms the prevalence of the blue component across most of the \ emitting region, with slight red-peak dominance in the outskirts. Similar to previous cases, we find larger peak separations ( 600 km/s) in the central region and smaller values ( 450 km/s) in the outskirts.

gmw_00001703.name: LBG-2377
gmw_00001703.overview: LBG-2377 is a bright Lyman-break galaxy located at a redshift of z=3.03 [arXiv:0803.3808]. It exhibits no evidence of active galactic nucleus activity or gravitational lensing, and it contains multiple massive components indicating a system in a late merging stage [arXiv:0803.3808].

sources.summary: The paper reports LBG-2377 as a bright z=3.03 Lyman-break galaxy, likely a massive late-stage merger in a group-scale halo that may become a brightest cluster galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a bright, m _R=22.2 , rest-frame FUV color-selected LBG (hereafter LBG-2377) at z=3.03 . Ground-based imaging and spectroscopy finds no evidence for AGN activity or gravitational lensing making this the most luminous z 3 LBG discovered to date. The deep images and high signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) spectroscopy show evidence for multiple massive components indicative of a system in the late stage of merging. We show that the component velocity dispersion and integrated FUV luminosity suggest that LBG-2377 resides in a very massive halo ( 10^ 13 M_ ) and is likely to evolve into a present-day massive brightest cluster galaxy ( sim ). Therefore, LBG-2377 provides a rare opportunity to study the dynamics and properties of a massive LBG halo in the process of formation.

What is the nature of LBG-2377? Possible interpretations are that LBG-2377 represents either (1) a single extended LBG with M 10^ 13 M_ exhibiting multiple regions of vigorous star formation, (2) a single, extended LBG with mass more typical of LBGs ( M 10^ 12 M_ ) that appears over-luminous due to exceptionally high bursts of star formation, (3) multiple unbound M 10^ 12 M_ LBGs in near proximity, or (4) multiple LBGs in a M 10^ 13 M_ halo observed in a late stage of merging. We discuss each scenario below and show that the evidence gathered to date favors interpretation 4.

gmw_00001704.name: NGC 4157
gmw_00001704.overview: NGC 4157 is a fast-rotating, edge-on barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2210.07709, arXiv:0902.4436] that exhibits a weak warp [arXiv:2004.04363] and hosts an escape-dominated radio halo [arXiv:1712.03780]. The galaxy features active star formation [arXiv:1408.5905], including an extreme off-disk star-forming region [arXiv:1906.07763], and contains persistent ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:2109.14407].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4157 as one of three clearest escape-dominated halo candidates, with similar scale heights at both frequencies and inferred cosmic-ray wind speeds up to 420 km/s.

quotes.quote: From the frequency dependence as discussed in Sect. sec:frequency we conclude that the clearest candidates for an escape-dominated halo are NGC 4157, NGC 4217, and NGC 4302. Their scale heights are similar at both frequencies. In this case, the CRE leave the halo so fast that no significant frequency dependence due to synchrotron losses can be observed. This means that the adiabatic loss time of CRE is smaller than their synchrotron life time within the observed distances from the galactic plane.

In escape-dominated halos, we can assume equipartition between magnetic fields and cosmic rays, so that the scale height of the cosmic-ray electrons h_ CRE is about twice the observed synchrotron scale height h_z , which is about 1 kpc for the three galaxies mentioned above (see Table halo scale heights ). Hence, we can estimate a mean cosmic-ray bulk speed v_ CRE (corresponding to the velocity of a galactic wind) of v_ CRE = 2 h_z / t_ esc > 2 h_z / t_ syn 140 km/s. The adiabatic loss time depends only on the reciprocal velocity gradient: t_ ad = 3 v z ^ 1 heesen+2009 . Assuming a constant increase in the CRE velocity with height gives v_ wind = 6 h_z / t_ ad = 420 km/s, significantly higher than 140 km/s and above the escape velocity from the disk, which is about 280 km/s for a rotation speed of 200 km/s heesen+2009 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies an extreme off-disk star-forming region in NGC 4157, coincident with an XUV disk feature and possibly caused by gas accretion and gravitational instability.

quotes.quote: We identified a source in the narrow-band H image of NGC 4157 located well outside of the star forming disk, centered at = 12^h11^m24.^s996 , =+50^ 30^m32^s.04 . The source is located 60 ( 4.5 kpc) from the next nearest region associated with the galaxy disk. The source shows no counterpart in the corresponding -band image, thus it is likely a region of active star formation associated with NGC 4157. Interestingly, the region is spatially coincident with an extended ultraviolet (XUV) disk feature.

Deep 21-cm observations of NGC 4157 are outlined in kennedy09 . As seen in Figure 3.9 of that work, the neutral gas emission seems to extend slightly asymmetrically toward the northeast, which is the side of the outer disk hosting the anomalous region. It is possible this gas asymmetry traces a recent accretion event onto the disk on NGC 4157 which led to the formation of stars at the location of the anomalous region. It is interesting that the XUV feature and emission is coincident with a star forming region at extreme distance from the disk.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4157 as a fast-rotating edge-on galaxy with high radio-to-optical ratio and larger low-frequency radio-halo scale heights.

quotes.quote: With newly reduced radio continuum data of five edge on galaxies (NGC 891, NGC 3432, NGC 4013, NGC 4157, and NGC 4631), we plan to set new constraints on the morphology of radio halos and the physical properties of galactic winds driven by stellar feedback. By distinguishing between the central and outer regions of the galaxies, our study setup allows us to search for variations in the radio halo profile or CR transport along the Galactic disk.

The scale height analysis reveals that most of the galaxies are equally well fitted with an one-component instead of a two-component exponential profile. We find a bi-modality within our sample. While NGC 3432 and NGC 4013 have similar scale heights in the L-band and HBA, the low-frequency scale heights of NGC 891, NGC 4157, and NGC 4631 exceed their high-frequency counterpart significantly.

Of the remaining galaxies, NGC 4631, NGC 891, NGC 2820, and NGC 4157 have the highest radio-to-optical ratios. We chose to omit NGC 2820 as previous analyses (e.g.: 1985A&A...150L...7V (VLA 1.5 GHz), 2015AJ....150...81W (CHANG-ES D-configuration), 2019AJ....158...21I (CHANG-ES B-configuration), 2005A&A...435..483K (GMRT), and (LOFAR LoTSS)) show a strong radio bridge, reaching from the galaxy to its closest interaction partner NGC 2814, which will most probably disturb the radio halo and therefore prevent the planned analysis. As NGC 891 and NGC 4157 are fairly fast rotating, compared to NGC 4631, we decided to also include NGC 3432, which has a less prominent radio halo, to have another slow rotating galaxy in our sample.

sources.summary: The paper presents higher-resolution H I observations of NGC 4157, combines them with previous data, resolves its weak warp, and checks model scale heights and velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4157 and NGC 5907 We presented the CO and \ images of NGC 4157 and 5907 in the previous study by . The angular resolutions of the VLA \ (C and D arrays) images are about 15 ( 1 kpc), which is not sufficient for comparison with the CO data ( 3.5 ; 245 pc) and to resolve the vertical structure of the disc. For that reasons, we observed \ emission with EVLA in B configuration toward NGC 4157 for 24 hours and NGC 5907 for 26 hours in 2012. In the case of NGC 4157, we used only 16 hours of data since the data taken on June 8 and July 5 were not good enough to use even after careful flagging. Once the data were flagged and calibrated using the CASA package, we combined the visibility data with the VLA (C and D) calibrated data using the CASA task CONCAT.
The strong warp in NGC 5907 and the weak warp in NGC 4157 shown in are more evident in the new maps.
In the case of NGC 4157 HI (bottom-left), the outermost red contour in the p-v diagram seems to suggest that the derived vertical velocity dispersion in the region is overestimated.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4157 as a small barred edge-on spiral, detects a small infrared warp, and finds flaring brings warp-onset theory into agreement.

quotes.quote: NGC 4157: NGC4157 is a small barred spiral edge-on galaxy lying in the constellation of Ursa Major roughly in between two big galaxies M106 and M109. Its neighbour NGC4088 is also an edge-on spiral. We detect a small warp in the 4.5 micron band image of the galaxy. The observed H I rotation curve is obtained from Marc Verheijen (Verheijen Sancisi 2001). Note that even though the galaxy is small, it is not dominated by the dark matter halo in our model. Infact, the ratio of dark to disc mass ( M_h/M_d ) is the smallest in the sample.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 4157 as one of three spiral bulges whose Hβ-based ages are overestimated, producing too-low metallicities, while higher-order Balmer indices reduce the discrepancy.

quotes.quote: For three bulges (NGC 4157, NGC 4217, NGC 4312) \ indicates significantly older ages, all three well above the age of the universe. Interestingly, exactly those objects show H _ emission greater than expected from [ O iii ] (PS02). Therefore their ages must clearly be overestimated, a problem by which the higher-order Balmer line \ is much less affected. In these three cases \ yields metallicities that are too low in line with the overestimated ages.
NGC 4157, NGC 4217, NGC 4312 still show slightly older ages from , but the deviation approaches the general scatter in the relationship.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4157 as an edge-on disk whose CO and HI inclinations differ slightly but agree where both tracers are analyzed; higher star formation corresponds to a thicker CO disk.

quotes.quote: However, in the case of NGC 4157, the CO (84 ) and \ (83 ) values show a difference, so we adopt the CO inclination value since the \ derived value may be affected by the outer warp. Even though there is the 1 difference between CO and \ inclinations in NGC 4157, the values match well in the region where both tracers can be analyzed.
From comparison between the CO disk thickness and the SFR surface density profile (Figure 24prof ), we notice that the galaxies with higher SFR (NGC 891 and NGC 4157) show a thicker CO disk.
The strongly star-forming galaxies (NGC 891 and NGC 4157) show a thicker CO disk compared to the other galaxies, suggesting a correlation between the disk thickness and star formation activity.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4157 as one of two galaxy rotation-curve examples for its new relation and EMOND, comparing the result with MOND and finding little deviation.

quotes.quote: We also show rotation curves for two galaxies, M33 and NGC4157, using the new relation and EMOND (Figures AMONDrotcurves1 and AMONDrotcurves2 ). These rotation curves have been used in m33rotcurve for M33 and NGC4157rotcurve for NGC 4157. We overplot the MOND prediction for comparison. There is little deviation from MOND for these two examples.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4157 with Chandra, XMM-Newton, and NuSTAR, reporting persistent ULX detections and a bright central-region ULX with a hard power-law spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 4157 was observed by the Nuclear Spectroscopic Telescope Array ( NuSTAR ) 2013ApJ...770..103H for 40ks on 2020-10-19 (program number 06058, ObsIDs 30601015001, 30601015002). NGC 4157 was observed by XMM-Newton on 2004-05-17 (ObsID 0203170101) and by Chandra on 2010-08-21 (ObsID 11310). The ULXs in NGC 4157 were detected in all of the the Chandra and XMM-Newton observations, but only two of the ULXs in NGC 891 were persistent across the archival data.
The first is a bright, 10^ 40 erg/s ULX in NGC 4157, associated with the central region of the galaxy, with a hard power-law spectrum.

gmw_00001705.name: MIPS J002621.7
gmw_00001705.overview: MIPS J002621.7 is a luminous infrared galaxy that appears nearly face-on to the line of sight [arXiv:0902.2780]. It features a bright core and distinctive spiral arms, and it contains a substantial amount of molecular gas [arXiv:0902.2780].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO emission from this Cl 0024+16 LIRG, describing it as nearly face-on with a bright core, spiral arms, substantial molecular gas, and a short depletion time.

quotes.quote: We detect CO(1--0) emission in both galaxies with significances of 9.3 and 9.5 respectively. Note that these observations represent the highest redshift CO detection in un-lensed LIRGs yet achieved (see Melchior Combes \ 2008).

MIPS J002621.7 appears to be nearly face-on to the line of sight, with a bright core and distinctive arms. MIPS J002721.0 is slightly more complicated; an un-sharp masked R band image reveals a ring of bright knots and a bright core or bar that appears to be slightly extended to the north-east.

Assuming i=10^ for MIPS J002621.7, we find M_ dyn =(1.1 0.2) 10^ 10 M_ . This ignores the systematic uncertainty on the inclination angle, which would clearly affect these mass estimates. The orientation of MIPS J002721.0 is similarly uncertain -- it could be a face-on merger, or an inclined disc.

gmw_00001706.name: MIPS J002721.0
gmw_00001706.overview: MIPS J002721.0 is a luminous infrared galaxy that exhibits carbon monoxide emission [arXiv:0902.2780]. Its double-peaked line profile and optical morphology suggest the galaxy is either a rotating disc or a line-of-sight merger [arXiv:0902.2780].

sources.summary: The paper detects CO emission from this Cl 0024+16 LIRG, interpreting its double-peaked line and optical morphology as either a rotating disc or a line-of-sight merger.

quotes.quote: We detect CO(1--0) emission in both galaxies with significances of 9.3 and 9.5 respectively. Note that these observations represent the highest redshift CO detection in un-lensed LIRGs yet achieved (see Melchior Combes \ 2008).

The CO(1--0) spectrum for MIPS J002721.0 exhibits a double peaked line profile (Fig. 2), and we show in Figure 3 that the peak of the CO emission traced by this velocity shear describes a locus that runs 10 kpc across the direction of the extended optical emission. This could be interpreted as a rotating disc, with an angular separation between the velocity peaks of (1.8 0.8)'' or (9.5 4.2) kpc. Note that the peak of the CO emission at the [ O ii ] redshift is centred on the bright central core. An alternative situation is that this galaxy is in the process of a merger along the line-of-sight, and the CO(1--0) emission profile originates from emission from two gas-rich clumps.

Assuming i=10^ for MIPS J002621.7, we find M_ dyn =(1.1 0.2) 10^ 10 M_ . This ignores the systematic uncertainty on the inclination angle, which would clearly affect these mass estimates. The orientation of MIPS J002721.0 is similarly uncertain -- it could be a face-on merger, or an inclined disc.

gmw_00001707.name: NGC 3605
gmw_00001707.overview: NGC 3605 is a dwarf elliptical galaxy with an estimated age of 13.8 Gyr [arXiv:1401.4003]. It features an asymmetric large-scale distribution of blue color, which is likely associated with an extensive nuclear starburst [arXiv:1009.6174].

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 3605 with an equal-mass merger simulation, describing it as an old dwarf elliptical whose simulated magnitude, SED, morphology, gas, and stellar mass match observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 3605.       A merger of two triaxial systems with a mass ratio of 1:1 and total mass in Table table2 matches well with the B band absolute magnitude, the SED, and the morphology of this dwarf E galaxy (Figures figure1 and figure3 ). The spins of the merging systems are equal ( =0.06, MC03), perpendicular, and both aligned with the shorter of their principal axes. The age of the galaxy is 13.8 Gyr. The SFR proceeds about 3 Gyr before the merger and contributes to 16 of the total SFR. The average stellar age within r_ e is 5 Gyr and becomes 6 Gyr within R_ 25 2 r_ e (weighted by B band luminosity: 3.4 Gyr and 4.3 Gyr, respectively).

sources.summary: The paper reports integral-field spectroscopy of NGC 3605, finding consistent MPFS and SAURON Lick-index profiles and an unusually extensive nuclear starburst compared with the other dwarfs.

quotes.quote: NGC 3605 has been observed with the MPFS in April 2005. All six dwarfs were also exposed with the SAURON in 2007--2008 in the frame of the project ATLAS-3D. I have calculated the Lick indices H , Mgb, and Fe5270 for every spaxel. The radial profiles of the Lick indices obtained for NGC 3605 with the MPFS and with the SAURON agree well.

The main bodies of all the dwarfs except NGC 3605 are older than 8 Gyr; in NGC 3605 the nuclear starburst has been perhaps more extensive than in other galaxies that is also implied by asymmetric large-scale distribution of the blue colour in this galaxy.

gmw_00001708.name: S0108
gmw_00001708.overview: S0108 is a starburst galaxy that exhibits Mg II emission and absorption in a P-Cygni profile [arXiv:1406.3356]. It features high-velocity redshifted shoulders in both Mg II and Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:1406.3356], as well as narrow nebular emission with an equivalent width of 0.33 [arXiv:1905.04314].

sources.summary: S0108+0624 uniquely shows significant narrow emission in the sample and has the largest binary-model enhancement of the inferred hard ionizing continuum.

quotes.quote: Finally, S0108+0624 is the only galaxy in the sample with statistically significant ( >3 ), narrow \ emission (equivalent width of 0.33 0.08 \ and FWHM = 96 , which is resolved by the 69 spectral resolution). This narrow emission appears nebular in origin when compared to the broad Wolf-Rayet feature of the Sunburst Arc (compare the bottom and second-to-bottom spectra in fig:heii ). This \ emission is at the weak end of the range of \ equivalent widths seen in local dwarf galaxies 0.4 to 3.4 ; berg16, berg19 .

S0108 + 0624 has the most pronounced difference in the \ and \ ionizing continua. The inferred , , and \ are 2, 4, and 12 times larger for the \ fits than . Surprisingly, the \ models in fig:ion predict larger \ for the older population of S0108 + 0624 than the youngest stellar populations in the sample, such as RCS Knot E (1.4 times larger) and the Sunburst Arc (1.7 times larger).

sources.summary: The paper finds S0108 has Mg II P-Cygni emission, broad redshifted Mg II, and high-velocity red shoulders in both Mg II and Lyman emission implying a strong outflow.

quotes.quote: The Mg II profiles of S0108 and RCS0327 Knot E show a ``shoulder'' of emission extending 500  km s ^ 1 redward of systemic. The \ emission in S0108 and S0957 also show redshifted shoulders to even higher velocities ( > 600  km s ^ 1 ).

While the five galaxies in this paper were merely selected from our larger sample as spectral coverage of Mg II and , in fact, all five show detected Mg II emission and absorption in a P Cygni profile.
In each galaxy, the Mg II emission is broader than the C III ] and [ O II ] lines, and the peak is redshifted with respect to systemic by 100 to 200 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00001709.name: S0957
gmw_00001709.overview: S0957 is a gravitationally lensed star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1406.3356]. Its spectrum exhibits magnesium II emission and absorption in a P-Cygni profile, alongside redshifted Lyman-alpha emission with a high-velocity shoulder [arXiv:1406.3356].

sources.summary: The paper treats S0957 as a lensed star-forming galaxy with detected Mg II P-Cygni emission, broad redshifted Mg II, and Lyman emission with a high-velocity red shoulder.

quotes.quote: The \ emission in S0108 and S0957 also show redshifted shoulders to even higher velocities ( > 600  km s ^ 1 ).

While the five galaxies in this paper were merely selected from our larger sample as spectral coverage of Mg II and , in fact, all five show detected Mg II emission and absorption in a P Cygni profile.
In each galaxy, the Mg II emission is broader than the C III ] and [ O II ] lines, and the peak is redshifted with respect to systemic by 100 to 200 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00001710.name: S1441
gmw_00001710.overview: S1441 is a gravitationally lensed galaxy with a systemic redshift arbitrarily set to z = 1.666 [arXiv:1406.3356]. Its spectrum exhibits Mg II emission and absorption in a P Cygni profile, with the emission peak redshifted relative to the systemic velocity [arXiv:1406.3356].

sources.summary: The paper includes S1441 among the five lensed galaxies with Mg II P-Cygni emission, but notes that its systemic redshift is arbitrary because systemic lines are undetected.

quotes.quote: S1441 lacks detected CIII ] or other systemic lines; we therefore arbitrarily choose a systemic redshift of z = 1.666, which puts the reddest Mg II 2796  \ absorption at zero velocity.

While the five galaxies in this paper were merely selected from our larger sample as spectral coverage of Mg II and , in fact, all five show detected Mg II emission and absorption in a P Cygni profile.
In each galaxy, the Mg II emission is broader than the C III ] and [ O II ] lines, and the peak is redshifted with respect to systemic by 100 to 200 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00001711.name: JADES-GS-z13
gmw_00001711.overview: JADES-GS-z13 is a compact, star-forming galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift of approximately 13.2 [arXiv:2212.12804, arXiv:2305.17959]. It has a young stellar population with a stellar mass of about 10^8 solar masses [arXiv:2212.12804, arXiv:2305.17959], but its deep spectra show no obvious strong ultraviolet emission lines [arXiv:2404.04325].

sources.summary: The paper treats JADES-GS-z13-0 as a central ultra-high-redshift target, finding no significant UV emission lines despite deep spectra and modeling its steep UV slope and line limits.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we explore two z > 10 sources from robertson2023 and curtislake2023 , JADES-GS-53.16476-27.77463 (hereafter, JADES-GS-z11-0) and JADES-GS-53.14988-27.7765 (hereafter, JADES-GS-z13-0).

JADES-GS-z13-0, which lies at a redshift where it was not visible to Hubble, is the highest-redshift spectroscopically confirmed galaxy found in the first year of JWST observations robertson2023, curtislake2023 . Deeper spectroscopy from NIRSpec taken as part of observations of the JADES Origins Field eisenstein2023b allows us to explore the UV properties of these distant galaxies, where we can re-evaluate their redshifts, UV slopes and magnitudes, and the inferred stellar masses, star-formation rates, and metallicities.

The Absence of Emission Lines in JADES-GS-z13-0 The JADES-GS-z13-0 spectrum shown in Figure fig:2d_1d_plots is notable in that we do not see any obvious strong emission lines given our fiducial redshifts. This is surprising given the detection of emission lines in other galaxies at z > 10 , including JADES-GS-z11-0, in multiple galaxies in arrabalharo2023a , MACS0647-JD hsiao2023 , GN-z11 bunker2023a , GLASS-z12 castellano2024, zavala2024 , and JADES-GS-z12-0 in . The depth of the spectra in this paper puts tight upper limits on the possible flux of any lines, as shown in Table tab:spectrum-fit-parameters . To interpret the spectrum of JADES-GS-z13-0, we first adopted the same fiducial model as the one used to model JADES-GS-z11-0 and discussed in Section sec:beagle-fits above, i.e. a model with a constant star formation history and no escape fraction of ionising photons.

sources.summary: The paper treats JADES-GS-z13-0 as one of the earliest spectroscopically confirmed JADES galaxies, with compact star-forming properties and a stellar mass compared to simulation limits.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopic confirmation has now arrived with the discovery in the JADES survey of 4 galaxies with z_ spec >10 and M_* 10^8 Curtis-Lake2022,Robertson2022 . By observing 65 arcmin ^2 of the GOODS-S field with JWST NIRCam and NIRSpec, JADES has confirmed the four earliest detected galaxies: JADES-GS-z10-0 at z=10.38^ +0.07 _ 0.06 , JADES-GS-z11-0 at z=11.58^ +0.05 _ 0.05 , JADES-GS-z12-0 at z=12.63^ +0.24 _ 0.08 , and JADES-GS-z13-0 at z=13.2^ +0.04 _ 0.07 . Curtis-Lake2022,Robertson2022 have measured stellar masses and star formation rates for these galaxies using the Prospector SED-fitting code Johnson2021 , finding them to be compact, star forming galaxies with young stellar populations and relatively high star formation surface densities. In this letter, we compare the observed JADES galaxies to predictions from an array of large cosmological hydrodynamical simulations.
As we move to higher redshift, only Simba and OBELISK produce even a single galaxy above the best-fit stellar masses of JADES-GS-z11-0, JADES-GS-z12-0, and JADES-GS-z13-0.

The deep observing area of JADES, using the JWST NIRCam, (9.7 square arcmin), yields a volume of V (9 9 494) ^3 4 10^4 ^3 bounded by the comoving distance of 494 between the JADES-GS-z10-0 at z=10.38 and JADES-GS-z13-0 at z=13.2 .

sources.summary: The paper models JADES-GS-z13 as a compact spectroscopically confirmed z > 10 galaxy, finding many analogues whose redshifts and luminosities are somewhat lower than observational estimates.

quotes.quote: The two targets studied here, JADES-GS-z13 and JADES-GS-z12 of redshifts z = 13.30_ 0.07 ^ +0.04 and z = 12.63_ 0.08 ^ +0.24 respectively, come from an epoch even earlier than the previous record of high-redshift galaxies with spectroscopic confirmation, GNz11 ( Oesch2016ApJ...819..129O , see also Bunker2023arXiv230207256B for an updated spectrum with NIRSpec). These galaxies are results from the JWST Advanced Deep Extragalactic Survey (JADES) that combines NIRCam and NIRSpec targeting at the GOODS (i.e., Great Observatories Origins Deep Survey) South (GS) field, reaching a 5 magnitude limit of 28.4 mag for spectroscopy. When fitting the SEDs, utilised the full spectra while focused on the photometric data, leading to similar (but not identical) physical properties -- both JADES-GS-z13 and JADES-GS-z12 are quite small with an intrinsic UV magnitude fainter than -19 mag and a stellar mass of only 10^8 M _ .

Our selection leads to 1296 and 397 analogues in our fiducial output for JADES-GS-z13 and JADES-GS-z12, respectively, whose properties are summarized in Figs. fig:JADEz13 and fig:JADEz12 . Galaxy properties We find both observed SEDs to be consistent with modelled star-forming galaxies at z 12.6 or 13.2 , as is evident from the two modes in the redshift distribution.

sources.summary: The paper identifies JADES-GS-53.14988-27.7765 as JADES-GS-z13-0, spectroscopically confirmed at z=13.20, bright in F200W, and photometrically consistent with the spectroscopic redshift.

quotes.quote: Our sample in this redshift range includes the other two high-redshift spectroscopically-confirmed galaxies from robertson2022 and curtislake2022 , JADES-GS-z12-0 ( z_ spec = 12.63^ +0.24 _ 0.08 ) and JADES-GS-z13-0 ( z_ spec = 13.20^ +0.04 _ 0.07 ). We estimate EAZY photometric redshifts for these targets of z_a = 12.46 for JADES-GS-z12-0, and z_a = 13.41 for JADES-GS-z13-0. While both photometric redshifts are quite uncertain due to the width of the bands used to probe the Lyman- break, the range of uncertainties based on the EAZY _ 68 redshifts are consistent with the spectroscopic redshifts.

JADES-GS-53.14988-27.7765 , ( z_a = 13.41 ) This source, also known as JADES-GS-z13-0, was spectroscopically confirmed to be at z_ spec = 13.20 in curtislake2022 , and the NIRCam photometry and morphology for the source was discussed in robertson2022 . The source is fairly bright ( m_ F200W, Kron = 28.81 ) with a strong observed Lyman- break, and the 68 range on the photometric redshift ( z_ 68 = 12.92 - 14.06 ) is in agreement with the observed spectroscopic redshift.

gmw_00001712.name: JADES-GS-z12
gmw_00001712.overview: JADES-GS-z12 is a compact, high-redshift galaxy with a spectroscopically confirmed redshift of approximately 12.48 to 12.63 [arXiv:2212.12804, arXiv:2402.18942]. It is characterized as a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2212.12804, arXiv:2305.17959] with a young stellar population [arXiv:2212.12804] and a stellar mass estimated around 10^7 to 10^8 solar masses [arXiv:2305.17959, arXiv:2402.18942].

sources.summary: The paper identifies JADES-GS-z12-0 as a compact star-forming z=12.63 JADES galaxy, then compares its stellar mass and implied abundance with cosmological simulation predictions.

quotes.quote: Spectroscopic confirmation has now arrived with the discovery in the JADES survey of 4 galaxies with z_ spec >10 and M_* 10^8 Curtis-Lake2022,Robertson2022 . By observing 65 arcmin ^2 of the GOODS-S field with JWST NIRCam and NIRSpec, JADES has confirmed the four earliest detected galaxies: JADES-GS-z10-0 at z=10.38^ +0.07 _ 0.06 , JADES-GS-z11-0 at z=11.58^ +0.05 _ 0.05 , JADES-GS-z12-0 at z=12.63^ +0.24 _ 0.08 , and JADES-GS-z13-0 at z=13.2^ +0.04 _ 0.07 . Curtis-Lake2022,Robertson2022 have measured stellar masses and star formation rates for these galaxies using the Prospector SED-fitting code Johnson2021 , finding them to be compact, star forming galaxies with young stellar populations and relatively high star formation surface densities. In this letter, we compare the observed JADES galaxies to predictions from an array of large cosmological hydrodynamical simulations.
As we move to higher redshift, only Simba and OBELISK produce even a single galaxy above the best-fit stellar masses of JADES-GS-z11-0, JADES-GS-z12-0, and JADES-GS-z13-0.

Each of the JADES galaxies lies outside of the excluded region of M_*=f_B M_ halo , but the most extreme two cases (JADES-GS-z11-0 and JADES-GS-z12-0) imply integrated baryon conversion efficiencies near 10 per cent ( M_*/(f_B M_ halo ) 0.1 ). For z 10 , we also show the independent measurements for the galaxy stellar mass function measured by the GREATS ALMA survey by Stefanon2021 . The highest predicted number density for massive halos at these redshifts is from Simba, and only JADES-GS-z11-0 and JADES-GS-z12-0 imply number densities higher than in Simba for galaxies at their stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper models JADES-GS-z12 as a compact spectroscopically confirmed z > 10 galaxy, finding analogues with broadly consistent SEDs but lower redshift and luminosity tendencies.

quotes.quote: The two targets studied here, JADES-GS-z13 and JADES-GS-z12 of redshifts z = 13.30_ 0.07 ^ +0.04 and z = 12.63_ 0.08 ^ +0.24 respectively, come from an epoch even earlier than the previous record of high-redshift galaxies with spectroscopic confirmation, GNz11 ( Oesch2016ApJ...819..129O , see also Bunker2023arXiv230207256B for an updated spectrum with NIRSpec). These galaxies are results from the JWST Advanced Deep Extragalactic Survey (JADES) that combines NIRCam and NIRSpec targeting at the GOODS (i.e., Great Observatories Origins Deep Survey) South (GS) field, reaching a 5 magnitude limit of 28.4 mag for spectroscopy. When fitting the SEDs, utilised the full spectra while focused on the photometric data, leading to similar (but not identical) physical properties -- both JADES-GS-z13 and JADES-GS-z12 are quite small with an intrinsic UV magnitude fainter than -19 mag and a stellar mass of only 10^8 M _ .

Our selection leads to 1296 and 397 analogues in our fiducial output for JADES-GS-z13 and JADES-GS-z12, respectively, whose properties are summarized in Figs. fig:JADEz13 and fig:JADEz12 . Galaxy properties We find both observed SEDs to be consistent with modelled star-forming galaxies at z 12.6 or 13.2 , as is evident from the two modes in the redshift distribution.

sources.summary: The paper uses GS-z12 as a high-redshift galaxy illustrating uncertain SED-derived properties, noting discrepant stellar-mass estimates and different reported redshift, SFR, and stellar-mass values between eugenio23 and H24.

quotes.quote: This is the case for example for the galaxy GS-z12 with a spectroscopic redshift z=12.48 eugenio23 . Using the PROSPECTOR SED fitting PROSPECTOR , H24 found a stellar mass M_ = 4.3^ +1.8 _ 2 10^8 for this galaxy, while analysis using the BEAGLE core BEAGLE performed by eugenio23 lead to M_ = 4.36^ +1.8 _ 1.27 10^7 for the same object.

The open square symbols refer to the galaxy GS-z12 for which different values of redshift, SFR and M_ are reported in eugenio23 and H24. Both sets of data are shown with eugenio23 being the point with the lower redshift ( z=12.43 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies JADES-GS-53.16635-27.82156 as JADES-GS-z12-0, spectroscopically confirmed by NIRSpec line emission and detected brightly as an F150W dropout.

quotes.quote: JADES-GS-53.16635-27.82156 ( z_a = 12.46 ) This galaxy, also known as JADES-GS-z12-0, was originally spectroscopically confirmed to lie at z_ spec = 12.63 in curtislake2022 , and the NIRCam photometry and morphology for the source was discussed in robertson2022 . Recent, deeper NIRSpec observations for this source showed C 3 ] 1907,1909 line emission, indicating a spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec = 12.479 deugenio2023 . This source is a bright ( m_ F277W, Kron = 28.64 ) F150W dropout ( m_ F150W m_ F200W = 2.01 ) with a 26 detection in F277W, and is observed at the 4-6 level in the relatively shallow F182M and F210M filters.

gmw_00001713.name: S5-z12-1
gmw_00001713.overview: S5-z12-1 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate located in the direction of Stephan's Quintet [arXiv:2305.17959]. Models suggest the galaxy has a low metallicity, minor dust attenuation, and adequate gas available for star formation [arXiv:2305.17959].

sources.summary: The paper studies S5-z12-1 as a z = 12.58 candidate, finding hundreds of analogues but only 22 matching its inferred UV luminosity within uncertainties.

quotes.quote: target in this section, S5-z12-1, which is a z = 12.58^ +1.23 _ 0.46 galaxy with an intrinsic UV magnitude of 20.2 0.1 found in Stephan's Quintet.

Photometry from only 3 filters was reported by Harikane2023ApJS..265....5H for S5-z12-1. For analogue selection, we require 2 consistency in band F200W and F356W between the forward modelling and the observational data; while the inferred magnitude in the non-detection band, F150W, must be lower than the 2 threshold. We use the same 31 snapshots as before and focus on z 11 -17. We end up with a surprisingly large number of 377 analogues, among which, however, only 22 have an intrinsic UV magnitude 2 consistent with the estimate of Harikane2023ApJS..265....5H .

Figure fig:S5-z12-1 shows that our inferred properties are mostly consistent with those of compare 1D distributions with grey shaded regions Harikane2023ApJS..265....5H , except for the intrinsic UV magnitude -- our model suggests S5-z12-1 is a more massive, larger and brighter galaxy than the two confirmed z 12 galaxies but still with a low metallicity and minor dust attenuation. It has adequate gas available for star formation thanks to its 10^ 10 - 10^ 10.5 M _ halo mass.

gmw_00001714.name: Maisie's Galaxy
gmw_00001714.overview: Maisie's Galaxy is a high-redshift, dust-poor galaxy spectroscopically confirmed at a redshift of approximately 11.4 [arXiv:2303.15431, arXiv:2304.06658]. It exhibits a compact physical size [arXiv:2305.17959, arXiv:2306.00647], a young stellar population [arXiv:2306.00647], and a high star-formation rate relative to its modest stellar mass [arXiv:2303.15431].

sources.summary: The paper identifies analogues for Maisie's Galaxy, describing it as a lower-luminosity z ~ 11.5 galaxy with low dust, modest star formation, and analogue evolutionary paths that diverge after z = 12.

quotes.quote: Maisie's Galaxy at z=11.44^ +0.09 _ 0.08 was reported by multiple teams including Finkelstein2022ApJ...940L..55F , Donnan2023MNRAS.518.6011D , Harikane2023ApJS..265....5H and haro2023spectroscopic showing consistent inferred properties Maisie's Galaxy was initially considered to be at z = 14.3^ +0.4 _ 1.1 Finkelstein2022ApJ...940L..55F , and therefore became the second object that we looked into as it had an even higher redshift than GLz12 which was previously thought at z 13 . Using the SED from the first version of its pre-print, we identified 2 analogues showing similar evolution and environment as the analogue presented here but with a burstier SFH. . Using its photometric data (e.g. Finkelstein2022ApJ...940L..55F ; z=11.8^ +0.2 _ 0.3 ), it is estimated that Maisie's Galaxy possesses a low SFR of 2 M _ yr ^ 1 , a stellar mass around 10^ 8.5 M _ , an intrinsic UV magnitude of 20.3 , a steep UV slope of 2.5 , a low dust extinction of A_ v 0.1 , and an effective radius around 0.34 pkpc.

Given its lower luminosity, identifying analogues for this galaxy should be less challenging than GLz12. Analogue selection Within our 31 simulation snapshots between z = 10.8 and 16.9, 7 galaxies are identified having fluxes consistent within at least 2 of the observed photometry Finkelstein2022ApJ...940L..55F in the filters F150W, F200W F277W, F356W, and F444W, as well as being lower than the 2 upper limit in the non-detection band F115W. Although we do not forward model luminosities from the HST or JWST F410M filters, the spectra of our analogues are found also consistent with these measurements/upper limits. Our analogues share similar physical properties with the observations, including an intrinsic UV magnitude around 20.3 , a stellar mass of 10^ 8.5 M _ , and a redshift between 11 and 12. To be concise, only two example analogues, dubbed analogue-a and b, are presented here which are found at z = 11.3 and 12.0.

sources.summary: The paper spectroscopically confirms Maisie's Galaxy at z = 11.44, finding a sharp continuum break, modest stellar mass, little dust, high specific star formation, and no robust strong emission lines.

quotes.quote: The spectrum of Maisie's Galaxy (Figure fig:maisiebreak ) shows well-detected continuum emission with a sharp break, below which no significant flux is detected. The most likely interpretations of this feature are the Ly IGM break ( _ rest = 1216 ) or the Balmer break ( _ rest = 3646 ); the latter is prominent in galaxies where star formation ceased more than 100 Myr before the time of observation. Integrating the spectra in wavelength intervals redder and bluer than the observed breaks, we measure a 3 limit on the flux density ratio > 2.6 , inconsistent with values expected for Balmer breaks curtis-lake2023 (see Methods).

Interpreting this feature as the Ly break (Fig. fig:maisiebreak b) yields a redshift z = 11.44 ^ +0.09 _ 0.08 , consistent with photometric redshift estimates. Performing stellar population modeling (see Methods for details) on the observed photometry of this galaxy, fixing the redshift to this spectroscopic value, we confirm earlier results that Maisie's Galaxy is a modestly massive ( _ 10 M_*/ M_ = 8.4^ +0.3 _ 0.4 ), dust-poor (A _V = 0.07^ +0.09 _ 0.05 mag) galaxy, with a high star-formation rate for its stellar mass ( _ 10 ( SFR /M_*) = -8.0^ +0.4 _ 0.5 yr ^ 1 ). The observed spectrum of Maisie's Galaxy shows no strong emission lines (a strong feature potentially consistent with emission from singly-ionized oxygen, \ rest-frame wavelength _ rest = 3727 , observed at 4.6 m, was discarded as a likely instrumental artifact -- see the Methods section for further explanation).

sources.summary: The paper checks long-wavelength data for Maisie's Galaxy and finds no dust continuum emission, making a lower-redshift dusty interpretation unlikely.

quotes.quote: Maisie's Galaxy Given that the recently reported z=14.3 galaxy candidate from Finkelstein2022 lies close to the two galaxies described above ( 78'' and 65'' away from \ and from the source reported Donnan2022 , respectively), we carefully examine the available long-wavelength observations to investigate any possible detection of dust emission. Because this source is not covered by our NOEMA observations, we started by looking at the deep SCUBA-2 850 \ map ( Zavala2017a ). As shown in Figure fig:maisies_source no significant detection is found (with a measured flux density of S _ 850 =-0.40 0.25 mJy at the position of the source). We also search for significant emission in the Spitzer 8 \ and 24 \ map, Herschel 100, 160, 250, 350, and 500 \ imaging, and SCUBA-2 450 \ observations, finding only non-detections. We thus conclude that a lower redshift, dusty scenario for \ is very unlikely.

sources.summary: The paper confirms Maisie's Galaxy at z=11.40 using O II emission, finding the line real despite earlier concerns and consistent with the Lyman-break estimate.

quotes.quote: Maisie's Galaxy was firstly photometrically identified in 2022ApJ...940L..55F , and was reported as CR2-z12-1 in 2023ApJS..265....5H . As shown in the top panel of Figure fig_spec1 , the spectrum of this galaxy shows the Lyman break, the Oii 3727 line (5.5 ), and the possible [ Ne iii ] 3869 line (2.4 ). The redshift of this galaxy was reported to be z_ spec =11.44^ +0.09 _ 0.08 based on the Lyman break in 2023arXiv230315431A . Given these facts, we consider this Oii line is real.
