We determine the redshift to be z_ spec =11.40 using the Oii line, which is consistent with the previous estimate based on the Lyman break but slightly lower than that ( z_ spec =11.44^ +0.09 _ 0.08 ; 2023arXiv230315431A ).

sources.summary: Maisie's galaxy is a CEERS high-redshift galaxy with spectroscopic redshift z=11.44; the paper includes it in simulation comparisons after excluding lower-redshift CEERS-93316.

quotes.quote: Additionally the CEERS project CEERS has also provided measurements for Maisie's galaxy which has a redshift of 11.44 Maisie . In total there are six spectroscopically confirmed galaxies against which we can directly compare.

Initial photometric measurements of Maisie's galaxy and of CEERS-93316 placed them at photometric redshifts of z_ phot = 11.08 Maisie_initial and z_ phot = 16.45 CEERS-93316-initial , respectively. Spectroscopic follow-up measurements of these galaxies confirmed their redshifts to be z_ spec = 11.44 and z_ spec = 4.912 Maisie , respectively. Due to the decrease in redshift of CEERS-93316 to a much lower redshift, we do not include it in the analysis in this paper and instead only include the high redshift Maisie's galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper treats CR2-z12-1 as an extended CEERS2 high-redshift candidate, matching ID 32395 and Maisie's Galaxy with a photometric redshift near z=11.6.

quotes.quote: We find that some bright candidates, i.e., GL-z9-1, GL-z12-1, CR2-z12-1, CR-z16-1, and S5-z16-1, are clearly more extended than the PSF, although GL-z12-1 is compact compared to other candidates, implying potential AGN activity.

In the version 2, 2022arXiv220712356D newly selected ID 32395 2 at z=12.29^ +0.91 _ 0.32 , which is also selected in this study as CR2-z12-1 at z=11.63^ +0.51 _ 0.53 , which was firstly identified in 2022arXiv220712474F 2022arXiv220712356D presented other three candidates at z>12 , but these candidates are not selected in this study due to ^2<9 . 2022arXiv220712474F One galaxy candidate at z 14 , dubbed Maisie's Galaxy in 2022arXiv220712474F , is also selected in this study as CR2-z12-1. We refer to the manuscript version 2 that was submitted to arXiv on September 7 . The photometric redshift presented in 2022arXiv220712474F is z=11.8^ +0.3 _ 1.2 , consistent with our estimate ( z=11.63^ +0.51 _ 0.53 ).

sources.summary: The paper measures CEERS-16943, also called Maisie's galaxy, at z=11.409 with nebular lines, an extreme DLA column, young stellar population, compact size, and high inferred gas fraction.

quotes.quote: The reduced and photometrically-calibrated 1D spectra of the three galaxies at z=9-11 are shown in Fig. 1. We determine the redshift of CEERS-43833 to be z=8.7622 0.0002 from the multitude of nebular emission lines detected in the spectrum (see Supplementary Materials, Fig. S2), consistent with the first analysis of this spectrum presented in . Similarly, we determine z=11.409 0.001 for CEERS-16943 based on the detection of [ ] 3727 , [ Ne iii ] 3870 , and He i 3889 . This is the most distant detection of nebular emission lines to date. For MACS0647-JD we derive z=10.170 0.003 , consistent with the previous spectroscopic analysis of this galaxy .

We derive best-fit models with (N_ H i / cm^ 2 )= 22.10 0.20 for CEERS-43833, (N_ H i / cm^ 2 )= 22.20 0.40 for CEERS-16943, and (N_ H i / cm^ 2 )= 22.40 0.15 for MACS0647-JD. The uncertainties represent the 16th and 84th percentiles of the posterior distributions and includes the systematic uncertainty of 0.1 dex due to variations in the intrinsic galaxy continuum emission templates.

The inferred column densities also correspond to \ gas surface densities of ( _ gas / M_ pc ^ 2 ) = 2.0 (CEERS-43833), ( _ gas / M_ pc ^ 2 ) = 2.1 (CEERS-16943), and ( _ gas / M_ pc ^ 2 ) = 2.3 (MACS0647-JD), respectively, larger than the gas surface densities observed in typical galaxies at z 0 . Based on the SFRs and physical sizes of the galaxies, we derive SFR surface densities of ( _ SFR / M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 ) = 1.3 0.3 (CEERS-43833) and ( _ SFR /M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 ) = 0.9 0.3 (CEERS-16943), and approximately ( _ SFR / M_ yr^ 1 kpc^ 2 ) 2 for MACS0647-JD.

gmw_00001715.name: SMACS z16a
gmw_00001715.overview: SMACS z16a is a compact, gravitationally lensed galaxy candidate identified at a redshift of approximately 16 [arXiv:2207.12338, arXiv:2305.17959]. It has an extremely blue UV slope [arXiv:2208.05473] and is characterized by a young stellar population that rapidly assembled its mass [arXiv:2207.12338].

sources.summary: SMACS z16a is consistently selected as a z~15.9 galaxy about 250 Myr after the Big Bang, compact, sub-L* at z>10, young, and rapidly assembling stellar mass.

quotes.quote: Our highest redshift galaxy, SMACS z16a, is consistently identified by the dropout selection and the two SED fitting procedures, as shown in Fig. fig:SEF-fit-id-2455 . The photometric redshifts derived by both codes (cf. section sec:SED-fitting ) are in excellent agreement: z=15.92^ +0.17 _ 0.15 and z=15.89 0.43 for BEAGLE and EAZY respectively. This places the galaxy just 250 Myr after the Big Bang. We derive a stellar mass of (M_ / M _ )=8.79^ +0.32 _ 0.33 and a stellar age of (t_ age / yr )=7.65^ +0.36 _ 0.39 for SMACS z16a. This galaxy is also very compact, as can be seen in Fig. fig:candidate-stamp-id-2455 , with an estimated size of 0.4 kpc based on the model fitting of SE++ (cf. section sec:se++ ) and after correcting for the lensing distortion.

With an intrinsic UV luminosity of M_ UV =-20.59 0.15 , we find that SMACS z16a is fainter than typical z>10 galaxies in the literature and close to the characteristic luminosity M^ at z 10 . The best-fit SED of SMACS z16a is compatible with a young age of (t_ age / yr )=7.65^ +0.36 _ 0.39 and a stellar mass of (M_ / M _ )=8.79^ +0.32 _ 0.33 . The bulk of its stellar mass build-up took place in the last 20 Myr.

sources.summary: The paper treats SMACS z16a as a possible z = 16 lensed galaxy, finding many high-redshift analogues only in a boosted star-formation model and also considering low-redshift contaminants.

quotes.quote: In the field of SMACS, Atek2023MNRAS.519.1201A identified two galaxies at z 16 - SMACS z16a and SMACS z16b (see also Adams2023MNRAS.518.4755A,Harikane2023ApJS..265....5H ), which have intrinsic UV magnitudes of around 20.5 . To account for gravitational lensing, we also demagnify their observed SEDs by a factor of 2.18 and 1.13 and update the uncertainties to further incorporate errors of the lensing models. The exact values are taken as the average magnification among different strong lensing models based on Furtak2023MNRAS.519.3064F .

From maxSF , we identify 2886(505) high-redshift galaxies possessing similar SEDs as the observed one for SMACS z16a(b). On the other hand, when looking at the fiducial model, only 34(3) low-redshift analogues are found at z 5 . As these two z 16 candidates possess qualitatively similar SEDs, we only discuss analogues of SMACS z16a, and present SED examples of its analogues as well as their distribution as functions of various properties in Fig. fig:smacs_z16a_lowz_analogues_prop .

For SMACS z16a, these two solutions correspond to the different redshifts inferred by z=15.92^ +0.17 _ 0.15 Atek2023MNRAS.519.1201A and by z=10.61^ +0.51 _ 8.55 Harikane2023ApJS..265....5H , with the latter rejecting SMACS z16a as being z 16 because the colour F200W-F277W is not red enough.

sources.summary: SMACS z16a is treated as the highest-redshift candidate; its SED fit has parameter degeneracies, an extremely blue UV slope, and a robust high-redshift interpretation.

quotes.quote: We also show an example of a best-fit SED and the posterior distribution of BEAGLE parameters for one object, SMACS z16a, in Fig. fig:z16a_fit as an example. As can be seen in Fig. fig:z16a_fit and in Tab. tab:galay_parameters , while the SED-fit is relatively good, there remain some parameter degeneracies between the stellar mass, the SFR and the age which result in the relatively large uncertainties on these parameters.

To illustrate this, we also fit the photometry of SMACS z16a in a separate BEAGLE run using only stellar continuum templates. As can be seen from the blue line in Fig. fig:continuum-fit , this run without nebular continuum much better fits the UV-slope of this galaxy.

We therefore estimate that the sources in our sample with the steepest UV-slopes 2.5 , which include our highest-redshift candidate SMACS z16a at z 16 and two of our z 12 candidates, are most probably robust high-redshift galaxies.

gmw_00001716.name: SMACS z16b
gmw_00001716.overview: SMACS z16b is a candidate high-redshift galaxy identified at a redshift of approximately 16 [arXiv:2305.17959]. The galaxy is gravitationally lensed, with its observed spectral energy distribution demagnified by an estimated factor of 1.13 to account for this lensing effect [arXiv:2305.17959].

sources.summary: The paper treats SMACS z16b as a possible z = 16 lensed galaxy, demagnifying its SED and finding hundreds of high-redshift analogues in the boosted star-formation model.

quotes.quote: In the field of SMACS, Atek2023MNRAS.519.1201A identified two galaxies at z 16 - SMACS z16a and SMACS z16b (see also Adams2023MNRAS.518.4755A,Harikane2023ApJS..265....5H ), which have intrinsic UV magnitudes of around 20.5 . To account for gravitational lensing, we also demagnify their observed SEDs by a factor of 2.18 and 1.13 and update the uncertainties to further incorporate errors of the lensing models. The exact values are taken as the average magnification among different strong lensing models based on Furtak2023MNRAS.519.3064F .

From maxSF , we identify 2886(505) high-redshift galaxies possessing similar SEDs as the observed one for SMACS z16a(b). On the other hand, when looking at the fiducial model, only 34(3) low-redshift analogues are found at z 5 . As these two z 16 candidates possess qualitatively similar SEDs, we only discuss analogues of SMACS z16a, and present SED examples of its analogues as well as their distribution as functions of various properties in Fig. fig:smacs_z16a_lowz_analogues_prop .

gmw_00001717.name: MCG 9-17-9
gmw_00001717.overview: MCG 9-17-9 is a small, fairly face-on Sb-Sc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1509.00350]. It is undergoing an interaction with the neighboring galaxy NGC 3079, as evidenced by a physical neutral hydrogen bridge and a stripped gas tail [arXiv:1509.00350, arXiv:0908.3979].

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG 9-17-9 as a small face-on Sb-Sc spiral near NGC 3079, with new H I data revealing an ongoing interaction traced by a physical bridge.

quotes.quote: MCG 9-17-9 is a small spiral galaxy located about 6.5 arcmin ( 33 kpc) north-west of NGC 3079 (see Fig. \ f:totHI2 ). It is classified as Sb-Sc, and seen fairly face on. Very little is known about this galaxy other than from observation of the NGC 3079 environment e.g., b1,piets98 . Bright X-ray emission ( L_ X 1.59 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 ) has been detected by piets98 which was considered rather high for such a small spiral galaxy. The \ appeared undisturbed in the interaction of the group in b1 .

The new \ data, however, reveal that an interaction between MCG 9-17-9 and NGC 3079 is ongoing. This is evident by the \ `bridge' detected between the two galaxies that overlaps both spatially (in projection) and in velocity space, suggesting a physical link between the two galaxies. This can be seen clearly in Fig. \ f:stream where the p-v slice along the bridge is shown.

sources.summary: The paper describes MCG 9-17-9 as interacting with NGC 3079, connected by an HI bridge, and likely losing gas through stripping by NGC 3079’s X-ray halo.

quotes.quote: MCG 9-17-9: This galaxy appears to have an interaction with NGC 3079. The HI bridge between the two galaxies is evidence for this (see Fig. f:interaction ), and it seems a gas tail which has been stripped off MCG 9-17-9 during the interaction. The X-ray halo of NGC 3079 is strong enough to be responsible for stripping off gas from MCG 9-17-9.

gmw_00001718.name: SDSS J100311.18+553557.6
gmw_00001718.overview: SDSS J100311.18+553557.6 is a member of the NGC 3079 galaxy group [arXiv:1509.00350]. It is interacting with the larger galaxy NGC 3079 [arXiv:1509.00350, arXiv:0908.3979], displaying an neutral hydrogen tail or bridge that points toward its companion [arXiv:1509.00350, arXiv:0908.3979].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J100311.18+553557.6 as an NGC 3079-group member interacting with NGC 3079, possibly through an H I tail pointing toward the main galaxy.

quotes.quote: Four of the group members (MCG 9-17-9, NGC 3073, SDSS J100311.18+553557.6 (labeled A) and [ YGK81 ] 078 (labeled C)) are seen interacting with NGC 3079.

The \ bridge appears to extend to the other side of NGC 3079, towards the other small galaxies SDSS J100311.18+553557.6, and SDSS J100331.69+553121.1 (marked A and B respectively in Fig. \ f:totHI2 ). While it is possible that this means that the bridge extends this far, it is also possible that this \ forms another tail, emanating from SDSS J100311.18+553557.6 because this galaxy seems to have an \ tail pointing towards NGC 3079.
Among these, two of them ( [ YGK81 ] 078 and SDSS J100311.18+553557.6) seem to be taking part in the interactions in the group.

sources.summary: The paper tentatively identifies SDSS J100311.18+553557.6 as a third galaxy in the NGC 3079 interaction, with HI at its optical position forming a bridge toward NGC 3079.

quotes.quote: Another interesting result (although tentative) obtained from our data is that the presence of a third galaxy that seems taking part in the interaction with NGC 3079. An HI gas has been detected at the position of the optical galaxy SDSS J100311.18+553557.6 (no previous redshift was available) at a velocity of 1050 km s ^ 1 . This HI gas exhibits a tail like structure forming a bridge toward NGC 3079 (see Fig f:interaction ).

gmw_00001719.name: W1835+4355
gmw_00001719.overview: W1835+4355 is a hyperluminous, hot dust-obscured galaxy at redshift z ~ 2 [arXiv:1807.06022]. It hosts a heavily obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1807.06022] near the starburst/AGN transition [arXiv:2010.10641], exhibiting a prodigious star formation rate and characteristics of a late-stage merger [arXiv:1807.06022].

sources.summary: The paper presents NuSTAR and archival X-ray/SED analysis of W1835+4355, confirming a hyperluminous Compton-thick hot DOG with extreme star formation and a likely late-stage merger phase.

quotes.quote: We present a 155 ks \ observation of the z 2 hot dust-obscured galaxy (hot DOG) W1835+4355. We extracted spectra from the two \ detectors and analyzed them jointly with the archival \ PN and MOS spectra. We performed a spectroscopic analysis based on both phenomenological and physically motivated models employing toroidal and spherical geometry for the obscurer. In all the modelings, the source exhibits a Compton-thick column density 10^ 24 , a 2-10 keV luminosity L_ 2-10 2 10^ 45 , and a prominent soft excess ( 5-10 of the primary radiative output), which translates into a luminosity 10^ 44 .

In all cases we obtained a bolometric luminosity L_ bol 3-5 10^ 47 , which confirms the hyperluminous nature of this active galactic nucleus. Finally, we estimate a prodigious star formation rate of 3000 yr^ 1 , which is consistent with the rates inferred for z 2-4 hyperluminous type I quasars. The heavily obscured nature, together with L_ bol , the ratio of X-ray to mid-infrared luminosity, the rest-frame optical morphology, and the host star formation rate are indicative of its evolutionary stage. We can interpret this as a late-stage merger event in the transitional, dust-enshrouded, evolutionary phase eventually leading to an optically bright AGN.

sources.summary: W1835+4355 has blended-line covariance, poor H-beta detections affecting extinction estimates, and a classification near the starburst/AGN transition.

quotes.quote: The effect is most notable for W1835+4355, W2216+0723, and W2313-2417 and is present to a lesser extent in W1801+1543 and W2238+2653.
The unphysical A_ V,narrow value obtained for W1835+4355 and large errors on other targets are a result of the poor quality of the \ detections in many targets.

W0338+1941 and W1724+3455 lack broad \ emission and are unambiguously in the star-formation dominated region, while W2238+2653 and W1835+4355 are near the starburst/AGN transition, with the classification depending on the inclusion of the broad/blueshifted \ emission in the / \ ratio.

gmw_00001720.name: object 1
gmw_00001720.overview: Object 1 is a dusty z-dropout galaxy candidate that exhibits evidence of reddening [arXiv:1002.1090]. Photometric modeling of the galaxy indicates broad allowed ranges for its age, stellar mass, and star-formation rate [arXiv:1002.1090].

sources.summary: The paper fits object 1 as a dusty z-dropout galaxy candidate, finding significant reddening and broad allowed ranges in age, stellar mass, and star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: For example, we find evidence of significant reddening in object 1, with A_V 1--2.6, in agreement with the best-fit value of A_V=1.2 given by Capak09 . The corresponding 2 \ distribution is shown in Fig. \ fig_capak_av .

The corresponding range of ages, SFRs, and stellar masses for both objects, obtained with and without nebular emission, are illustrated in Figs. \ fig_capak_age to fig_capak_sfr . Approximately (within 2 1-2.3) object number 1 (3) has thee best-fit model parameters t 0--30 (10-200) Myr, (M_ ) 10.8--12. (10.6--11.6) , and SFR 100--10 ^6 (10--10 ^4 ) .

For comparison, using the standard SFR(UV) calibration of Kenn98 and assuming z=8 , one obtains SFR _ obs 240 \ without extinction correction for object 1, and SFR _ corr 6500 \ adopting A_V=1.2 and Calzetti's attenuation law.

gmw_00001721.name: object 3
gmw_00001721.overview: Object 3 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with moderate extinction [arXiv:1002.1090]. It has a possible flux dip between the J and K bands, which may be explained by the 2175 Angstrom dust absorption feature [arXiv:1002.1090].

sources.summary: The paper models object 3 as a high-redshift candidate with moderate extinction, uncertain age and mass, and a possible flux dip consistent with dust absorption.

quotes.quote: The situation is similar for object 3, although for more moderate extinction ( A_V 0.6--1.6). The corresponding range of ages, SFRs, and stellar masses for both objects, obtained with and without nebular emission, are illustrated in Figs. \ fig_capak_age to fig_capak_sfr . Approximately (within 2 1-2.3) object number 1 (3) has thee best-fit model parameters t 0--30 (10-200) Myr, (M_ ) 10.8--12. (10.6--11.6) , and SFR 100--10 ^6 (10--10 ^4 ) .

As noted by Capak09 , object 3 is not detected in H, possibly indicating a dip in the flux between J and K (see Fig. \ fig_capak_3 ). If real, this dip could be explained by the 2175 \ dust absorption feature, as also mentioned by Capak09 . Using the Galactic extinction law from Seat79 provides excellent fits with A_V 1.6 , as shown in Fig. \ fig_capak_3 .

gmw_00001722.name: object 2502
gmw_00001722.overview: Object 2502 is a high-redshift galaxy with a very young stellar population of solar metallicity [arXiv:1002.1090]. The galaxy exhibits strong intrinsic Lyman-alpha emission and nebular continuum emission, which produce unusually blue colors in photometric observations [arXiv:1002.1090].

sources.summary: The paper uses object 2502 to illustrate how nebular emission can produce unusually blue WFC3 colors and alter z-dropout selection at redshifts near 7-8.

quotes.quote: Such a case is illustrated in Fig. \ fig_lyaboost , showing a fit to object 2502 from the Mclure09 sample. Shown here is a model of a very young stellar population with solar metallicity including lines and nebular continuum emission redshifted to either z=6.96 (dashed, magenta line) or z=7.97 (solid, black line). In the latter case, the strong intrinsic \ emission (with W( )^ rest 200 ) boosts the flux in both \ and \ since these filters overlap by 0.1 . This provides a very blue ( ) colour, and enough flux in \ to ensure that this object does not appear as a drop even at z 8 .

gmw_00001723.name: Galaxy 8
gmw_00001723.overview: Galaxy 8 is a red galaxy characterized by a disturbed morphology [arXiv:1312.0950]. It exhibits submillimeter emission and has an uncertain redshift that is nominally modeled at z=2.9 [arXiv:1312.0950].

sources.summary: The paper describes Galaxy 8 as a singly imaged red galaxy with disturbed morphology, uncertain redshift, and submillimeter emission important for modeling the arc.

quotes.quote: Galaxy 8 is not multiply imaged, but has similar colours to the rest of the multiply-imaged galaxies and has a disturbed morphology. If it is at the same redshift as the interacting group, our lensing model predicts no multiple images, and thus we have no constraints on its redshift from the lensing model. However, we have found that submm emission originating from near its position is important for reproducing the morphology of the submm arc (see   section:results ), and thus we have included it in our model (see   model ).
The redshift of Galaxy 8 is set to a nominal value of z=2.9 and we report the lensed far-IR luminosity and SFR for this galaxy.

gmw_00001724.name: PKS 1502 + 036
gmw_00001724.overview: PKS 1502+036 is a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1305.0572, arXiv:1406.1934] that hosts a core-jet structure [arXiv:1305.0572, arXiv:1205.0402] and a relativistic jet [arXiv:1305.0572, arXiv:1503.01480]. It exhibits multi-wavelength flux variations [arXiv:2006.09411, arXiv:2009.00630], including subdaily gamma-ray flares [arXiv:1911.03500, arXiv:1609.04434] and polarization plane rotations [arXiv:1807.10485].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PKS 1502 + 036 as a gamma-ray-emitting narrow-line Seyfert 1 whose luminosity, radio variability, one-sided structure, and 15 GHz VLBA morphology indicate a relativistic jet.

quotes.quote: We report on multifrequency observations of the ray emitting narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy PKS 1502 + 036 performed from radio to rays during 2008 August--2012 November by Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT), Swift (X-Ray Telescope and Ultraviolet/Optical Telescope), Owens Valley Radio Observatory, Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA), and Very Large Array. No significant variability has been observed in rays, with 0.1--100 GeV flux that ranged between (3--7) 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 using 3-month time bins. The photon index of the LAT spectrum ( = 2.60 0.06) and the apparent isotropic ray luminosity (L _ 0.1-100 GeV = 7.8 10 ^ 45 erg s ^ 1 ) over 51 months are typical of a flat spectrum radio quasar. The radio spectral variability and the one-sided structure, in addition to the observed ray luminosity, suggest a relativistic jet with a high Doppler factor.

A core-jet structure has been resolved at 15 GHz in VLBA images of PKS 1502 + 036. The main difference with respect to SBS 0846 + 513 seems to be a fainter jet-like structure and no observed apparent superluminal motion. In Fig. MWL , we compare the ray light curve collected by Fermi LAT during 2008 August--2012 November to the X-ray (0.3--10 keV), UV ( m2 filter), optical ( u and V filters) and radio (15 GHz) light curves collected by Swift , CRTS and OVRO. No significant flaring activity was detected in rays.

sources.summary: The paper reports a 2015 gamma-ray flare from PKS 1502 + 036 and models average and flaring SEDs with external-Compton emission requiring changed electron and magnetic-field parameters.

quotes.quote: Received; in original form After a long low-activity period, a ray flare from the narrow-line Seyfert 1 PKS 1502 + 036 ( z=0.4089 ) was detected by the Large Area Telescope (LAT) on board Fermi in 2015. On 2015 December 20 the source reached a daily peak flux, in the 0.1--300 GeV band, of (93 19) 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , attaining a flux of (237 71) 10 ^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 on 3-hr time-scales, which corresponds to an isotropic luminosity of (7.3 2.1) 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 . The ray flare was not accompanied by significant spectral changes. We report on multi-wavelength radio-to- ray observations of PKS 1502 + 036 during 2008 August--2016 March by Fermi LAT, Swift , XMM-Newton , Catalina Real-Time Transient Survey, and the Owens Valley Radio Observatory (OVRO).

We compared the broad-band SED of the 2016 flaring activity state with that from an average state of PKS 1502 + 036 observed in 2012. Both the SED show a Compton dominance 10. This high value indicates that the EC emission is the main mechanism for producing rays, such as for FSRQ e.g., finke13 , confirming the similarities between ray emitting NLSy1 and FSRQ. The two SED, with the high-energy bump modelled as an EC component of seed photons from a dust torus, could be modelled by changing both the electron distribution parameters and the magnetic field.

sources.summary: The review describes PKS 1502 + 036 as a Fermi-detected NLSy1 with large flares, subdaily variability, boosting signatures despite limited proper motion, and a likely structured jet.

quotes.quote: Four radio-loud NLSy1 galaxies have been detected at high significance by Fermi LAT in the first year of operation (i.e., 1H 0323 + 342, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, and PKS 2004 447 PKS 2004 447 has a weak Fe II emission line with respect to the typical NLSy1 (for a discussion about the nature of this source see e.g., oshlack01,gallo06, baldi16 ). ) and are included in the First Fermi LAT source catalog, based on the first 11 months of LAT operation (1FGL; abdo10 ).

Some of the ray-emitting NLSy1 do exhibit large-amplitude ray flares: SBS 0846 + 513 dammando12,dammando13a (see Figure LATflare , left panel); PMN J0948 + 0022 dammando15b,foschini11b (see Figure LATflare2 , left panel), 1H 0323 + 342 paliya14 , PKS 1502 + 036 dammando16b , PKS 2004 447 gokus19 , with an apparent isotropic ray luminosity of 10 ^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , comparable to that of bright FSRQ. This is another indication that NLSy1 are able to host relativistic jets as powerful as those in FSRQ. Variability was observed during all ray flares on a daily time-scale, and a subdaily time-scale in case of PKS 1502 + 036 dammando16b,paliya16a (see Figure LATflare , right panel).

On the contrary, no apparent superluminal motion has been detected for PKS 1502+036 during 2008--2012, although the radio spectral variability, the one-sided jet-like structure, and the ray properties seem to require the presence of boosting effects dammando13b . The lack of significant proper motion in PKS 1502 + 036 has been confirmed by lister16 , with only a sub-luminal component detected. This result seems to resemble the `Doppler factor crisis' observed in bright TeV BL Lacs (e.g., piner04, piner10 ). However, the SED of PKS 1502+036, in particular the high Compton dominance Compton dominance is the ratio of the peak Compton luminosity to peak synchrotron luminosity. (see Section SED ) does not resemble a TeV BL Lac, but an FSRQ. This may suggest the presence of a structured jet in this NLSy1, with different regions having different Lorentz factors (i.e., a fast spine and a slower layer) (see e.g., piner18 ).

sources.summary: PKS 1502 + 036 is included among the Swift-monitored jetted NLSy1, with optical, UV, and X-ray variability used to assess jet-driven emission.

quotes.quote: A strong correlation between X-ray, UV, and optical emission and simultaneous flux variations have been observed in 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022 as expected in case the jet radiation is the dominant mechanism. Correlated multiband variability favours the jet-dominated scenario also in FBQS J1644 + 2619 and PKS 2004--447. The summed X-ray Telescope spectra of 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and FBQS J1644 + 2619 are well fitted by a broken power law with a break around 2 keV.

Moreover, in order to compare the variability at different frequencies, we quantify the observed variability of 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, FBQS J1644 + 2619, and PKS 2004--447 in optical, UV, and X-rays bands through the variability amplitude, V_ amp , calculated as the ratio of maximum to minimum flux corrected for Galactic extinction. The variability amplitude may depend on the sampling of the light curves at the different frequencies. However, at least for a comparison between different bands, the simultaneity of observations in optical, UV, and X-rays guaranteed by the Swift observations helps mitigate this problem.

The variability amplitude in X-rays of the six sources varies between 2.7 and 8.5. These jetted NLSy1 are less variable than the other non-jetted NLSy1, suggesting that a different emission mechanism is driving the flux variations.

sources.summary: The paper says PKS 1502+036 is a variable high-frequency peaker rejected as a young radio source, with VLBA evidence for a core-dominated jet and possible moving component.

quotes.quote: Based on simultaneous multifrequency (from 1.4 to 22 GHz) VLA observations carried out in several epochs, PKS 1502+036 turned out to possess strong spectral and flux density variability (Fig. FMJ-f4 ). During these epochs the spectral peak was at 7.6, 7.1, 6.4, 6.5, and 8.8 GHz, respectively. Variability is a typical property displayed by blazars, while young radio sources are non-variable objects. For this reason PKS 1502+036 was rejected from the HFP sample of young radio sources mo08 .

PKS 1502+036 is unresolved on the typical VLA scales (Fig. FMJ-f5 ). However, when imaged with the parsec scale resolution provided by VLBA observations, its radio structure is marginally resolved and a second component seems to emerge from the core. In the VLBA images at 15 GHz the double radio structure is clearly resolved suggesting a core-jet structure.

sources.summary: The paper presents J1505+0326 as a gamma-ray active RLNLS1 with low WISE-era flux, possible star-forming activity masked by jet synchrotron emission, and compact multifrequency radio structure.

quotes.quote: J0324+3410, J1505+0326, and J2007 4434, which were detected after the first year of Fermi operations (Abdo et al. 2009c).

Specifically, J0948+0022 and J1505+0326 were among the most ray active RLNLS1s of the present sample, therefore this result could be counterintuitive (except for J0948+0022, which is still near the synchrotron line). The explanation is in the epochs of the WISE observations, performed between 2010 Jan 7 (MJD 55203 ) and Aug 6 (MJD 55414 ). Comparing with the ray light curves displayed in Foschini et al. (2012), it is seen that J0948+0022 was almost undetected during the WISE observations. In the case of J1505+0326, D'Ammando et al. (2013a) detected the source by integrating over three-month time bins, but the flux in the first half of 2010 remained at low level of order 10^ 8 ph cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . Therefore, it is likely that these sources could have strong star-forming activity that is sometimes overwhelmed by synchrotron emission from the jet.

The only source for which there are multifrequency observations is J1505+0326, from 2 to 43 GHz. A detailed analysis is reported b D'Ammando et al. (2013a). It is very compact (0.95--0.97 at 15 GHz during flares), at level comparable to J0948+0022.

sources.summary: The paper finds PKS 1502+036 is usually Fermi-detected at low flux, non-variable in its analysis, shows a possible softer-when-brighter trend, and is fit well by a power law.

quotes.quote: PKS 1502+036 has been detected by Fermi LAT most of the times, however, at a low flux level of 4.5 10 ^ 8 , as can be seen in its monthly binned light curve in Figure lc . The five years averaged analysis gives a TS value of 435 which corresponds to 20 detection. On comparing the results obtained here with that of 2013MNRAS.433..952D , the average flux and photon index values are found to be quite similar despite the fact that we have included one more year of the LAT data as compared to theirs. The similarity seen in both analyses can be justified by the fact that PKS 1502+036 is hardly detected by Fermi LAT in the period MJD 56,235 56,597 (see the third panel from top in Figure lc ) which is not covered by them. The ^2 analysis classifies the source to be a non-variable.
PKS 1502+036 shows a `softer when brighter' behavior whereas no conclusion can be drawn about PKS 2004 447 owing to the small number of data points.
For PKS 1502+036 and PKS 2004 447, the PL model fits the observations very well.

sources.summary: The paper says PKS 1502 + 036 shows type-1b-like radio variability, highly variable convex spectra, high-frequency events, and characteristic timescales of roughly 60-80 days.

quotes.quote: PKS 1502 + 036: This source shows a variability behaviour similar to type 1b although more epochs are needed for a definite classification. Its spectrum is highly variable displaying periods of convex shape. Its low-band part ( 8 GHz) varies between flat and highly inverted, while the higher frequencies ( 10 GHz ) can show spectral index as steep as 0.5 .

The light curve shows at least 2 events better seen at higher frequencies. The high frequency cut-off of the spectrum prohibits IRAM monitoring. The typical time scales identified here are of the order of 60 - 80 days.

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1502 + 036 as a Fermi-detected gamma-ray NLSy1 whose radio variability and one-sided jet imply relativistic Doppler boosting.

quotes.quote: Five radio-loud NLSy1 galaxies have been detected at high significance by Fermi LAT so far: 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, and PKS 2004 447 , with a redshift between 0.061 and 0.585. The average apparent isotropic luminosity of these sources in the 0.1--100 GeV energy band is between 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 and 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , a range of values typical of blazars . This may be an indication of a small viewing angle with respect to the jet axis and thus a high beaming factor for the ray emission, similarly to blazars.

On the contrary, VLBA observations did not detect apparent superluminal motion at 15 GHz for PKS 1502 + 036 during 2002 2012, although the radio spectral variability and the one-sided jet-like structure seem to require the presence of boosting effects in a relativistic jet .  Strong radio variability was observed at 15 GHz during the monitoring of the OVRO 40-m telescope of PMN J0948+0022 , PKS 1502+036 , and SBS 0846+513 . An inferred variability brightness temperature of 2.5 10 ^ 13  K, 1.1 10 ^ 14  K, and 3.4 10 ^ 11  K was obtained for PKS 1502 + 036, SBS 0846 + 513, and PMN J0948 + 0022, respectively.

sources.summary: PKS 1502+036 is described as a radio-loud NLSy1 galaxy undergoing its first GeV outburst, with soft gamma-ray spectrum, high luminosity, and modeled synchrotron plus inverse-Compton emission.

quotes.quote: Stalin ^1 ^1 Indian Institute of Astrophysics, Block II, Koramangala, Bangalore-560034, India ^2 Department of Physics, University of Calicut, Malappuram-673635, India vaidehi@iiap.res The ray loud narrow line Seyfert 1 ( NLSy1) galaxy PKS 1502+036 ( z=0.409 ) exhibited its first ray outburst on 2015 December 20. In the energy range of 0.1-300 GeV, the highest flux measured by Fermi Large Area Telescope is (3.90 1.52) 10 ^ 6 , which is the highest ray flux ever detected from this object. The associated spectral shape is soft ( _ 0.1-300  GeV =2.57 0.17 ) and this corresponds to an isotropic ray luminosity of (1.2 0.6) 10 ^ 48 .

We generate the broadband spectral energy distribution (SED) during the GeV flare and reproduce it using a one zone leptonic emission model. The optical-UV spectrum can be explained by a combination of synchrotron and the accretion disk emission, whereas, the X-ray to ray SED can be satisfactorily reproduced by inverse-Compton scattering of thermal photons originated from the torus. The derived SED parameters hint for the increase in the bulk Lorentz factor as a major cause of the flare and the location of the emission region is estimated as outside the broad line region but still inside torus.

sources.summary: J1505 + 0326 is one of the best-sampled jetted NLSy1s; the paper reports two EVPA-rotation candidates, including a 309.5 deg event, and argues intrinsic EVPA evolution is more likely than pure noise.

quotes.quote: J1505 + 0326: a possible long rotation of the polarisation plane We start with J1505 + 0326 (Fig. fig:t_p_evpa_long lower panel) because it: (i) comprises a study case for our approach for the assessment of the reliability of the detected long rotation, and (ii) because it is the case with the largest probability that the detected apparent rotation is driven by an intrinsic event. From the periods of significant EVPA variability two qualify to long ones and are marked with solid lines in Fig. fig:t_p_evpa_long . For the longest event (lower panel, blue solid line) the polarisation plane rotates by almost 309.5^ at a mean rate of / t 3.7 deg d ^ 1 .

After 25 10^4 iterations we find that the probability of finding a full rotation, is P full rotation; | _ intr | 309.5^ | d _ intr /dt=0 =6 10^ 4 fairly lower than the 23 estimated earlier. From this we conclude that although it is indeed possible that noise alone can cause the observed phenomenology, it is much more unlikely that an intrinsic evolution of the EVPA is driving the observed behaviour. On the basis of the assumption that the intrinsic event is obeying a constant rotation rate ( d _ intr /dt= constant ) we compute the most probable rate and examine whether the observed rotation is consistent with the predictions of this assumption.

sources.summary: The paper includes PKS 1502 + 036 among three promising NLSy1 galaxies for high-energy modeling, noting its first-year Fermi-LAT discovery and model comparisons.

quotes.quote: A comprehensive sample of narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxies has been discussed by , who collected from literature twenty sources. Among them, three sources seem to be the most promising ones to be investigated at energies above a few tens of GeV, namely SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and PKS 1502 + 036. We briefly report here their discovery literature, while for a detailed description of their high-energy characteristics we refer to and references therein.
PKS 1502 + 036 ( z=0.408 ) was discovered as a source in , by using the first year of Fermi-LAT data (2008 August 4--2009 August 5).

As reported in Section Sec:sample , we chose SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and PKS 1502 + 036 because, in principle, during flaring events they could be detected by the next generation Cherenkov Telescope Array , as discussed in , where the authors considered a detailed treatment of absorption in the radiation fields of the BLR as a function of the location of the ray emission region with parameters inferred from observational constraints . In their Figure 7, the authors compared the log-parabola models for the three sources (whose spectral parameters where derived from the 4FGL), renormalised to the integrated 100 MeV to 100 GeV flux as extrapolated by adopting the power-law models detailed in their Table 1, with respect to the expected spectra due to the BLR internal absorption.

sources.summary: The paper reports previous day-scale optical/UV flux changes for PKS 1502+036, but no significant hour-scale Swift-UVOT variability because the source is faint and sparsely observed.

quotes.quote: In dammando20 , we have analysed the optical, ultraviolet (UV), and X-ray observations performed by the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory during 2006 July--2019 April of six ray-emitting NLSy1 (i.e., 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, FBQS J1644 + 2619, and PKS 2004--447) to investigate their flux variability and spectral changes. A significant flux change has been observed in the optical and UV bands on time-scale of one day for 1H 0323 + 342 and a few days for SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and PKS 1502 + 036, clearly related to an increase of the non-thermal emission from a relativistic jet. On a long-term scale, a large variability amplitude has been observed for all six ray-emitting NLSy1, significantly higher than the variability observed in other radio-loud NLSy1 not detected in rays. These results have confirmed the dominance of the jet emission in the optical and UV part of their spectrum.

For PKS 1502 + 036 and FBQS J1644 + 2619, the lack of variability on hours time-scale can be mainly related to the small number of Swift -UVOT observations with multiple exposures available at the same epoch. Moreover, in case of PKS 1502 + 036, the source is relatively faint (see the average magnitude in Table Average_summed in Appendix UVOT_Appendix ), therefore the large uncertainties obtained due to the short observation time of the single exposures prevent us to detect significant changes of magnitude.

sources.summary: The paper says J1505+0326 has a radio spectrum peaking at 7 GHz, compact VLBI emission, centimeter-wave variability, and new evidence for relativistic beaming.

quotes.quote: J1505+0326 : It has radio fluxes peaked at 7 GHz tin05 . Its VLBI images are dominated by a very compact component, marginally resolved at 2.3 GHz band dal98 . The radio emission at centimeter wavelengths shows variability.

For the remaining two, J1505+0326 and J1548+3511, the evidence for relativistic beaming is presented for the first time. For J1505+0326, this result is consistent with its VLBI images made at 2.3 GHz, which are dominated by a very compact component, marginally resolved at a few tens milli-arcsec resolution dal98 .

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 1502+036 as a very radio-loud NLS1 with VLBA core-jet structure, detected rapid variability, and a literature SED used for modeling.

quotes.quote: PKS 1502+036 ( z=0.409 , Orienti et al. 2012). It is also a very RL NLS1 galaxy, with R_ RL =1549 (Yuan et al. 2008). The VLBA observations at 15 GHz show that it has a core jet structure (Orienti et al. 2012). The intranight optical variability (Paliya et al. 2013b) and the rapid IR variability (Jiang et al. 2012) were also detected. We get only one broadband SED from literature for this source, as shown in Figure SEDs (b), which is taken from Paliya et al. (2013a).

sources.summary: The paper characterizes J1505 + 0326 as a radio-loud NLSy1 with weak flares, mild spectral evolution, a mostly convex spectrum, and expected high-frequency polarization.

quotes.quote: In these terms J1505 + 0326 shows very weak flaring events.

J1505 + 0326 however imitates the behaviour of type ``5b'' in with only mild spectral evolution. Its spectrum is mostly convex, and at times shows high frequency components expanding fast towards lower bands.
Polarisation measurements above 43 GHz and above 10.45 GHz for J0948 + 0022 and J1505 + 0326, respectively, should disclose a significant degree of polarisation.

sources.summary: The paper finds a likely genuine absorption feature in PKS 1502+036, with a narrow Gaussian line at about 0.57 keV identified as Galactic O VII absorption.

quotes.quote: PKS 1502+036: In this case, there is no improvement to the fit by freeing the column density of the Galactic absorption, and the MOS spectra are consistent with the pn. It is likely, therefore, that there is a genuine absorption feature in the spectrum of PKS 1502+036. Fitting with a narrow Gaussian line improves the fit by ^2 of 17, for 2 additional degrees of freedom, and gives an energy of 0.57 0.01 keV ( 22 ) in the observed frame. This is the rest frame energy of the 1s--2p line of O vii , so the feature is most likely produced by additional Galactic O vii absorption not accounted for in the model.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1502 + 036 as a flaring NLSy1 with subdaily gamma-ray variability, a 2012 XMM-Newton power-law spectrum with residuals, and compared average and flaring SEDs.

quotes.quote: Variability was observed during all ray flares on a daily time-scale ; in case of PKS 1502 + 036 sub-daily variability was observed  .
The XMM-Newton spectrum of PKS 1502 + 036 collected in 2012 was quite well fit by a simple power-law model, although some residuals are observed at low and high energies.
For PKS 1502 + 036 the SED of an average activity state was compared with that of the 2015 flaring state.

gmw_00001725.name: PKS 2004 447
gmw_00001725.overview: PKS 2004 447 is a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1205.0402, arXiv:1301.5785] hosted within a face-on, pseudo-bulge spiral galaxy [arXiv:2303.12109]. It features a relativistic, core-dominated radio jet [arXiv:1503.01480, arXiv:2209.11376] that exhibits strong beaming [arXiv:2209.11376] and drives multi-wavelength variability [arXiv:2006.09411, arXiv:2009.00630].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2004 447 as a gamma-ray radio-loud NLS1 with uncertain classification, compact radio structure, and possible young-radio-source or blazar interpretations.

quotes.quote: PKS 2004 447 is a powerful ( L_ 1.4 GHz 1.3 10^ 26 W/Hz) radio source at redshift z=0.24 and it is one of the four radio-loud NLS1 detected in gamma rays during the first year of Fermi operation abdo09b . However its classification is still uncertain. oshlack01 classified PKS 2004 447 as a genuine radio-loud NLS1 based on the optical definition. At radio wavelengths it is characterized by a steep ( > 0.5, S( ) ^ ) synchrotron spectrum at high frequency (above 8.4 GHz) and the absence of significant flux density variability.

The radio structure has an angular size of 45 mas (170 pc) with a position angle of 50 ^ , and it is resolved into 3 main components. The lack of the spectral index information does not allow us to unambiguously classify the sub-components. For example the compact component A can be either the core of a core-jet boosted blazar, or a bright hotspot of an asymmetric young radio source.

sources.summary: The paper finds multiband variability in PKS 2004--447 favoring a jet-dominated scenario and includes it among jetted NLSy1 with optical, UV, and X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: A strong correlation between X-ray, UV, and optical emission and simultaneous flux variations have been observed in 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022 as expected in case the jet radiation is the dominant mechanism. Correlated multiband variability favours the jet-dominated scenario also in FBQS J1644 + 2619 and PKS 2004--447. The summed X-ray Telescope spectra of 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and FBQS J1644 + 2619 are well fitted by a broken power law with a break around 2 keV.

Moreover, in order to compare the variability at different frequencies, we quantify the observed variability of 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, FBQS J1644 + 2619, and PKS 2004--447 in optical, UV, and X-rays bands through the variability amplitude, V_ amp , calculated as the ratio of maximum to minimum flux corrected for Galactic extinction. The variability amplitude may depend on the sampling of the light curves at the different frequencies. However, at least for a comparison between different bands, the simultaneity of observations in optical, UV, and X-rays guaranteed by the Swift observations helps mitigate this problem.

The variability amplitude in X-rays of the six sources varies between 2.7 and 8.5. These jetted NLSy1 are less variable than the other non-jetted NLSy1, suggesting that a different emission mechanism is driving the flux variations.

sources.summary: Paper identifies PKS 2004-447 as the only source above the hybrid jet-model line, attributing its high jet power to strong beaming or magnetization-driven outflows.

quotes.quote: Here, the jet power of only one source (PKS 2004-447) is above the red line. There are two possible explanations for one source. One explanation is that the one source has a strong beaming effect, and it has high jet power (PKS 2004-447).

found a compact core by using the Very-Long-Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) observation in PKS 2004-447. The PKS 2004-447 is a core-dominated, one-sided parsec-scale jet with a strong beaming effect. also found that the PKS 2004-447 have a high Doppler factor ( D_ factor =30 ). These results suggest that the PKS 2004-447 have a strong beaming effect. Thus, it has high jet power.

sources.summary: The paper studies PKS 2004-447, a jetted gamma-ray narrow-line Seyfert 1 in a face-on spiral host, finding post-flare redshifted broad-line enhancements linked to jet-BLR interaction.

quotes.quote: PKS 2004-447 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) harbouring a relativistic jet with ray emission. On 2019-10-25, the Fermi Large Area Telescope captured a ray flare from this source, offering a chance to study the broad-line region (BLR) and jet during such violent events. This can provide insights to the BLR structure and jet interactions, which are important for active galactic nuclei and host galaxy coevolution. We report X-Shooter observations of enhancements in the broad line components of Balmer, Paschen and He i lines seen only during the post-flare and vanishing 1.5 years after. These features are biased redward up to 250 km s ^ 1 and are narrower than the pre-existing broad line profiles.

PKS 2004-447 (R.A. 20h 07m 55s, Dec. -44d 34m 44s, z 0.24), the main focus of this paper, is a ray NLS1. The source has the spectral energy distribution of beam jetted AGN gallo06, kreikenbohm16, gokus21 and is hosted in a face-on, pseudo-bulge spiral galaxy kotilainen16 . The radio morphology of PKS 2004-447 suggests an angle relative to line-of-sight of <50 schulz16 , similar to the NLS1, 3C 286 berton17, an17, yao21 , although different to other sources with <10 angles e.g., d13, lister16 .

sources.summary: The paper presents X-ray monitoring of PKS 2004 447 as a gamma-ray loud narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy with power-law X-ray spectrum, variability, and possible harder-when-brighter behavior.

quotes.quote: The Innermost Regions of Relativistic Jets and Their Magnetic Fields X-ray monitoring of the radio and ray loud Narrow-Line Seyfert 1 Galaxy PKS 2004 447 A. Kreikenbohm 1,2 akreikenbohm@astro.uni-wuerzburg.de M. Kadler 1 J. Wilms 2 R. Schulz 1,2 C. M u ller 2,1 R. Ojha 3 E. Ros 4,5,6 K. Mannheim 1 D. Els a sser 1 Lehrstuhl f u Astronomie, Universit a t W u rzburg, W u rzburg, Germany Dr. Remeis Sternwarte ECAP, Universit a t Erlangen-N u rnberg, Bamberg, Germany ORAU/NASA/GSFC Greenbelt, MD, USA Observatori Astron o mic, Univ. Val e ncia, Spain Dept. Astronom i a y Astrof i sica, Univ. Val e ncia, Spain Max-Planck-Institut f u Radioastronomie, Bonn, Germany We present preliminary results of the X-ray analysis of and observations as part of a multi-wavelength monitoring campaign in 2012 of the radio-loud narrow line Seyfert 1 galaxy . The source was recently detected in rays by among only four other galaxies of that type. The 0.5 10 keV X-ray spectrum is well-described by a simple absorbed powerlaw ( 1.6 ). The source brightness exhibits variability on timescales of months to years with indications for spectral variability, which follows a ``bluer-when-brighter`` behaviour, similar to blazars.

The active galaxy is a ray loud NLS1 galaxy at a redshift of z=0.24 . First observations yield optical properties consistent with the formal NLS1 classification, (flux ratio O [ III ] /H =1.6 , H _ FWHM =1447 km s ^ 1 ) , but exhibit atypical weak Fe II emission ( EW_ Fe II 10 ) . The black hole mass is estimated to be approximately 10^ 7 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper reports low-energy spectral residuals in PKS 2004 477, finds simple Galactic absorption inadequate, and suggests intrinsic ionized absorption can substantially improve the fit.

quotes.quote: PKS 2004 477: This object shows a similar low energy feature as the other two sources, and the pn spectrum shows some additional possible features around 2--3 keV. These higher energy residuals are not present in the MOS data however, so we focus on the low energy feature. As with PKS 1502+036, freeing the column density does not improve the overall fit. Including a narrow line with the energy fixed at 0.57 keV improves the fit by ^2 of 6, for 1 degree of freedom, but does not remove the residuals and the fit is still not acceptable ( ^2_ =1.38 ), so the residual cannot be due simply to Galactic O vii .

The other possibility is that the absorption is intrinsic to the source. Because of the source redshift ( z=0.24 ) the feature is too high energy to be due to neutral absorption, but absorption from ionized gas could produce such a feature. Fitting for warm absorption in the rest frame of the source with a general xstar grid from Walton13 , we find that the fit is greatly improved ( ^2/ dof of 72/63) relative to just Galactic absorption.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2004 447 as an NLS1 candidate/narrow-line radio galaxy with changing optical flux, no big blue bump, and unusually high jet power.

quotes.quote: PKS 2004 447 ( z=0.24 , Oshlack et al. 2001). It was identified as a candidate of NLS1 galaxy since its optical spectrum is consistent with the criteria of NLS1 galaxy classification, i.e., FWHM (H ) =1447 km s ^ 1 and [ OIII ] /H 1.6 (Drinkwater et al. 1997). The weak Fe II lines make it classified as a narrow-line radio galaxy (Komossa et al. 2006). It was included in the CRATES catalog of flat spectrum objects (Healey et al. 2007) due to its flat spectrum ( 0.3 , S( ) ^ ) below 4.8 GHz, but it has a steep spectrum ( > 0.5 ) above 8.4 GHz (Orienti et al. 2012).

Observations show that the optical flux of PKS 2004 447 drops by a factor of 4 over an interval of several years and there is no evidence of a big blue bump (Oshlack et al. 2001). Abdo et al. (2009a) suggested that the jet power of PKS 2004 447 is well above the typical range of radio galaxies, favoring the hypothesis of a genuine NLS1 galaxy. We get only one broadband SED from literature for this source, as shown in Figure SEDs (b).

sources.summary: The review treats PKS 2004 447 as a distinctive gamma-ray NLSy1 whose steep radio spectrum coexists with beamed-FSRQ-like broadband properties and a one-sided parsec-scale jet.

quotes.quote: By integrating the first year of the LAT data, three more NLSy1s (1H 0323+342, PKS 1502+036, and PKS 2004 447) were detected in the ray band with > 5 significance (Abdo et al. 2009c).

In particular, one of the NLSy1s, PKS 2004 447, stands out unique as far as the radio properties of this class is concerned. This object exhibits a steep radio spectrum (Oshlack et al. 2001; Gallo et al. 2006; Schulz et al. 2016) suggesting its similarity with the parent population of flat spectrum NLSy1 galaxies (Berton et al. 2015). However, its broadband spectral properties resembles well with FSRQs, i.e., beamed AGNs. This includes the detection of a flat X-ray spectrum, a core-dominated one-sided parsec-scale jet, and a Compton dominated broadband SED (Gallo et al. 2006; Paliya et al. 2013b). These observations has led to the inclusion of PKS 2004 447 in the sample of flat radio spectrum, i.e., beamed, NLSy1 galaxies (e.g., Foschini et al. 2015).

sources.summary: The paper detects hour-scale optical variability in PKS 2004--447 for the first time, constraining a compact jet-emitting region and noting additional lower-significance events.

quotes.quote: Rapid variability has been significantly detected on hours time-scale for 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and PKS 2004--447 in 18 observations for a total of 34 events. In particular, we report the first detection of significant variability on short time-scale (3--6 ks) in optical for PKS 2004--447, and UV for 1H 0323 + 342 and PMN J0948 + 0022. The shortest variability time-scale observed for 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and PKS 2004--447 (assuming a Doppler factor = 10) gives a lower limit on the size of emission region between 9.7 10 ^ 14 (for SBS 0846 + 513) and 1.6 10 ^ 15 cm (for 1H 0323 + 342), suggesting that the optical and UV emission during these events is produced in compact regions within the jet. These observations provide unambiguous evidence about the relativistically beamed synchrotron emission in these sources, similar to blazars.

PKS 2004--447 : similarly to SBS 0846 + 513, the quite large uncertainties on the magnitudes estimated in single exposures, related to the relatively faintness of the source (see Table Average_summed in Appendix UVOT_Appendix ) reduce the significance of the change of magnitude observed. A few other events with a large change of magnitude but at a significance of 2 < < 3 have been observed, with a mag of 0.387 (on 2012 September 30) and 0.791 (on 2014 March 14) in w1 filter, and 0.712 (on 2013 October 20) and 0.854 (on 2016 May 1) in w2 filter.

sources.summary: The review discusses PKS 2004 447 as a radio-loud narrow-line Seyfert 1, a compact steep-spectrum example, and one of the early Fermi gamma-ray detections in the class.

quotes.quote: The third case followed soon: PKS 2004 447 ( z=0.24 ) was discovered by Oshlack et al. (see also ).

Gallo et al. found that one clear example of RLNLS1/CSS was PKS 2004 447. Although Komossa et al. cited this source, they did not include in their sample on the basis of a weak FeII bump, which in turn would suggest a classification of this AGN as narrow-line radio galaxy or type II AGN. However, Gallo et al. noted that there is no formal threshold for the intensity of Fe II, thus holding the classification as RLNLS1.
After one year of Fermi operations, the number of ray detected RLNLS1s increased to four , with the addition of 1H 0323 + 342 (cf ), PKS 2004 447 (cf ), and PKS 1502 + 036 (cf ).

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2004 447 as the faintest gamma-ray source in the sample, with low photon statistics, no reliable spectral curvature, and a power-law spectrum.

quotes.quote: PKS 2004 447 has the lowest values for the ray flux (1.42 0.24 10 ^ 8 ) and the TS (106) among NLSy1 galaxies and was detected by Fermi LAT only on a few occasions. Hence no definite conclusion can be drawn about its flux variability behavior owing to the low photon statistics as we could not get a significant value for the F_ var .

Further, the curvature index for PKS 2004 447 is found to be high and appears non-realistic as there is no significant curvature found in the ray spectrum of this source. Thus, the unusual value may be indicating the poor photon statistics associated with the observation and hence seems unreliable.
For PKS 1502+036 and PKS 2004 447, the PL model fits the observations very well.

sources.summary: The paper cites PKS 2004-447 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 whose spectropolarimetry supports equatorial scattering and shows virial black-hole mass can be severely underestimated.

quotes.quote: We recently obtained spectropolarimetric observations with FORS2 at the VLT of the narrow line Seyfert 1 PKS 2004-447 (NLSy1 are type 1 AGN in which the broad-line FWHM are < 2000 km/s) (see baldi16 ). We recovered all features typical of the equatorial scattering model (ESM, smith05 ); in particular its polarized spectrum shows two peaks separated by 9,100 km/s. The broadening with respect to the FWHM of 1447 km s ^ 1 measured in direct-light oshlack01 is a dramatic indication that in this source the M_ BH value based on the virial method is underestimated by a factor 40 (). PKS 2004-447 is a very challenging target for spectropolarimetric observations. Indeed, the ESM predicts that the percentage of polarization decreases for objects seen close-to face-on, a condition met by this source as witnessed by its detection (and rapid variability) in rays by Fermi calderone11 , clear evidence for a relativistic jet oriented at a small angle to the line-of-sight.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2004-447 as a Fermi-detected compact steep-spectrum NLSy1 at z 0.240, using VLT/FORS2 spectra to include it among three jetted narrow-line Seyfert 1 sources.

quotes.quote: The ray detection supports the presence of a relativistic jet as the origin of the radio power for these sources komossaetal06 . They were selected because high-quality optical spectra were available for the three of them. The three ray sources are 1H 0323+342 ( B2 0321+33 1H 0323+342 J03246+341), 3C 286 ( J13311+305), and PKS 2004-447 ( J20079--445).
Also J20079 ( z 0.240 ) is classified as both a CSS and a NLSy1 oshlacketal01,galloetal06,bertonetal21 and, as J03246, it belongs to the first batch of NLSy1s detected by Fermi abdoetal09 .

The spectra of these three sources were obtained using the Asiago 1.22m Telescope for J03246 (full description of the data reduction in foschinietal15 ), the SDSS archive for J13311, and the FORS2 instrument on the Very Large Telescope for J20079--445 bertonetal21 .

sources.summary: The paper includes J2007 4434 despite classification doubts, noting proposed narrow-line radio-galaxy status, gamma-ray detection, and an unusually low Eddington rate for the sample.

quotes.quote: We also note that there has been some doubt about the classification of J2007 4434 as NLS1 because of its weak Fe ii emission: Komossa et al. (2006a) proposed a classification as narrow-line radio galaxy, while Gallo et al. (2006) argued that since there is no quantitative criterion on the intensity of Fe ii , the source can be considered to be a genuine RLNLS1. We follow the latter interpretation and include J2007 4434 in our sample.
J0324+3410, J1505+0326, and J2007 4434, which were detected after the first year of Fermi operations (Abdo et al. 2009c).

It is worth noting one outlier, J2007 4434, has a low Eddington rate ( 0.003L_ Edd ): this was also one of the RLNLS1s whose nature is questionable on account of its weak Fe ii emission (Gallo et al. 2006, Komossa et al. 2006).

sources.summary: The paper includes PKS 2004 447 among Fermi-detected gamma-ray NLSy1 galaxies and reports a pc-scale core-jet structure in radio observations.

quotes.quote: Five radio-loud NLSy1 galaxies have been detected at high significance by Fermi LAT so far: 1H 0323 + 342, SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, and PKS 2004 447 , with a redshift between 0.061 and 0.585. The average apparent isotropic luminosity of these sources in the 0.1--100 GeV energy band is between 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 and 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 , a range of values typical of blazars . This may be an indication of a small viewing angle with respect to the jet axis and thus a high beaming factor for the ray emission, similarly to blazars.

On pc scale a core-jet structure was observed for SBS 0846+513 (Fig.  Fig3 ), PKS 2004 447 , PKS 1502 + 036 , and PMN J0948 + 0022 , although the jet in the two latter sources is significantly fainter than that observed in the former two sources.

sources.summary: The paper reports VLT spectropolarimetry of PKS 2004-447, finding polarization consistent with equatorial scattering seen face-on, a much broader polarized H line, and a revised black-hole mass typical of radio-loud AGN.

quotes.quote: We here report the results of a pilot study of VLT spectropolarimetric observations of the RL NLSy1 PKS 2004-447. Its polarization properties are remarkably well reproduced by models in which the scattering occurs in an equatorial structure surrounding its broad line region, seen close to face-on. In particular, we detect a polarized H line with a width of 9,000 km s ^ 1 , 6 times broader than the width seen in direct light. This corresponds to a revised estimate of M_ BH 6 10^8 M _ , well within the typical range of RL AGN.

sources.summary: The paper notes weak soft-excess evidence in PKS 2004-447, unusual X-ray break energies across observations, and previous host-galaxy work reporting a pseudo-bulge morphology.

quotes.quote: We note that the evidence for a soft excess is weak in PKS 2004-447, where there is a tentative detection in only one of the three observations Gallo2006,Orienti2015,Kreikenbohm2016 .

For all the sources plotted in Fig. bknpow_all , the break energies are in the range 1.6--2.1 keV, with the exception of PKS 2004-447. In this source the break energy is 0.6 keV in the first observation Gallo2006 and 2--3 keV in the later observations Orienti2015 , though the broken power law is not statistically significant in the latter case.

The other two host galaxies that have been studied are those of 1H0323+323, which exhibits an irregular ring morphology, possibly indicating a recent merger Leon2014 , and PKS 2004-447, for which reported a pseudo-bulge morphology.

sources.summary: The review describes PKS 2004 447 as a Fermi-detected NLSy1 with weak Fe II emission, little soft X-ray excess, and a weak-disc SED dominated by torus-seeded external Compton emission.

quotes.quote: Four radio-loud NLSy1 galaxies have been detected at high significance by Fermi LAT in the first year of operation (i.e., 1H 0323 + 342, PMN J0948 + 0022, PKS 1502 + 036, and PKS 2004 447 PKS 2004 447 has a weak Fe II emission line with respect to the typical NLSy1 (for a discussion about the nature of this source see e.g., oshlack01,gallo06, baldi16 ). ) and are included in the First Fermi LAT source catalog, based on the first 11 months of LAT operation (1FGL; abdo10 ).

Only two of these sources do not have a significant soft excess: TXS 2116 077 yang18 and PKS 2004 447 orienti15, kreikenbohm16 , although a tentative soft excess was reported in one of the two XMM-Newton observations performed in 2012 for the latter source gallo06 .

As for SBS 0846 + 513, PMN J0948 + 0022, and PKS 1502 + 036, the high-energy bump of the SED of PKS 2004 447 is modelled with an external Compton scattering of dust torus seed photons in orienti15 . The disc luminosity of PKS 2004 447 obtained by the SED modelling is particularly weak, L_ disc = 1.8 10 ^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , consistent with the value estimated on the basis of the optical spectrum foschini15 .

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 2004 447 as a gamma-ray NLSy1 with XMM-Newton soft-excess constraints, hard power-law spectra, and inverse-Compton modeling with dust-torus seed photons.

quotes.quote: An indication of a weak soft excess was observed in the XMM-Newton spectrum of PKS 2004 447 in 2004  .

On the contrary, the X-ray spectra of PKS 2004 447 collected in May and October 2012 are well reproduced by a single power-law with a hard photon index ( _ X 1.7) and no significant soft X-ray excess  .
In the case of PKS 2004 447, the inverse Compton component is modelled with an EC scattering of dust torus seed photons, as for the SED of SBS 0846 + 513 and PMN J0948 + 0022 .

gmw_00001726.name: Arp 10
gmw_00001726.overview: Arp 10 is a collisional ring galaxy characterized by an inner ring, an outer ring, and an extended outer arc [arXiv:astro-ph/0601589, arXiv:astro-ph/0501601]. Both rings exhibit radial gas motions [arXiv:astro-ph/0601589, arXiv:astro-ph/0501601] and asymmetric expansion velocities [arXiv:astro-ph/0601589, arXiv:0908.0603], likely triggered by a dwarf elliptic satellite intruder [arXiv:astro-ph/0501601].

sources.summary: The paper reports FPI observations of Arp 10, finding two rings, radial motions, asymmetric outer-ring expansion, and evidence that the rings have a collisional origin.

quotes.quote: We have observed with FPI a pecular galaxy Arp 10, which has two rings (the inner and outer one), and extended outer arc. The H _ velocity field shows evidence for significant radial motions in both outer and inner galactic rings. We fit a model velocity field taking into account the regular rotation and projection effects. The expansion velocity of the NW part of the outer ring exceeds 100 km/s, whereas it attain only 30 km/s at the SE part . Therefore, the asymmetric shape of the outer ring (fig.  mois:fig1 b) may be caused by a systematic difference in the ring expansion velocity and collisional origin of the rings is a proven fact.

sources.summary: The paper studies Arp 10 as a collisional ring galaxy, finding expanding rings, radial gas motions, stellar age gradients, and a likely dwarf satellite intruder.

quotes.quote: We present results of Fabry-Perot and long-slit spectroscopy of the peculiar galaxy Arp 10. The ionized gas velocity field shows evidence for significant radial motions in both outer and inner galactic rings. Long-slit spectroscopy reveals gradients of age and metallicity of stellar population in agreement with the propagating nature of star formation in the galaxy. We present strong evidence that a small ``knot'' at 5 arcsec from the center of Arp 10 is its dwarf elliptic satellite, the most probable ``intruder'' responsible for triggering the expanding rings in Arp 10.

sources.summary: The review treats Arp 10 as a collisional ring galaxy with observed gas motions, asymmetric outer-ring expansion velocities, and little evidence for powerful ring-edge shocks.

quotes.quote: The kinematical studies of the gas motion in Arp 10 and the Cartwheel were performed by fosbury77 , charmandaris96 , higdon96 , amram98 .
One more argument for this scenario is a lack of powerful shockwaves at the outer edge of the rings, as it is noticed for the case of Arp 10 bizyaev07 .
The expansion velocity in the NW part of the outer ring in Arp 10 exceeds 100 km s ^ 1 , whereas it attains only 30 km s ^ 1 in the SE part.

gmw_00001727.name: GX
gmw_00001727.overview: GX is a probable galaxy located in the vicinity of image C of the gravitationally lensed system SDSS J1029+2623 [arXiv:1209.0458]. Mass modeling indicates that this galaxy acts as a substructure whose mass can explain the anomalous flux ratio of the lensed image [arXiv:1209.0458].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GX as a probable galaxy near quasar image C whose modeled mass can explain the anomalous flux ratio in SDSS J1029+2623.

quotes.quote: In the ACS/F814W image, we find a probable galaxy in the vicinity of image C, as shown in Figure  fig:sdss1029bc_sub . We fit the galaxy, named GX, assuming a Sersic profile with index n=4 using galfit peng02 , and find the centroid of the galaxy to be 0 4 from image C (R.A.=157.309482, Decl.=26.394158), and that the magnitude of the galaxy is m_ F814W 24.3 . These estimates have large uncertainties due to the significant residuals from the subtraction of image C (see Figure  fig:sdss1029bc_sub ).

The inferred velocity dispersion of GX is large enough to mean that it will significantly perturb the flux of image C. Mass modelling  We next explore the impact of GX on the strong lens models. Here we assume the same mass model as used in Section  sec:slens , but include an additional component modelled by pseudo-Jaffe model at the location of GX with the velocity dispersion and the cutoff radius as free parameters. We find that the best-fit model has ^2=7.5 including the flux ratios, which is significantly smaller than the ^2 of the original best-fit model ( ^2=30 ) and the large improvement is due to improvements in fitting the flux ratios. This confirms the argument by kratzer11 that a substructure in the vicinity of image C can explain the anomalous flux ratio.

gmw_00001728.name: Galaxy A
gmw_00001728.overview: Galaxy A is the brighter dwarf galaxy in a pair located at a redshift of z=0.30 [arXiv:1902.02360]. It exhibits an irregular morphology with a faint tidal tail, star-forming emission, and ordered gas rotation [arXiv:1902.02360].

sources.summary: Galaxy A is the brighter dwarf in a z=0.30 pair, with irregular morphology, star-forming emission, ordered gas rotation, and a possible tidal tail.

quotes.quote: A zoom-in on the system demonstrates the irregular morphology of Galaxy  A , which is the brighter of the two galaxies, with bright knots that may be due to short-lived periods of enhanced star formation, and a faint tidal tail extending North-West from the galaxy. This disturbed morphology is reminiscent of NGC7796-DW1, the early type dwarf companion to the isolated elliptical NGC 7796 that is thought to have accreted cold, metal-rich gas from NGC 7796 Richtler_2019 . No obvious bridge can be seen connecting Galaxies  A and B , and no other companions lie within the field-of-view. Due to seeing effects on the ground based data, these features are not evident in the data used throughout this study, although the light profiles of both galaxies are visibly different from that of the nearby globular cluster also marked in the zoom-in image.

This map reveals that Galaxy  A is rotating, with a global kinematic position angle of 111 10 \ as measured by the method described in Appendix C of Krajnovic_2006 , which is roughly aligned with the major axis. Rotation in Galaxy  B is much more tentative due to its smaller size on the sky, with the same analysis resulting in a kinematic position angle of 140 44 . In order to confirm these results, the emission velocity in each spaxel was measured again using cross correlation for the brightest emission lines in the spectrum (H , [ O iii ] _ 5007 and [ O ii ] _ 3727/3729 ). Cross correlation was applied to every spaxel in both galaxies, using the same emission features in the brightest spaxel of Galaxy  A as the template since measuring offsets relative to this spaxel would emphasize any significant offsets in the gas emission between the galaxies. The relative pixel shifts measured by this technique showed the same rotation in Galaxy  A , confirming that it is rotating, while no clear rotation was detected in Galaxy  B .

gmw_00001729.name: Galaxy B
gmw_00001729.overview: Galaxy B is a dwarf companion galaxy located in a pair at a redshift of z=0.30 [arXiv:1902.02360]. It has a stellar mass of 10^8.2 solar masses, a Sersic index of 0.5, and shows no clear rotation [arXiv:1902.02360].

sources.summary: Galaxy B is the smaller dwarf companion in the z=0.30 pair, with a Sersic fit, tentative/no clear rotation, lower stellar mass, and metallicity similar to Galaxy A.

quotes.quote: The fit to Galaxy A resulted in a S ersic index of 1.7 and 1.4 in the g^ and i^ bands respectively, while the fit to Galaxy B required a S ersic index of 0.5 in both bands. Galaxy B was modelled similarly well with a PSF profile, with consistent magnitudes, but in this study we will use the S ersic profile for consistency between the two targets. The foreground globular cluster was also modelled as a PSF profile at the same time to reduce contamination to the galaxy light from this source.

The stellar masses of galaxies  A and B were found to be 10 ^ 8.8 0.3   M _ and 10 ^ 8.2 0.2   M _ respectively, putting them into the dwarf mass regime. The uncertainties were estimated through a series of Monte Carlo simulations, creating a normal distribution for the magnitudes with the same mean and standard deviation as those measured from the galaxy pair, and taking 1000 random values from within this distribution and repeating the stellar mass calculation outlined above.

Rotation in Galaxy  B is much more tentative due to its smaller size on the sky, with the same analysis resulting in a kinematic position angle of 140 44 . In order to confirm these results, the emission velocity in each spaxel was measured again using cross correlation for the brightest emission lines in the spectrum (H , [ O iii ] _ 5007 and [ O ii ] _ 3727/3729 ). Cross correlation was applied to every spaxel in both galaxies, using the same emission features in the brightest spaxel of Galaxy  A as the template since measuring offsets relative to this spaxel would emphasize any significant offsets in the gas emission between the galaxies. The relative pixel shifts measured by this technique showed the same rotation in Galaxy  A , confirming that it is rotating, while no clear rotation was detected in Galaxy  B .

gmw_00001730.name: NGC 7742
gmw_00001730.overview: NGC 7742 is a face-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1209.2429, arXiv:2307.04590] hosting a weakly active nucleus [arXiv:2307.04590] and a circumnuclear starburst ring [arXiv:1209.2429, arXiv:astro-ph/0603161]. This ring contains counter-rotating gas and young star clusters [arXiv:1711.08982, arXiv:2202.01759], which likely originated from a past minor merger [arXiv:1711.08982, arXiv:1803.01501].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7742 with MUSE, finding a counter-rotating, externally accreted gas component, a warped inner disk, and prolonged ring star formation tied to a minor merger.

quotes.quote: We present results from MUSE observations of the nearly face-on disk galaxy NGC 7742. This galaxy hosts a spectacular nuclear ring of enhanced star formation, which is unusual in that it is hosted by a non-barred galaxy, and also because this star formation is most likely fuelled by externally accreted gas that counter-rotates with respect to its main stellar body. We use the MUSE data to derive the star-formation history (SFH) and accurately measure the stellar and ionized-gas kinematics of NGC 7742 in its nuclear, bulge, ring, and disk regions. We map the previously known gas counter-rotation well outside the ring region and deduce the presence of a slightly warped inner disk, which is inclined 6 degrees compared to the outer disk.

We find a prolonged SFH in the ring with stellar populations as old as 2--3 Gyr and an indication that the star formation triggered by the minor merger event was delayed in the disk compared to the ring. There are two separate stellar components: an old population that counter-rotates with the gas, and a young one, concentrated to the ring, that co-rotates with the gas. We recover the kinematics of the old stars from a two-component fit, and show that combining the old and young stellar populations results in the erroneous average velocity of nearly zero found from a one-component fit.
We show further evidence that this ring has its origin in a minor merger event, possibly 2--3 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC7742's circumnuclear ring, treating counter-rotating gas and lack of bars as minor-merger evidence while finding its young clusters formed nearly simultaneously.

quotes.quote: We have analyzed the circumnuclear ring of the spiral galaxy NGC7742 in order to understand its formation and evolution. We have obtained gaseous abundances, characterized the interstellar medium of the clusters and studied the properties of the ionizing clusters.

In this work we have studied NGC7742, which is associated to a group of disk galaxies without stellar bars and without evidence for current interactions with other galaxies. An alternative mechanism for the formation of nuclear rings is a minor merger and this idea has already already been suggested by 2006ApJ...649L..79M and 2006AJ....131.1336S . Moreover, its ring is counter-rotating 2002MNRAS.329..513D,2018A&A...612A..66M , i.e. the gas rotates in the opposite sense to the stellar component. That is why this galaxy constitutes an ideal candidate for such hypothesis and an interesting object of study.

We can conclude that young stars in the ring of NGC7742 have been formed, almost simultaneously, and they have an age younger than 7.5 Ma. In most cases an underlying stellar population can be identified, besides that of the galaxy disk, pointing to a composite population in the ring.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 7742 as a face-on SA()b galaxy with a weakly active nucleus, circumnuclear star-forming ring, counter-rotating gas, and a likely minor-merger origin.

quotes.quote: In this first paper we present the study of the physical properties of the CNSFRs in the ring of the face-on galaxy NGC 7742 using MUSE observations publicly available and the full spectral region observed, from 4800 to 9300 Å. NGC 7742 is classified as an SA()b galaxy.
