Several observable quantities give consistent evidence that the CR transport in the halo of NGC 4013 is diffusive: the frequency dependence of the synchrotron scale height, the disk/halo flux density ratio, the vertical profile of the synchrotron spectral index, the small propagation speed measured modeled with spinnaker , and the low temperature of the X-ray emitting hot gas. galaxies: magnetic fields - individual galaxies: NGC 4013 - galaxies: halos CHANG-ES XIX: The galaxy NGC 4013 Stein et al.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4013 is a highly inclined Sa galaxy with a small X-shaped B/P bulge, weak warping, foreground-star contamination, and no visible surface-brightness asymmetry.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013 is a highly-inclined Sa galaxy, hosting an X-shaped structure (visible in the unsharp mask in Fig.  fig:sample ) associated with a B/P bulge, of small size compared to the galaxy Buta2015 . The disc shows evidence of weak warping, at |x| 6.0 kpc, which produces a weak quadrupolar signal visible in the outer part of the mid-plane asymmetry map. Moreover, there is a bright star near the centre of the galaxy, which causes a clear dipolar signal in the central region of the asymmetry image.

The mid-plane asymmetry diagnostics are shown in Fig.  fig:sample and Fig.  fig:AMP_real_galaxies , bottom-right panel. The X-shaped feature extends to x 1.5  kpc. A clear dipolar signal is visible at x 0.8 kpc, due to presence of the bright star near the galaxy's centre. No asymmetries in the surface brightness are visible. We adopt z_ min =0.3 kpc and z_ max =1.0 kpc to calculate the mid-plane asymmetry profile, which remains flat in the entire range (i.e. x < 5.7  kpc).

sources.summary: NGC 4013 is described as a LINER spiral with a warped disc, clear dust lane, modeled dust scalelengths, and marginally resolved vertical FIR/submillimetre emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013 At a distance of 18.6 Mpc, NGC 4013 has a D_ 25 diameter of 5 2, is variously classified as an Sbc 1999A&A...344..868X , an Sb or an SAb type galaxy with H ii regions and is considered a LINER galaxy. Furthermore, the galaxy possesses a box-shaped bulge 1986AJ.....91...65J, 1987A&AS...70..465D and shows a very clear warp in various bands but sometimes in opposite directions for the dust, gas and stellar component of the galaxy disc 1987Natur.328..401B, 1990MNRAS.246..458S, 1991A&A...242..301F, 1995A&A...295..605B, 1996A&A...306..345B , although 1999A&A...344..868X claim the stellar distribution in their optical images has no warp significant enough to affect their model. The inclination angle estimates for this galaxy vary from 89 7 0 1 1999A&A...344..868X to 89 89 2007A&A...471..765B . Apart from a bright foreground star blocking the view, the dust lane in this galaxy can be clearly traced from the centre out to the furthest edges as well as out of the central plane in optical images from 1999AJ....117.2077H , especially at larger radii where the bulge no longer dominates. Its dust distribution has been previously modelled with global fits to optical extinction data in 1999A&A...344..868X and 2007A&A...471..765B , resulting in dust scalelengths varying from 45 or 3.9 kpc 1999A&A...344..868X to 30 or 2.7 kpc 2007A&A...471..765B , corresponding to about 25 longer 1999A&A...344..868X , respectively shorter 2007A&A...471..765B , than the stellar disc scalelength.

Finally, for NGC 4013, we resolve the vertical structure marginally at the 5 limit in the PACS 100 and 160 m bands. With an inclination of 89 7, this galaxy has the closest to exactly edge-on orientation of all galaxies in the sample, and the vertical structure cannot be due to the projection along the line-of-sight of the radial structure.

sources.summary: The radiative-transfer fit works well in both bands for NGC 4013, revealing a visible clumpy dust lane and a V-band dust scale-length smaller than the stellar one.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013: the fit is generally good for both bands, with residuals smaller than 30 in 85 of the fitted data points. The dust lane can be easily seen in both bands. It shows a clumpy structure which lead to larger residuals along the major axis, especially in the V-band.

A bulge with Sersic index n=4 was used for this object. The V-band fit has parameters similar to those obtained by XilourisSub1998 , with the exception of h_ d , which I find to be smaller than h_ s , rather then larger. This also lead to a larger _0^V obtained here.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4013 as an edge-on Sb galaxy with thin and thick disks plus an extended component, arguing its structure reflects merger-driven heating and later disk formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013 is a nearby Sb edge-on galaxy known for its ``prodigious'' \ warp and its ``giant'' tidal stream. Previous work on this unusual object shows that it cannot be fitted satisfactorily by a canonical thin+thick disk structure. We have produced a new decomposition of NGC 4013, considering three stellar flattened components (thin+thick disk plus an extra and more extended component) and one gaseous disk.

We find evidence for NGC 4013 indeed having a thin and a thick disk and an extra flattened component. This smooth and extended component (scaleheight z_ EC 3 kpc) could be interpreted as a thick disk or as a squashed ellipsoidal halo and contains 20 of the total mass of all three stellar components. We argue it is unlikely to be related to the ongoing merger or due to the off-plane stars from a warp in the other two disk components.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4013 needs three nested disks with truncations plus bar and nuclear components, while its unusually thick structure may reflect warp, flare, or tidal disturbance.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013 Comeron11b performed 1D disk fits to this galaxy and found that three stellar disks were required to fit the IRAC 3.6 m vertical light profile. They adopt the same distance for NGC 4013 as we do, and their thinnest disk has h_ z 125 pc while their intermediate-height disk has h_ z 565 pc, comparable to the super-thin and thin disks in the MW and NGC 891. Furthermore, they found that the thickest of these three disks had a scaleheight of 3 kpc, about two times larger than `normal' for a traditional thick disk.

It is clear from Figure fig:ngc4013fitplot that NGC 4013 requires an inner truncation for all three disks as well as an outer truncation for the thinnest disk only. Therefore, as for NGC 891 we add an abrupt inner truncation to all three disk components, as well as an outer truncation for just the super-thin disk. Following our procedure for NGC 891, to fit the inner portions of NGC 4013 we added an exponential disk with an outer truncation at the radius of the inner disk truncation to mimic a bar component, as well as a nuclear star-forming disk.

sources.summary: The paper adds NGC 4013 to an edge-on galaxy sample, deriving CO and H I maps, rotation curves, scale heights, volume densities, and star-formation-law behavior.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The galactic centre of the map ( = 11 ^h 58 ^m 31 34 and = 43 56 50.72 ) is placed at the x offset=0 and the minor axis offset=0.

quotes.quote: We observed \ toward NGC 4013 using CARMA in C and D configurations with a velocity coverage of 446 \ in 2008 and reduced them using the MIRIAD package: the task MFCAL for bandpass and gain calibration and the task BOOTFLUX for flux calibration. The calibrated data were combined with OVRO observations in the C, L, and H (U) configurations using a 7-point mosaic 2004IAUS..217..166S via the MIRIAD task INVERT to increase the sensitivity and image fidelity. We obtained the average rms noise of 0.2 K from line-free edge channels of the combined cube. The spatial and velocity resolutions of the combined image are 2.80 \ 2.08 \ (natural weighting) and 10 , respectively. The systemic velocity ( V_ sys ) is 835 1995A&A...295..605B .
The galactic centre of the map ( = 11 ^h 58 ^m 31 34 and = 43 56 50.72 ) is placed at the x offset=0 and the minor axis offset=0.

sources.summary: The paper re-analyzes NGC 4013's radio halo with consistent methods, finding similar L-band/HBA scale heights and a central flat spectral-index structure.

quotes.quote: With newly reduced radio continuum data of five edge on galaxies (NGC 891, NGC 3432, NGC 4013, NGC 4157, and NGC 4631), we plan to set new constraints on the morphology of radio halos and the physical properties of galactic winds driven by stellar feedback. By distinguishing between the central and outer regions of the galaxies, our study setup allows us to search for variations in the radio halo profile or CR transport along the Galactic disk.

We further include NGC 4013, which was already studied by 2019A&A...632A..13S who found a diffusion-dominated radio halo. We chose to re-analyse this galaxy with consistent methods, as we expect a more accurate measurement of the radio halo intensity profile due to the newly processed data sets.

In NGC 4013, we also detect a gradient in the non-thremal SPIX. However, we find an extended region in the centre of the galaxy with very flat SPIX values ( _ nth < -0.7 ) and the gradient in the outer regions seems to be less prominent.

sources.summary: The paper models the optical-to-far-infrared SED of NGC 4013 as an edge-on spiral and finds young stars dominate dust heating while normalized star-formation rates resemble comparable disk galaxies.

quotes.quote: Using tools previously described and applied to the prototype galaxy NGC 891, we model the optical to far-infrared spectral energy distributions (SED) of four additional edge-on spiral galaxies, namely NGC 5907, NGC 4013, UGC 1082 and UGC 2048. Comparing the model predictions with IRAS and, where available, sub-millimeter and millimeter observations, we determine the respective roles of the old and young stellar populations in grain heating. In all cases, the young population dominates, with the contribution of the old stellar population being at most 40 , as previously found for NGC 891.

In the present paper we have extended the analysis from Paper I to a sample of four additional edge-on galaxies (NGC 5907, NGC 4013, UGC 1082, UGC 2048) and proved that the solution obtained for NGC 891 is generally valid. We have also shown that NGC 891 is not an exceptional system in terms of its SFR density. In all the systems the dust is predominantly heated by the young stellar population. The contribution of the old stellar population can account for at most 40 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4013 as an edge-on galaxy with a strong, symmetric warp whose onset coincides with the stellar disk truncation and related H I changes.

quotes.quote: The most extreme (`prodigious') warp in an edge-on system was observed by Bottema et al. (1987; see also Bottema 1995, 1996) in NGC 4013. The earliest observations of warps suggested that these warps start at the edges of the optical disks. It certainly was very obvious in the warp for the edge-on galaxies NGC 5907 and NGC 4013. It was suggested by Bottema et al. (1987) that the coincidence of the onset of the warp in NGC 4013 with the truncation radius might be a factor in explaining the persistence of warps.

The warp in NGC 4013 was discoverd by Bottema et al. (1987) and studied by Bottema (1995, 1996). It is a extensive and very symmetric warp, starting at about the truncation radius. The onset of the warp is accompanied by a sudden drop in the rotation curve and the deconvolved \ surface density shows a steep decrease to a much lower level there.

sources.summary: The paper reports a giant loop-like tidal stream around NGC 4013, interpreting its morphology as debris from a disrupted dwarf satellite around the warped disk galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of a giant, loop-like stellar structure around the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 4013. This arcing feature extends 6 ( 26 kpc in projected distance) northeast from the center and 3 ( 12 kpc) from the disk plane; likely related features are also apparent on the southwest side of the disk, extending to 4 ( 17 kpc). The detection of this low surface-brightness ( _ R = 27.0^ +0.3 _ 0.2 ) structure is independently confirmed in three separate datasets from three different telescopes. Although its true three dimensional geometry is unknown, the sky- projected morphology of this structure displays a match with the theoretical predictions for the edge-on, projected view of a stellar tidal streams of a dwarf satellite moving in a low inclined ( 25^ ), nearly circular orbit. Using the recent model of the Monoceros tidal stream in the Milky Way by Pe narrubia et al. as template, we find that the progenitor system may have been a galaxy with an initial mass 6 10^8 M_ , of which current position and final fate is unknown.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4013 as a small edge-on spiral with a strong known H I warp and compares observed warp onset with disk flaring models.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013: This is a comparatively smaller sized, perfectly edge-on spiral galaxy lying in the constellation of Ursa Major. NGC 4013 is famous for its prodigious warp, studied in great detail in H I by Bottema (1996). The galaxy shows a mild warping of the disc in the 4.5 micron image as well as in the optical R band observation (Schwartzkopf Dettmar 2001). Our rotation curve modeling yields a dynamical mass of the disc that is quite similar to that of Bottema (1996). The shape of the available H I rotation curve (from Bottema 1996) seems to require a rather larger core radius for the dark matter halo.

sources.summary: NGC 4013 is a known tidal-stream test where the SDSS processing shows only a faint hint, illustrating how image degradation can attenuate real streams.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013 As a fourth and last test, we chose NGC 4013 which shows a tidal stream delgado2009 . As the comparison in Fig. 4013comp shows, the stream is not convincingly visible in our data. There is a hint of something faint, but if one would not know that there indeed is a faint feature, it is unlikely that one would visually detect it in the SDSS data. Because it has a similar surface brightness as the stream at NGC 5907 delgado2008 , the missing feature implies that some of the afore mentioned effects lead to a decrease in image quality. This example shows that though the images go deep, they sometimes suffer from degrading effects which attenuate existing streams.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4013 as a warped disk with extra-planar H i, finding a thin flaring disk and a steep rotational lag that shallows with radius.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013 is a distinctly warped galaxy with evidence of disk-halo activity. Through deep H i observations and modeling we confirm that the H i disk is thin (central exponential scale height of with an upper limit of 4" or 280 pc), but flaring. We detect a vertical gradient in rotation velocity (lag), which shallows radially from a value of 35 ^ +7 _ 28 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 at 1.4' (5.8 kpc), to a value of zero near R _ 25 (11.2 kpc). Over much of this radial range, the lag is relatively steep. Both the steepness and the radial shallowing are consistent with recent determinations for a number of edge-ons, which have been difficult to explain.

sources.summary: The paper summarizes prior H I modelling of NGC 4013, noting an edge-on and line-of-sight warp, a flared gas disk, a radial lag, and no extraplanar H I.

quotes.quote: The H i content of this galaxy was recently extensively modelled by , according to the same strategy as followed in this work. In summary, they find that the gas disk of NGC 4013 has both a substantial edge-on warp and a LOS warp. In addition, the gas disk is flared, with the scale height increasing from 280 pc in the center to about 1 kpc at radii larger than 7 kpc. Finally they report a lag (decrease of rotational velocity with increasing scale height) that becomes more shallow with radius. No extraplanar H i gas is found, despite the presence of extraplanar dust and diffuse ionised gas.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4013 as a comparison galaxy with low star formation, a diffusion-dominated radio halo, strongly frequency-dependent disk-halo flux ratio, and older cosmic-ray electrons extending into the halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013 has a relatively low star formation rate and its radio halo is diffusion dominated steinetal2019b .
NGC 4217 exhibits a stronger frequency dependence in comparison to the superwind galaxy NGC 4666 and a less strong frequency dependence in comparison to NGC 4013.

In NGC 4013, we find the highest inclination of alpha CL = 0.2, where the absolute flux density is highly frequency dependent. In contrast, this galaxy is diffusion dominated. The fraction of the disk to the total flux density decreases toward lower frequencies, as we trace older CREs, which have moved further out in the halo.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4013 as an edge-on disk with a symmetric H I warp beginning near the optical edge and a clear stellar truncation.

quotes.quote: The ``prodigious warp'' in NGC 4013 Bot87,Bot96 is very symmetric and starts suddenly near the end of the optical disk. Inside the warp the stellar disk and HI-layer are exceedingly flat. The stellar disk has a clear truncation vdKS82 . The three-dimensional analysis by Bottema confirms that in deprojection the warp starts very close to the truncation radius.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4013 as a robust three-disc system, arguing that its third disc remains even after modelling thin-disc scattered light with a better PSF.

quotes.quote: Eight galaxies (ESO 79-3, NGC 693, NGC 1381, NGC 4013, NGC 4217, NGC 4452, NGC 5010, and NGC 5403) have high- z light excesses even when fitted with two discs. We interpret those galaxies as having two thick discs or a high-surface-brightness halo component (although in the case of ESO 79-3, this could also be the consequence of the presence of a nearby saturated star). NGC 4013 was already identified in CO11A as a three-disc galaxy, but in that study we considered the PSF to be Gaussian. Here we prove that the third disc remains, even when properly accounting for the effect of the thin disc scattered light.

sources.summary: The paper confirms NGC 4013's stream complex, box-like stellar halo, and a newly detected west loop consistent with one dwarf-disruption stream.

quotes.quote: NGC 4013 We confirm NGC 4013's stream complex presented in as well as the box-like shape of its stellar halo. We also detect a large loop on the west side of the galaxy. The loop connects the west and southwest ``wings'' previously noted by (``D'' and ``E'' in their Figure 3) and extends 8.3' in projection ( 45 kpc) from the center of the galaxy at its outer edge. The maximum projected distance between the new west loop and the galaxy midplane is similar to that of the east loop and therefore is compatible with the hypothesis of one connected stream caused by the disruption of a dwarf galaxy on a low inclination orbit.

sources.summary: Using Herschel profiles, the paper finds that NGC 4013 requires an additional thicker dust component: the vertical emission dominates above the model at 1-2 kpc, making it an outlier from single-component fits.

quotes.quote: The vertical profiles for 6 galaxies (ESO 209-009, NGC 0891, NGC 3628, NGC 4013, NGC 4437, and NGC 4631) show the presence of another (thicker) dust emission component in all or almost all wavebands. This component clearly dominates over the model at larger vertical distances above the galaxy mid-plane, usually starting at z=1-2 kpc. For these galaxies, the average ^2=19.8 10.1 compared to ^2=1.04 0.45 for the whole sample without these outliers. This proves that another dust component is required to better describe the observed profiles of these six galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4013 as one of three edge-on disk galaxies whose residual profiles reveal X-like morphology attributed to a bar after subtracting axisymmetric components.

quotes.quote: In a pilot survey we compare the orbital structures which lead to the appearance of ``peanuts'' and ``X''-like features with the edge-on profiles of three disc galaxies (IC 2531, NGC 4013 and UGC 2048). The subtraction from the images of a model representing the axisymmetric component of the galaxies reveals the contribution of the non-axisymmetric terms. We find a direct correspondence between the orbital profiles of 3D bars in models and the observed main morphological features of the residuals.

The main conclusion of the present study is that the careful subtraction of axisymmetric components from the profiles of the three edge-on disk galaxies (NGC 4013, IC 2531 and UGC 2048) reveals an ``X''-like morphology, which indicates the presence of a bar.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4013 as an observed warped-disk comparison, noting its strongly warped hydrogen layer, optical-disk warp onset, comparable warp amplitude, and modestly wound line of nodes.

quotes.quote: To illustrate that the warp in our simulation indeed resembles those observed, we compare it to the strongly warped hydrogen layer of NGC 4013 bottem_96 . The correspondence is not perfect; , our simulated warp starts at around 5 whereas the warp of NGC 4013 starts to develop at the edge of its optical disc, about 4 bottem_96 .
The warp amplitude at later times in Figure fig:thetaR (b) seems to compare well with that in NGC 4013, both visually and quantitatively cf. Figure 6 bottem_96 .

Also the LON is fairly loosely wound throughout our simulation. bottem_96 concluded that the LON of NGC 4013 winds modestly, by about 20^ , which is consistent with that in our simulated warp, in addition to the nice visual resemblance in Figure fig:morph .

sources.summary: The chapter presents NGC 4013 as an isolated warped spiral whose deep imaging reveals a faint loop-like stream, likely from a low-inclination satellite accreted about 2.8 Gyr ago.

quotes.quote: One striking case of such a system is NGC 4013 NGC 4013 , an isolated spiral galaxy with a prominent HI warp bottema1987 that has been revealed by deep imaging MD2009 to contain a faint loop-like stellar tidal stream at fairly low inclination to the disk (see the upper panels of Figure n4013_m63 ). The sky-projected morphology of this structure displays a remarkable resemblance to an edge-on view of models of the Monoceros Ring Monoceros Ring feature (see Chapter 3) in the Milky Way as a tidal debris structure penarrubia2005 . This suggests that the progenitor progenitors of streams system of the NGC 4013 stream may have been a low-mass satellite on a low-inclination, nearly-circular orbit that was accreted approximately 2.8 Gyr ago.

gmw_00001737.name: NGC 4217
gmw_00001737.overview: NGC 4217 is an inclined SAb or Sb spiral galaxy [arXiv:1305.3130, arXiv:1507.03095] featuring a compact bulge [arXiv:1804.09071], a thick, warped dust lane [arXiv:1804.09071], and an outer atomic gas ring likely originating from a minor merger [arXiv:1507.03095, arXiv:1804.09071]. The galaxy hosts a large-scale, X-shaped ordered magnetic field [arXiv:2007.03002] and a helical outflow extending nearly 7 kiloparsecs into its halo [arXiv:2007.03002].

sources.summary: NGC 4217 is described as an inclined Sb/SAb spiral with an extensive dust lane and vertical dust distribution, smooth horizontal profiles, and wavelength-dependent slope breaks.

quotes.quote: NGC 4217 For NGC 4217, we assume a distance of 19.6 Mpc. The galaxy has a D_ 25 diameter of 5 2 and is classified as an Sb or SAb sp type galaxy, showing H ii emission regions. Having an inclination of 88 01 2007A&A...471..765B , the galaxy is displaying a dust lane, visible along the entire disc and with an extensive vertical dust distribution 1999AJ....117.2077H,2004AJ....128..662T , although we note that 2000A&AS..145...83A consider this galaxy to be too highly inclined to make any unambiguous claims about extraplanar dust. A global fit of extinction by the dust distribution to optical images was carried out in 2007A&A...471..765B , giving a dust scalelength of 70 or 6.7 kpc, which is 75 larger than the stellar disc scalelength. Compared to the other galaxies in the sample, the horizontal profiles for NGC 4217 are remarkably smooth: only the 100 m \ profile -- and the 160 m \ one to a lesser extent -- shows some small secondary peaks at 0 4 and 0 6 from the centre, and two more, less prominent, at 1 and 1 4, but all on the SW side.

However, the overall behaviour is quite peculiar and differs between wavelengths. At 100 m, the profile first drops off from the centre outward, until about 1 6 on the SW side and about 1 on the NE side of the disc. At these points, a break in behaviour occurs and the slope of the profile becomes much steeper, up to about 2 on either side, where the background takes over.

sources.summary: NGC 4217 is an SAb galaxy whose H I map shows an optically invisible outer ring, spatially and kinematically separated from the main disk and probably external.

quotes.quote: NGC 4217 NGC 4217 is located at a distance of 19.6 Mpc and is classified as an SAb galaxy. Its atomic gas content has previously been studied by , who used the kinematics of the atomic gas in a sample of galaxies in the Ursa Major cluster to investigate the statistical properties of the Tully-Fisher relations. However, their sample consisted largely of moderately inclined galaxies and their method to derive the surface density distribution of the H i is actually not suitable for an edge-on geometry, with Verheijen and Sancisi stating that the ``method for extracting the surface density profiles from integrated H i maps breaks down for nearly edge-on systems.'' The data In the total H i map of NGC 4217 (Figs. fig:N4217_r_mom0 , fig:N4217_models_mom0 ) two blobs of gas on either side of the main disk immediately catch the eye. They suggest the presence of a ring, which, strikingly, has no optical counterpart in the -band image in Fig. fig:N4217_r_mom0 . In what follows we will refer to this feature as the outer ring.

Models In the major axis position-velocity diagram (see Fig. fig:N4217_pv_models later) the outer ring corresponds to the sudden drop in rotational velocities at major axis offsets of about 3 arcmin. The outer ring thus seems both spatially and kinematically separated from the main disk, indicating that it probably has an external origin. Because of this, we start by modelling only the main gas disk and add the outer ring as an extra feature in a later stage.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4217 as a typical high-surface-brightness spiral, comparing observed rotation curves with MDM and CDM predictions and showing model dark-matter density profiles for the galaxy.

quotes.quote: Rotation curves predicted by MDM for NGC 4217, a typical HSB galaxy, and NGC 3917, a typical LSB galaxy in the sample are shown in Fig. fig1.2 . (See Ref. 5 for the rotation curves for the other 28 galaxies.) In these figures, observed rotation curves are depicted as filled circles with error bars, and for the two curves at the bottom, the dotted and dash-dotted lines show the stellar and interstellar gas rotation curves, respectively. The solid lines and dashed lines are rotation curves predicted by MDM and the standard cold dark matter (CDM) paradigm respectively. For the CDM fits, we use the Navarro, Frenk White (NFW) density profile, employing three free parameters (one of which is the mass-to-light ratio.)

It is fair to say that both models fit the data well; We should point out that the rotation curves predicted by MDM and MOND have been found to be virtually indistinguishable over the range of observed radii and both employ only 1 free parameter. but we remind the readers that while the MDM fits use only 1 free parameter, for the CDM fits one needs to use 3 free parameters. Thus the MDM model is a more economical model than CDM in fitting data at the galactic scale. Since MDM employs only the minimal number of free parameters, the speaker (YJN) called it the ``minimal dark matter" model in his talk at MG14. But later he found out that the name had been used before for another model. His collaborators and he decided to change the name to ``modified dark matter". Shown in Fig. fig3.4 are the dark matter density profiles predicted by MDM (solid lines) and CDM (dashed lines) for the HSB galaxy NGC 4217 and the LSB galaxy NGC 3917 in the sample respectively.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4217 as one of three clearest escape-dominated halo candidates, with similar scale heights at both frequencies and inferred cosmic-ray wind speeds up to 420 km/s.

quotes.quote: From the frequency dependence as discussed in Sect. sec:frequency we conclude that the clearest candidates for an escape-dominated halo are NGC 4157, NGC 4217, and NGC 4302. Their scale heights are similar at both frequencies. In this case, the CRE leave the halo so fast that no significant frequency dependence due to synchrotron losses can be observed. This means that the adiabatic loss time of CRE is smaller than their synchrotron life time within the observed distances from the galactic plane.

In escape-dominated halos, we can assume equipartition between magnetic fields and cosmic rays, so that the scale height of the cosmic-ray electrons h_ CRE is about twice the observed synchrotron scale height h_z , which is about 1 kpc for the three galaxies mentioned above (see Table halo scale heights ). Hence, we can estimate a mean cosmic-ray bulk speed v_ CRE (corresponding to the velocity of a galactic wind) of v_ CRE = 2 h_z / t_ esc > 2 h_z / t_ syn 140 km/s. The adiabatic loss time depends only on the reciprocal velocity gradient: t_ ad = 3 v z ^ 1 heesen+2009 . Assuming a constant increase in the CRE velocity with height gives v_ wind = 6 h_z / t_ ad = 420 km/s, significantly higher than 140 km/s and above the escape velocity from the disk, which is about 280 km/s for a rotation speed of 200 km/s heesen+2009 .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4217 as an Sb edge-on galaxy with thick warped dust lane, outer H I ring likely from a minor merger, compact bulge, and FIR flux underestimation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4217 NGC 4217 is another Sb-type galaxy in our sample. Visually, it has a noticeably thick (as compared to the other HER OES galaxies) and somewhat warped dust lane, which extends up to the galaxy outskirts. For the main disc of NGC 4217, 2015A&A...582A..18A measured an atomic hydrogen mass of 2.50 10^9 M_ . In addition, they also detected a separate ring of gas outside (and coplanar with) the main gas disc, with an H i mass of at least 3.81 10^8 M_ . This ring has no optical counterpart and could be the remnant of a recent minor merger.

Our imfit fitting was performed for the model consisting of two broken thin and thick discs and a bulge (see Fig. imfit_NGC4217 ). As one can see from Table tab:IRAC_dec_res , the thin disc is less luminous than the thick disc, but they have similar inner disc scale lengths. The bulge is surprisingly compact (the \ index is small) and its fraction to the total galaxy luminosity is minor.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4217 radio halo and magnetic field, measuring disk/halo scale heights and fluxes, finding an X-shaped ordered field, inward disk field, and a 7 kpc helical outflow.

quotes.quote: In order to gain a better understanding of the influence of cosmic rays (CRs) and magnetic fields in the disk-halo interface of edge-on spiral galaxies, we investigate the radio continuum halo, the magnetic field, and the transport processes of the CRs of the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 4217 using CHANG-ES radio data at two frequencies, 6 GHz (C-band) and 1.5 GHz (L-band), and supplemental LOFAR data of this galaxy at 150 MHz. With additional X-ray Chandra data, we study the connection of radio features to the diffuse hot gas around NGC 4217. We investigate the total intensity (Stokes I) data in detail and determine the integrated spectral behavior. The radio scale heights of all three radio frequencies for NGC 4217 were extracted via exponential fits to the intensity profiles. From these, individual absolute flux densities of the disk and the halo were also calculated.

NGC 4217 shows a large-scale X-shaped magnetic field structure, covering a major part of the galaxy with a mean total magnetic field strength in the disk of 9 G. From the analysis of the rotation measure map at C-band, we found that the direction of the disk magnetic field is pointing inward. A helical outflow structure is furthermore present in the northwestern part of the galaxy, which is extended nearly 7 kpc into the halo.

sources.summary: NGC 4217 has an edge-on fit, dust geometry differing from prior B-band work, a Type II truncation beyond 10 kpc, and K-band dust asymmetry.

quotes.quote: NGC 4217: the same considerations as for NGC 4013 apply to this object. The geometrical parameters of the stellar and dust disk, together with , are given for the B-band by AltonA&AS2000 : the fit here is more edge-on and h_ d is larger by 30 , while h_ s is consistent with their fit. The difference in is also the cause of the smaller z_ s I obtain. For this object I also have z_ d > z_ s : since the bulge dominates the surface brightness apart from regions close to the galactic plane, an extinction lane can appear even if the dust disk is thicker that the stellar.

The major axis profiles clearly show a steepening of the profile beyond 10 kpc: this is the signature of a Type II truncation in the notation of PohlenA&A2006 , which cannot be reproduced by the sharp cut used in the model. A truncation at the same distance is also found in the K _s band by FloridoA&A2001 , although the value of h_ s they obtain is larger by 40 than in the present fit. Dust extinction can be seen in the K'-band image, although not in the form of a dust lane but rather as a y-axis asymmetry in images, because of the inclination smaller than edge-on.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4217 as a representative SPARC galaxy whose baryonic rotation-curve contribution is insufficient, while an Einstein-cluster dark halo gives the best fit.

quotes.quote: The posterior distributions for a representative galaxy NGC4217 are shown in Fig. ( fig:cornerplot ). Note that, unlike the Newtonian Einasto halo models used in , R is an additional parameter that directly determines the metric functions (see Eq. ( eqn:gtt ) and ( eqn:phi )).

Using the median values of the posteriors, we fit the observed rotation curve of NGC4217 with the Einstein cluster model (see the top right panel in Fig. ( fig:cornerplot )). As shown in the figure, baryonic contributions to the rotation curve, including proper scaling of the mass-to-light ratios, are plotted separately. For this example, all three components, i.e., gas (shown in orange), disk (green), and the bulge (red), have non-zero contributions. As expected, their total baryonic contribution (shown in purple) cannot explain the observed velocity data (blue dots). When we add the contribution of the Einstein cluster on top of the baryonic contribution (using Eq. ( eqn_vtot )), we get the best fit (denoted by the black curve).

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 4217 as one of three spiral bulges whose Hβ-based ages are overestimated, producing too-low metallicities, while higher-order Balmer indices reduce the discrepancy.

quotes.quote: For three bulges (NGC 4157, NGC 4217, NGC 4312) \ indicates significantly older ages, all three well above the age of the universe. Interestingly, exactly those objects show H _ emission greater than expected from [ O iii ] (PS02). Therefore their ages must clearly be overestimated, a problem by which the higher-order Balmer line \ is much less affected. In these three cases \ yields metallicities that are too low in line with the overestimated ages.
NGC 4157, NGC 4217, NGC 4312 still show slightly older ages from , but the deviation approaches the general scatter in the relationship.

gmw_00001738.name: NGC 4302
gmw_00001738.overview: NGC 4302 is an edge-on Sc spiral galaxy [arXiv:2210.09620, arXiv:1208.2845] that hosts a bar, a boxy-peanut bulge, and dust-lane corrugations [arXiv:2005.10290]. It likely interacts with its companion NGC 4298 [arXiv:1208.2845, arXiv:0902.4436], exhibiting a faint gas bridge [arXiv:2210.09620, arXiv:0902.4436] and an escape-dominated cosmic-ray halo [arXiv:1712.03780].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4302 as one of three clearest escape-dominated halo candidates, with similar scale heights at both frequencies and inferred cosmic-ray wind speeds up to 420 km/s.

quotes.quote: From the frequency dependence as discussed in Sect. sec:frequency we conclude that the clearest candidates for an escape-dominated halo are NGC 4157, NGC 4217, and NGC 4302. Their scale heights are similar at both frequencies. In this case, the CRE leave the halo so fast that no significant frequency dependence due to synchrotron losses can be observed. This means that the adiabatic loss time of CRE is smaller than their synchrotron life time within the observed distances from the galactic plane.

In escape-dominated halos, we can assume equipartition between magnetic fields and cosmic rays, so that the scale height of the cosmic-ray electrons h_ CRE is about twice the observed synchrotron scale height h_z , which is about 1 kpc for the three galaxies mentioned above (see Table halo scale heights ). Hence, we can estimate a mean cosmic-ray bulk speed v_ CRE (corresponding to the velocity of a galactic wind) of v_ CRE = 2 h_z / t_ esc > 2 h_z / t_ syn 140 km/s. The adiabatic loss time depends only on the reciprocal velocity gradient: t_ ad = 3 v z ^ 1 heesen+2009 . Assuming a constant increase in the CRE velocity with height gives v_ wind = 6 h_z / t_ ad = 420 km/s, significantly higher than 140 km/s and above the escape velocity from the disk, which is about 280 km/s for a rotation speed of 200 km/s heesen+2009 .

sources.summary: The paper finds greater and prominent dust-lane corrugations in NGC 4302, while GALFIT leaves central residuals because the galaxy hosts a bar and boxy-peanut bulge.

quotes.quote: The outer discs of all the five galaxies display clear corrugations. UGC 7522 and IC 2531 show sub-arcsecond mid-plane deviations but NGC 4607, NGC 4302 and NGC 5470 show greater waviness.

GALFIT does fit the stellar discs pretty well except for large residuals in the central regions of NGC 4302 and IC 2531. These two galaxies host a bar and a boxy-peanut bulge and hence a simple two-component model will not suffice. Since our primary concern here is to derive a reasonable estimate of disc parameters, we refrain from improving bulge fits. We note that for NGC 4302, the R_s derived using the above method is very close to that obtained from a double exponential profile fit to its Spitzer/IRAC image Sahaetal2009 .
Corrugations are prominent in NGC 4302, NGC 5470 and UGC 7522.

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA and VLA observations of edge-on NGC 4302 to measure gas disk thickness, derive gas and SFR densities, and test volumetric star-formation laws.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The galactic center ( = 12 ^h 21 ^m 42 32 and = 14 35 52.23 ) is located at x = 0 and z = 0 in the map.

quotes.quote: We observe the almost edge-on ( i 90 ) galaxy NGC 4302 using ALMA (CO) and VLA ( ) to measure the gas disk thickness for investigating the volumetric star formation law (SFL). The recent star formation rate (SFR) is estimated based on a linear combination of IR 24 \ and H emissions. The measured scale heights of CO and \ increase significantly with radius.

NGC 4302 is an excellent target, allowing us to measure the disk thickness in the most straightforward way since its inclination is almost 90 ( Heald_2007 ; Zschaechner_2015 ). It is located at a distance of 14 Mpc based on a local flow model by with H_0 = 73 \ Mpc ^ 1 . NGC 4302 has a companion galaxy NGC 4298, but the disk of NGC 4302 is not obviously distorted by the interaction ( 1996ApJ...462..712R ; Howk_1999 ). On the other hand, Zschaechner_2015 detected a faint \ bridge between NGC 4302 and NGC 4298 in a few channel maps, probably due to ram pressure stripping by the intracluster medium in the Virgo Cluster.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4302 as a peripheral edge-on Sc galaxy likely tidally interacting with NGC 4298 and showing extended radio and polarized emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 4302 NGC 4302 is an edge-on Sc galaxy located in the cluster peripheries, about 3 1 (930 kpc in the sky plane) from Virgo A. It probably interacts with the neighboring galaxy NGC 4298 (Koopmann Kenney ). The map of total power intensity (Fig. 4302tp ) reveals an extension in the eastern direction.

The galaxy visible at this position, PGC 169114 is not a source of measurable radio emission, as checked with NVSS and FIRST data. The polarization B-vectors in the disk plane of NGC 4302 are plane-parallel, while those further away to the east are inclined towards the extension. An emission south of NGC 4298 is due to a background NVSS radio source J 122131+143352 with total flux density (at 1.4 GHz) of 12.7 mJy (Condon ).

sources.summary: NGC 4302 is the poorest fit because of asymmetry, contamination from NGC 4298, and complex vertical structure including a second thicker stellar disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4302: this is the worst object to fit, because of the major axis asymmetry around the center, the presence of a surface brightness contribution from the nearby face on galaxy NGC 4298 (whose centre is at about 2 3 from the galactic plane in the direction of the negative y axis in Fig. n4302_f ) and because of the complex vertical structure. Indeed the model does not represent well the observed surface brightness, especially in the V-band. The contribution of the fitted bulge is negligible. It appears that there is a second, thicker stellar disk (of V-band vertical scalelength 1.5 kpc, a quarter of the fitted h_ s ), with radial scalelength increasing with the distance from the galactic plane. Extinction in K' appears clumpy and does not alter significantly the symmetry above the plane.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4302 as an edge-on spiral with interaction evidence, H I rotation data, infrared and optical warps, and a high measured flaring rate.

quotes.quote: NGC 4302: NGC4302 is a very nearly edge-on spiral galaxy lying in the constellation of Coma Berenices and has a nearby companion galaxy NGC4298 with apparently no sign of interactions in the optical images. However, radio observations with GMRT 330 MHz by Kantharia et al. (2005) shows quite a clear sign of interactions in terms of tidal bridge between the two galaxies. This galaxy has been studied recently in great detail to understand the distinct nature of extra-planar diffused ionized gas by Heald et al. (2007). We have adopted the major axis H I rotation curve of this galaxy from Heald et al. (2007). The galaxy shows a mild warping of the underlying thin disc in the 4.5 micron band.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4302 as an edge-on pair member with a truncated H I distribution, a long tail, a thin symmetric radio disk, and steep spectral index.

quotes.quote: NGC 4302 This edge-on galaxy forms a pair with NGC 4298. The H i distribution is mildly truncated to within the optical disk to the south, while a long tail is present in the opposite side (Chung et al. 2009). The radio continuum disk is symmetric and thin at 20 and 6 cm. The latter emission is radially and vertically more extended than the 20 cm emission. The spectral index is steep ( 1 ) over the whole galactic disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4302 to illustrate a high-mass disk whose outer dust is more porous and vertically extended than its central dust lane, with a rapid radial transition.

quotes.quote: Namely, in the outer regions of the high mass galaxy NGC 4302 (lower panel), the distribution of dust is far more porous and has a noticeably larger scale height than in the center of the galaxy. The dust in the outer regions is distributed with a morphology similar to those in the low mass galaxies, but perhaps with a higher overall opacity. In NGC 4302, the transition in the distribution of dust occurs rapidly over a very small range radius, possibly reflecting a change in physical conditions within the disk comparable to the change from galaxy-to-galaxy at V_c=120 .

gmw_00001739.name: NGC 5965
gmw_00001739.overview: NGC 5965 is a warped disk galaxy featuring an outer disk and a ring-like dust lane [arXiv:0705.1471]. These structures lie on a different plane than the galaxy's inner peanut-shaped bulge and its K-band stellar ring [arXiv:0705.1471].

sources.summary: NGC 5965 is described as warped, with its outer disk and ring-like dust lane offset from an inner peanut-shaped bulge and a K-band stellar ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 5965: the galaxy is warped, with the outer disk and a ring-like dust lane on a different plane with respect to the inner peanut-shaped bulge. The stellar ring is evident in the K' image, while no extinction features can be detected. As with NGC 5746, the fit to the extinction lane in the V-band is poor, despite the global fit quality is not much different from the other objects. An estimate for h_ s is available for the K _s band which is about 20 smaller than the present value BizyaevA&A2002 . The fitted is consistent with the aspect of the K' ring, which suggests =83.5^ 1.5^ .

gmw_00001740.name: UGC 4277
gmw_00001740.overview: UGC 4277 is an isolated, nearly edge-on spiral galaxy classified as type Sc or Scd [arXiv:1305.3130, arXiv:1804.09071]. It features a visible optical dust lane [arXiv:1305.3130, arXiv:0705.1471], an integral-shaped optical warp [arXiv:1804.09071], and a clumpy, irregular atomic gas disk [arXiv:1507.03095, arXiv:1804.09071].

sources.summary: UGC 4277 is the most distant HEROES galaxy, an Sc/Scd system with slightly warped stellar and H I disks and clumpy, irregular H I and dust structure.

quotes.quote: UGC 4277 With a distance of 76.5 Mpc, UGC 4277 is the most distant of the HEROES galaxies. It is classified as an Sc or Scd galaxy and, like NGC 973, the details of its atomic gas content have not been investigated before. The data As can be seen in Fig. fig:U4277_V_mom0 , both the stellar disk and the atomic gas disk of UGC 4277 are slightly warped.

Similarly to the case of NGC 973, the limited observing time results in a rather low signal-to-noise ratio in our data cube, making the individual channel maps quite noisy and the shape of the emission quite irregular (see Fig. fig:U4277_final_channels of the appendix). This is also reflected in the major axis position-velocity diagram (see Fig. fig:U4277_pv_models later), where chunks of emission seem to be missing. However, the higher brightness contours, which should be less affected by the noise, are also very irregular. This suggests that UGC 4277 has a rather clumpy H i disk. Similarly, the FIR morphology, especially in the PACS bands, suggests that the dust disk of UGC 4277 is also rather irregular verstappen13 .

sources.summary: UGC 4277 is treated as the most distant HER OES galaxy, with a visible dust lane, modeled dust scalelength, erratic PACS profiles, and smoother SPIRE emission.

quotes.quote: UGC 4277 UGC 4277 is the most distant galaxy in our sample at a distance of 76.5 Mpc, with a D_ 25 diameter of 3 9 and a classification as an Sc or Scd type galaxy. It has an inclination of 88 89 2007A&A...471..765B , with a clear dust lane visible from the centre to the edges. Modelling of the dust distribution has been previously carried out in 2007A&A...471..765B , giving a dust scalelength of 35 or 12.5 kpc, which is about the same as the fitted stellar disc scalelength. The horizontal profiles for UGC 4277 -- maps are given in Figure UGC4277.fig - show a rather erratic behaviour.

At 100 m, a number of small peaks are visible, but none of these can be pinned down as the central one. On top of that, a smaller secondary peak about 1 3 off centre toward the east end of the galaxy is almost separated from the rest of the disc by a clear drop in emission at this wavelength and emission levels fall sharply just beyond this peak, while on the other side, the profile gets dominated by the background somewhere around 1 5 away from the centre. The other PACS wavelength is more symmetrical, with one central peak and one secondary about 0 5 off centre on either side -- though all three have roughly the same strength -- and the profile drops off very steeply beyond these secondaries, but first levels out at a reduced plateau before falling off farther beyond 1 3 on both sides.

sources.summary: The paper models UGC 4277 as an isolated flat Sc/Scd edge-on galaxy with a prominent optical warp, clumpy H I disc, bar evidence, and remaining FIR dust-emission discrepancy.

quotes.quote: UGC 4277 UGC 4277 is a flat Sc/Scd galaxy, one of the largest galaxies (the semi-major axis sma=36.95 kpc) in our sample. It is seen almost exactly edge-on; the dust lane is very prominent in the optical. One of the noticeable features is an integral-shaped optical warp. 2015A&A...582A..18A recently showed that the outer regions of the H i disc demonstrate a small warp as well, with the position angle deviating at most about 4 degrees from its central value.

UGC 4277 has a total atomic hydrogen mass of 2.03 10^ 10 M_ and, similarly to NGC 973, the H i disc has a clumpy and irregular structure. Interestingly, this galaxy is included in the Catalogue of Isolated Galaxies CIG, 1973AISAO...8....3K , therefore its evolutionary status may differ from those galaxies residing in a group or a cluster.

sources.summary: UGC 4277 is well fit by the model, with a clear V-band dust lane, weak K-band bulge asymmetry, and a stellar scale-length consistent with prior work.

quotes.quote: UGC 4277: the fit is good, with 90 of the data points fitted by the model with residuals smaller than 30 . While the dust lane is evident for this nearly edge-on object in the V-band, in the K'-band only a weak asymmetry can be seen for the bulge above and below the galactic plane. The K- _s band value for h_ s derived by BizyaevA&A2002 is consistent with the result of the present fit.

gmw_00001741.name: MRC 0943-242
gmw_00001741.overview: MRC 0943-242 is a massive, high-redshift radio galaxy at a redshift of z=2.92 [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567, arXiv:1711.10601]. It features a double-lobed radio morphology [arXiv:1711.10601], an active galactic nucleus driving turbulent outflows [arXiv:1808.08251], and a surrounding metal-enriched gas halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567, arXiv:1904.05114].

sources.summary: The paper studies MRC 0943-242 as a z=2.9 high-redshift radio galaxy, reporting a molecular-gas upper limit, tentative off-nuclear CO, unresolved 30 GHz continuum, and a steep spectrum.

quotes.quote: We set a firm upper limit to the mass of molecular gas in MRC 2104-242 ( z = 2.5 ) of M _ H2 < 2 10^ 10 \ ( _ x /0.8) M _ . For MRC 0943-242 ( z=2.9 ) we derive an upper limit of M _ H2 < 6 10^ 10 \ ( _ x /0.8) M _ . We also find a tentative 3 CO detection in the outer part of the giant \ halo that surrounds MRC 0943-242. The 30-33 GHz radio continuum of MRC 2104-242 and MRC 0943-242 is reliably detected. Both radio sources show a spectral index of 1.5 between 1.4 and 30 GHz, with no evidence for spectral curvature within this range of frequencies.

The radio source MRC 0943-242 (Fig. fig:MRC0943 a ) has a flux density of 3.3 \ and is unresolved in our data. Higher resolution continuum observations at 4.7 and 8.2 GHz by car97 show that the radio source consists of two lobes that are separated by 4 arcsec. When comparing the flux of our 30 GHz data with the integrated flux at 1.5, 4.7 and 8.2 GHz car97 , Figure fig:index shows that MRC 0943-242 has a steep spectral index between 1.5 GHz and 30 GHz with = -1.44 .

sources.summary: The paper describes MRC 0943--242 as a powerful z=2.923 radio galaxy with a metal-enriched gas halo and a surrounding overdensity of Ly-alpha emitters.

quotes.quote: This powerful radio source with a flux density of 1.1 Jy at 408 MHz has a redshift of z=2.923 rot95,rot97 . The radio galaxy is surrounded by a metal-enriched, low surface brightness gas halo extending for at least 8 \ 67 kpc, vil03 . The estimated dynamical mass of the gas halo is 7-44 10^ 11 \ vil03,jar03 .

Combined with the two emitters with \ in their spectrum, the total number of \ emitters near the radio galaxy MRC 0943--242 is 28. The properties of the \ emission lines of these z = 2.9 galaxies are printed in Table 0943table .

The volume densities of emitters near MRC 0943--242 and near the radio galaxy MRC 0316--257 (V05) are very similar. Down to a narrow-band magnitude of m_ nb = 26.1 there are 63 emitters in the 0943 field compared to 59 in the 0316 field. Correcting for the difference in observed comoving volume, the density ratio is n_ 0943 /n_ 0316 = 1.0^ +0.2 _ 0.2 . This translates to a density of n_ 0943 = 3.2^ +0.9 _ 0.7 times the field density.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA and MUSE observations of MRC0943-242, finding a complex radio-galaxy system with a protocluster environment, extended gas, dust, molecular emission, and star formation outside the host.

quotes.quote: MRC0943-242 is an ultra steep spectrum radio source with a spectral index of =-1.5 between 1.4-30 GHz and shows no evidence of a spectral curvature within this range carilli97, emonts11 . MRC0943-242 is located in a proto-cluster with an over density of almost 5 ; wylezalek13 surrounded by many nearby companions detected in \ with known redshifts venemans07 and a giant quiescent \ halo with a diameter of 100 kpc - extending far beyond the radio structure villar-martin03 . A deep absorption trough in the \ emission line suggests the presence of a large amount of neutral gas roettgering95 .

The nature of the gas and dust The MRC0943-242 system has a complex distribution of the ionised and neutral gas. The high ionisation lines are distributed mainly around the nucleus and are confined within the radio lobes. The exception to this is the extended , , and \ emission which is most extended to the west -- beyond the western radio lobe (see panel A-C of Fig. fig:carbon_he ).

The \ halo in MRC0943-242 shows a linear filament, , connecting \ with the two brightest dust continuum components ( \ and ), strongly suggesting that they are physically connected (see Fig fig:RGB ).

sources.summary: The paper studies MRC 0943-242/Yggdrasil as a z=2.92 radio galaxy whose metal-enriched absorbing halo and ionized outflow are mapped with MUSE.

quotes.quote: The subject of this work, MRC 0943-242, is an HzRG at z = 2.92 which has a distinct profile featuring four discrete absorption troughs, first revealed by long-slit, high resolution spectroscopy rottgering1995 . At even higher resolutions, the four discrete absorbers initially detected have been confirmed with evidence for an asymmetric underlying emission profile also being seen (e.g., jarvis2003,wilman2004 ; S18). Three of the absorbers fall into the class of weak absorption-line gas as defined by wilman2004 while one of the absorbers has an unusually high H I column density of 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 . MUSE (Multi-unit Spectroscopic Explorer) observations of the source have provided a spatially resolved view of the variation in the line and shown that the strong absorber in this source is extended to radial distances of 65 kpc from the nucleus (e.g., G16).

We have examined the absorbing gas surrounding the host galaxy of MRC 0943-242, Yggdrasil, using MUSE data. Our results prove that the H I absorbers measured from the line are at the same velocities as C IV and N V absorbers. The new integral field unit (IFU) dataset shows that the high H I column density absorbing gas ( absorber 2) with N_ H I / cm ^ 2 = 10^ 19.2 is a non-isotropic gas medium extending outwards from 60 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper models 0943--242 as a high-redshift radio galaxy with extended absorption gas, arguing that a low-metallicity outer halo predates the observed AGN phase.

quotes.quote: Observations are presented showing the doublet \ absorption lines superimposed on the \ emission in the radio galaxy 0943--242. Within the errors, the redshift of the absorption system that has a column density of = 10^ 14.5 0.1 coincides with that of the deep \ absorption trough observed by R ottgering et al. (1995). The gas seen in absorption has a resolved spatial extent of at least 13 kpc (the size of the extended emission line region).

In absence of confining pressure comparable to that of the emission gas, the outer halo of 0943--242 is considered to have a very low density allowing the metagalactic ionizing radiation to keep it higly ionized. In other radio galaxies where the jet has pressurized the outer halo, the same gas would be seen in emission (since the emissivity scales as n_H^2 ) and not in absorption as a result of the lower filling factor of the denser condensations. This would explain the anticorrelation found by Ojik et al. (1997) between \ emission sizes (or radio jet sizes) and the observation (or not) of \ in absorption. The estimated low metallicity for the absorption gas in 0943--242 ( Z 0.01 ) and its proposed location --outer halo outside the radio cocoon-- suggest that its existence preceeds the observed AGN phase and is a vestige of the initial starburst at the onset of formation of the parent galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper studies MRC 0943--242 as a massive z=2.92 radio galaxy whose Ly halo, H I absorber, jets, metallic gas, and feedback trace its evolution toward a spheroidal galaxy.

quotes.quote: The radio continuum of MRC 0943--242 has a simple double-morphology (Fanaroff-Riley Class II), consisting of two bright hotspots separated by 3.9 , with no core detected, at 1.4, 4.7 and 8.2 GHz Ro95,carilli1997 . Its spectral index between 1.5 GHz and 30 GHz is = -- 1.44 emonts2011 . With a 1.4 GHz radio power of 10 ^ 35.4 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 Bre2000 , this is among the most radio-luminous galaxies known.

With a stellar mass of 10 ^ 11.2 M _ seymour2007 , MRC 0943--242 is remarkably massive for its redshift of 2.92. Significant star formation activity is present in the host galaxy ( 200 M _ yr ^ 1 ) and in companion galaxies ( 1400 M _ yr ^ 1 ; Gu ), and the powerful active nucleus appears to be subjecting its associated extended gas reservoir to substantial radio- and quasar-mode feedback. In the long-run, this feedback activity may quench the modest star formation activity found in the host galaxy, but our kinematic results suggest that processes related to the radio-mode feedback are drawing gas deeper into the potential well of the galaxy, and we speculate this could give rise to a short-lived enhancement in star formation and the fueling of the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper detects narrow [C I] in MRC 0943-242, finding redshift agreement with He II, 40-50 km/s dispersion, and molecular gas constraints consistent with prior CO work.

quotes.quote: Due to our different extraction aperture, we recover a slightly different line profile from the same dataset, which has an intermediate in line width between TN J0121+1320 and two galaxies, 4C+03.24 and MRC 0943-242, which have line-widths with the same order of magnitude ( 50 km s ^ 1 ). [ C i ] velocity dispersions of this width are not commonly observed and should be followed up with deeper observations to trace the full extent of the [ C i ] emission line. We note that there is a significant agreement in redshift between [ C i ] and He II , especially in the galaxy MRC 0943-242 (see Fig. fig:galaxy-spectra-images ).

In MRC 0943-242 and 4C+03.24, the dispersions range from 40-50 km s ^ 1 and are insufficiently broad to cover the rotational velocity of the host galaxy and can therefore only trace single molecular clouds within the galaxy.

In comparison the [ C I ] (1-0) line detections in 4C+03.24 and MRC 0943-242 from our results have line-widths which are a factor of 5-10 narrower than what is expected based on the previous studies we have discussed here Gullberg2016b,Emonts2018,Lelli2018,Man2019 . In the case of MRC 0943-242, however, Gullberg2016b report a CO(8-7) FWHM of 43 13 km s ^ 1 , fully consistent with the 40 14 km s ^ 1 we find in [ C I ] .

sources.summary: The paper uses MRC 0943-242 as a high-redshift radio-galaxy example where AGN emission and CGM absorption reveal ionized, turbulent gas and outflows near the nucleus.

quotes.quote: A special case combining the effect of AGN, going from the host galaxy to the surrounding CGM, is the high-redshift radio galaxy MRC 0943-242.

Kolwa presented nuclear emission and CGM absorption observations of MRC 0943-242 (see Fig. f:agn_kolwa ), whose emission is spatially integrated over a 0.6 arcsec (4.75 kpc) radius aperture centered at what is assumed to be an AGN. Hence, the lines reveal gas in the ISM in close proximity to the nucleus of the galaxy and ionized by the AGN radiation. The emitting gas is turbulent with a velocity width larger than 1000 km s ^ 1 .

As an observational example, the high-redshift galaxy MRC 0943-242 is believed to contain an AGN, with line emission (and absorption) revealing ionized ISM in close proximity to the nucleus as well as outflows and high levels of turbulence.

gmw_00001742.name: NGC0784
gmw_00001742.overview: NGC 0784 is an Sdm-type [arXiv:0808.1789] starburst dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2105.05100] with a resolved, edge-on disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0411231]. It features numerous HII regions [arXiv:0808.1789], a lopsided outer structure possibly caused by gas accretion [arXiv:1103.4928], and potential gravitational interactions with the nearby dwarf AGC 111164 [arXiv:2105.05100].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 784 as a resolved edge-on dwarf disk and finds the sample's thick-disk stellar populations have weak color gradients and consistently red giant branches.

quotes.quote: In the present paper we analyze NGC 784, 1560, UGC 1281, and UGCA 442. They span a range of rotation velocities from 95 to 150 km/sec (21 cm line full width at 20 peak). WFPC2 on HST reaches below the tip of the red giant branch (RGB) in these galaxies, allowing us to diagnose the star formation history of their thick disk stellar populations.

The noteworthy feature of Figure 6 is the weakness of the color gradient in the disks of these galaxies out to 2 kpc from the plane. The average over the four galaxies is 0.06 0.02 mag/kpc in the sense that the thick disk is redder further from the thin disk at the 3 level. Column (2) of Table 2 records the weighted average V--I for the region of the disk beyond 900 pcs, and column (3) gives the reddening in the Milky Way according to NED and Schlegel (1998). The giant branches of the thick disk regions of these galaxies are consistently red, with V--I colors ranging from 1.39 to 1.54 (column 4).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 784 as an Sdm galaxy with high H flux, many HII regions, and a possible edge-on circumnuclear ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 784: the measured colours compared with the model predictions indicate that 31 10 of the galaxy was created in a burst more than 10 Gyr ago, where the rest of the light is produced by stars formed 9 Myr ago. The colours do not fit well a two-burst model, with ^ 2 =16.78 for the best fit caused mainly by an excessive V-R colour The high H flux (2.2 10^ 12 erg/cm ^ 2 /s) indicates a relatively high SFR ( 1.95 10^ 2 M_ /yr ). This galaxy also shows a large number of HII regions (at least 20 ). The nH image (Figure fig:NGC784 ) shows a complex of HII regions just below the nucleus; a similar but fainter complex is visible in a diametrically opposite part of the galaxy (upper part in the figure). This might be caused by an almost edge-on circum-nuclear ring.

sources.summary: NGC 784 is described as the main dwarf galaxy in a loose group, located in Void Number 8, starbursting, and close enough to AGC 111164 for possible interaction.

quotes.quote: The main galaxies in these two loose groups are also dwarf galaxies, namely NGC 784 and NGC 672. The NGC 784 group is located in Void Number 8 by Pustilnik2019 . Despite being in low-density environments, loose associations of dwarf galaxies are not uncommon e.g., Tully2006 . One SHIELD galaxy is within 200 kpc of its nearest neighbors and warrants additional discussion. AGC 111164 is located 140 kpc from NGC 784, the main galaxy in one of the aforementioned groups. NGC 784 is a starburst dwarf galaxy with a stellar mass of 6 10^8 \ McQuinn2010b . At this 3D physical separation, the evolution of each of the systems in this dwarf galaxy pair may be impacted by the gravitational presence of the other.

sources.summary: NGC0784 shows little difference between optical and infrared stellar velocity profiles, with possible dust absorption causing surface-density and stellar-component differences.

quotes.quote: NGC0784 : Mass modelling of this galaxy shows that there is little difference between the stellar velocity profile and hence the dark matter halo profile found using the -band and 3.6 m data. This difference may happen due to the fact that light observed in the -band is absorbed by the dust while observation at 3.6 m is not affected by it. Due to the presence of the dust, the stellar surface density profile may differ when observed through the -band and 3.6 m . The difference in surface density profile will result in a difference in the stellar velocity component. The detailed verification of this point is beyond the scope of this study.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 1501 as Type 3: symmetric inside but distorted outside, with western-edge gas suggesting possible gas accretion as the lopsidedness cause.

quotes.quote: UGC 1501 is very symmetric in the inner parts, but shows distortions in the outer parts (Type 3). The velocity field reveals gas which seems to be attached to the western edge. Therefore, gas accretion might be the possible cause of the lopsidedness.

gmw_00001743.name: NGC3359
gmw_00001743.overview: NGC 3359 is a nearby, strongly barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1708.01260, arXiv:astro-ph/0504393] that is primarily dominated by dark matter across all radii [arXiv:2307.13738]. It is interacting with a nearby dwarf companion galaxy, which is connected to NGC 3359 by a long gas tail or bridge [arXiv:1708.01260, arXiv:0803.0109].

sources.summary: NGC 3359 is discussed as the nearby barred spiral host or interaction partner of the Little Cub, with similar distance estimates, 90 kpc projected separation, and gas stripping evidence.

quotes.quote: Environment The Little Cub has been previously and independently identified as a UV source embedded in an isolated \ cloud near the barred spiral galaxy NGC 3359 2012MNRAS.426.2441D and suggested to be a potential satellite of NGC 3359 due to their proximity on the sky ( 14.9 ^ separation) and similar heliocentric velocities 1986ApJ...307..453B . While more precise distance measurements to both NGC 3359 and the Little Cub are required to confirm that the two systems are indeed interacting, the relative velocity of 53 km s ^ 1 suggests that the Little Cub is a satellite of NGC 3359. A recent estimate of the distance to NGC 3359 (20.8 Mpc; 2013AJ....146...86T ) using the Tully--Fisher Relation is in agreement with our estimate of 20.6 Mpc to the Little Cub using the 2000ApJ...529..786M model. At this distance, the separation between the Little Cub and NGC 3359 on the sky places the Little Cub at a projected distance of 90 kpc from its potential host galaxy.

In Figure fig:SDSSWSRT , we show a three-color SDSS image of NGC 3359 and the Little Cub, overlaid with contours from WSRT. \ gas is clearly detected around the Little Cub, exhibiting the highest column density in the region of current star formation. We also note an elongation of \ gas in the direction of NGC 3359, which is a strong indication that gas is being stripped from the Little Cub.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 3359 as a nearby barred spiral with an H I companion, a long tail or bridge, distorted gas, and regular interaction kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 3359 is a nearby SBc galaxy with a dynamical mass of 1.6 10^ 11 and an \ mass of 1.9 10^ 10 . It has well developed spiral structure both in the optical and in . The observations have revealed the presence of an \ companion and a long tail/bridge connecting to the outer spiral structure of NGC 3359 (bottom Fig. \ f_interactions ). The companion has an \ mass of 4.4 10^ 8 . There is also evidence for an optical counterpart.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3359 as having statistically significant metallicity-distribution breaks near its corotation radius, supported by multiple calibration methods and model fits.

quotes.quote: NGC3359 : Galaxy with 94 regions observed by and . In both references the authors found breaks in the radial metallicity distribution using different calibration methods and associated it to a possible effect of the corotation radius. Zahid2011 confirmed the statistical significance of the flattening using the an F-test.

Similarly, according our procedure, polynomials of order 4 and 5, two connected lines and two straight lines with a step fit the data better than the linear model. For all this cases a break in the metallicity distribution is found around 7.9 kpc, near the corotation radius determined by .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3359 as strongly barred, with studied emission regions, clumpy gas affected by bar sweeping, axisymmetric disk rotation, and strong streaming motions in spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 3359: NGC 3359 is a strongly barred galaxy. Its \ regions have been studied ( 1995ApJ...445..161M 1995ApJ...445..161M ) and cataloged ( 2000A&A...354..823R 2000A&A...354..823R ). The structure and kinematics of the \ were analyzed in detail by 1982AJ.....87..751G ( 1982AJ.....87..751G ) and 1986ApJ...307..453B ( 1986ApJ...307..453B ) showing a clumpy distribution and a low surface density within the annular zone of strong star formation, which can be explained as due to the effect of the bar sweeping up gas as it rotates. Analysis of the velocity map shows a disc with an axisymmetric rotation and also evidence of strong non circular motions as confirmed by 2000A&AS..142..259R ( 2000A&AS..142..259R ). Analysis of the residual velocity map (not shown here) shows strong streaming motions in the spiral arms and a strong gradient of gas in the bar.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3359 as a barred spiral with extended, gap-free arms whose near-infrared potential is best matched by precessing-ellipse flow and regular-orbit support.

quotes.quote: A case of a barred-spiral system with a radially extended spiral structure without major gaps and discontinuities along the arms, has been studied by Boonyasait et al. (2005) and Patsis et al. (2009) using a potential estimated from near-infrared observations of NGC 3359. For these `well organized' spiral arms the best matching of the flow at their region has been obtained by a `precessing-ellipses' flow. The spiral structure in the barred-spiral galaxy NGC 3359 is supported by regular orbits. This is an indication that there is a correspondence between morphological features of the arms (extent, presence of gaps etc.) and the dynamical mechanism that sustains them in each individual case.

sources.summary: The paper bases its barred-spiral potential on near-infrared photometry of NGC 3359 and cites its bar-to-rising-rotation-curve ratio as evidence for slow bar rotation.

quotes.quote: Our general model is the same as in tp13 . It is based on a modified version of a potential estimated from the near-infrared photometry of the late type barred-spiral galaxy NGC 3359 boony03, pkgb09 . We used it as a general barred-spiral potential with _p being a free parameter. It is worth noticing that , based on observation by sg82 , estimate the ratio of the semi-major axis of the bar to the length of the rising part of the rotation curve for NGC 3359 to be R_ bar /R_ rise =0.75 . According to this work, the fact that the bar ends before the velocity curve turns over, is an indication for slow bar rotation.

sources.summary: NGC3359 is primarily dark-matter dominated across all radii; its stellar component falls sharply outward because of low modelled mass-to-light values.

quotes.quote: NGC3359 : This one is also primarily a dark matter dominant galaxy, with dark matter dominating over other components across all the radius. The stellar component is comparable with the gas component in the innermost radius. However, as we go towards the outer radius, the stellar component decreases drastically, and the gas component dominates over it. This lower contribution of the star velocity component is a result of very low M/L found from the MCMC modelling for both 3.6 m and -band data.

sources.summary: NGC 3359 is described as having enhanced bar radio emission where star formation is strong, southern-arm emission peaks, weak interarm polarization, and no detected bar polarization.

quotes.quote: NGC 3359 (Fig. 9) has enhanced total radio emission from the bar where star formation is strong as well. The total emission from the southern spiral arm peaks in star-forming regions, which are best visible at 6 cm. Weak polarized emission has been detected in the interarm regions with field lines parallel to the adjacent optical arm. No polarized emission has been detected from the bar, possibly due to insufficient angular resolution and depolarization.

sources.summary: The paper studies H II-region emission-line profiles in the barred spiral NGC 3359, finding low-intensity high-velocity wing features in more than a third of the regions and interpreting them as shell signatures.

quotes.quote: We study the presence of low intensity high velocity components, which we have termed wing features in the integrated \ emission line profiles of the \ region populations of the spiral barred galaxies NGC 1530, NGC 3359 and NGC 6951. We find that more than a third of the \ region line profiles in each galaxy show these components. The highest fraction is obtained in the galaxy whose line profiles show the best S:N, which suggests that wing features of this type may well exist in most, if not all, \ region line profiles.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3359 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3359 : J104636.3+631328 The inner disk shows an extended bar (roughly R 45 ), which is not visible in the final profile, and two bright spiral arms. The broad extension of this arms build the outer disk, so there is no clear elliptical outer structure visible which makes the photometric inclination (ellipticity) and PA measurements highly uncertain. The outer slope is influenced by a rather uncertain sky estimate in both bands, but the apparent downbending break at 200 is not due to a sky error, but coincides with the region where the two outer arms dominate.

gmw_00001744.name: NGC7741
gmw_00001744.overview: NGC 7741 is a bulgeless, barred spiral galaxy characterized by high surface brightness [arXiv:2307.13738] and a strong central bar [arXiv:0909.1816, arXiv:astro-ph/0504393]. The galaxy features two short, flocculent spiral arms [arXiv:0909.1816] and hosts numerous H II emission regions distributed along both the bar and its arms [arXiv:2307.13738, arXiv:astro-ph/0504393].

sources.summary: NGC7741 differs strongly between infrared and optical stellar/halo components, is poorly fit relative to the sample, and is identified as a barred high-surface-brightness galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC7741 : There is a significant difference in stellar and hence the halo component for this galaxy while using the 3.6 m and -band data. The fitting of this galaxy is not good in comparison to other sources of this study. For the 3.6 m data, it shows similar distribution as mentioned in case of NGC1156, NGC4068, NGC7292 and NGC7610, but for the -band data, the stellar component dominates the dark matter and gas components in all the radii. This one is a barred, high-surface brightness galaxy with many HII regions in the bar and spiral arms.

ghasp_mass_model_wise also studied the mass models of this galaxy. They combined H rotation curves from GHASP survey ghasp_rotc_epinat2008 with H i rotation curves from archival data. They used two dark matter profiles, i.e., pseudo-Isothermal and NFW; and the maximal disk model for the mass modelling. Our results are similar to them in two cases.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7741 as a bulgeless, strongly barred galaxy with flocculent arms, weak bar molecular content, strong bar gas flows, and derived resonance radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 7741 has a prominent bar with axis ratio 0.36, two short and flocculent spiral arms including a significant amount of diffuse ionised gas and many \ regions e.g., Duvaletal1991, Blocketal2004, Laurikainenetal2004 . This bulge-less galaxy has a mean star formation efficiency of 0.1 \ MartinFriedli1997 , with very weak \ content in the bar Braineetal1993 . Duvaletal1991 found strong ionised gas flows in the bar region and a ratio of 30 between the bar mass and that of the inner disk inside the bar radius. Our rotation curve agrees with the low-resolution curve of Duvaletal1985 and Garridoetal2002 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7741 as a strongly barred galaxy with many emission regions along the bar and clear non-circular motions, but only a modest velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: NGC 7741: This galaxy shows a strong bar in the , blue and Ks band images. Many \ regions are located along the bar. The velocity map clearly shows non-circular motions, whereas the velocity gradient is not too strong (compared with NGC 2903 for example).

gmw_00001745.name: KUG 0810+227
gmw_00001745.overview: KUG 0810+227 is a post-starburst, E+A galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.022 [arXiv:2201.03744, arXiv:2312.03842]. The galaxy features rotating ionized gas [arXiv:2304.10419] and hosted the optical, ultraviolet, and X-ray variable tidal disruption event AT2019azh [arXiv:2304.10419, arXiv:2312.03842].

sources.summary: KUG 0810+227 is the E+A host of AT2019azh at z=0.022; spectra show host absorption/emission, FIRST non-detection limits its radio/AGN contribution, though low-luminosity AGN cannot be excluded.

quotes.quote: The source was coincident with the nucleus of the E+A galaxy KUG 0810+227, with a redshift of z=0.022 (luminosity distance of 96 Mpc). Spectra obtained by the Nordic Optical Telescope Unbiased Transient Survey (NUTS) on 2019 February 22 NUTS_Atel , ePESSTO on 2019 February 25 ePESSTO_Atel , and the Spectral Energy Distribution Machine mounted on the Palomar 60-in telescope on 2019 February 24 and March 10 ZTF_Atel all revealed a blue, featureless spectrum with narrow emission and Balmer absorption features associated with the host galaxy.

Archival Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-cm (FIRST) survey observations from January 1996 of the host galaxy of AT2019azh show no detection at 1.4 GHz, with a 3 upper limit of 0.41 mJy Becker1995 . These observations place a strong upper bound on the host galaxy contribution to the radio emission and make recent AGN activity in the galaxy unlikely. Whilst Hinkle2021 were also able to rule out strong AGN activity based on optical properties of the host galaxy, they found that optical and X-ray observations cannot rule out the presence of a low-luminosity AGN in the host galaxy, KUG 0810+227.
